revision.comment: Correcting a link.: Changed link from [[Shazam (wizard)]] to [[Wizard Shazam]] using [[User:BilledMammal/Move+|Move+]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|2013–2023 superhero media franchise}} {{pp|small=yes}} {{Redirect|DC Movie Universe|the series of direct-to-video animated films|DC Animated Movie Universe|its successor|DC Universe (franchise)}} {{Use American English|date=November 2022}} {{Use mdy dates|date=May 2025}} {{Infobox media franchise | italic_title = no | title = {{noitalic|<!--WARNING! THERE IS CONSENSUS NOT TO USE "DC UNIVERSE" -->DC Extended Universe<!-- THERE IS CONSENSUS NOT TO USE "DC UNIVERSE" -->}} | image = DC Comics logo.svg | image_size = 150px | caption = DC logo used from 2016 to 2024 | origin = ''[[Man of Steel (film)|Man of Steel]]'' (2013) | owner = [[Warner Bros. Discovery]] | years = 2013–2023 | based_on = [[DC Universe]] by [[DC Comics]] }} The '''DC Extended Universe''' ('''DCEU''') is<!-- DO NOT CHANGE TO "WAS" PER MOS:FILMLEAD --> an American [[media franchise]] and [[shared universe]] centered on a series of [[superhero film]]s distributed by [[Warner Bros. Pictures]]. It is based on characters that appear in [[American comic book]]s published by [[DC Comics]]. The DCEU also includes comic books, short films, novels, and video games. Like the original [[DC Universe]] in comic books, the DCEU is established by [[Crossover (fiction)|crossing over]] common plot elements, settings, cast, and characters.
Warner Bros. began trying to bring various DC Comics superheroes together in films in 2002, when [[Wolfgang Petersen]] was to direct a crossover of the ''[[Superman in film|Superman]]'' and ''[[Batman in film|Batman]]'' film franchises. A planned [[Justice League]] film was put on hold in 2008. Initial universe plans were scrapped after the 2011 film ''[[Green Lantern (film)|Green Lantern]]'' was a critical and commercial failure. Warner Bros. finally established its shared universe with the 2013 film ''[[Man of Steel (film)|Man of Steel]]'' and 2016's ''[[Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice]]''. This was followed by 13 [[DC Extended Universe#Films|films]] and the [[Peacemaker season 1|first season]] of ''[[Peacemaker (TV series)|Peacemaker]]'', a television series for [[HBO Max]]. The DCEU's 15th and final film, ''[[Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom]]'', was released in 2023.
The DCEU is the [[List of highest-grossing films#Highest-grossing franchises and film series|ninth-highest-grossing film franchise]] and the [[List of highest-grossing superhero films#Highest-grossing superhero film franchises and series|fifth-highest-grossing superhero film franchise]], having grossed more than $7 billion at the global box office. The [[List of films based on DC Comics publications|highest-grossing DC Comics–based film]] is ''[[Aquaman (film)|Aquaman]]'' (2018), which earned more than $1.15 billion worldwide, while several of the franchise's films failed to [[break even]] theatrically, being considered [[box-office bomb]]s. Reception to the franchise was generally mixed among critics and fans.
A new rebooted franchise of films and television series, the [[DC Universe (franchise)|DC Universe]] (DCU), was released in 2024 and was created by [[James Gunn]] and [[Peter Safran]], who were appointed co-chairmen and co-CEOs of [[DC Studios]] in a late-2022 restructuring. Certain DCEU characters, such as [[Peacemaker (DC Extended Universe)|Peacemaker]], [[Amanda Waller (DC Extended Universe)|Amanda Waller]], and [[Jamie Reyes|Blue Beetle]], are played by the same actors in the DCU, while the [[Peacemaker season 2|second season of ''Peacemaker'']] takes place in the new universe.
== Etymology == Upon announcement of the film series, the universe was commonly called the "DC Cinematic Universe" (DCCU) by fans and the media, in keeping with the naming convention of the already established [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] (MCU).<ref name="DCMarvelForbes" /><ref name="DCCUYahoo" /><ref name="DCCU-EW" /> Keith Staskiewicz, writing for ''[[Entertainment Weekly]]'', jokingly coined the term "DC Extended Universe" (DCEU) in an article about ''[[Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice]]'' on July 1, 2015.<ref name="DCEU-EW" /><ref name="DCEU-SR" /> This term and the abbreviation DCEU quickly spread among the press and fans who took it as the official name of the franchise over the following years.<ref name="TheyAreLegend" /><ref name="BvSEmpireCover" /> According to ''[[Vulture.com|Vulture]]'' writer Abraham Riesman, DC confirmed to him in September 2017 that the term was not used internally and they did not consider it official.<ref name="DCEU-SR" />
In 2016, as part of ''DC Films Presents: Dawn of the Justice League'', both [[Geoff Johns]] and [[Kevin Smith]] referred to the franchise's name as being the "Justice League Universe".<ref name="JLU-SR" /> During the [[DC Films]] panel at [[San Diego Comic-Con]] 2018, a video banner displayed the words "Welcome to the Worlds of DC", after showcasing some upcoming films. As a result, some media outlets interpreted this as DC officially naming their shared film universe as the "Worlds of DC",<ref name="WoDC-SR" /><ref name="WoDCForbes" /> but in March 2020, [[Jim Lee]] referred to the franchise as the DC Extended Universe at [[C2E2]].<ref name="LeeDCEU" /> The franchise was officially titled DC Extended Universe when the WarnerMedia streaming service [[HBO Max]] launched the following May.<ref name="HBOMaxDCEU" />
Following the appointment of [[Peter Safran]] and [[James Gunn]] as heads of [[DC Studios]], [[Warner Bros. Discovery]] (created from the merger between [[WarnerMedia]] and [[Discovery, Inc.]]) <!-- DO NOT CHANGE THIS WORDING, SEE TALK PAGE -->referred to DC's film and television series as part of the [[DC Universe (franchise)|"DC Universe" (DCU)]], which some media outlets interpreted as a rebranding of the DCEU.<ref name="RebrandDCU" />
== Development and history == {{main|Development of the DC Extended Universe}} In 2002, [[Wolfgang Petersen]] was set to direct a ''[[Superman in film#Batman vs. Superman|Batman vs. Superman]]'' film from a script by [[Akiva Goldsman]],<ref name="BvSNewsweek" /> but the project was shelved by Warner Bros. to focus on individual Superman and Batman projects.<ref name="DevelopmentHell" /><ref name="BalePetersenMTV" /> In 2008, a planned ''[[Justice League]]'' film directed by [[George Miller (director)|George Miller]] and titled ''[[Justice League: Mortal]]'' was placed on indefinite hold after it failed to secure [[tax breaks]] for filming in Australia.<ref name="MillerVariety" /><ref name="JusticeLeagueCanceled" /> ''[[Man of Steel (film)|Man of Steel]]'', a [[Reboot (fiction)|reboot]] of the [[Superman in film|''Superman'' film series]] released in 2013,<ref name="ManOfSteelEW" /> was intended to launch a shared universe if successful.<ref name="MoSBleedingCool" /><ref name="ManofSteelSR" /> Following the film's release, Warner Bros. announced a follow-up entitled ''[[Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice]]'' (2016), establishing the DCEU.<ref name="LATimesCC2013" /><ref name="EWCC2013" /> A slate of 11 DCEU films was then announced in 2014.<ref name="2014Slate" />
In 2016, Warner Bros. established [[DC Films]] after ''Batman v Superman'' did not meet Warner Bros.' box office expectations and received negative responses from fans and critics. The division, led by [[Geoff Johns]] and [[Jon Berg]], was to oversee production and form a cohesive creative direction for the DCEU.<ref name="AffleckEP" /><ref name="Shakeup" /><ref name="WarnerBrosResponse" /> After the failure of ''[[Justice League (film)|Justice League]]'' (2017), Johns and Berg were replaced by [[Walter Hamada]] in 2018.<ref name="HamadaVariety" /> The DCEU expanded to television with the release of the [[Peacemaker season 1|first season]] of ''[[Peacemaker (TV series)|Peacemaker]]'' (2022) on [[HBO Max]].<ref name="PeacemakerDeadline" />
[[Discovery, Inc.]] completed its acquisition of DC and Warner Bros. parent company [[WarnerMedia]] in 2022, forming a new company named [[Warner Bros. Discovery]] (WBD) with [[David Zaslav]] as CEO. Zaslav sought to overhaul the DCEU, and began searching for a creative leader akin to [[Marvel Studios]] president [[Kevin Feige]] to lead DC's film and television projects.<ref name="OverhaulVariety" /> [[James Gunn]] and [[Peter Safran]] were subsequently announced as the co-chairmen and CEOs of DC Studios, the successor to DC Films.<ref name="GunnSafranVarietyNov2022" /> In 2023, the duo unveiled their slate of ten DC projects which were to be part of the [[DC Universe (franchise)|DC Universe]] (DCU), a [[Soft reboot (fiction)|soft reboot]] and [[spiritual successor]] to the DCEU.<ref name="DCUJan2023Announcement" />

=== ''Man of Steel'' (2013) === {{Main|Man of Steel (film){{!}}''Man of Steel'' (film)}} [[File:Zack Snyder by Gage Skidmore 2.jpg|thumb|upright|[[Zack Snyder]], the director of ''Man of Steel'', ''Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice'', and co-story writer/director of ''Justice League'']]
[[Clark Kent (DC Extended Universe)|Kal-El / Clark Kent]], who arrived on Earth years ago as an infant from [[Krypton (comics)|Krypton]], struggles with why he was sent to Earth. Raised by his adoptive parents, [[Jonathan and Martha Kent]], Clark adopts the persona "[[Superman]]", and learns if his abilities are meant to maintain peace or conquer the world.<ref name="MoSSynopsis" />
During story discussions for ''[[The Dark Knight Rises]]'' (2012), [[David S. Goyer]] told [[Christopher Nolan]] his idea regarding how to present Superman in a modern context.<ref name="NolanVariety" /> Impressed with Goyer's concept, Nolan pitched the idea to the studio,<ref name="NolanLATimes" /> who hired Nolan to produce and Goyer to write based on the financial and critical success of ''[[The Dark Knight]]'' (2008).<ref name="NolanDeadline" /><ref name="FewerBetterWSJ" /> In October 2010, [[Zack Snyder]] was hired to direct the film,<ref name="SnyderMoS" /> and [[Henry Cavill]] was cast as Clark Kent / Superman in January 2011.<ref name="CavillMoS" /> [[Principal photography]] began in August 2011.<ref name="ChicagoBusiness2" /> ''Man of Steel'' was released in North America on June 14, 2013.<ref name="MoSRelease" />
=== ''Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice'' (2016) === {{Main|Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice{{!}}''Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice''}} [[Gotham City]]-based vigilante [[Bruce Wayne (DC Extended Universe)|Batman]] travels to [[Metropolis (comics)|Metropolis]] to preemptively combat Superman, fearing what would happen if the latter is kept unchecked, while another threat endangers humankind.<ref name="BvSSynopsis" />
By June 2013, Warner Bros. were fast-tracking a sequel to ''Man of Steel'', with Snyder and Goyer returning as director and writer, respectively.<ref name="GoyerMoSSequel" /> Nolan was involved in an advisory role as executive producer.<ref name="NolanBvS" /> The sequel included [[Batman]] in a main role, and serves as a reboot of the [[Batman in film|''Batman'' film series]].<ref name="LATimesCC2013" /><ref name="EWCC2013" /> Cavill, [[Amy Adams]], [[Diane Lane]], and [[Laurence Fishburne]] reprised their ''Man of Steel'' roles,<ref name="MomoaBvS" /> while [[Ben Affleck]] was cast as Bruce Wayne / Batman.<ref name="AffleckBvS" /> [[Chris Terrio]] was hired to rewrite Goyer's script.<ref name="TerrioBvS" /> Originally scheduled to be released in July 2015, the release date was changed to May 6, 2016, to give the filmmakers "time to realize fully their vision, given the complex visual nature of the story".<ref name="BvSDelayed" /> The film's title was revealed to be ''Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice''.<ref name="DawnOfJustice" /> Initial filming occurred on October 19, 2013, at [[East Los Angeles College]],<ref name="BvSFilmingLACollege" /> before full [[principal photography]] began in May 2014 and concluded that December.<ref name="BvSFilmingBegins" /><ref name="BvSFilmingEnds" /> Production took place in Detroit, Illinois, [[New Mexico]], Africa and the South Pacific.<ref name="BvSFilmingBegins" /><ref name="BvSNewMexico" /> After another date change, the film was released in North America on March 25, 2016.<ref name="BvSRelease" />
''Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice'' is set 18 months after the events of ''Man of Steel''.<ref name="BvS18MoS" /> The film introduces [[Gal Gadot]] as [[Diana Prince (DC Extended Universe)|Diana Prince / Wonder Woman]],<ref name="GadotBvS" /> [[Ezra Miller]] as [[Barry Allen (DC Extended Universe)|Barry Allen / The Flash]],<ref name="MillerBvS" /> [[Jason Momoa]] as [[Arthur Curry (DC Extended Universe)|Arthur Curry / Aquaman]],<ref name="MomoaBvS" /> [[Ray Fisher (actor)|Ray Fisher]] as [[Victor Stone (DC Extended Universe)|Victor Stone / Cyborg]],<ref name="FisherBvS" /> and [[Joe Morton]] as Dr. [[Silas Stone]].<ref name="MortonBvS" /> [[Steppenwolf (character)|Steppenwolf]], who serves as the main antagonist in ''[[Justice League (film)|Justice League]]'' (2017), was introduced through a brief scene not included in the film's theatrical release but instead revealed online by Warner Bros. on March 28,<ref name="BvSDeletedScene" /> before being included in the ''Ultimate Edition'' home video release.<ref name="BvSUltimateEdition" />
=== ''Suicide Squad'' (2016) === {{Main|Suicide Squad (2016 film){{!}}''Suicide Squad'' (2016 film)}} After Superman's death, a secret government agency recruits imprisoned supervillains to execute dangerous black ops missions and rescue the world from a powerful threat, in exchange for clemency.<ref name="SuicideSquadSynopsis" />
In February 2009, prior to the development of the DCEU, Warner Bros. was developing a [[Suicide Squad]] film, with [[Dan Lin]] producing and [[Justin Marks (writer)|Justin Marks]] writing the script.<ref name="SSDevelopment" /> Warner Bros. announced ''Suicide Squad'' in October 2014, with [[David Ayer]] as director,<ref name="2014Slate" /> also serving as screenwriter.<ref name="SSAyerWriter" /> The main cast included [[Will Smith]], [[Margot Robbie]], [[Jared Leto]], [[Jai Courtney]], [[Jay Hernandez]], [[Adewale Akinnuoye-Agbaje]], [[Karen Fukuhara]], [[Cara Delevingne]], [[Viola Davis]], and [[Joel Kinnaman]].<ref name="SSCast" /> Principal photography took place from April to August 2015 in [[Toronto]].<ref name="SSFilmingBegin" /><ref name="SSFilmingEnd" /><ref name="SSFilmingToronto" /> ''Suicide Squad'' was released in North America on August 5, 2016.<ref name="2016Dates" />
''Suicide Squad'' is set after the events of ''Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice''.<ref name="SSAfterBvS" /> Affleck and Miller reprise their roles as Bruce Wayne / Batman and Barry Allen / The Flash from that film.<ref name="SSAfterBvS" /> In a [[post-credits scene|mid-credits scene]], [[Amanda Waller (DC Extended Universe)|Amanda Waller]] (portrayed by Davis) meets Wayne in a restaurant and hands him a dossier containing information on future members of the [[Justice League]].<ref name="SSPostCredits" />
=== ''Wonder Woman'' (2017) === {{Main|Wonder Woman (2017 film){{!}}''Wonder Woman'' (2017 film)}} [[File:Patty Jenkins by Gage Skidmore (cropped).jpg|thumb|upright|left|[[Patty Jenkins]], the director of ''Wonder Woman'' and co-writer/director of ''Wonder Woman 1984'']] Diana of Themyscira, an [[Amazons (DC Comics)|Amazon]] warrior who is also the demigoddess daughter of the god [[Zeus (DC Comics)|Zeus]], uses her talents and abilities to help humanity during [[World War I]].
In December 2013, Gal Gadot was cast as Diana Prince / Wonder Woman and signed a three-picture deal which included a solo film.<ref name="GadotBvS" /><ref name="GadotCast" /> The film was announced in October 2014,<ref name="2014Slate" /> and [[Michelle MacLaren]] was confirmed to direct from a screenplay by [[Jason Fuchs]] the next month.<ref name="MacLarenWW" /><ref name="FuchsWW" /> MacLaren left the project the following year in April due to creative differences,<ref name="MacLarenExit" /> and [[Patty Jenkins]] was hired to replace her.<ref name="JenkinsWW" /> Filming occurred from November 2015 to May 2016 and took place in the United Kingdom, France and Italy.<ref name="WWFilmingBegin" /><ref name="WWFilmingEnd" /> ''Wonder Woman'' was released in North America on June 2, 2017.<ref name="2016Dates" />
=== ''Justice League'' (2017) === {{Main|Justice League (film){{!}}''Justice League'' (film)}} Motivated by Superman's death at the hands of [[Doomsday (DC Comics)|Doomsday]], Batman and Wonder Woman assemble a team of [[metahumans]] to stop the threat of Steppenwolf, who is in search of three [[Mother Box]]es scattered across Earth.<ref name="JLSynopsis" />
By June 2013, Goyer was set to write ''Justice League'' as part of a three-film deal he signed for ''Man of Steel''.<ref name="GoyerMoSSequel" /> Snyder was confirmed to return as director in April 2014,<ref name="SynderJL" /> and the film was announced in October as ''Justice League Part One''.<ref name="2014Slate" /> In 2016, Chris Terrio was revealed to have written the script,<ref name="TerrioJL" /> and the title was changed to simply ''Justice League'' by June.<ref name="JLTitleChange" /> Affleck, Cavill, Gadot, Momoa, Miller, and Fisher reprised their respective roles from previous films.<ref name="JLCastReprise" /><ref name="JLCastReprise2" /><ref name="JLCastReprise3" /> Filming took place from April to October 2016 at [[Warner Bros. Studios, Leavesden]] in England, as well as locations around London and in [[Iceland]].<ref name="JLFilming" /><ref name="JLFilmingEnd" /> In May 2017, Snyder stepped down from his duties on the film due to his daughter's death; [[Joss Whedon]] filled his position on post-production, with additional scenes being written and directed by him.<ref name="WhedonJL" /> Though Whedon was not officially credited for his role as post-production director, he completed enough additional work for a screenwriting credit on the film.<ref name="WhedonCredit" /> ''Justice League'' was released worldwide on November 17, 2017.<ref name="JLFilming" />
Reprising their roles from previous films are [[Jeremy Irons]] as [[Alfred Pennyworth (DC Extended Universe)|Alfred Pennyworth]], Diane Lane as [[Martha Kent (DC Extended Universe)|Martha Kent]], Amy Adams as [[Lois Lane (DC Extended Universe)|Lois Lane]], [[Jesse Eisenberg]] as [[Lex Luthor (DC Extended Universe)|Lex Luthor]], [[Connie Nielsen]] as [[Hippolyta (DC Extended Universe)|Hippolyta]], [[Robin Wright]] as [[Antiope (character)#Film|Antiope]], and Joe Morton as Silas Stone.<ref name="JLCastReprise" /><ref name="JLCastReprise2" /><ref name="JLCastReprise3" /> ''Justice League'' also introduces [[J. K. Simmons]] as [[Jim Gordon (character)|James Gordon]],<ref name="SimmonsJL" /> [[Amber Heard]] as [[Mera (character)|Mera]],<ref name="HeardJL" /> and [[Billy Crudup]] as [[List of Flash supporting characters|Henry Allen]],<ref name="CrudupJL" /> with [[Joe Manganiello]] appearing as [[Deathstroke|Slade Wilson / Deathstroke]] in a post-credits scene.<ref name="JLPostCredits" />
==== ''Zack Snyder's Justice League'' (2021) ==== {{Main|Zack Snyder's Justice League{{!}}''Zack Snyder's Justice League''}} The divisive reaction to the theatrical cut of ''Justice League'', with Snyder leaving directorial duties and the final cut of the film in the hands of Whedon, led to a fan-driven campaign for a "Snyder Cut" of the film. Arguments were made that Snyder's vision would be more cohesive to the previous films.<ref name="SnyderCutMovement" /> In March 2019, Snyder confirmed his original cut did exist, and stated that it was up to Warner Bros. to release it.<ref name="SnyderCutSR" /> Warner Bros. remained silent regarding the matter, though ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]'' reported in November that Warner Bros. was unlikely to release Snyder's version of ''Justice League'', with one studio insider describing it as "a pipe dream".<ref name="SnyderCutVariety" /> Snyder announced in May 2020 that [[HBO Max]] would be releasing his cut of ''Justice League'' on their service on March 18, 2021.<ref name="HBOMaxZSJL" /> Initially conceived as a four-part miniseries, it was ultimately released as a four-hour film,<ref name="ZSJLOneShot" /> twice the length of the theatrical version.<ref name="ZSJLLength" /> Snyder stated this version is intended to not affect the future of the DCEU continuity, but that it takes place in a slightly alternate universe.<ref name="ZSJLContinuity" /> The new cut cost an estimated $70 million to complete, primarily for visual effects and additional photography.<ref name="ZSJLBudget" /> Some actors returned to their roles for the additional photography.<ref name="ZSJLReshoots" /><ref name="ZSJLReshoots2" /> Work on the cut was completed in January 2021.<ref name="ZSJLComplete" />
Characters in the film who did not appear in the theatrical version include the [[Joker (DC Extended Universe)|Joker]] (with Jared Leto reprising the role from ''Suicide Squad''), General Swanwick / [[Martian Manhunter]] (with [[Harry Lennix]] reprising the role from ''Man of Steel'' and ''Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice''),<ref name="LennixZSJL" /><ref name="LetoZSJL" /> [[Darkseid]] (portrayed by [[Ray Porter]]),<ref name="PorterZSJL" /> [[DeSaad]] (portrayed by [[Peter Guinness (actor)|Peter Guinness]]),<ref name="GuinnessZSJL" /> [[Nuidis Vulko]] (portrayed by [[Willem Dafoe]]),<ref name="DafoeZSJL" /> [[Iris West]] (portrayed by [[Kiersey Clemons]]),<ref name="ClemonsZSJL" /> [[Ryan Choi]] (portrayed by [[Zheng Kai]]),<ref name="ZhengZSJL" /> a [[Green Lantern]], as well as [[Granny Goodness]].<ref name="ZSJLEasterEggs" />
=== ''Aquaman'' (2018) === {{Main|Aquaman (film){{!}}''Aquaman'' (film)}} [[File:James Wan at the 2016 San Diego Comic Con (27976781713) (1).jpg|thumb|left|upright|[[James Wan]], the co-story writer/director of the ''Aquaman'' films]] [[Arthur Curry]], a half-Atlantean assistant lighthouse keeper, sets out to lead the underwater kingdom of [[Atlantis (Aquaman)|Atlantis]] against his half-brother, [[Ocean Master|King Orm]], who seeks to unite the seven underwater kingdoms against the surface world.
Jason Momoa was cast as Arthur Curry / Aquaman in June 2014.<ref name="MomoaBvS" /> By August, [[Will Beall]] and [[Kurt Johnstad]] were writing competing scripts for the character's solo film.<ref name="AquamanWriters" /> ''Aquaman'' was announced in October.<ref name="2014Slate" /> [[James Wan]] was hired as director the following year, as well as to oversee the screenplay by Johnstad.<ref name="WanAquaman" /> [[David Leslie Johnson]] was later brought to write a new script.<ref name="JohnsonNewScriptAquaman" /> Beall returned to write the script, based on a story treatment by Wan and [[Geoff Johns]].<ref name="BeallAquaman" /> Johnson worked on a rewrite of Beall's script with Wan and producer [[Peter Safran]].<ref name="SaframAquamanRewrite" /> Principal photography began in May 2017 in [[Queensland]], Australia and wrapped that October.<ref name="HeardAquaman" /><ref name="AquamanFilmingEnd" /> ''Aquaman'' was released in North America on December 21, 2018.<ref name="AquamanRelease" />
''Aquaman'' is set after the events of ''Justice League'', though critics have noticed numerous inconsistencies between the two theatrically released films.<ref name="AquamanAfterJL" /> Furthermore, Momoa claimed that the film takes place after "[[Zack Snyder's Justice League|Zack's cut]]", with the ending of that version of the film directly tying into the events of ''Aquaman''.<ref name="MomoaAquamanZSJL" /> Heard reprises her role as Mera from ''Justice League'',<ref name="HeardAquaman" /> while Dafoe is introduced as Nuidis Vulko.<ref name="DafoeAquaman" />
=== ''Shazam!'' (2019) === {{Main|Shazam! (film){{!}}''Shazam!'' (film)}} [[File:David F. Sandberg by Gage Skidmore 2.jpg|thumb|upright|[[David F. Sandberg]], the director of the ''Shazam!'' films]] By speaking "Shazam", [[Billy Batson]], a 14-year-old [[foster child]], can turn into an adult superhero, courtesy of an ancient wizard. He attempts to master his powers and defeat the evil forces controlled by [[Doctor Sivana|Dr. Thaddeus Sivana]].<ref name="ShazamSynopsis" />
In August 2014, [[Dwayne Johnson]] announced his attachment to a project about the superhero [[Shazam (DC Comics)|Shazam]], formerly known as Captain Marvel.<ref name="JohnsonShazam" /> Johnson was revealed to be portraying the antihero [[Black Adam]], Shazam's nemesis in the film, with [[Darren Lemke]] set to write the script in September.<ref name="JohnsonBlackAdam" /> In October, Warner Bros. announced ''Shazam'' as a film in its upcoming slate.<ref name="2014Slate" /> By January 2017, [[Henry Gayden]] was working on the script.<ref name="GaydenShazam" /> In July, [[David F. Sandberg]] was hired to direct ''Shazam!''.<ref name="SandbergShazam" /> Johnson was dropped from the project, instead starring in a Black Adam solo film.<ref name="JohnsonShazamNo" /> By October, [[Zachary Levi]] was cast as Shazam,<ref name="LeviShazam" /> and in November, [[Asher Angel]] was cast as Shazam's child-age alter ego, Billy Batson.<ref name="AngelShazam" /> Principal photography began in January 2018 and wrapped that May,<ref name="ShazamFilming" /><ref name="ShazamFilming2" /><ref name="ShazamFilmingEnd" /> with most of the filming taking place at [[Pinewood Toronto Studios]], among other locations around [[Toronto]].<ref name="ShazamPinewood" /> ''Shazam!'' was released in North America on April 5, 2019.<ref name="ShazamRelease" />
''Shazam!'' is set after the events of ''Justice League''.<ref name="ShazamAfterJL" /> Superman makes a [[cameo appearance]] at the end of the film, but his face is not shown as Henry Cavill was unable to reprise the role due to scheduling conflicts.<ref name="ShazamCameo" /> The villain [[Mister Mind]] is introduced in a mid-credits scene.<ref name="ShazamMidCredits" />
=== ''Birds of Prey'' (2020) === {{Main|Birds of Prey (2020 film){{!}}''Birds of Prey'' (2020 film)}} [[File:Cathy Yan, January 2020.jpg|thumb|left|upright|[[Cathy Yan]], the director of ''Birds of Prey'']] When [[Cassandra Cain]], a young girl, comes across a diamond belonging to crime lord [[Black Mask (character)|Black Mask]], [[Harley Quinn (DC Extended Universe)|Harley Quinn]] joins forces with [[Black Canary]], [[Huntress (Helena Bertinelli)|Huntress]], and [[Renee Montoya]] to help protect her.<ref name="BoPSynopsis" /><ref name="WinsteadSmollett-BellBoP" />
By November 2016, a film based on the female superhero team [[Birds of Prey (team)|Birds of Prey]] was in development, with [[Christina Hodson]] signed on as screenwriter.<ref name="HodsonBoP" /> By April 2018, Warner Bros. selected [[Cathy Yan]] as director, with Margot Robbie reprising her role as Harley Quinn from ''Suicide Squad''.<ref name="YanBirdsOfPrey" /> Robbie also acted as producer alongside Sue Kroll and Bryan Unkeless. The film is the first theatrical [[R rating (Motion Picture Association of America)|R-rated]] installment in the DCEU and has a smaller budget than most of the other films.<ref name="BoPRatedR" /> In addition to Robbie's starring role, the cast also includes [[Mary Elizabeth Winstead]], [[Jurnee Smollett-Bell]], [[Rosie Perez]], and [[Ella Jay Basco]].<ref name="PerezBoP" /><ref name="WinsteadBoP" /><ref name="BoPCast" /> The full title of the film was revealed as ''Birds of Prey (and the Fantabulous Emancipation of One Harley Quinn)'' by Robbie.<ref name="BirdsOfPreyTitle" /> Principal photography commenced in January 2019 in Los Angeles and lasted until April.<ref name="BoPFilmingBegin" /><ref name="BoPFilming" /><ref name="BoPFilmingEnd" /> ''Birds of Prey'' held its world premiere in London on January 29, 2020, and was later released in North America on February 7.<ref name="BoPRelease" />
''Birds of Prey'' is set after the events of ''Suicide Squad''.<ref name="BoPSetting" />
=== ''Wonder Woman 1984'' (2020) === {{Main|Wonder Woman 1984{{!}}''Wonder Woman 1984''}} Diana Prince comes into conflict with the [[Soviet Union]] during the [[Cold War]] in the 1980s and finds two formidable foes in the form of [[Maxwell Lord]] and [[Cheetah (character)#Barbara Minerva|Cheetah]].<ref name="WW84Synopsis" />
By June 2017, Geoff Johns and Patty Jenkins had begun work on the story treatment for a ''Wonder Woman'' sequel.<ref name="WWSequelTreatment" /> In July, Johns was working on the script,<ref name="JohnsWWSequel" /> and the sequel was officially announced at [[San Diego Comic-Con]], with Gadot reprising the lead role.<ref name="USATodayCC2017" /> In September, Jenkins signed a deal to return as director,<ref name="JenkinsWWSequel" /> and brought on [[David Callaham]] to pen the script with her and Johns.<ref name="WW84Writers" /> Production began in June 2018 and concluded that December.<ref name="WW84Wrapped" /> Filming locations included the [[District of Columbia]], [[Northern Virginia]], Warner Bros. Studios, Leavesden in the United Kingdom and the islands of [[Tenerife]] and [[Fuerteventura]] in Spain.<ref name="WW84Production" /><ref name="WW84Filming" /><ref name="WW84Filming2" /><ref name="WW84Filming3" /> ''Wonder Woman 1984'' was released in the United States on HBO Max and in theaters simultaneously on December 25, 2020.<ref name="WW84Dec2020Release" />
In a mid-credits scene, [[Lynda Carter]], who starred as Wonder Woman in a [[Wonder Woman (TV series)|1970s television series]], is introduced as [[Asteria (mythology)|Asteria]], a legendary Amazon warrior who possessed ancient golden armor that Gadot's Wonder Woman wears in the film.<ref name="WW84LyndaCarter" />
=== ''The Suicide Squad'' (2021) === {{Main|The Suicide Squad (film){{!}}''The Suicide Squad'' (film)}} [[File:James Gunn (28557194032) (cropped).jpg|thumb|upright|right|[[James Gunn]], the writer/director of ''The Suicide Squad'' and [[showrunner]] of the spin-off series ''Peacemaker'']] Amanda Waller sends a new [[Task Force X]], consisting of both old and new members, on a mission to destroy a [[Nazi Germany|Nazi]]-era laboratory containing experiments.
In March 2016, a ''Suicide Squad'' sequel was announced to be in development, with Ayer returning as director.<ref name="SS2Announced" /> [[Adam Cozad]] was hired to write the script by March 2017.<ref name="CozadSS2" /> By July, a new story treatment was written by [[Zak Penn]].<ref name="PennSS2" /> In September, [[Gavin O'Connor (director)|Gavin O'Connor]] was hired as director and writer after Ayer dropped out.<ref name="O'ConnorSS2THR" /><ref name="O'ConnorSS2Variety" /> O'Connor completed the script with David Bar Katz and [[Todd Stashwick]] by September 2018.<ref name="SS2Writers" /><ref name="SS2DraftComplete" /> The next month, O'Connor left the project as Warner Bros. believed the script was too similar to ''Birds of Prey''.<ref name="O'ConnorExit" /> In 2019, the film was re-titled ''The Suicide Squad'', with [[James Gunn]] directing, from a new script he wrote.<ref name="GunnTheSS" /> Gunn chose the project over various other films Warner Bros. had in development, including a Superman film.<ref name="GunnChoice" /> In March, [[Idris Elba]] joined the cast. He was initially hired to replace Smith as [[Deadshot]], who dropped out due to scheduling conflicts, but was later decided that Elba would play a new character so Smith could return in the future.<ref name="ElbaSmith" /><ref name="ElbaDavisTheSS" /> Robbie, Kinnaman, Courtney, and Davis reprise their roles from ''Suicide Squad''.<ref name="TheSSCast" /> Production began in September 2019 at [[Pinewood Atlanta Studios]] in [[Atlanta]], with additional filming following in [[Panama]], and concluded in February 2020.<ref name="TheSSProductionBegin" /><ref name="CapaldiTheSS" /><ref name="TheSSFilmingBegin" /><ref name="TheSSFilmingEnds" /> ''The Suicide Squad'' began its international release on July 30, 2021, in the United Kingdom, and was released theatrically and on the streaming platform HBO Max in the United States on August 5.<ref name="TheSSReleaseDate" />
=== ''Black Adam'' (2022) === {{Main|Black Adam (film){{!}}''Black Adam'' (film)}} Nearly 5,000 years after he was bestowed with the powers of the Egyptian gods and imprisoned, Black Adam is freed from his tomb, ready to unleash his form of justice on the modern world.<ref name="BlackAdamSynopsis" />
In September 2014, after being considered to play Shazam and [[Lobo (DC Comics)|Lobo]], Dwayne Johnson was cast as Black Adam, a central villain of DC's ''Shazam'' franchise.<ref name="JohnsonBlackAdam" /><ref name="JohnsonLobo" /> The character was intended to be introduced in ''Shazam!'', but Johnson convinced the film's production studios to divide the narrative to focus on Black Adam's origin,<ref name="GaydenShazam" /> with Black Adam and Shazam instead set to cross paths in a future film.<ref name="BlackAdamShazam" /> [[Adam Sztykiel]] was hired as screenwriter.<ref name="SztykielBlackAdam" /> [[Jaume Collet-Serra]] was hired as director in June 2019, with Johnson, [[Beau Flynn]], Hiram Garcia, and [[Dany Garcia]] attached as producers.<ref name="Collet-SerraBlackAdam" /><ref name="Collet-SerraBlackAdam2" /> By September 2020, [[Rory Haines and Sohrab Noshirvani]] had written a new draft of the script.<ref name="HainesNoshirvaniBlackAdam" /> Filming began in April 2021 and wrapped that July.<ref name="BlackAdamFilmingStart" /><ref name="BlackAdamFilmingEnd" /> ''Black Adam'' was released in the United States on October 21, 2022.<ref name="BlackAdamReleaseDate" />
The film features the [[Justice Society of America]], consisting of [[Aldis Hodge]] as [[Hawkman (Carter Hall)|Hawkman]],<ref name="HodgeBlackAdam" /> [[Noah Centineo]] as [[Atom Smasher (DC Comics)|Atom Smasher]],<ref name="CentineoBlackAdam" /> [[Quintessa Swindell]] as [[Cyclone (DC Comics)|Cyclone]],<ref name="SwindellBlackAdam" /> and [[Pierce Brosnan]] as [[Doctor Fate (Kent Nelson)|Doctor Fate]].<ref name="BrosnanBlackAdam" /> Reprising their roles from previous DCEU media are Davis as Amanda Waller, [[Djimon Hounsou]] as the wizard Shazam, and [[Jennifer Holland]] as Emilia Harcourt,<ref name="DavisBlackAdam" /><ref name="Holland_CBM" /> with Henry Cavill appearing as Kal-El / Clark Kent / Superman in a mid-credits scene.<ref name="Cavill_ET" /><ref name="Cavill_CBR" />
=== ''Shazam! Fury of the Gods'' (2023) === {{Main|Shazam! Fury of the Gods{{!}}''Shazam! Fury of the Gods''}} [[Billy Batson (DC Extended Universe)|Billy Batson]] and his foster siblings, who transform into superheroes by saying "Shazam!", are forced to get back into action and fight the Daughters of [[Atlas (mythology)|Atlas]], who want to use a weapon that could destroy the world.
A sequel to ''Shazam!'' entered development in April 2019, with Henry Gayden returning as writer.<ref name="Shazam2" /> David F. Sandberg and Peter Safran returned as director and producer, respectively, and [[Chris Morgan (filmmaker)|Chris Morgan]] was hired as an additional screenwriter.<ref name="Shazam2SandbergSafran" /><ref name="Shazam2ChrisMorgan" /> Zachary Levi reprises his role as the titular superhero, as most of the adult and child cast in the [[Shazam Family]].<ref name="BorthShazam2" /><ref name="Shazam2FamilyReturn" /> At [[DC FanDome]] in August 2020, the film's title was announced as ''Shazam! Fury of the Gods''.<ref name="Shazam2Title" /> Filming began in May 2021 in [[Atlanta]] and concluded that August.<ref name="Shazam2FilmingBegin" /><ref name="Shazam2FilmingEnd" /> ''Shazam! Fury of the Gods'' was released in the United States on March 17, 2023.<ref name="ScheduleShiftAugust2022" />
In January 2023, co-CEOs of DC Studios James Gunn and Peter Safran stated that ''Shazam! Fury of the Gods'' will lead right into the events of ''The Flash''.<ref name="GodsandMonstersDC" />
=== ''The Flash'' (2023) === {{Main|The Flash (film){{!}}''The Flash'' (film)}} Barry Allen travels back in time to prevent his mother's murder, which brings unintended consequences.<ref name="CrudupFlashTalks" />
By July 2013, [[Greg Berlanti]] was developing a film that centered around [[Flash (Barry Allen)|Barry Allen / The Flash]],<ref name="BerlantiFlash" /> and drafted a script alongside Geoff Johns, [[Chris Brancato]], [[Michael Green (writer)|Michael Green]], and [[Marc Guggenheim]].<ref name="FlashWriters" /> In October 2014, Warner Bros. announced ''The Flash'' with a scheduled 2018 release date, and that Ezra Miller was cast in the starring role.<ref name="2014Slate" /><ref name="MillerBvS" /> A new story treatment was being written by [[Phil Lord and Christopher Miller]] the following year in April.<ref name="LordMillerFlash" /> Over the next two years, [[Seth Grahame-Smith]] and [[Rick Famuyiwa]] had individually dropped out as director due to creative differences with the studio.<ref name="Grahame-SmithFlashExit" /><ref name="FamuyiwaFlashExit" /> [[Joby Harold]] was hired to do a page-one rewrite of the script in January 2017, which previously had drafts written by Grahame-Smith and Famuyiwa.<ref name="HaroldFlash" /> In July, the title for the film was changed to ''Flashpoint'',<ref name="USATodayCC2017" /> based on [[Flashpoint (comics)|the comic book storyline of the same name]].<ref name="Flashpoint" /> [[Dan Mazeau]] was brought on as co-writer.<ref name="MazeauFlash" /> In March 2018, [[John Francis Daley]] and [[Jonathan Goldstein (filmmaker)|Jonathan Goldstein]] were hired to co-direct.<ref name="DaleyGoldsteinFlash" /><ref name="DaleyGoldsteinFlash2" /> In 2019, following creative differences with the co-directors' work on the script, Miller was hired to co-write a new draft in collaboration with [[Grant Morrison]].<ref name="MillerMorrisonFlash" /> By July, Daley and Goldstein had left the project, while [[Andy Muschietti]] and Christina Hodson were hired to replace them as director and screenwriter, respectively. [[Barbara Muschietti]] and Michael Disco serve as producers.<ref name="MuschiettiFlash" /> Filming began in April 2021,<ref name="FlashFilmingBegin" /> at Warner Bros. Studios, Leavesden in [[Hertfordshire]], England,<ref name="BarbaraMuschiettiFlash" /> and [[wrap (filmmaking)|wrapped]] that October.<ref name="FlashWrap" /> ''The Flash'' was released on June 16, 2023.<ref name="Jun2023ReleaseUp" />
''The Flash'' explores the [[Multiverse (DC Comics)|multiverse]] concept using the [[Speedster (fiction)|Speed Force]] and features multiple superheroes,<ref name="SpeedForceFlash" /><ref name="FlashRestartDCEU" /> including [[Michael Keaton]] as [[Bruce Wayne (1989 film series character)|Bruce Wayne / Batman]] (reprising his role from the 1989 film ''[[Batman (1989 film)|Batman]]'' and its 1992 sequel ''[[Batman Returns]]'')<ref name="KeatonFlashTalks" /><ref name="MultiverseVanityFair" /> and [[Sasha Calle]] as [[Supergirl (Kara Zor-El)|Supergirl]].<ref name="CalleFlash" /> Ben Affleck also reprises his role as the DCEU version of Bruce Wayne / Batman.<ref name="MultiverseVanityFair" /> The film also "restarts" the DCEU, according to Barbara Muschietti.<ref name="FlashRestartDCEU" /> [[Michael Shannon]] and [[Antje Traue]] reprise their roles as [[General Zod]] and [[Faora|Faora-Ul]], respectively, from ''Man of Steel'',<ref name="ShannonTraueFlash" /> with [[Ron Livingston]] replacing Billy Crudup in the role of Henry Allen, Barry's father, who appeared in ''Justice League'' and its director's cut,<ref name="LivingstonFlash" /><ref name="VerduFlash" /> and Kiersey Clemons returning as Iris West from the latter cut.<ref name="ClemonsFlash" />
In January 2023, co-CEOs of DC Studios James Gunn and Peter Safran stated that ''The Flash'' would "reset" the DCEU continuity and lead right into the events of ''Blue Beetle'', ''Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom'', and their future slate of films in the new DCU continuity, starting with "[[Chapter One: Gods and Monsters]]".<ref name="GodsandMonstersDC2" /><ref name="GodsandMonstersDC" /> Gunn later explained that ''The Flash'' changes some aspects and characters, but not every aspect of the franchise that came before.<ref name="GunnResetCB" />
=== ''Blue Beetle'' (2023) === {{Main|Blue Beetle (film){{!}}''Blue Beetle'' (film)}} [[File: Angel Manuel Soto 01.jpg|thumb|left|upright|[[Ángel Manuel Soto]], the director of ''Blue Beetle'']] After [[Jaime Reyes]] graduates from college and returns to his hometown Palmera City, he is chosen to become a symbiotic host to the Scarab, an ancient alien biotechnological relic that grants him a powerful [[Powered exoskeleton|exoskeleton]] armor, turning him into the superhero [[Blue Beetle]].
In November 2018, a film centered on the Jaime Reyes incarnation of Blue Beetle was in development, with a script written by Gareth Dunnet-Alcocer.<ref name="BlueBeetle"/> By February 2021, [[Ángel Manuel Soto]] signed on as director,<ref name="SotoBlueBeetle"/> and [[Xolo Maridueña]] was cast as the titular character in August.<ref name="XoloBlueBeetle"/> John Rickard and [[Zev Foreman]] serve as producers.<ref name="BlueBeetleCredits" /> Principal photography commenced in May in Atlanta, and wrapped on July 18, 2022, in [[Puerto Rico]].<ref name="BlueBeetleFilming"/><ref name="BlueBeetleWrap" /> Initially developed as an HBO Max exclusive film,<ref name="BlueBeetleHBOMax"/> ''Blue Beetle'' was later changed to a theatrical release, and was released on August 18, 2023.<ref name="BlueBeetleDated"/>
In January 2023, co-CEOs of DC Studios James Gunn and Peter Safran stated that ''Blue Beetle'' directly follows the events of ''The Flash'',<ref name="GodsandMonstersDC"/> where much of the franchise will be "reset" in such a way that ''Blue Beetle'' will be "totally disconnected (from everything that came before) and can be a part of the DCU".<ref name="GodsandMonstersDC2"/><ref name="GunnResetCB"/>
=== ''Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom'' (2023) === {{Main|Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom{{!}}''Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom''}} Four years after the events of [[Aquaman (film)|the first film]], Arthur Curry / Aquaman is forced to protect Atlantis and his loved ones from devastation after an ancient power is unleashed by [[Black Manta|David Kane / Black Manta]] obtaining the cursed Black Trident. To achieve this, he seeks help from an unlikely ally, his brother Orm.
In January 2019, Warner Bros. confirmed development of an ''Aquaman'' sequel, with intentions for James Wan to return as director.<ref name="Aquaman2Confirmed" /> In addition to starring, Momoa has a larger creative role in the sequel; together with his writing partner Thomas Pa'a Sibbett,<ref name="MomoaPa'aSibbett" /> he pitched their idea for the sequel to Warner Bros. and co-wrote the first story treatment.<ref name="Aquaman2Writers" /><ref name="AquamanFollowup" /><ref name="MomoaAquaman2Story" /> In February, David Leslie Johnson-McGoldrick signed on as screenwriter; he completed the script alongside Wan.<ref name="DLJAquaman2" /><ref name="Aquaman2Writers" /> Producer Peter Safran said that the film will explore the other [[Atlantis (Aquaman)|Seven Kingdoms of Atlantis]].<ref name="SafranTHR2019" /> The title was announced as ''Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom''.<ref name="Aquaman2Title" /> Filming occurred from June 2021 to January 2022 in the United Kingdom, Hawaii and Los Angeles.<ref name="Aquaman2FilmingStart" /><ref name="Aquaman2FilmingWrap" /> ''Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom'' was released on December 22, 2023.<ref name="Aquaman2NewDate" />
In January 2023, co-CEOs of DC Studios James Gunn and Peter Safran stated that ''Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom'' would take place following the "reset" of the timeline of the DC film by ''The Flash'', and that it would be directly followed by the DCU's first slate of content, "Chapter One: Gods and Monsters",<ref name="GodsandMonstersDC2" /><ref name="GodsandMonstersDC" /><ref name="GunnResetCB" /> with that franchise's "true start" being the 2025 film ''[[Superman (2025 film)|Superman]]''.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://deadline.com/2023/01/dc-movie-tv-plan-james-gunn-peter-safran-batman-swamp-thing-green-lantern-1235244926/|title=James Gunn & Peter Safran Unveil Big DC Plan With New Movies For Batman & Robin, 'Swamp Thing', 'The Authority'; 'Lanterns' TV Series & More|author=Anthony D'Alessandro|work=Deadline Hollywood|date=January 31, 2023|access-date=February 17, 2023|archive-date=January 31, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230131171031/https://deadline.com/2023/01/dc-movie-tv-plan-james-gunn-peter-safran-batman-swamp-thing-green-lantern-1235244926/|url-status=live}}</ref>
== Short film == === ''Epilogue: Etta's Mission'' (2017) === ''Epilogue: Etta's Mission'' is a short film directed by Patty Jenkins and released on the [[Blu-ray]] edition of ''[[Wonder Woman (2017 film)|Wonder Woman]]'' (2017). It takes place sometime after the main events of the film and focuses on Etta recruiting the remainder of the "Wonder Men" for a new mission involving the Mother Box.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Dumaraog |first=Ana |date=August 11, 2017 |title=Wonder Woman Blu-ray Includes Etta-Focused Epilogue |url=https://screenrant.com/wonder-woman-blu-ray-features-etta/ |access-date=May 23, 2025 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Giroux |first=Jack |date=August 10, 2017 |title='Wonder Woman' Available On Blu-Ray Next Month |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/552671/wonder-woman-blu-ray-release-details/ |access-date=May 23, 2025 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref>
== Television series == {{Series overview | infoA = [[Showrunner]] | width = auto | multiseries = y | dontclose = y }} {{Series overview | series = ''[[Peacemaker (TV series)|Peacemaker]]'' | infoA = y | infoB = y
| color1 = #66543F | link1 = Peacemaker season 1 | episodes1 = 8 | start1 = {{Start date|2022|1|13}} | end1 = {{End date|2022|2|17}} | infoA1 = [[James Gunn]]<ref name="PeacemakerSeasonTwoOrdered" /> | infoA1span = }} {{Series overview/end}}
=== ''Peacemaker'' season 1 (2022) === {{Main|Peacemaker (TV series){{!}}''Peacemaker'' (TV series)|Peacemaker season 1{{!}}''Peacemaker'' season 1}} Task Force X member [[Christopher Smith (DC Extended Universe)|Christopher Smith / Peacemaker]] is sent on a black-ops mission to target "butterflies", individuals possessed by parasitic, insect-like creatures invading the human race.<ref name="PeacemakerPremise" />
By September 2020, a television series spin-off of ''The Suicide Squad'' centered on Peacemaker was in development for [[HBO Max]]. [[James Gunn]] served as an executive producer alongside [[Peter Safran]], wrote its eight episodes and directed several of them.<ref name="PeacemakerDeadline" /> [[John Cena]] reprises his role as Peacemaker from the film.<ref name="PeacemakerDeadline" /> Filming began in [[Vancouver]], Canada in January 2021 and concluded that July.<ref name="PeacemakerFilmingBegin" /><ref name="PeacemakerFilmingEnd" /> The first three episodes of ''Peacemaker'' were released on January 13, 2022, with subsequent episodes released weekly until February 17.<ref name="PeacemakerRelease" /> Soon after the series ended, the series was renewed for a [[Peacemaker season 2|second season]], with Gunn set to write and direct every episode.<ref name="PeacemakerSeasonTwoOrdered" />
The first season of ''Peacemaker'' is set five months after the events of ''The Suicide Squad'',<ref name="PeacemakerTimeline" /> with [[Steve Agee]] and [[Jennifer Holland]] reprising their respective roles as John Economos and Emilia Harcourt from that film.<ref name="AgeePeacemaker" /><ref name="PeacemakerCast" /> [[Viola Davis]],<ref name="DavisPeacemaker" /> [[Jason Momoa]], and [[Ezra Miller]] also make uncredited appearances as their respective DCEU roles of [[Amanda Waller (DC Extended Universe)|Amanda Waller]], [[Arthur Curry (DC Extended Universe)|Arthur Curry / Aquaman]], and [[Barry Allen (DC Extended Universe)|Barry Allen / Flash]], with [[Superman (DC Extended Universe)|Superman]] and [[Diana Prince (DC Extended Universe)|Wonder Woman]] also appearing portrayed by stand-ins, with their faces obscured by the darkness. The series contains references to the wider DC universe, including confirmation that [[Bat-Mite]], [[Doll Man]], [[Matter-Eater Lad]], [[Kite Man]], and [[Green Arrow]] exist in the DCEU.<ref name="PeacemakerMinorCharacters" /> Additionally, a newspaper headline detailing the [[Intergang]] can be briefly seen, ahead of their debut in ''Black Adam''.<ref name="PeacemakerIntergang" />
After the second season was ordered following the positive reactions garnered by the series, with the show's creator James Gunn confirmed to write and direct all planned episodes, and John Cena set to reprise the title role,<ref name="PeacemakerSeasonTwoOrdered" /> Gunn stated that the new season would explore the greater ramifications from the aftermath of the first season's events.<ref name="GunnPeacemakerS2" /> Following the surprise cancellation of the HBO Max film ''[[Batgirl (film)|Batgirl]]'' in August 2022, Gunn reaffirmed that ''Peacemaker''{{'s}} second season was "safe" and would continue development.<ref name="GunnPeacemakerS2Safe" /> Later in the same month he stated that filming was scheduled to begin in 2023,<ref name="PeacemakerS2FilmingBegin" /> but in January 2023 Gunn said that the second season was delayed as he was busy working on other projects.<ref name="PeacemakerS2Delay" />
== Canceled and reworked projects == Over the years, many projects that were at one point supposed to be part of the DCEU have either been moved into a different continuity, outright canceled, or unreleased. Following the end of the DCEU and reboot into the DCU, various projects that were in development have been shelved though they may be repurposed for the new continuity: * {{Italics colon|'''The Amazons'''}} In December 2019, [[Patty Jenkins]] announced a ''Wonder Woman'' spin-off film, focusing on the [[Amazons (DC Comics)|titular race]] of [[Themyscira (DC Comics)|Themyscira]]. Jenkins was intended to serve as a producer,<ref name="JenkinsAmazons">{{cite web |url=https://www.newsarama.com/48175-jenkins-developing-wonder-woman-spin-off-for-the-amazons-report.html |title=Jenkins Developing Wonder Woman Spin-Off for the Amazons – Report |last=Arrant |first=Chris |website=[[Newsarama]] |date=December 9, 2019 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191210010503/https://www.newsarama.com/48175-jenkins-developing-wonder-woman-spin-off-for-the-amazons-report.html |archive-date=December 10, 2019 |url-status=dead}}</ref> while it was later revealed that she and [[Geoff Johns]] had written the story, with a plot that was set chronologically between ''Wonder Woman 1984'' and the planned third film.<ref name="JenkinsWW3">{{cite web |url=https://collider.com/wonder-woman-3-update-patty-jenkins/ |title=Patty Jenkins Already Knows the Story to 'Wonder Woman 3', But Fans Will Have to Wait |last=Chitwood |first=Adam |website=[[Collider (website)|Collider]] |date=December 9, 2019 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191210135136/https://collider.com/wonder-woman-3-update-patty-jenkins/ |archive-date=December 10, 2019 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="JenkinsAmazons2">{{cite web |url=https://www.gamesradar.com/wonder-woman-3-amazons-patty-jenkins-story-arc/ |title=Patty Jenkins on Wonder Woman 3 and The Amazonians spin-off: "There's an arc that I have in mind" |last1=Shepherd |first1=Jack |last2=Maytum |first2=Matt |work=[[Total Film]] |date=April 29, 2020 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200726173727/https://www.gamesradar.com/wonder-woman-3-amazons-patty-jenkins-story-arc/ |archive-date=July 26, 2020 |url-status=live}}</ref> A similar television series for [[HBO Max]] was later announced in January 2023 as a part of the [[DC Universe (franchise)|DC Universe]], titled ''[[DC Universe (franchise)#Paradise Lost|Paradise Lost]]''.<ref name="DCUSlateVariety">{{Cite magazine |last=Vary |first=Adam B. |date=January 31, 2023 |title=New DC Universe Unveils First 10 Projects: 'Superman: Legacy' in 2025, Batman & Robin Movie, Green Lantern Series, Wonder Woman Prequel and More |url=https://variety.com/2023/film/news/dc-universe-superman-legacy-batman-green-lantern-supergirl-booster-gold-1235507426/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230131171516/https://variety.com/2023/film/news/dc-universe-superman-legacy-batman-green-lantern-supergirl-booster-gold-1235507426/ |archive-date=January 31, 2023 |access-date=March 4, 2024|magazine=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}}</ref> * {{Italics colon|'''[[Batgirl (film)|Batgirl]]'''}} In March 2017, [[Joss Whedon]] was hired to write, direct and produce a film centered around [[Barbara Gordon|Barbara Gordon / Batgirl]].<ref name="WhedonBatgirl" /> Production was scheduled to commence in 2018,<ref name="WhedonBatgirlProduction" /> but Whedon stepped down in February.<ref name="WhedonBatgirlExit" /> In April, [[Christina Hodson]] was hired as the screenwriter for ''Batgirl'',<ref name="HodsonBatgirl" /> which she would begin writing after completing work on ''The Flash''.<ref name="DCFilmsFutureNov2019" /> In May 2021, [[Adil El Arbi and Bilall Fallah]] were hired as the film's co-directors, with [[Kristin Burr]] serving as producer.<ref name="ArbiFallahBatgirl" /> In July, [[Leslie Grace]] was cast in the titular role.<ref name="GraceBatgirl"/> Principal photography began in Glasgow in November 2021, and wrapped by the end of March 2022.<ref name="BatgirlFilming" /><ref name="BatgirlFilmingEnd" /> ''Batgirl'' was scheduled to be released sometime in 2022 on [[HBO Max]],<ref name="SimmonsBatgirl" /> though it was reportedly being reconsidered for theatrical release by [[Warner Bros. Discovery]] by April.<ref name="TheatricalBatgirl" /> That August, Warner Bros. Discovery canceled the plans to release the film on HBO Max or theatrically following poor test screenings. The film was deemed not "big" enough for the big screen yet too expensive to be released on HBO Max. Despite the project's cancellation, the studio still hoped to work with Arbi, Fallah and Grace on other DC projects.<ref name="BatgirlNoRelease" /> In January 2023, co-CEO of DC Studios [[Peter Safran]] commented that Zaslav had made the right decision with canceling the project, while providing positive statements regarding its creative team he stated that it was "not releasable". Safran elaborated that "it would not have been able to compete in the theatrical marketplace; it was built for the small screen". The studio however looks to reintroduce the character elsewhere in the franchise, while also intending to work with Arbi and Fallah in the future.<ref name="Batgirl_Variety">{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2023/film/news/batgirl-movie-shelved-dc-studios-head-peter-safran-1235506921/|work=Variety|title=Shelving Batgirl Was the Right Decision, Says New DC Studios Head Peter Safran: 'It Would Have Hurt DC'|author=Earl, William|date=January 31, 2023|access-date=January 31, 2023|archive-date=February 1, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230201032703/https://variety.com/2023/film/news/batgirl-movie-shelved-dc-studios-head-peter-safran-1235506921/|url-status=live}}</ref> * '''[[Ben Affleck]]{{'}}s''' {{Italics colon|'''[[The Batman (film)|The Batman]]'''}} In July 2015, Ben Affleck was in negotiations to direct, produce, star in, and co-write (with Geoff Johns) a Batman film.<ref name="AffleckTheBatmanTalks" /> Affleck was confirmed as the film's director at [[San Diego Comic-Con]] in 2016.<ref name="AffleckTheBatmanConfirmed" /> Affleck stepped down as director in January 2017,<ref name="AffleckTheBatmanExit" /> and [[Matt Reeves]] was hired to direct and co-produce the film the next month.<ref name="ReevesTheBatman" /> Under Reeves, the film shifted focus towards a younger and inexperienced iteration of the character, with the filmmaker focusing on noir and detective fiction aspects from the comics. Affleck eventually left the project altogether and was replaced by [[Robert Pattinson]] as [[Batman]], allowing the film to drop its connections to the DCEU and establishing a separate universe.<ref name="TheBatmanSeparate" /> The film launched what Reeves referred to as "''The Batman'' Epic Crime Saga,"<ref>{{Cite web |last=Yin-Poole |first=Wesley |date=2024-07-24 |title=The Batman Universe Is Called the 'Batman Epic Crime Saga' |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/the-batman-universe-is-called-the-batman-epic-crime-saga |access-date=2025-09-22 |website=IGN |language=en}}</ref> being set to comprise an eventual trilogy of films and spin-off series for television such as [[The Penguin (TV series)|''The Penguin'']].<ref name="BatVerse_Collider">{{cite web|url=https://collider.com/matt-reeves-james-gunn-peter-safran-meeting/|work=Collider|title=Matt Reeves to Meet With James Gunn & Peter Safran for "BatVerse"|author=Vito Oddo, Marco|date=January 12, 2023|accessdate=March 6, 2024|archive-date=January 12, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230112222918/https://collider.com/matt-reeves-james-gunn-peter-safran-meeting/|url-status=live}}</ref> [[DC Studios]] co-CEO [[James Gunn]] confirmed in January 2023 that Reeves' franchise would continue production under the [[DC Elseworlds]] banner, separate from the new [[DC Universe (franchise)|DC Universe]] (DCU) franchise that succeeded the DCEU and was planned to introduce its own iteration of Batman.<ref name="ElseworldsVariety">{{Cite magazine |last=Sharf |first=Zack |date=January 31, 2023 |title='The Batman Part II' Sets 2025 Release Date as Part of Newly Branded 'DC Elseworlds' Projects |url=https://variety.com/2023/film/news/the-batman-2-release-date-october-2025-1235506926/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230201011511/https://variety.com/2023/film/news/the-batman-2-release-date-october-2025-1235506926/ |archive-date=February 1, 2023 |access-date=March 6, 2024|magazine=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}}</ref> * {{Italics colon|'''[[Batman Beyond (live action film)|Batman Beyond]]'''}} In December 2022, it was reported that a ''Batman Beyond'' film starring [[Michael Keaton]] was in development, with a script by Christina Hodson. The plot would have involved an aged Bruce Wayne, continuing the plot threads from his appearance in ''The Flash'', and would have included Catwoman.<ref name="GunnSafran.future_THR">{{cite web|url=https://view.email.hollywoodreporter.com/?qs=27dca022a3daaae65e34d5c9adf6995782cfc61eaab8e66bfca2252281f2c4f81e01033071a71a9c0ecc309f5e4c7262de4febdb0635727011fb3e07e2b20074e11fc572b6d036b7|work=The Hollywood Reporter|title=Heat Vision: December 9, 2022|author=Couch, Aaron & Borys Kit|date=December 9, 2022|access-date=December 10, 2022|archive-date=December 10, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221210034351/https://view.email.hollywoodreporter.com/?qs=27dca022a3daaae65e34d5c9adf6995782cfc61eaab8e66bfca2252281f2c4f81e01033071a71a9c0ecc309f5e4c7262de4febdb0635727011fb3e07e2b20074e11fc572b6d036b7|url-status=live}}</ref> Development on the film was shelved after Gunn and Safran were appointed as co-heads of [[DC Studios]].<ref name="BatmanBeyond_TD">{{cite web|url=https://thedirect.com/article/batman-michael-keaton-reboot-warner-bros|work=The Direct|title=Michael Keaton's Batman Reboot Reportedly Killed at Warner Bros|author=Nebens, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230306234140/https://thedirect.com/article/batman-michael-keaton-reboot-warner-bros|url-status=live}}</ref> In January 2023, Gunn and Safran said that there is potential for a future multiverse project in which they may incorporate Keaton's incarnation of Batman.<ref name="Batman.Keaton_Collider">{{cite web|url=https://collider.com/michael-keaton-batman-james-gunn-comments/|work=Collider|title=James Gunn Says There Are "Possibilities" for Michael Keaton's Batman|author=Massoto, Erick|date=January 31, 2023|access-date=January 31, 2023|archive-date=March 6, |first=Crystal |date=April 10, 2017 |title=The Rock teases his villainous turn as DC's Black Adam |url=https://www.mtv.com/news/3001670/the-rock-black-adam-movie-dc/ |url-status=dead He added that both DC Studios and his production company [[Seven Bucks Productions]] would continue collaborating in the future, and that the studio intended to "continue exploring the most valuable ways Black Adam can be utilized in future DC multiverse chapters".<ref>{{Cite web |last=D'Alessandro |first=Anthony |date=December 20, 2022 |title=Dwayne Johnson Says 'Black Adam' Won't Be Part Of DC's New First Chapter |url=https://deadline.com/2022/12/dwayne-johnson-black-sequel-dc-james-gunn-1235203405/ |access-date=December 20, 2022 |website=Deadline |language=en-US |archive-date=December 20, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221220222240/https://deadline.com/2022/12/dwayne-johnson-black-sequel-dc-james-gunn-1235203405/ |url-status=live}}</ref> * {{Italics colon|'''Black Canary'''}} By August 2021, an HBO Max-exclusive film centered around [[Black Canary|Dinah Lance / Black Canary]] entered development with [[Jurnee Smollett]] reprising her role from ''Birds of Prey'', [[Misha Green]] writing the script, and Sue Kroll producing.<ref name="BlackCanary" /> In June 2022, the script was continuing to be written.<ref name="BlackCanaryCB" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Booster Gold'''}} In September 2015, a [[buddy cop film]] centered around [[Booster Gold|Mike Carter / Booster Gold]] and [[Ted Kord|Ted Kord / Blue Beetle]] was announced to be in development with [[Greg Berlanti]] attached as producer.<ref name="BoosterGold_TB">{{cite web|url=https://www.tracking-board.com/exclusive-warner-bros-developing-booster-goldblue-beetle-movie-with-greg-berlanti-helming/|work=Tracking Board|title={Exclusive} Warner Bros. became an [[Creature Commandos (TV series)|animated series]], and was released as a part of the DC Universe franchise.<ref name="DCUSlateVariety" /> * '''Untitled ''[[Crisis on Infinite Earths]]'' film''': In August 2022, when the Warner Bros. Discovery merger was completed and [[Walter Hamada]] began preparations to leave his role as President of DC Films, it was revealed that prior to these events Warner Bros. had been developing a project based on ''Crisis on Infinite Earths''.<ref name="Crisis_CB.com">{{cite web|url=https://comicbook.com/dc/news/dc-films-plans-reportedly-crisis-on-infinite-earths-movie-event-warner-bros-merger/|work=ComicBook.com|title=DC Films Plans Reportedly Included a Crisis on Infinite Earths Movie Event Before Warner Bros. Following Hamada's departure from the studios, the project's future realization is dependent on Gunn and Safran's plans for the franchise.<ref name="GunnSafran.future_THR" /><ref
