colon|'''Cyborg'''}} In April 2014, [[Ray Fisher (actor)|Ray Fisher]] was cast as [[Cyborg (DC Comics)|Victor Stone / Cyborg]],<ref name="FisherBvS" /> and Warner Bros. announced a film centered around the character was in development the following October.<ref name="2014Slate_EW" /> [[Joe Morton]] was set to reprise his role as Dr. [[Silas Stone]].<ref name="MortonCyborg" /> Morton commented that there were discussions to include scenes involving Cyborg that were [[Versions of Justice League|cut during post-production]] on the theatrical release of ''Justice League''.<ref name="MortonJLCyborg" /> ''Cyborg'' was scheduled to be released on April 3, 2020, before being delayed.<ref name="2014Slate2" /><ref name="CyborgGLCDelayed" /> In April 2020, developments continued;<ref name="CyborgPremise" /> though by 2021, amidst a dispute between the actor and Warner Bros. Pictures regarding an investigation in the reshoot process on ''Justice League'',<ref name="FisherFlashTHR"/> Fisher stated that he would not play the role in any film that had Hamada's involvement.<ref name="FisherRefuses" /><ref name="CyborgDeparture" /> DC Films responded that they would recast the role.<ref name="CyborgNoRecast" /><ref name="FisherJusticeLeague2" /> In March, Fisher said that though he is not opposed to reprising the role in future DCEU films, he would prefer ''Cyborg'' to be directed by either Snyder or [[Rick Famuyiwa]].<ref name="FisherCyborgFamuyiwa" /> In February 2022, when discussing the Justice League's appearance in the [[It's Cow or Never|final episode]] of ''Peacemaker'', Gunn revealed that Cyborg did not feature in the scene due to the studio's "future" plans with the character.<ref name="Cyborg_CB">{{cite web|url=https://www.cinemablend.com/superheroes/the-cryptic-reason-james-gunn-had-for-leaving-batman-and-cyborg-out-of-the-peacemaker-finale|work=CinemaBlend|title=The Cryptic Reason James Gunn Had For Leaving Batman And Cyborg Out Of The Peacemaker Finale|author=Holmes, Adam|date=February 21, 2022|access-date=November 13, 2022|archive-date=November 13, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221113071155/https://www.cinemablend.com/superheroes/the-cryptic-reason-james-gunn-had-for-leaving-batman-and-cyborg-out-of-the-peacemaker-finale|url-status=live}}</ref> In October 2023, however, it was reported that no cast members from ''Justice League'' would reprise their roles in the DC Universe.<ref name="MillerNoReturn">{{Cite magazine |last=Siegel |first=Tatiana |date=October 10, 2023 |title=''Aquaman 2'' Flooded With Drama: Jason Momoa Allegedly Drunk on Set, Amber Heard Scenes Cut, Elon Musk's Letter to WB and More |url=https://variety.com/2023/film/news/aquaman-2-jason-momoa-drunk-claims-amber-heard-cut-scenes-elon-musk-letter-1235747775/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231010150151/https://variety.com/2023/film/news/aquaman-2-jason-momoa-drunk-claims-amber-heard-cut-scenes-elon-musk-letter-1235747775/ |archive-date=October 10, 2023 |access-date=October 10, 2023 |magazine=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}}</ref> * {{Italics colon|'''Deadshot'''}} By December 2016, a film centered around [[Deadshot|Floyd Lawton / Deadshot]] was in development,<ref name="GothamCitySirens" /> with [[Will Smith]] reprising his role from ''Suicide Squad''.<ref name="SmithDeadshot" /> In February 2019, Smith left due to scheduling conflicts,<ref name="SmithExit" /> and by April the character was written out of ''The Suicide Squad'' to allow Smith an opportunity to return in a future film.<ref name="ElbaDavisTheSS" /> By April 2022, production on the film was delayed in favor of other projects due to the production payroll figures that Smith had requested.<ref name="DeadshotDelayRS" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Deathstroke'''}} In October 2017, a film centered around [[Deathstroke|Slade Wilson / Deathstroke]] was announced to be in development with [[Gareth Evans (director)|Gareth Evans]] attached as screenwriter and director from a story by [[Joe Manganiello]],<ref name="DeathstrokeCanceled" /> who was also set to reprise his role from ''Justice League''.<ref name="ManganielloEvansDeathstroke" /> The project was greenlit after Evans impressed executives with his pitch.<ref name="ManganielloEvansDeathstroke" /> By April 2020, Evans was no longer in negotiations to work on the project, while the filmmaker described the story as a "dark" and "unforgiving" origin story, similar in style to Korean noir films.<ref name="EvansDeathstroke" /><ref name="EvansDeathstroke2" /><ref name="DeathstrokeDelay" /> In March 2021 after numerous delays, Manganiello stated that ''Deathstroke'' had been canceled because Warner Bros. did not consider it a priority.<ref name="DeathstrokeCanceled" /> The project was brought back into discussions with the possibility of a revival for Gunn and Safran's future plans of the DCEU in December 2022.<ref name="GunnSafran.future_THR" /> In February 2024, Manganiello revealed that he had written a screenplay during this time, before Gunn later convinced him to let the project and role go due to the soft-reboot occurring with the DC Universe franchise. Despite this, [[Jim Lee]] approached Manganiello to create a graphic novel series based on his unproduced script.<ref name="DeathstrokeGraphicNovelSeries?">{{Cite web|url=https://comicbook.com/movies/news/joe-manganiello-teases-surprising-new-deathstroke-plans-after-advice-from-james-gunn-exclusive/|work=ComicBook.com|title=Joe Manganiello Teases Surprising New Deathstroke Plans After Advice From James Gunn (Exclusive)|author=Burlingame, Russ|date=February 21, 2024|access-date=February 21, 2024|archive-date=February 21, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240221145722/https://comicbook.com/movies/news/joe-manganiello-teases-surprising-new-deathstroke-plans-after-advice-from-james-gunn-exclusive/|url-status=live}}</ref> * '''Untitled ''[[The Flash (film)|The Flash]]'' sequel''': By October 2022, the script for a sequel to ''The Flash'' had already been completed by [[David Leslie Johnson-McGoldrick]], with Warner Bros. anticipating a successful box office run for ''The Flash''.<ref name="GunnSafranFutureDeadline" /> The script was stated to included Keaton's iteration of Batman and Calle's Supergirl.<ref name="VarietyPremiereSecretSequel" /> The studio was not expected to retain [[Ezra Miller]] for future films because of the actor's various controversies and legal issues,<ref name="THRMillerAug2022" /> although some Warner Bros. executives were open to continuing to work with Miller.<ref name="MillerWBJan2023" /> Later that month, DC Studios co-CEOs James Gunn and Peter Safran said that until a decision was made there was potential for Miller to reprise the role in the upcoming DC Universe franchise.<ref name="DCUSlateGizmodo" /> Gunn confirmed that ''The Flash'' would reset the [[Continuity (fiction)|continuity]] of the DCEU and alongside ''[[Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom]]'', would lead into the DCU's first feature film titled ''[[Superman (2025 film)|Superman]]'' (2025).<ref name="DCUSlateGizmodo" /><ref name="MuschiettiMillerJun2023" /> In June, [[Andy Muschietti]] expressed interest in featuring [[Eobard Thawne|Eobard Thawne / Reverse-Flash]], a character who was only hinted at in the first film, as an antagonist for a sequel.<ref name="ReverseFlash" /> Though it was believed that a sequel could happen if the film could achieve financial success,<ref name="FlashSequelProspects" /> financial underperformance and the announcement that no actors from the Snyder's ''Justice League'' would reprise their roles ended chances of a sequel being realized.<ref name="LATWoes" /><ref name="USATodayBomb" /><ref name="BudgetBreakdown" /><ref name="MillerNoReturn" /> * {{Italics colon|'''[[Green Lantern (film)|Green Lantern]]'''}} Originally, the 2011 ''Green Lantern'' film was planned to be the first film in a shared DC cinematic universe, but its planned sequels were canceled in September 2011<ref name="LanternCancellation" /> and the film was eventually left out of continuity due to its critical and financial disappointment.<ref name="GreenLanternTHR" /> The film was later [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively]] established to be set within the multiverse of DC media, designated as Earth-12 in the [[Arrowverse]] crossover event "[[Crisis on Infinite Earths (Arrowverse)|Crisis on Infinite Earths]]".<ref name="ArrowverseMultiverse" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Green Lantern Corps'''}} A film centered around the [[Green Lantern Corps]] was originally announced to be in development in October 2014.<ref name="2014Slate_EW">{{cite magazine |url=https://ew.com/article/2014/10/15/justice-league-green-lantern-wonder-woman-flash-movies/ |title=Warner Bros. announces 10 DC movies, including 'Wonder Woman' |last=Franich |first=Darren|magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |date=October 15, 2014 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170730223226/http://ew.com/article/2014/10/15/justice-league-green-lantern-wonder-woman-flash-movies/ |archive-date=July 30, 2017 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="GLC_IGN">{{cite web |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2015/07/11/comic-con-2015-green-lantern-corps-movie-announced |title=Comic Con 2015: Green Lantern Corps Movie Announced |last=Albert |first=Brian |website=[[IGN]] |date=July 11, 2015 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150713020357/http://www.ign.com/articles/2015/07/11/comic-con-2015-green-lantern-corps-movie-announced |archive-date=July 13, 2015 |url-status=live}}</ref> In January 2017, [[David S. Goyer]] and Justin Rhodes were hired to write the screenplay, based on a story written by Goyer and Geoff Johns, with Goyer intended to also serve as producer. The plot was stated to feature various members of the titular team, with [[Hal Jordan]] and [[John Stewart (character)|John Stewart]] planned to be the lead characters, while Warner Bros. described the film as "''[[Lethal Weapon]]'' in space".<ref name="GLC_Deadline">{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2017/01/green-lantern-corps-warner-bros-david-goyer-justin-rhodes-dc-1201884133/ |title=Warner Bros Sets David Goyer, Justin Rhodes For Green Lantern Corps |last=Fleming |first=Mike Jr. |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=January 12, 2017 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170112210620/http://deadline.com/2017/01/green-lantern-corps-warner-bros-david-goyer-justin-rhodes-dc-1201884133/ |archive-date=January 12, 2017 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="GLC_CB.com">{{cite web |url=https://comicbook.com/dc/2018/06/11/green-lantern-corps-dc-movie-hal-jordan-and-john-stewart/ |title='Green Lantern Corps' Confirmed to Feature Hal Jordan and John Stewart |last=Ridgely |first=Charlie |website=ComicBook.com |date=June 11, 2018 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180612141712/http://comicbook.com/dc/2018/06/11/green-lantern-corps-dc-movie-hal-jordan-and-john-stewart/ |archive-date=June 12, 2018 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="GLC_IGN2">{{cite web |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2018/01/09/green-lantern-corps-still-in-the-works-despite-dceu-shakeup-david-goyer-says |title=Green Lantern Corps. 'Still in the Works' Despite DCEU Shakeup, David Goyer Says |last=Barnett |first=Brian |website=[[IGN]] |date=January 9, 2018 |access-date=March 2, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180110040718/http://www.ign.com/articles/2018/01/09/green-lantern-corps-still-in-the-works-despite-dceu-shakeup-david-goyer-says |archive-date=January 10, 2018 |url-status=live}}</ref> By June 2018, Geoff Johns was hired to do a rewrite on the script in addition to serving as producer, stating that the script at that time was influenced by his work on the [[New 52]] [[Green Lantern]] comics.<ref name="GLC_Variety">{{cite news |url=https://variety.com/2018/film/news/geoff-johns-exiting-as-dc-entertainment-president-1202840461/ |title=Geoff Johns Exiting as DC Entertainment President and Chief Creative Officer |last=Lang |first=Brent |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=June 11, 2018 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180611175617/https://variety.com/2018/film/news/geoff-johns-exiting-as-dc-entertainment-president-1202840461/ |archive-date=June 11, 2018 |url-status=live}}</ref> In July 2019, [[Christopher McQuarrie]] stated that he had worked on a [[Pitch (filmmaking)|pitch]] over a year prior, where the story would have connected to the ''Man of Steel'' sequel that was also in development, explaining that he had since moved on to other projects due to what he perceived as no further movement by Warner Bros. to proactively develop the film.<ref name="GLC_CB">{{cite web |url=https://www.cinemablend.com/news/2476094/looks-like-christopher-mcquarrie-pitched-both-superman-and-green-lantern-movies-to-dc |title=Looks Like Christopher McQuarrie Pitched Both Superman And Green Lantern Movies To DC |last=El-Mahmoud |first=Sarah |website=CinemaBlend |date=July 5, 2019 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190705204956/https://www.cinemablend.com/news/2476094/looks-like-christopher-mcquarrie-pitched-both-superman-and-green-lantern-movies-to-dc |archive-date=July 5, 2019 |url-status=live}}</ref> By November 2019, Johns was expected to deliver his script to Warner Bros. before the end of the year.<ref name="DCFilmsFutureNov2019" /> During the development of ''Zack Snyder's Justice League'', John Stewart / Green Lantern was removed from the film due to the studio already having other plans for character.<ref name="GLC_VF">{{cite web|url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2021/03/zack-snynder-justice-league-ending-explained|title=Zack Snyder Explains That Enigmatic Justice League Ending|first=Anthony|last=Breznican|date=March 18, 2021|access-date=April 1, 2021|publisher=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]]|language=en|archive-date=March 18, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210318160523/https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2021/03/zack-snynder-justice-league-ending-explained|url-status=live}}</ref> ''Green Lantern Corps'' was originally scheduled for a July 24, 2020, release,<ref name="2014Slate2" /> though it was later delayed and scheduled for a tentative release in Warner Bros. Pictures 2022–2023 schedule.<ref name="CyborgGLCDelayed" /><ref name="Update_THR">{{Cite web|date=April 1, 2021|title=Ava DuVernay's 'New Gods', James Wan's 'The Trench' DC Movies Not Moving Forward at Warner Bros.|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/heat-vision/ava-duvernays-new-gods-james-wans-the-trench-dc-movies-not-moving-forward-at-warner-bros|access-date=March 2, 2024|website=The Hollywood Reporter|language=en|archive-date=April 2, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210402191839/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/heat-vision/ava-duvernays-new-gods-james-wans-the-trench-dc-movies-not-moving-forward-at-warner-bros|url-status=live}}</ref> A similar project was later announced as a part of the DC Universe franchise titled, ''[[DC Universe (franchise)#Lanterns|Lanterns]]''.<ref name="DCUSlateVariety" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Gotham City Sirens'''}} A film based on the [[Gotham City Sirens]] (a team consisting of [[Harley Quinn]], [[Catwoman]], and [[Poison Ivy (character)|Poison Ivy]]) was in development by December 2016, with [[David Ayer]] signed on as director and co-producer from a script by [[Geneva Robertson-Dworet]]. [[Margot Robbie]] and [[Jared Leto]] were set to reprise their roles of Quinn and the Joker from ''Suicide Squad'', with Robbie also taking the role of an executive producer.<ref name="GothamCitySirens" /><ref name="LetoGCS" /> The film's development was later postponed in favor of ''Birds of Prey''.<ref name="GCS-BoP" /> In January 2020, Ayer confirmed that the project was still in development,<ref name="GCSOnHold" /> while Robbie stated that ''Birds of Prey'' took priority, as she chose to first to introduce audiences to other lesser-known characters. She also stated that she was still "pushing" forward with ''Gotham City Sirens'', and expressed hope of exploring the dynamics of the characters on screen.<ref name="RobbieGCS" /><ref name="RobbieGCS2" /> In 2022, Robbie revealed that there were plans for the character to return in the franchise,<ref name="HQ-ET" /> and stated that she was continuing to advocate for a romantic relationship to be portrayed between Quinn and Ivy.<ref name="HQDec22CB" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Harley Quinn vs. The Joker'''}} By July 2017, a film featuring Harley Quinn and the Joker was in development with the working title of ''Harley Quinn vs. the Joker'', scheduled to begin production after the completion of ''The Suicide Squad''.<ref name="HQJSpinOff" /><ref name="HQJSpinOff2" /> [[Glenn Ficarra]] and [[John Requa]] were hired as co-writers, co-directors, and co-producers.<ref name="FicarraRequaHQJ" /> In September 2018, the script was completed and submitted to Warner Bros. with principal photography intended to commence after the release of ''Birds of Prey''.<ref name="FicarraRequaHQJ2" /> The film was canceled in February 2019.<ref name="ForbesFuture" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Hourman'''}} In March 2021, a film centered around the character of [[Hourman]] was announced as being in development, after a similar project had previously been considered in 2013 for a television series on [[The CW]]. Written by Gavin James and Neil Widener, the project was intended to continue plans of expanding the franchise with lesser-known characters.<ref name="HourmanDeadline"/> * {{Italics colon|'''John Constantine'''}} In February 2021, a television series focusing on [[John Constantine]] entered development at HBO Max. The series, featuring a young Constantine in contemporary London,<ref name="ConstantineMadameXCanceled" /> was planned to be horror-oriented and would tie in with ''Justice League Dark''. Guy Bolton was hired to write the pilot, while J. J. Abrams was attached as an executive producer.<ref name="ConstantineVariety" /> The creatives involved were determined to have a diverse cast pool, and looked to the non-white actors for the lead role.<ref name="ConstantineTHR" /> By August 2022, production was tentatively scheduled to commence in early-2023.<ref name="Constantine_Deadline" /><ref name="ConstantineMadameXCB" /><ref name="ConstantineSR" /> In September of the same year after four episodes of the series were written, the show was no longer moving forward at HBO Max, in favor of the studio's intentions of developing a [[DC Universe (franchise)#DC Elseworlds|sequel film]] to ''[[Constantine (film)|Constantine]]'' (2005).<ref name="ConstantineMadameXCanceled" /> In October, the series was confirmed to still be in development and was being shopped around to other streaming services.<ref name="HRTurningPoint" /> * '''Untitled Joker film''': In June 2018, a film featuring the Joker entered development with Jared Leto attached as an executive producer in addition to reprising his titular role as the Joker. Though Leto was also initially involved with hiring the film's production crew,<ref name="JokerVariety" /> the film was canceled by February of the following year; a [[Joker (2019 film)|film set within another universe]] was released in 2019,<ref name="ForbesFuture" /> and was later designated a part of the DC Elseworlds franchise.<ref name="ElseworldsVariety" /> * '''Untitled ''Justice League'' sequel''': In October 2014, ''Justice League Part Two'' was announced, with [[Zack Snyder]] returning as director.<ref name="2014Slate" /> [[Deborah Snyder]] later revealed that ''Justice League'' would not be [[List of films split into multiple parts|one film split into two parts]].<ref name="JLOneMovie" /> The film was scheduled to be released on June 14, 2019,<ref name="2016Dates" /> but production was pushed back to accommodate ''The Batman''.<ref name="JL2Delayed" /> Joss Whedon, the director of ''Justice League''{{'s}} reshoots, would eventually rework one of the film's post-credit scenes to tease the Injustice League as villains of a possible sequel.<ref name="InjusticeLeague" /> In October 2017, [[J. K. Simmons]] stated that work on the script was in-progress,<ref name="SimmonsJL2" /> while in December, ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]'' reported that there were "no immediate plans" for Snyder to return as director.<ref name="Shakeup2" /> By 2019, Warner Bros. had prioritized standalone films over the project.<ref name="TsujiharaLATimes" /><ref name="SafranTHR2019" /> [[WarnerMedia]] CEO [[Ann Sarnoff]] stated in March 2021 that ''Zack Snyder's Justice League'' was considered "a storytelling [[cul-de-sac]]" by the studio, with no sequels planned.<ref name="MultiverseNYTimes" /> In August, producer [[Charles Roven]] confirmed the studio's interest in a ''Justice League'' sequel, but added that it was still "a number of years away".<ref name="RovenTHR" /> A sequel was once again under consideration while [[Michael De Luca]] and [[Pamela Abdy]] were in charge of DC Films, before Gunn and Safran took over.<ref name="WW3_THR">{{Cite web |last=Kit |first=Borys |date=December 8, 2022 |title=Patty Jenkins' 'Wonder Woman 3' Not Moving Forward as DC Movies Hit Turning Point (Exclusive) |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-features/wonder-woman-3-not-moving-forward-dc-movies-1235276804/ |access-date=December 8, 2022 |website=The Hollywood Reporter |language=en-US |archive-date=December 8, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221208011649/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-features/wonder-woman-3-not-moving-forward-dc-movies-1235276804/ |url-status=live}}</ref> ''[[The Wall Street Journal]]'' reported in July 2025 that DC Studios was planning to eventually develop a ''Justice League'' film set in the new [[DC Universe (franchise)|DC Universe]] (DCU) franchise, while the DCU film [[Superman (2025 film)|''Superman'']] introduced a prototype team known as the "[[Justice Gang]]."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Fritz |first=Ben |title=Saving a Studio? This Looks Like a Job for Superman! |url=https://www.wsj.com/business/media/superman-james-gunn-peter-safran-dc-studios-6148aced |access-date=2025-09-22 |website=The Wall Street Journal |language=en-US}}</ref> * {{Italics colon|'''Justice League Dark'''}} In January 2013, [[Guillermo del Toro]] began developing a film centered on the [[Justice League Dark]] and submitted a script in November 2014,<ref name="delToroJLD" /><ref name="delToroJLDScript" /> but was no longer attached by June 2015.<ref name="delToroExit" /> In August 2016, [[Doug Liman]] joined to direct with the title ''Dark Universe'', alongside [[Scott Rudin]] producing and [[Michael Gilio]] rewriting the script.<ref name="LimanRudinGilioJLD" /> Liman left the following May due to scheduling conflicts.<ref name="LimanJLDExit" /> In mid-2017, it was retitled ''Justice League Dark'' with [[Gerard Johnstone]] polishing the script.<ref name="USATodayCC2017" /><ref name="JohnstoneJLD" /> By April 2020, the project was redeveloped as a series for HBO Max, with [[J. J. Abrams]] serving as an executive producer.<ref name="AbramsJLD" /> ''Justice League Dark'' was intended to premiere after the individual team members were introduced in their own series, with the studios involved inspired to a similar approach as [[Marvel Television]]'s [[Marvel's Netflix television series|Netflix series]] individually premiering before crossing over in ''[[The Defenders (miniseries)|The Defenders]]'' (2017).<ref name="ConstantineVariety" /> By February 2023, the series was no longer moving forward.<ref name="JLDScrapped" /> * {{Italics colon|'''[[Krypton (TV series)|Krypton]]'''}} In October 2014, ''Man of Steel'' writer David S. Goyer was attached to develop a television series focused on the planet [[Krypton (comics)|Krypton]].<ref name="KryptonAnnouncement" /> During the development, Goyer revealed that the series would use unused concepts for the planet that were created for ''Man of Steel''.<ref name="GoyerInterviewKrypton" /> In early marketing materials for the series and the first teaser that debuted at the 2017 San Diego Comic-Con, the [[Superman logo]] designed for the DCEU films was used to represent the House of El, implying that the series would have been a direct prequel to ''Man of Steel''.<ref name="KryptonLogo" /><ref name="KryptonDCEU" /> During the casting process, the production crew sought an actor who resembled a young [[Henry Cavill]] to portray Seg-El, Superman's grandfather,<ref name="Seg-El" /> while Goyer stated that the series was planned to take place 200 years prior to the events of ''Man of Steel''.<ref name="KryptonTimeline" /> Ultimately, all connections to ''Man of Steel'' were dropped by the series' release in March 2018.<ref name="KryptonDCEU" /> Executive producer [[Cameron Welsh]] later described the series as "adjacent" to both the DCEU and the Arrowverse.<ref name="WelshKrypton" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Lobo'''}} In September 2009, a film centered around [[Lobo (DC Comics)|Lobo]] was in development. [[Guy Ritchie]] and [[Brad Peyton]] were attached to direct at different times,<ref name="RitchieLobo" /><ref name="PeytonLobo" /> while Dwayne Johnson was originally intended to star.<ref name="JohnsonLobo" /><ref name="PeytonLobo2" /> After various iterations, in 2016, [[Jason Fuchs]] was hired as screenwriter.<ref name="FuchsLobo" /> By February 2018, [[Michael Bay]] was attached to direct. Fuchs rewrote the script at Bay's request so the budget could be considerably lowered.<ref name="BayLobo" /> In November 2022, [[Jason Momoa]] revealed that under the direction of Gunn and Safran, a project that he categorized as a "dream come true" was in development,<ref name="MomoaVarietyNov2022" /> which included his favorite comic book character.<ref name="MomoaCBR" /> The project was later reported to be ''Lobo''.<ref name="LoboCollider" /><ref name="LoboCB" /> In January 2023, Gunn and Safran addressed Momoa's involvement with future adaptations of the character, while stating that the actor would not portray two characters in the franchise.<ref name="GodsandMonstersDC" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Madame X'''}} In June 2021, a series focusing on [[Madame Xanadu]] entered development at HBO Max. [[Angela Robinson (filmmaker)|Angela Robinson]] was attached to write and executive produce the series along with J.J. Abrams. The series was planned to tie in with ''Justice League Dark''.<ref name="MadameXTHR" /> By September 2022, the project was dropped by HBO Max and was being shopped around to other streaming services.<ref name="MadameXDeadline" /><ref name="ConstantineMadameXCB" /><ref name="ConstantineMadameXCanceled" /> * '''Untitled ''Man of Steel'' sequel''': By October 2014, a sequel to ''Man of Steel'' was in development,<ref name="2014Slate" /> with Henry Cavill set to reprise his role.<ref name="CavillMoS2" /> [[Matthew Vaughn]] had discussions with the studio to direct the film in 2017,<ref name="VaughnMoS2" /> but after two years, the talks with Warner Bros. ended in March and Vaughn left the film.<ref name="VaughnExit" /> Shortly after in July, Christopher McQuarrie said that he and Cavill had pitched a sequel idea to Warner Bros. over a year prior, with the plot having ties to ''Green Lantern Corps'', but he moved on to other projects due to what he perceived as no movement on the films.<ref name="McQuarrieGLC">{{cite web |last=El-Mahmoud |first=Sarah |date=July 5, 2019 |title=Looks Like Christopher McQuarrie Pitched Both Superman And Green Lantern Movies To DC |url=https://www.cinemablend.com/news/2476094/looks-like-christopher-mcquarrie-pitched-both-superman-and-green-lantern-movies-to-dc |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190705204956/https://www.cinemablend.com/news/2476094/looks-like-christopher-mcquarrie-pitched-both-superman-and-green-lantern-movies-to-dc |archive-date=July 5, 2019 |access-date=July 18, 2019 |website=[[CinemaBlend]]}}</ref> Later, [[Michael B. Jordan]] had pitched his own take on the character, but was not ready to commit due to an already busy schedule.<ref name="DCFilmsFutureNov2019" /> By November, Warner Bros. had entered negotiations with J. J. Abrams about taking control of the project.<ref name="DCFilmsFutureNov2019" /> The film was no longer in active development by May 2020, but Cavill entered negotiations to reprise the role in a different film.<ref name="CavillReturn" /> Snyder later stated that his plans for the plot had included [[Brainiac (character)|Brainiac]] and the Kryptonians who were banished to the [[Phantom Zone]] at the end of ''Man of Steel''.<ref name="SnyderMoS2" /> In 2022, after Cavill reprised his role in ''Black Adam'',<ref name="Cavill_ET"/><ref name="Cavill_CBR"/> a film centered around his iteration of Superman was back in active development, intended to serve as the first film focusing solely on the character since ''Man of Steel''. Charles Roven was hired as producer at that time, with a story pitch written by [[Steven Knight]]. The studio was actively searching for a director.<ref name="HRTurningPoint" /> Cavill confirmed in October that he would reprise the role in multiple future installments,<ref name="Cavill_People">{{cite web|url=https://people.com/movies/henry-cavill-confirms-he-will-return-as-superman-very-small-taste-of-things-to-come/|work=People|title=Henry Cavill Confirms He Will Return as Superman: 'Very Small Taste of Things to Come'|author=McArdle, Tommy|date=October 24, 2022|access-date=December 7, 2022|archive-date=March 22, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230322235101/https://people.com/movies/henry-cavill-confirms-he-will-return-as-superman-very-small-taste-of-things-to-come/|url-status=live}}</ref> but later in December, while De Luca and Abdy had worked closely with Cavill to announce his return as Superman, Gunn and Safran instead opted to develop a film with the character at a younger age.<ref name="GunnSafranFutureDeadline" /><ref name="GunnSafran.future_THR"/> Cavill confirmed that he would no longer play Superman.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2022/film/news/james-gunn-writing-superman-movie-henry-cavill-out-1235461889/|title=James Gunn Writing New 'Superman' Film; Henry Cavill Will Not Return, but Eyed to Play Different DC Character|last=Donnelly|first=Matt|website=Variety.com|date=December 14, 2022|access-date=December 14, 2022|language=en|archive-date=December 15, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221215023607/https://variety.com/2022/film/news/james-gunn-writing-superman-movie-henry-cavill-out-1235461889/|url-status=live}}</ref> Gunn and Safran optioned to move ahead with ''[[Superman (2025 film)|Superman]]'' as a part of the DC Universe franchise instead. * {{Italics colon|'''The Metal Men'''}} In April 2007, a film centered around the [[Metal Men]] entered development with Eric Champnella as screenwriter; [[Lauren Shuler Donner]] and Jack Leslie also joined as producers.<ref name="MetalMenAnnouncement" /> In May 2012, [[Barry Sonnenfeld]] entered negotiations with Warner Bros. to direct the film<ref name="SonnenfeldAnnouncement" /> and was hired the next month.<ref name="MetalMenConfirmed" /> DC Entertainment president [[Diane Nelson (comics)|Diane Nelson]] reiterated in 2013 the company's intentions to make the film.<ref name="NelsonTHR" /> The development was in limbo for several years until October 2021, when Sonnenfeld revealed that the film's story was still being written.<ref name="Sonnenfeld2021" /> In November 2022, Gunn confirmed that he and Safran had plans for the team in the future of the franchise.<ref name="GunnSafranDeadlineNov2022" /> The Metal Men's creator, [[Will Magnus (DC Universe)|Will Magnus]], appeared in the [[HBO Max]] animated series [[Creature Commandos (TV series)|''Creature Commandos'']], the first installment of the new [[DC Universe (franchise)|DC Universe]] (DCU) franchise, voiced by [[Alan Tudyk]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Zalben |first=Alex |date=2024-12-12 |title=Did Creature Commandos Just Introduce Alan Tudyk's Superman Character, Will Magnus? |url=https://comicbookclublive.com/2024/12/12/creature-commandos-alan-tudyk-will-magnus-superman/ |access-date=2025-09-22 |website=Comic Book Club |language=en-US}}</ref> * {{Italics colon|'''New Gods'''}} In March 2018, [[Ava DuVernay]] signed on to direct a film centered around the [[New Gods]]. Initially with a script written by [[Kario Salem]], [[Tom King (comics)|Tom King]] was later brought to co-write the film with DuVernay. [[Darkseid]] was planned to be the main antagonist of the film, and the [[Female Furies]] were set to appear.<ref name="PorterJL" /> The fourth draft of the script was ongoing in 2020.<ref name="DuVernayMay2021" /> In December, DuVernay said that the [[COVID-19 pandemic]] had given her and King time to dig into "the mind and musings of [[Jack Kirby]]".<ref name="DuVernayDec2020" /> ''New Gods'' was canceled in April 2021, though Warner Bros. stated that it could be revived in the future. According to ''[[The Hollywood Reporter]]'', the project faced difficulties due to Darkseid's role in ''Zack Snyder's Justice League'', and Warner Bros. wanted time to pass before the character appeared again.<ref name="THRApr2021" /> Later, DuVernay revealed that [[Mister Miracle]], [[Big Barda]], [[Granny Goodness]], and [[Highfather]] would have appeared in the film.<ref name="DuVernayJul2021" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Nightwing'''}} By February 2017, a film centered around [[Dick Grayson|Dick Grayson / Nightwing]] was in development, with [[Chris McKay]] and [[Bill Dubuque]] signed on as director and screenwriter, respectively.<ref name="McKayDubuqueNightwing" /> Although the script was nearing its final draft,<ref name="McKayNightwing" /> McKay said in June 2021 that the project was delayed due to DC having "other priorities", but also reaffirmed his intentions to still make the film. He also said that the film could possibly be reworked to remove its connections to the DCEU continuity.<ref name="NightwingCB" /> In August 2023, the project was canceled.<ref name=NightwingCanceled/> * '''Untitled ''Black Manta'' film''': In February 2019, Warner Bros. announced a horror-themed spin-off from ''Aquaman'' focusing on the villainous [[The Trench (comics)|Trench]] kingdom. Safran and [[James Wan]] were set to produce, with Noah Gardner and Aidan Fitzgerald writing the script. The film was planned to have a lower production budget than other DCEU films.<ref name="TheTrench" /> The film was expected to be released before ''Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom''.<ref name="SafranTHR2019" /> In April 2021, the project was canceled, though Warner Bros. said that it may be revived in the future.<ref name="THRApr2021" /> In October, Wan revealed that the initially announced name of ''The Trench'' was a working title, to misdirect the audience that the movie was secretly a [[Black Manta]] film.<ref name="TrenchManta" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Plastic Man'''}} By December 2018, a [[Comedy film|comedy]]-action [[Adventure film|adventure]] film based on [[Plastic Man]] was in development with Amanda Idoko hired to write the script.<ref name="IdokoPlasticMan" /> In December 2020, Cat Vasko was hired to do a rewrite of Idoko's screenplay, with the project reworked to be a female-centered film.<ref name="VaskoPlasticMan" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Static Shock'''}} At DC FanDome held in August 2020, a live-action film centered around [[Static (DC Comics)|Static]] was revealed to be in development,<ref name="HudlinStaticShock" /> with Michael B. Jordan joining the production team as a producer alongside [[Reginald Hudlin]] in October.<ref name="JordanStaticShock" /> Walter Hamada described the film as a project that could be developed as an HBO Max-exclusive film.<ref name="MultiverseNYTimes" /> In March 2021, Randy McKinnon was hired as screenwriter.<ref name="McKinnonStaticShock" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Supergirl'''}} In August 2018, a film centered around [[Supergirl (Kara Zor-El)|Kara Zor-El / Supergirl]], was announced to be in development with [[Oren Uziel]] penning the script.<ref name="UzielSupergirl">{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2018/08/supergirl-movie-warner-bros-dc-comics-universe-oren-uziel-script-superman-1202440687 |title='Supergirl' Movie On Drawing Board For Warner Bros/DC; Oren Uziel Scripting |last=Fleming |first=Mike Jr. |date=August 6, 2018 |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]]|access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180806191052/https://deadline.com/2018/08/supergirl-movie-warner-bros-dc-comics-universe-oren-uziel-script-superman-1202440687/ |archive-date=August 6, 2018 |url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="UzielSupergirl2">{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/heat-vision/supergirl-movie-oren-uziel-write-script-warner-bros-1132421 |title='Supergirl' Movie in the Works with '22 Jump Street' Writer |last1=Galuppo |first1=Mia |last2=McMillan |first2=Graeme |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=August 6, 2018 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180806231712/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/heat-vision/supergirl-movie-oren-uziel-write-script-warner-bros-1132421 |archive-date=August 6, 2018 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="SupergirlFemaleDirector">{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2018/08/female-director-scorecard-warner-bros-superhero-wonder-woman-1202441259/ |title=Female Director Scorecard: Warner Bros Lining Up Its Superheroes |last=Busch |first=Anita |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=August 13, 2018 |access-date=March 2, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180814001852/https://deadline.com/2018/08/female-director-scorecard-warner-bros-superhero-wonder-woman-1202441259/ |archive-date=August 14, 2018 |url-status=live}}</ref> In February 2021, [[Sasha Calle]] was officially cast in the role, with the character debuting in ''The Flash''.<ref name="Supergirl_Deadline">{{Cite web|last1=D'Alessandro|first1=Anthony|date=February 19, 2021|title=DC Universe's New Supergirl Is 'Young And The Restless' Actress Sasha Calle; Will Make Debut In Upcoming 'Flash' Film|url=https://deadline.com/2021/02/supergirl-young-and-the-restless-actress-sasha-calle-the-flash-movie-dc-1234697190/|access-date=March 2, 2024|website=Deadline|language=en-US|archive-date=July 31, 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210731140539/https://deadline.com/2021/02/supergirl-young-and-the-restless-actress-sasha-calle-the-flash-movie-dc-1234697190/|url-status=live}}</ref> A similar project was later announced as a part of the DC Universe franchise titled ''[[DC Universe (franchise)#Supergirl (2026)|Supergirl]]'',<ref name="DCUSlateVariety" /> which will star [[Milly Alcock]] in the titular role, and adapt a script written by [[Ana Nogueira]].<ref name="SupergirlDCU_THR">{{cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/supergirl-movie-woman-of-tomorrow-writer-1235646953/|work=The Hollywood Reporter|title=DC Movie 'Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow' Finds Its Writer in Ana Nogueira (Exclusive)|author=Kit, Borys|date=November 14, 2023|accessdate=March 4, 2024|archive-date=November 14, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231114203951/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/supergirl-movie-woman-of-tomorrow-writer-1235646953/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="SupergirlDCU_THR2">{{cite web|url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/supergirl-movie-casts-milly-alcock-1235129348/|work=The Hollywood Reporter|title=Supergirl Found: Milly Alcock to Play Heroine in James Gunn's DC Movies|author=Couch, Aaron & Borys Kit|date=January 29, 2024|access-date=March 5, 2024|archive-date=January 30, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240130022642/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/supergirl-movie-casts-milly-alcock-1235129348/|url-status=live}}</ref> * '''Untitled Val-Zod television series''': By July 2021, a [[Limited series (television)|limited series]] centered around [[Val-Zod]] was in development for HBO Max. Michael B. Jordan was set to produce the series and also potentially star, while Darnell Metayer and Josh Peters were attached to write.<ref name="ValZodCollider" /><ref name="ValZodWriters" /> Jordan previously pitched a feature film centered around an African-American Superman, only to place development on hold due to his busy production schedule.<ref name="DCFilmsFutureNov2019" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Waller'''}} In January 2021, Gunn revealed that he was developing additional spin-off installments to ''The Suicide Squad'' alongside Warner Bros. Entertainment,<ref name="TSS.Spinoff2_CB" /><ref name="TSS.Spinoff2_THR" /> and further clarified his intentions to develop the projects the following year in February.<ref name="TSS.spinoffs_Deadline">{{cite web|url=https://deadline.com/2022/02/peacemaker-renewed-season-2-hbo-max-new-suicide-squad-spinoff-james-gunn-1234934696/|work=Deadline|title=HBO Max Chief "Feeling Good" About Season 2 Of 'Peacemaker', Addresses Potential New 'Suicide Squad' Spinoff Series From James Gunn|author=Andreeva, Nellie|date=February 16, 2022|accessdate=March 4, 2024|archive-date=March 5, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240305042838/https://deadline.com/2022/02/peacemaker-renewed-season-2-hbo-max-new-suicide-squad-spinoff-james-gunn-1234934696/|url-status=live}}</ref> By May of the same year, it was revealed that a spin-off continuation television series of ''Peacemaker'' was in development, which will center around the character of Amanda Waller. Viola Davis was announced to be reprising her role from other DCEU media, in addition to serving as an executive producer on the show. Christal Henry will serve as [[Head writer|writer]], in addition to executive producing alongside James Gunn and Peter Safran.<ref name="Waller_Variety">{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2022/tv/news/viola-davis-amanda-waller-series-hbo-max-peacemaker-suicide-squad-1235257198/|work=Variety|title=Viola Davis in Talks to Star as Amanda Waller in 'Peacemaker' Spinoff Series at HBO Max|author=Otterson, Joe|date=May 3, 2022|accessdate=March 4, 2024|archive-date=May 3, 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220503200127/https://variety.com/2022/tv/news/viola-davis-amanda-waller-series-hbo-max-peacemaker-suicide-squad-1235257198/|url-status=live}}</ref> [[DC Universe (franchise)#Waller|The project]] was later repositioned as a part of the DC Universe franchise.<ref name="DCUSlateVariety" /> * {{Italics colon|'''The Wonder Twins'''}} In February 2022, a film centered around [[Wonder Twins|Zan and Jayne / the Wonder Twins]] entered development for HBO Max. [[Adam Sztykiel]] was slated to make his directorial debut and write the screenplay, with Marty Bowen and [[Wyck Godfrey]] serving as producers.<ref name="WonderTwinsTHR" /> By that April, [[KJ Apa]] and [[Isabel May]] were cast in the lead roles of Zan and Jayna, respectively.<ref name="KJApaIsabelMay" /> Principal photography was scheduled to commence in [[Atlanta]], Georgia in July,<ref name="KJApaIsabelMay" /><ref name="WonderTwinsTHR2" /> but the project was canceled in May following WarnerMedia's merge with Discovery Inc. and the creation of Warner Bros. Discovery. The CEO of the newly formed conglomerate, [[David Zaslav]], felt that the film's estimated $75 million-plus budget would not bolster enough of a return as a straight-to-streaming release. Additionally, Zaslav has a directive mandate that DC Films will first focus on theatrical releases, with internal criticism also stating that the project was conceived in a style deemed "too niche".<ref name="ZaslavProfileWSJ" /><ref name="WonderTwinsTHR2" /> * {{Italics colon|'''Wonder Woman 1854'''}} In April 2024, Zack Snyder revealed that initially there had been plans to have the character of [[Wonder Woman]] in a project with the title of ''Wonder Woman 1854''. Snyder explained that the photograph which Bruce Wayne discovers during the events of ''Justice League'' was the story that was intended to be explored first, depicting the character's continued search for her villainous uncle [[Ares (DC Comics)|Ares]] throughout various time periods of history, and the romantic relationships she had while living as an immortal who does not age. The filmmaker stated that the image was intended to give the audience of glimpse of her time fighting in the [[Crimean War]], while crediting Stephen Berkman with arranging the behind-the-scenes layout of the image. Further elaborating, Snyder stated that Wonder Woman's story in the DCEU would have shown her traveling to anywhere in history where there was conflict with the intent of ending Ares' influence on mankind. After the success of the 2017 [[#Wonder Woman (2017)|titular film]] however, the decision was made to create a sequel instead of another prequel.<ref name="WW1854_Empire">{{cite web|url=https://www.empireonline.com/movies/news/wonder-woman-1854-zack-snyder-explains-scrapped-story-exclusive/|work=Empire|title=Zack Snyder Explains Scrapped Wonder Woman 1854 Story: 'We Talked About It So Much' – Exclusive|author=Travis, Ben|date=April 10, 2024|accessdate=April 13, 2024|archive-date=April 11, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240411155037/https://www.empireonline.com/movies/news/wonder-woman-1854-zack-snyder-explains-scrapped-story-exclusive/|url-status=live}}</ref> * '''Untitled ''Wonder Woman 1984'' sequel''': After principal photography on ''Wonder Woman 1984'' was completed, director and co-writer Patty Jenkins said that the plot for a third ''Wonder Woman'' film had been written. She revealed that the story arc for Wonder Woman was planned over three films, with the third taking place in the present.<ref name="JenkinsWW3-THR" /><ref name="JenkinsWW3-VF" /> Jenkins and Gadot planned to work on other projects before proceeding with the sequel.<ref name="JenkinsWW3Collider" /><ref name="WW84Impact" /> In 2020, Jenkins said she had stories written for two more ''Wonder Woman'' films completed.<ref name="JenkinsWWFuture" /> Two days after ''1984'' was released, a third film was officially greenlit by [[Warner Bros. Pictures]]. Jenkins was set to return as director, with a script she wrote, while Gadot was planned to reprise her role as the titular heroine.<ref name="WW3Deadline" /> In October 2021, [[Gal Gadot]] stated that [[Lynda Carter]] would reprise her role as Asteria from ''1984''.<ref name="CarterWW3" /><ref name="CarterTHR" /> A year later, Jenkins revealed that the script for the film was completed, while also stating that she had tentative plans for further installments.<ref name="WW3_SR">{{cite web |author=Fink, Richard |date=October 7, 2022 |title=Wonder Woman 3 Script Is Complete, Says Patty Jenkins |url=https://screenrant.com/wonder-woman-3-script-sequel-production-updates/ |access-date=October 10, 2022 |work=Screen Rant |archive-date=October 10, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221010200451/https://screenrant.com/wonder-woman-3-script-sequel-production-updates/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Filming was expected to begin in mid-2023,<ref name="WW3GadotFilming" /> but in December the third film was revealed to be no longer moving forward because the script conflicted with the current plans for the DCEU formulated by Gunn and Safran.<ref name="WW3_THR" /><ref name="Walsh">{{Cite magazine |last=Walsh |first=Savannah |date=December 14, 2022 |title=Wonder Woman Director Patty Jenkins Denies Quitting: "I Never Walked Away" |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2022/12/wonder-woman-director-patty-jenkins-denies-quitting-i-never-walked-away |access-date=December 15, 2022 |magazine=Vanity Fair |language=en-US |archive-date=December 15, 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221215122318/https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2022/12/wonder-woman-director-patty-jenkins-denies-quitting-i-never-walked-away |url-status=live}}</ref> * {{Italics colon|'''Zatanna'''}} In November 2018, Warner Bros. was considering developing a film based on [[Zatanna]].<ref name="ZatannaDeadline"/> By March 2021, ''Zatanna'' project was in development as an HBO Max-exclusive film,<ref name="ZatannaCollider"/> and [[Emerald Fennell]] was hired as screenwriter later that month. The film was to be produced by J. J. Abrams.<ref name="FennellZatanna"/> By October 2022, the film was initially scrapped at HBO Max, but later began being shopped around to other streaming services.<ref name="HRTurningPoint" />
== Expanded setting == {{Further|List of DC Multiverse worlds}} In October 2014, [[Geoff Johns]] explained that DC's approach to their films and television series would be different from [[Marvel Studios]]' cinematic universe, stating that their film universe and TV universes would be kept separate within a multiverse to allow "everyone to make the best possible product, to tell the best story, to do the best world".<ref name="GoeffJonesBuzzFeed"/> This divide lasted until January 2020, when the DCEU was retroactively established to have connections to [[The CW]]'s [[Arrowverse]] [[List of DC Multiverse worlds#Arrowverse|continuity]] via an expansion of the multiverse concept, during [[Arrow season 8#ep168|Part 4]] of the "Crisis on Infinite Earths" crossover. [[Ezra Miller]] reprised their role as [[Barry Allen (DC Extended Universe)|Barry Allen]] in a cameo appearance alongside [[Grant Gustin]]'s [[Barry Allen (Arrowverse)|version of the character]]. During their conversation, Miller's Barry gets the idea to call himself "[[The Flash]]" from his alternate self and mentions "Victor" ([[Ray Fisher (actor)|Ray Fisher]]'s [[Victor Stone (DC Extended Universe)|Cyborg]]).<ref name="CoIE-SR" /> Despite their cameo, the episode did not confirm the designated Earth for the DCEU characters.<ref name="CoIEVariety" /><ref name="CoIE-TVLine" />
Miller's cameo opened up more possibilities for crossovers between DC films and the Arrowverse. President of DC Films, [[Walter Hamada]], revealed that prior to "Crisis on Infinite Earths", DC had been structured in a way that the television division had to clear the use of characters with the film division. Now, the company could "really lean into this idea of [the multiverse] and acknowledge the fact there can be a Flash on TV and one in the movies, and you don't have to pick one or the other, and they both exist in this multiverse".<ref name="MultiverseEW" /> Arrowverse creator and executive producer Greg Berlanti agreed, feeling that "moving forward, there's more opportunity to do more things like this",<ref name="MultiverseTHR" /> and was open to more film characters appearing in the Arrowverse.<ref name="MultiverseEW" />
In August 2020, Hamada announced plans to develop a multiverse inspired by the comics. In doing so, the studio also stated that all past, present, and future projects are a part of the same unified multiverse.<ref name="TheBatmanSeparate" /><ref name="MultiverseVanityFair" /><ref name="MultiverseNYTimes" /> ''The Flash'' director [[Andy Muschietti]] further explained that all previous adaptations of DC Comics were a part of the multiverse: "...all the cinematic iterations that we've seen before are valid... all that you've seen exists, and everything that you will see exists, in the same unified multiverse".<ref name="MultiverseVanityFair" /><ref name="MultiverseNYTimes" /> In 2023, the DCEU version of Shazam made a cameo appearance through archival footage in the ''[[Titans (2018 TV series)|Titans]]'' [[Titans (season 4)|season 4]] episode "Dude, Where's My Gar?".<ref>{{cite news |title=Titans Cameo Proves DC Really Didn't Care About Shazam 2 |url=https://screenrant.com/titans-shazam-cameo-dc-not-care/ |work=[[Screen Rant]] |access-date=August 10, 2023 |archive-date=August 10, 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230810232413/https://screenrant.com/titans-shazam-cameo-dc-not-care/ |url-status=live}}</ref>
== Timeline == In the DCEU, the events of the films rarely explicitly make their exact years known.<ref name="DCFilmsTimeline1" /> ''Man of Steel'' takes place after the events of ''Wonder Woman'' and ''Wonder Woman 1984'', which are set in 1918 and 1984, respectively. ''Wonder Woman'' also has bookend scenes set in the present after the events of ''Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice''. The events of ''Batman v Superman'' occur 18 months after the events of ''Man of Steel'', which follows the consequences and the emergence of aliens and metahumans such as [[Superman]].<ref name="BvS18MoS" /> The film concludes with the death of Superman, which is emphasized in ''Suicide Squad'' and ''Justice League'', taking place approximately one and two years later, respectively.<ref name="TimelineCollider" />
[[Jason Momoa]] confirmed that ''Aquaman'' takes place right after ''Zack Snyder's Justice League''.<ref name="MomoaAquamanZSJL" /> ''Aquaman'' is followed by ''Shazam!'', which occurs during the Christmas season of 2018.<ref name="DCFilmsTimeline2" /> The events of ''Birds of Prey'' are set in 2020.<ref name="BoPSetting" /> ''Shazam! Fury of the Gods'' is set about two years after its predecessor, but at least in 2021, because Billy Batson was fourteen in ''Shazam!'' whereas in ''Shazam! Fury of the Gods'' he is seventeen years old.<ref name="Shazam2Timeline" /> ''The Suicide Squad'' is confirmed to take place in 2021.<ref name="TheSuicideSquadTimeline" /> ''Peacemaker'' is set five months after the events of ''The Suicide Squad''.<ref name="PeacemakerTimeline" /> In an episode of ''Peacemaker'' a newspaper page can be seen talking about the upheavals happening in Kahndaq caused by the [[Intergang]] from which it can be inferred that the events of ''Black Adam'' happen shortly after ''Peacemaker''.<ref name="BlackAdamTimeline" /> ''Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom'' is set four years after the first ''Aquaman''.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.msn.com/en-us/movies/news/aquaman-2-premiere-date-actors-film-locations-and-more-hollywood-news/ar-BB1hGNup|title=Aquaman 2 is set four years after its predecessor|website=[[MSN]]|access-date=March 12, 2024|archive-date=January 26, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250126193215/https://www.msn.com/en-us/movies/news/aquaman-2-premiere-date-actors-film-locations-and-more-hollywood-news/ar-BB1hGNup|url-status=live}}</ref> ''The Flash'' is set in 2023 and 2013 of an alternative timeline where [[Michael Keaton]] is Batman.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://screenrant.com/flash-movie-barry-allen-michael-keaton-batman-universe-reason/|title=Why The Flash Goes To The Michael Keaton Batman Timeline|website=[[Screen Rant]] |date=February 14, 2022 |access-date=March 12, 2024|archive-date=September 29, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230929052115/https://screenrant.com/flash-movie-barry-allen-michael-keaton-batman-universe-reason/|url-status=live}}.</ref> ''Blue Beetle'' is the final film in the DCEU timeline, but [[Xolo Maridueña]] will reprise his role in the DCU.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://gamerant.com/james-gunn-dcu-canon-explained-blue-beetle-peacemaker-actors/|title=James Gunn Clarifies What Counts As DCU Canon And Which DCEU Actors Are Staying|date=September 28, 2023 |access-date=March 12, 2024|archive-date=October 14, 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231014054437/https://gamerant.com/james-gunn-dcu-canon-explained-blue-beetle-peacemaker-actors/|url-status=live}}.</ref>

== Reboot == {{main|DC Universe (franchise)}} In January 2023, Gunn and Safran announced the initial ten installments for the DCU, which make up a portion of the slate subtitled "Chapter One: Gods and Monsters". They included the films ''[[Superman (2025 film)|Superman]]'' (2025), ''[[The Authority (film)|The Authority]]'', ''[[The Brave and the Bold (film)|The Brave and the Bold]]'', ''[[Supergirl (2026 film)|Supergirl]]'', and ''[[Swamp Thing (upcoming film)|Swamp Thing]]'', as well as the television series ''[[Waller (TV series)|Waller]]'', ''[[Creature Commandos (TV series)|Creature Commandos]]'', ''[[Lanterns (TV series)|Lanterns]]'', ''[[Paradise Lost (upcoming TV series)|Paradise Lost]]'', and ''[[Booster Gold (TV series)|Booster Gold]]''. The duo were open to DCEU actors such as Miller, Gadot, Momoa, and Levi reprising their respective roles,<ref name="DCUJan2023Announcement" /> though Gunn clarified that ''Superman'' would serve as the start of the new continuity, with the DCU being able to change events from the DCEU, with the DCEU properties serving "like a rough memory of what happens in the DCU".<ref name="GunnJan2023io9" /> ''The Flash'' film would be used to reset some aspects of the universe heading into the DCU.<ref name="GunnResetCB" /> In October 2023, ''[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]'' reported that no actors from Snyder's DCEU films would reprise their roles in the DCU.<ref name="MillerNoReturn"/>

Those didn't have numbers on the front, but they had orange, white and blue striping on the top, as well as the Tiger walking through the Olde English D on the front.|archive-date=May 15, 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200515211020/https://www.mlb.com/tigers/news/featured/tigers-1995-blue-alternate-jerseys|url-status=live}}{{cite news|last=Beck|first=Jason|title=Tigers will don navy blue jerseys this spring|url=https://www.mlb.com/news/tigers-will-wear-navy-spring-training-jerseys-c164063556|publisher=[[MLB Advanced Media]]|website=[[MLB.com]]|date=February 9, 2016|access-date=February 11, 2019|quote=Much like Tigers caps during the regular season, the navy jerseys will feature white trim for lettering and numbers for home games, and orange for the road games.|archive-date=August 12, 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200812014612/https://www.mlb.com/news/tigers-will-wear-navy-spring-training-jerseys-c164063556|url-status=live}}{{cite news|last=Clair|first=Michael|title=The best baseball caps ever, by team|url=https://www.mlb.com/news/best-baseball-cap-for-every-team|publisher=[[MLB Advanced Media]]|website=[[MLB.com]]|date=February 26, 2021|access-date=May 27, 2024|url-status=live|quote=The Tigers know you don't mess with perfection. The orange and navy road version is nice, but nothing compares with the crisp white letter of the home cap.|archive-date=April 5, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240405132246/https://www.mlb.com/news/best-baseball-cap-for-every-team}}
