Way (United States)|Third Way]]}}{{Cite news |last=Riccardi |first=Nicholas |date=October 11, 2024 |title=Trump seizes on one block of a Colorado city to warn of migrant crime threat, even as crime dips |url=https://apnews.com/article/election-2024-trump-aurora-colorado-crime-immigration-c76df9f1dcda7e06a983105e5e970aeb |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250405033223/https://apnews.com/article/election-2024-trump-aurora-colorado-crime-immigration-c76df9f1dcda7e06a983105e5e970aeb |archive-date=April 5, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} To resolve this imagined crime wave, he planned for mass deportations and more aggressive [[Police brutality in the United States|police]] [[use of force]]. He suggested sending the [[National Guard (United States)|National Guard]] into crime-struck cities and reserving [[United States Department of Justice|Justice Department]] grants to cities that adopt his preferred policing methods such as [[Frisking|stop-and-frisk]].{{Cite web |last=Jacobs |first=Rebecca |date=July 11, 2024 |title=Trump has repeatedly outlined plans to militarize law enforcement |url=https://www.citizensforethics.org/reports-investigations/crew-investigations/trump-has-repeatedly-outlined-plans-to-militarize-law-enforcement/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250207131505/https://www.citizensforethics.org/reports-investigations/crew-investigations/trump-has-repeatedly-outlined-plans-to-militarize-law-enforcement/ |archive-date=February 7, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |website=[[Citizens for Responsibility and Ethics in Washington]]}} The former President voiced support for shooting suspected shoplifters and having police carry out "one really violent day" against those committing property crimes.{{Cite news |last=Mathis |first=Joel |date=October 3, 2024 |title=How Trump's 'dark' rhetoric could motivate undecided voters |url=https://theweek.com/politics/how-trumps-dark-rhetoric-could-motivate-undecided-voters |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250410002020/https://theweek.com/politics/how-trumps-dark-rhetoric-could-motivate-undecided-voters |archive-date=April 10, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |work=[[The Week]]}} He pledged to expand use of the [[Capital punishment|death penalty]], including for drug dealers, [[Smuggling|smugglers]], and migrants who kill American citizens and law enforcement officers.{{Cite news|last1=Richards |first1=Zoë |last2=Traylor |first2=Jake |date=2024-10-12 |title=Trump calls for death penalty for migrants who kill U.S. citizens |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trump-calls-death-penalty-migrants-kill-us-citizens-rcna175131 |access-date=2024-12-09 |work=[[NBC News]] |language=en |archive-date=December 7, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241207080849/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trump-calls-death-penalty-migrants-kill-us-citizens-rcna175131 |url-status=live }} Regarding [[Homelessness in the United States|homelessness]], he campaigned on banning [[Camping|urban camping]] and instead creating "tent cities" on inexpensive land. These would be staffed with doctors and social workers to help the homeless seek treatment. Trump repeatedly voiced support for outlawing political dissent and criticism he considers misleading or challenges his claims to power.{{Cite news |last=Blake |first=Aaron |date=2024-09-24 |title=Trump keeps talking about criminalizing dissent |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/09/24/trump-keeps-talking-about-criminalizing-dissent/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240927204013/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/09/24/trump-keeps-talking-about-criminalizing-dissent/ |archive-date=September 27, 2024 |access-date=2024-10-01 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}}{{Cite news |last=Kapur |first=Sahil |date=October 13, 2024 |title='Totally illegal': Trump escalates rhetoric on outlawing political dissent and criticism |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/totally-illegal-trump-escalates-rhetoric-outlawing-political-dissent-c-rcna174280 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241013141931/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/totally-illegal-trump-escalates-rhetoric-outlawing-political-dissent-c-rcna174280 |archive-date=October 13, 2024 |access-date=October 13, 2024 |work=[[NBC News]]}} Trump and his allies have reportedly drafted executive orders to invoke the [[Insurrection Act of 1807|1807 Insurrection Act]] on the first day of his presidency to allow the military to shut down civil demonstrations against him. Campaigning in Iowa, Trump stated he would deploy the military in Democratic cities and states.{{cite news |last1=Fields |first1=Gary |date=November 27, 2023 |title=Trump hints at expanded role for the military within the US. A legacy law gives him few guardrails |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-military-insurrection-act-2024-election-03858b6291e4721991b5a18c2dfb3c36 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231210210436/https://apnews.com/article/trump-military-insurrection-act-2024-election-03858b6291e4721991b5a18c2dfb3c36 |archive-date=December 10, 2023 |access-date=December 10, 2023 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} Trump suggested investigating [[MSNBC]] and [[NBC]]'s parent corporation [[Comcast]] should he return to office, calling their news coverage of him "treason". Similarly, he pledged to prosecute [[Google]] for only displaying "bad stories" about him.{{cite news |last1=Ward |first1=Jasper |last2=Singh |first2=Kanishka |date=September 27, 2024 |title=Trump says he will seek Google's prosecution if he wins election |url=https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-says-he-will-seek-googles-prosecution-if-he-wins-election-2024-09-27/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241002032117/https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-says-he-will-seek-googles-prosecution-if-he-wins-election-2024-09-27/ |archive-date=October 2, 2024 |access-date= |work=[[Reuters]]}} He also stated that [[ABC News (United States)|ABC]] and [[CBS]] should lose their broadcast licenses and their journalists sent to jail if they refused to name confidential sources.{{Cite news |last=Folkenflik |first=David |date=2024-10-21 |title=Could Trump's threats against news outlets carry weight if he wins the presidency? |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/10/21/nx-s1-5150039/could-trumps-threats-against-news-outlets-carry-weight-if-he-wins-the-presidency |access-date=2024-10-22 |work=[[NPR]]}} === Transgenderism and civil rights === {{see also|2020s anti-LGBTQ movement in the United States|Social policy of the first Trump administration#LGBTQ rights|Reverse racism}} [[File:Chaya Raichik and MTG.jpg|alt=MTG and Chaya Raichik|thumb|[[Georgia (U.S. state)|Georgia]] representative [[Marjorie Taylor Greene]] and [[Libs of TikTok]] founder Chaya Raichik, two Trump advocates and proponents of the [[2020s anti-LGBTQ movement in the United States|2020s reaction against the LGBTQ community]]; Trump's policies reflected this sentiment]] Trump's campaign has stated its intention to reinterpret existing Civil Rights-era protections for minorities to counter "[[Reverse racism|anti-white racism]]". According to ''Axios'', Trump's Justice Department would "push to eliminate or upend programs in government and corporate America that are designed to counter racism that has favored whites".{{cite news|title=Exclusive: Trump allies plot anti-racism protections — for white people|url=https://www.axios.com/2024/04/01/trump-reverse-racism-civil-rights|work=[[Axios (website)|Axios]]|date=April 1, 2024|access-date=April 3, 2024|last=Thompson|first=Alex|archive-date=May 14, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240514182849/https://www.axios.com/2024/04/01/trump-reverse-racism-civil-rights|url-status=live}} Trump has stated that there is a "definite anti-white feeling in the country". Trump's advisors have stated Trump will rescind Biden's Executive Orders designed to boost diversity and racial equity. Trump pledged a federal task force to fight the “persecution against Christians in America”.{{Cite news |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=May 18, 2024 |title=Jesus is their savior, Trump is their candidate. Ex-president's backers say he shares faith, values |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-christian-evangelicals-conservatives-2024-election-43f25118c133170c77786daf316821c3 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240524232811/https://apnews.com/article/trump-christian-evangelicals-conservatives-2024-election-43f25118c133170c77786daf316821c3 |archive-date=May 24, 2024 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |newspaper=[[Associated Press]]}} Trump promised a rollback on trans rights.{{Cite news |date=March 27, 2024 |title=How a second Trump presidency could impact the LGBTQ+ community |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MsoGfOesEEA |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241009121351/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MsoGfOesEEA |archive-date=October 9, 2024 |access-date=July 1, 2024 |work=[[PBS News Hour]]}}{{cite news |last=Chen |first=Shawna |date=January 31, 2023 |title=Trump unveils sweeping attack on trans rights |url=https://www.axios.com/2023/01/31/trump-transgender-rights-lgbtq |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231216044358/https://www.axios.com/2023/01/31/trump-transgender-rights-lgbtq |archive-date=December 16, 2023 |access-date=December 16, 2023 |work=[[Axios (website)|Axios]]}} Trump stated he will rescind Biden's [[Title IX]] protections "on day one" for transgender students using bathrooms, locker rooms, and pronouns that align with their gender identities.{{cite news|title=Trump Promises Rollback On Trans Rights: Here's What He's Said|url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/saradorn/2024/05/10/trump-promises-rollback-on-trans-rights-heres-what-hes-said/?sh=51549ef4dae0|work=[[Forbes]]|date=May 10, 2024|access-date=May 11, 2024|last=Dorn|first=Sara|archive-date=May 12, 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240512031225/https://www.forbes.com/sites/saradorn/2024/05/10/trump-promises-rollback-on-trans-rights-heres-what-hes-said/?sh=51549ef4dae0|url-status=live}} Trump has stated that he will ask Congress to pass a bill stating that the U.S. will only recognize two genders as determined at birth, and has promised to crackdown on gender-affirming care. Trump has stated that hospitals and health care providers that provide transitional hormones or surgery will no longer qualify for federal funding, including Medicare and Medicaid funding. Trump has stated he will push to prohibit hormonal and surgical intervention for minors in all 50 states. Trump's campaign has been more accepting on lesbian, gay, and bisexual rights. During the drafting of the Republican Party's 2024 presidential platform, he advocated for a more tolerant position on [[Same-sex marriage in the United States|same-sex marriage]] and successfully removed language that supported [[conversion therapy]].{{Cite news|last=Dixon |first=Matt |date=2024-05-23 |title=Trump team moves behind the scenes to shift the GOP platform on abortion and marriage |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trump-team-shift-rnc-gop-platform-abortion-marriage-rcna152677 |access-date=2024-09-26 |work=[[NBC News]] |language=en |archive-date=June 13, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240613152932/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trump-team-shift-rnc-gop-platform-abortion-marriage-rcna152677 |url-status=live }}{{Cite news |last1=Messerly |first1=Megan |last2=Allison |first2=Natalie |last3=Sentner |first3=Irie |date=July 8, 2024 |title=RNC committee approves dropping national limits on abortion from party platform |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2024/07/08/rnc-platform-national-abortion-limits-00166788 |access-date=July 24, 2024 |work=[[Politico]] |archive-date=July 18, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240718045835/https://www.politico.com/news/2024/07/08/rnc-platform-national-abortion-limits-00166788 |url-status=live }}{{Cite news |last1=Scherer |first1=Michael |last2=Dawsey |first2=Josh |date=July 8, 2024 |title=GOP adopts platform that softens language on abortion, same-sex marriage |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/07/08/trump-abortion-republican-platform/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240709005523/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/07/08/trump-abortion-republican-platform/ |archive-date=July 9, 2024 |access-date=July 24, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} ==Rhetoric== {{Main|Rhetoric of Donald Trump}}{{See also|Trumpism#Methods of persuasion|False or misleading statements by Donald Trump#2024 presidential campaign}}{{Quote box | quote = The great silent majority is rising like never before. And under our leadership, the forgotten man and woman will be forgotten no longer. You’re going to be forgotten no longer. With your help, your love and your vote, we will put [[America First (policy)|America first]]. And today, especially in honor of our great veterans on [[Veterans Day]], we pledge to you that we will root out the [[communists]], [[Marxists]], [[fascists]] and the [[far-left politics|radical-left]] thugs that live like vermin within the confines of our country, that [[election denial movement in the United States|lie and steal and cheat on elections]] and will do anything possible—they’ll do anything, whether legally or illegally, to destroy America and to destroy the [[American dream]]. The real threat is not from the [[far-right politics|radical right]]. The real threat is from the radical left. And it is growing every day. Every single day. | source = — Donald Trump in a November 2023 rally at [[Claremont, New Hampshire]] | width = 25% }} Donald Trump's campaign rhetoric received immense media coverage. According to myriad journalists and scholars, and even—to an extent—Trump's own team, it was dark, vulgar, incendiary, and extreme, more so than that of any political candidate in U.S. history.{{cite news |last1=Homans |first1=Charles |date=April 27, 2024 |title=Donald Trump Has Never Sounded Like This |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/04/27/magazine/trump-rallies-rhetoric.html |url-access=limited |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240719002646/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/04/27/magazine/trump-rallies-rhetoric.html |archive-date=July 19, 2024 |access-date=April 27, 2024 |work=[[New York (magazine)|New York]]}}{{Cite web |last1=Kivowitz |first1=Elizabeth |last2=Kung |first2=Ethan |date=August 21, 2024 |title=UCLA study tracks former President Donald Trump's weaponization of words |url=https://newsroom.ucla.edu/releases/ucla-study-tracks-former-president-donald-trumps-weaponization-of-words |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250415153636/https://newsroom.ucla.edu/releases/ucla-study-tracks-former-president-donald-trumps-weaponization-of-words |archive-date=April 15, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |website=[[University of California, Los Angeles]]}} His rhetoric was noted to degenerate as the campaign progressed.{{Cite news |last1=Savin |first1=Nikita |last2=Treisman |first2=Daniel |date=October 26, 2024 |title=We analyzed 9 years of Trump political speeches, and his violent rhetoric has increased dramatically |url=https://theconversation.com/we-analyzed-9-years-of-trump-political-speeches-and-his-violent-rhetoric-has-increased-dramatically-238962 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250321194537/https://theconversation.com/we-analyzed-9-years-of-trump-political-speeches-and-his-violent-rhetoric-has-increased-dramatically-238962 |archive-date=March 21, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[The Conversation (website)|The Conversation]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Colvin |first1=Jill |last2=Barrow |first2=Bill |date=2023-12-08 |title=Trump's vow to only be a dictator on 'day one' follows growing worry over his authoritarian rhetoric |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-hannity-dictator-authoritarian-presidential-election-f27e7e9d7c13fabbe3ae7dd7f1235c72 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231208055611/https://apnews.com/article/trump-hannity-dictator-authoritarian-presidential-election-f27e7e9d7c13fabbe3ae7dd7f1235c72 |archive-date=December 8, 2023 |access-date=2023-12-19 |work=[[Associated Press]] |language=en}}{{Cite news |last=Gold |first=Michael |date=November 6, 2024 |title=At a Pennsylvania Rally, Trump Descends to New Levels of Vulgarity |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/19/us/politics/trump-vulgarity-pennsylvania-rally.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250428162136/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/19/us/politics/trump-vulgarity-pennsylvania-rally.html |archive-date=April 28, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} For instance, in a November 2023 rally, Trump said, "[W]e pledge to you that we will root out the [[Communism|communists]], [[Marxism|Marxists]], [[Fascism|fascists]], and the [[Far-left politics|radical-left]] thugs that live like vermin within the confines of our country". Eleven months later, he stated, "I think the bigger problem is the enemy from within. … We have some very bad people. We have some sick people, radical left lunatics. And I think they’re the big—and it should be very easily handled by, if necessary, by the [[National Guard (United States)|National Guard]], or if really necessary, by [[United States Armed Forces|the military]]".{{Cite news |last=Groves |first=Stephen |date=October 13, 2024 |title=Trump suggests he'll use the military on 'the enemy from within' the U.S. if he's reelected |url=https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/trump-suggests-hell-use-the-military-on-the-enemy-from-within-the-u-s-if-hes-reelected |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250425072226/https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/trump-suggests-hell-use-the-military-on-the-enemy-from-within-the-u-s-if-hes-reelected |archive-date=April 25, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[PBS News Hour]]}} Two days before the [[2024 United States presidential election|election]], he told rallygoers, "[T]o get me somebody would have to shoot through the [[Fake news#Misuse of term|fake news]] [reporters]. And I don't mind that so much".{{Cite news |last1=Gold |first1=Michael |last2=Haberman |first2=Maggie |last3=Goldmacher |first3=Shane |date=November 3, 2024 |title=Trump, Sluggish and Aggrieved, Says He 'Shouldn't Have Left' the White House |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/11/03/us/politics/trump-pa-rally-election.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241103212701/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/11/03/us/politics/trump-pa-rally-election.html |archive-date=November 3, 2024 |access-date=November 3, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |issn=0362-4331}} In deploying such vitriolic language, Trump aimed to energize his base as well as undecided voters, in order to maximize turnout. Trump's way of speaking throughout the campaign, especially in its final months, was described as aggressive and erratic. In fact, many commentators remarked that he "rambled" more than he spoke.{{Cite news |last=McGreal |first=Chris |date=August 26, 2024 |title=Are Trump's campaign rallies energizing his base – or sowing doubt? |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/aug/26/trumps-campaign-rallies-harmful-or-helpful |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250314054907/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/aug/26/trumps-campaign-rallies-harmful-or-helpful |archive-date=March 14, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}}{{Cite news |last=Fowler |first=Stephen |date=October 29, 2024 |title=What are Trump's final rally speeches like? Lower energy, less focused and running late |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/10/28/nx-s1-5164570/trump-rally-speech-2024-election |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250423231942/https://www.npr.org/2024/10/28/nx-s1-5164570/trump-rally-speech-2024-election |archive-date=April 23, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[NPR]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Arnsdorf |first1=Isaac |last2=Dawsey |first2=Josh |date=October 25, 2024 |title=How Trump talks: Abrupt shifts, profane insults, confusing sentences |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/10/25/trump-words-speeches-campaign-president/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241027061744/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/10/25/trump-words-speeches-campaign-president/ |archive-date=October 27, 2024 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} According to a ''[[The New York Times|New York Times]]'' computer analysis, since the [[Donald Trump 2016 presidential campaign|initiation]] of Trump's political career in 2015, his speeches had grown "darker, harsher, longer, angrier, less focused, more profane and increasingly fixated on the past". The former President would talk about one subject and then abruptly go off on a [[Tangential speech|tangent]], often droning on about a different matter, and eventually return to the main subject.{{Cite magazine |last=Olmsted |first=Edith |date=October 16, 2024 |title=Trump Derails His Own Rally With Bizarre Old-Man Tangents |url=https://newrepublic.com/post/187206/donald-trump-derails-rally-bizarre-tangents |url-access=registration |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250428184528/https://newrepublic.com/post/187206/donald-trump-derails-rally-bizarre-tangents |archive-date=April 28, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |magazine=[[The New Republic]]}} Trump often mumbled words; he once confused "[[double entendre]]" for "double standard," referred to [[Assyrian people|Assyrians]] as "Azurasians", and, in 2022, mixed up [[JD Vance]] with [[Josh Mandel]] and thus produced "JD Mandel".{{Cite news |last=O'Brien |first=Rebecca Davis |date=July 15, 2024 |title=J.D. Vance Was Not Always His Name. But It's the One That Felt Closest to Home. |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/07/15/us/politics/jd-vance-name.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250428145919/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/07/15/us/politics/jd-vance-name.html |archive-date=April 28, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} His speech teemed with [[hyperbole]] and [[Superlative case|superlatives]].{{cite news |last1=Dawsey |first1=Josh |last2=Arnsdorf |first2=Isaac |date=June 22, 2024 |title=Trump cranks up false, inflammatory messages to rake in campaign cash |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/elections/2024/06/22/trump-fundraising-false-aggressive/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240623025349/https://www.washingtonpost.com/elections/2024/06/22/trump-fundraising-false-aggressive/ |archive-date=June 23, 2024 |access-date=June 22, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |issn=0190-8286}} [[Peter Baker (journalist)|Peter Baker]] of ''The New York Times'' wrote, "Nuance, subtlety, precision and ambiguity play no role in the version that Mr. Trump promotes with relentless repetition",{{Cite news |last=Baker |first=Peter |date=November 3, 2024 |title=Trump's Wild Claims, Conspiracies and Falsehoods Redefine Presidential Bounds |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/11/03/us/politics/trump-falsehoods-claims-election.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250427180536/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/11/03/us/politics/trump-falsehoods-claims-election.html |archive-date=April 27, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} as Trump attacked Biden for being "the worst president in U.S. history",{{Cite news |last=Agencies |date=July 22, 2024 |title=Trump calls Biden 'worst president' in US history, says Harris will be easier to beat |url=https://www.timesofisrael.com/trump-calls-biden-worst-president-in-us-history-says-harris-will-be-easier-to-beat/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241203062911/https://www.timesofisrael.com/trump-calls-biden-worst-president-in-us-history-says-harris-will-be-easier-to-beat/ |archive-date=December 3, 2024 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[The Times of Israel]]}} and spoke of himself as "the greatest president" in U.S history.{{Cite web |last= |date= |title=Remarks: Donald Trump Attends a Campaign Roundtable in Drexel Hill, Pennsylvania - October 29, 2024 |url=https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-remarks-campaign-roundtable-drexel-hill-pennsylvania-october-29-2024/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250503170109/https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-remarks-campaign-roundtable-drexel-hill-pennsylvania-october-29-2024/ |archive-date=May 3, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |website=[[Roll Call]]}} Vulgarities were also a hallmark of the former President's rhetoric. In one of his final rallies, for example, he rambled about the size of [[Arnold Palmer]]'s genitals. Overall, Trump's language took on a more negative and violent tone, with a ''[[The Conversation (website)|Conversation]]'' analysis finding that 1.6% of the total words uttered in his 2024 campaign denoted violence, compared to 0.6% in 2016, and the aforementioned ''New York Times'' survey finding a 50% increase in negative words.{{Cite news |last1=Baker |first1=Peter |last2=Freedman |first2=Dylan |date=October 6, 2024 |title=Trump's Speeches, Increasingly Angry and Rambling, Reignite the Question of Age |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/06/us/politics/trump-speeches-age-cognitive-decline.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241008051103/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/06/us/politics/trump-speeches-age-cognitive-decline.html |archive-date=October 8, 2024 |access-date=October 8, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |issn=0362-4331}} === Attacks === [[File:Donald Trump Truth Social Post on June 12, 2023—Now that the seal is broken.jpg|thumb|A Trump post on [[Truth Social]] dated June 2023, in which he condemns the [[United States Department of Justice|U.S. Justice Department]] for supposedly targeting him, and vows to go after political opponents]]Trump's campaign deployed [[Dehumanization|dehumanizing]], violent attacks against his political opponents.{{cite news |last1=Haberman |first1=Maggie |last2=Nehamas |first2=Nicholas |last3=McFadden |first3=Alyce |date=October 3, 2023 |title=Trump Said Shoplifters Should Be Shot, Part of a String of Violent Remarks |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/10/03/us/politics/trump-indictments-shoplifters-violence.html |url-access=subscription |access-date=October 9, 2023 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} {{subscription required}}{{Cite news |last=Gabriel |first=Trip |date=2023-10-06 |title=Trump Escalates Anti-Immigrant Rhetoric With 'Poisoning the Blood' Comment |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/10/05/us/politics/trump-immigration-rhetoric.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240117102659/https://www.nytimes.com/2023/10/05/us/politics/trump-immigration-rhetoric.html |archive-date=January 17, 2024 |access-date=2023-12-19 |work=[[The New York Times]] |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}} {{subscription required}}{{Cite news |last=LeVine |first=Marianne |date=November 12, 2023 |title=Trump calls political enemies 'vermin,' echoing dictators Hitler, Mussolini |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2023/11/12/trump-rally-vermin-political-opponents |access-date=November 12, 2023 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} His election rival, Harris, was a prime target. In a July 2024 interview, he said that she had claimed [[Indian Americans|Indian]] heritage "until a number of years ago when she happened to turn Black, and now she wants to be known as Black".{{Cite news |last1=Bradner |first1=Eric |last2=Pellish |first2=Aaron |date=2024-07-31 |title=Donald Trump falsely suggests Kamala Harris 'happened to turn Black' {{!}} CNN Politics |url=https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/31/politics/donald-trump-kamala-harris-black-nabj/index.html |access-date=2024-08-08 |work=[[CNN]]}} Oftentimes, Trump intentionally mispronounced her name as "Ka-MA-la",{{Cite news |last=McWhorter |first=John |date=August 1, 2024 |title=What Trump Means When He Mispronounces 'Kamala' |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/08/01/opinion/trump-kamala-harris-pronounce.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250417073956/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/08/01/opinion/trump-kamala-harris-pronounce.html |archive-date=April 17, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} or "Kamabla",{{Cite news |date=2024-08-07 |title=Trump picks a nickname for Harris that no one seems to understand |url=https://www.msnbc.com/rachel-maddow-show/maddowblog/donald-trump-nickname-kamala-harris-kamabla-no-one-understands-rcna165602 |access-date=2024-08-09 |website=[[MSNBC]] |language=en}} and called her "low IQ", "mentally disabled", and "a shit vice president". Other political opponents got similar treatments. In September 2023, Trump said that [[Mark Milley]], his appointed [[chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff]] who had come to criticize him, deserved "DEATH!" for his phone calls with a [[Chinese people|Chinese]] general. He urged deploying the military to fight "the enemy from within": the "enemy" being "radical left lunatics" and certain Democratic politicians.{{Cite news |last=Vazquez |first=Maegan |date=October 13, 2024 |title=Trump urges using military to handle 'radical left lunatics' on Election Day |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/10/13/trump-military-enemies-within/ |access-date=October 13, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |issn=0190-8286}}{{Cite news |last1=Lerer |first1=Lisa |last2=Gold |first2=Michael |date=2024-10-15 |title=Trump Escalates Threats to Political Opponents He Deems the 'Enemy' |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/15/us/politics/trump-opponents-enemy-within.html |work=[[The New York Times]]}} Another enemy, according to him, was the media. He called [[Facebook]] "an enemy of the people",{{cite news |last=Rosen |first=Jacob |date=March 11, 2024 |title=Trump, in reversal, opposes TikTok ban, calls Facebook "enemy of the people" |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-reversal-tiktok-ban-says-facebook-enemy-of-people/ |access-date=July 31, 2024 |work=[[CBS News]]}} and complained that the media was "so damn bad".{{cite web |title=Speech: Donald Trump Holds a Campaign Rally in Lititz, Pennsylvania - November 3, 2024 |url=https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-speech-campaign-rally-lititz-pennsylvania-november-3-2024/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250116200311/https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-speech-campaign-rally-lititz-pennsylvania-november-3-2024/ |archive-date=January 16, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |website=[[Roll Call]]}} Moreover, Trump attacked the witnesses, judges, juries, and families of individuals involved in his criminal trials.{{cite news |last1=Schayna |first1=Jacobs |date=March 29, 2024 |title=After Trump attacks hush money judge's daughter, DA seeks broader gag order |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/03/29/trump-gag-order-judge-daughter-merchan/ |access-date=March 30, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |issn=0190-8286}}{{cite news |last1=Blake |first1=Aaron |date=October 25, 2023 |title=Trump's latest attack on witnesses is one of his most brazen |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2023/10/25/trumps-latest-attack-witnesses-is-one-his-most-brazen/ |access-date=March 30, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |issn=0190-8286}}{{cite news |last=Jalonick |first=Mary Clare |date=May 30, 2024 |title=Republican lawmakers react with fury to Trump verdict and rally to his defense |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-verdict-republicans-guilty-reaction-congress-election-c8193404866565c55b093086890cbef8 |url-status=live 2024 |access-date=June 1, 2024 |work=[[The Associated Press]]}} In the aftermath of his [[Prosecution of Donald Trump in New York|prosecution in New York]], he called Judge [[Juan Merchan]], "a devil"{{Cite news |last=Qiu |first=Linda |date=May 31, 2024 |title=Trump rails against hush money judge in post-conviction remarks: 'He's really a devil' |url=https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/4696253-trump-conviction-hush-money-judge-devil/ |url-status=live 2024 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} and urged his supporters to "go after" [[Letitia James]], the attorney who filed the suit. ===Extremist statements=== {{see also|Comparisons between in [[World War One]]{{cite news |last=Blumenthal |first=Sidney |date=October 7, 2024 |title=Donald Trump's Hitlerian logic is no mistake |first=Benjamin |date=May 23, 2024 |title=Trump and GOP repeatedly echo Nazi and far-right ideology as they aim to retake White House 2024 |work=[[Los Angeles Times]] |issn=0458-3035}} He proclaimed that undocumented immigrants were "poisoning the blood of our country" and had "bad genes," which, according to some commentators, strikingly resembled Hitler and [[White supremacy|white supremacists]]' [[racial hygiene]] rhetoric.{{cite news |last=Sullivan |first=Kate |date=October 6, 2023 |title=Trump's anti-immigrant comments draw rebuke |url=https://www.cnn.com/2023/10/06/politics/trump-anti-immigrant-comments/index.html |access-date=November 16, 2023 |work=[[CNN]]}} On [[Veterans Day]] 2023, he called some of his political opponents "vermin," which also seemed to echo Hitler and [[Benito Mussolini]]'s language.{{cite news |last=Gold |first=Michael |date=November 13, 2023 |title=After Calling Foes 'Vermin,' Trump Campaign Warns Its Critics Will Be 'Crushed' |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/11/13/us/politics/trump-vermin-rhetoric-fascists.html |access-date=November 16, 2023 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} {{subscription required}}{{Cite news |last=Dorn |first=Sara |date=November 12, 2023 |title=Trump Compares Political Foes To 'Vermin' On Veterans Day—Echoing Nazi Propaganda |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/saradorn/2023/11/12/trump-compares-political-foes-to-vermin-on-veterans-day-echoing-nazi-propaganda/ |access-date=November 12, 2023 |work=[[Forbes (magazine)|Forbes]]}} In May 2024, Trump's campaign posted an advertisement which showed hypothetical newspaper headlines in the event of a Trump victory. Under one headline titled "What's next for America?" was a subtitle that read, "[[Industrialization in Germany|German industrial strength]] significantly increased after [[Unification of Germany|1871]], driven by the creation of [[German Empire|a unified Reich]]".{{cite news |last1=Cameron |first1=Chris |date=May 22, 2024 |title=Trump Posts, Then Takes Down, Video Online With Headlines About a 'Unified Reich' |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/05/20/us/donald-trump-reich-video.html |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240526023136/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/05/20/us/donald-trump-reich-video.html |archive-date=May 26, 2024 |access-date=May 26, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} Some of Trump's statements were perceived as an open embrace of authoritarianism. In a December 2023 interview with [[Sean Hannity]], the former President said he would only be a [[President for life|dictator]] on "day one" of his presidency and not after.{{cite news |author1=Adam Wren |date=December 6, 2023 |title=Trump's 'dictator' remark jolts the 2024 campaign – and tests his GOP rivals on debate day |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2023/12/06/trump-dictator-remark-2024-campaign-00130392 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231206192854/https://www.politico.com/news/2023/12/06/trump-dictator-remark-2024-campaign-00130392 |archive-date=December 6, 2023 |access-date=December 6, 2023 |work=[[Politico]]}}{{cite news |last=Baker |first=Peter |date=2023-12-09 |title=Talk of a Trump Dictatorship Charges the American Political Debate |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/12/09/us/politics/trump-dictatorship.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231209203046/https://www.nytimes.com/2023/12/09/us/politics/trump-dictatorship.html |archive-date=December 9, 2023 |access-date=2023-12-10 |work=[[The New York Times]] |language= |issn=}}{{cite news |last1=LeVine |first1=Marianne |last2=Arnsdorf |first2=Isaac |date=December 13, 2023 |title=Trump backers laugh off, cheer 'dictator' comments, as scholars voice alarm |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/elections/2023/12/13/trump-dictator-comments-supporters/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231215131632/https://www.washingtonpost.com/elections/2023/12/13/trump-dictator-comments-supporters/ |archive-date=December 15, 2023 |access-date=December 16, 2023 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} His campaign aides later stated that he was merely attempting to "[[Owning the libs|trigger the left]]" and media establishment.{{cite news |author1=Jill |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-hannity-dictator-authoritarian-presidential-election-f27e7e9d7c13fabbe3ae7dd7f1235c72 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231208055611/https://apnews.com/article/trump-hannity-dictator-authoritarian-presidential-election-f27e7e9d7c13fabbe3ae7dd7f1235c72 |archive-date=December 8, 2023 |access-date=December 8, 2023 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} Trump also stated that, in order to reverse his loss in the [[2020 United States presidential election|2020 election]], the [[Constitution of the United States|U.S. Constitution]] had to be terminated.{{Cite web |last=Ibrahim |first=Nur |date=December 5, 2022 |title=Did Trump Say Election Fraud Allows for 'Termination' of US Constitution? |url=https://www.snopes.com/fact-check/trump-termination-us-constitution/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250331163622/https://www.snopes.com/fact-check/trump-termination-us-constitution/ |archive-date=March 31, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |website=[[Snopes]]}} Several [[Republican Party (United States)|Republicans]], including [[Ted Cruz]],{{Cite news |last=Choi |first=Matthew |date=October 6, 2022 |title=Cruz: "Of course" Trump was wrong to call for terminating Constitution |url=https://www.texastribune.org/2022/12/06/ted-cruz-john-cornyn-trump-constitution/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250423023329/https://www.texastribune.org/2022/12/06/ted-cruz-john-cornyn-trump-constitution/ |archive-date=April 23, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |work=[[The Texas Tribune]]}} denounced this remark.{{Cite news |last=Murphy |first=Matt |date=December 4, 2022 |title=Trump's call for 'termination' of constitution condemned |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-63851751 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250407115708/https://www.bbc.com/news/world-us-canada-63851751 |archive-date=April 7, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}} Trump publicly praised several authoritarian leaders during his campaign{{cite news |last=Sciutto |first=Jim |date=March 12, 2024 |title=Former advisers sound the alarm that Trump praises despots in private and on the campaign trail |url=https://www.cnn.com/2024/03/11/politics/trump-despots-advisers-sound-alarm/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240507000021/https://www.cnn.com/2024/03/11/politics/trump-despots-advisers-sound-alarm/index.html |archive-date=May 7, 2024 |access-date=March 17, 2024 |work=[[CNN]]}} such as [[Viktor Orbán]]: "There’s nobody that’s better, smarter or a better leader than Viktor Orbán. He’s fantastic".{{Cite news |last1=Holmes |first1=Kristen |last2=Millman |first2=Andrew |date=March 9, 2024 |title=Trump praises 'fantastic' Viktor Orbán while hosting Hungarian autocrat at Mar-a-Lago for meeting and concert |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/03/08/politics/trump-orban-mar-a-lago/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250401082107/https://edition.cnn.com/2024/03/08/politics/trump-orban-mar-a-lago/index.html |archive-date=April 1, 2025 |access-date=April 13, 2025 |work=[[CNN]]}} === Ideology === Scholars and commentators contended that Trump's rhetoric stemmed from [[Populism in the United States|populism]]. A common theme in his rallies was the struggle between "us"—the majority, or his supporters—and "them"—the [[Deep state conspiracy theory in the United States|elites]], or his political enemies. In contrast with previous runs, Trump stressed the "them," not the "us," who he claimed had targeted him; "When they start playing with your elections and trying to arrest their political opponent — I can do that, too!", he once said. This led the [[University of California, Los Angeles]] to deem Trump's 2024 brand of populism "negative populism". A study of theirs found that it was less focused on policy, such as economic performance, and more on violent attacks on opponents.{{Cite news |last=Rector |first=Kevin |date=August 16, 2024 |title=News Analysis: Trump seeks to reclaim spotlight with old playbook of lying, talking smack to media |url=https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2024-08-16/2024-election-trump-news-conference-analysis |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240823194457/https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2024-08-16/2024-election-trump-news-conference-analysis |archive-date=August 23, 2024 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[Los Angeles Times]]}} Frequent targets of his attacks were [[Illegal immigration to the United States|illegal immigrants]], [[Transgender history in the United States|transgender people]], and the elites, made in an attempt to create an [[In-group and out-group|outgroup]] to stir up fear and [[moral panic]] among his supporters.{{Cite web |last=Thulin |first=Lila |date=January 21, 2025 |title=There's a term for Trump's political style: authoritarian populism |url=https://news.berkeley.edu/2025/01/21/theres-a-term-for-trumps-political-style-authoritarian-populism/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250414004930/https://news.berkeley.edu/2025/01/21/theres-a-term-for-trumps-political-style-authoritarian-populism/ |archive-date=April 14, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |website=[[UC Berkeley|UC Berkeley News]]}} The [[University of California, Berkeley]] ties this strategy to "authoritarian populism". It elaborates, "[The] sense of fear and antagonism [promoted] leads people to accept authoritarian measures to protect themselves and their [[In-group and out-group|in-group]]". Another effect of Trump's framing of certain people as an outgroup was airing the public's grievances, especially on the [[Mexico–United States border crisis|surge in illegal immigration]] and the political establishment. This turned him into the "ultimate" symbol of victimhood. In fact, a central motif of Trump's campaign was martyrdom. He portrayed himself as a victim of the "[[Deep state in the United States|deep state]]" actively attempting to undermine him and the country.{{Cite news |last=Feuer |first=Alan |date=June 5, 2024 |title=It Was Legal Boilerplate. Trump Made It Sound Like a Threat to His Life. |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/06/05/us/politics/trump-fbi-documents-threats.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240609234054/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/06/05/us/politics/trump-fbi-documents-threats.html |archive-date=June 9, 2024 |access-date=June 10, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |issn=0362-4331}} {{subscription required}} His criminal trials made him, in his words, a "political prisoner," similar to [[Alexei Navalny]].{{Cite news |last=Dixon |first=Matt |date=May 31, 2024 |title='These are bad people': Trump unloads after his historic guilty verdict |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/donald-trump/trump-unloads-historic-guilty-verdict-press-conference-rcna154867 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240609220025/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/donald-trump/trump-unloads-historic-guilty-verdict-press-conference-rcna154867 |archive-date=June 9, 2024 |access-date=June 10, 2024 |work=[[NBC News]]}} Alongside martyrdom, a common motif was retribution. He framed the election as "[[Book of Revelation|the final battle]]",{{Cite news |last=Smith |first=David |date=March 25, 2023 |title=Trump describes 2024 election as 'the final battle' from podium in Waco |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2023/mar/26/trump-waco-texas-2024-election-final-battle-january-6 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250429222259/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2023/mar/26/trump-waco-texas-2024-election-final-battle-january-6 |archive-date=April 29, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} and his presidential campaign as a "righteous crusade" against "[[Atheism in the United States|atheists]], [[Globalism|globalists]] and the [[Marxism|Marxists]]". Trump referred to the [[January 6 United States Capitol attack|January 6 Capitol attack]] to back his retribution narrative. During rallies, imprisoned participants of the attack were brought up as patriotic "warriors" and "hostages," symbols of political injustice.{{Cite news |last=Fortinsky |first=Sarah |date=June 9, 2024 |title=Trump calls J6 defendants 'warriors' |url=https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/4713140-trump-calls-j6-defendants-warriors/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240610025451/https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/4713140-trump-calls-j6-defendants-warriors/ |archive-date=June 10, 2024 |access-date=June 9, 2024 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}}{{Cite news |last=Basu |first=Zachary |date=March 13, 2024 |title=Trump "floods the zone" as general election tests desensitized voters |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/03/13/trump-election-rhetoric-numb |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250430042039/https://www.axios.com/2024/03/13/trump-election-rhetoric-numb |archive-date=April 30, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[Axios (website)|Axios]]}} Furthermore, Trump's populism blended with [[American nationalism|nationalism]], as his calls for retribution against illegal immigrants and [[New World Order conspiracy theory|globalist]] elites were enmeshed with calls to defend the American identity.{{Cite web |last=Gultasli |first=Selcuk |date=October 30, 2024 |title=Dr. Wojczewski: Trump Externalizes US Problems, Presenting Them as Originating from Abroad |url=https://www.populismstudies.org/dr-wojczewski-trump-externalizes-us-problems-presenting-them-as-originating-from-abroad/ |access-date=May 1, 2025 |website=European Center for Populism Studies}} A core feature of the former President's populist rhetoric was his defiance of norms of political speech. This was captured through vulgar insults against opponents and violent diction.{{cite news |last=Gold |first=Michael |date=August 28, 2024 |title=Trump Reposts Crude Sexual Remark About Harris on Truth Social |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/08/28/us/politics/trump-truth-social-posts.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240828230508/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/08/28/us/politics/trump-truth-social-posts.html |archive-date=August 28, 2024 |access-date=August 28, 2024 |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |issn=0362-4331 |quote=Though he has a history of making crass insults about his opponents, the reposts signal Mr. Trump's willingness to continue to shatter longstanding norms of political speech.}} {{subscription required}}{{cite news |last1=VandeHei |first1=Jim |author-link=Jim VandeHei |last2=Allen |first2=Mike |date=August 16, 2024 |title=Behind the Curtain: Trump's "caught on tape" women problem |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/08/16/trump-vance-women-problem-voters |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240828202432/https://www.axios.com/2024/08/16/trump-vance-women-problem-voters |archive-date=August 28, 2024 |access-date=August 28, 2024 |newspaper=Axios}} According to [[Lilie Chouliaraki]] and Kathryn Claire Higgins of the [[London School of Economics|London School of Economics and Political Sciences]], Trump spoke with "an irreverent, improvised and unencumbered brashness that suggests that he is saying out loud what everyone else is too afraid to say". [[Robert C. Rowland]], author of ''The Rhetoric of Donald Trump'', opined that his breaking of rhetorical norms "can be seen as proof of authenticity, but if taken too far it can lead to ridicule, dealing a devastating blow to someone who has styled himself as the strongman protector of ordinary people".{{Cite web |last=Rowland |first=Robert C. |date=September 17, 2024 |title=Donald Trump and Rhetorical Norms in Debates |url=https://kansaspress.ku.edu/blog/2024/09/17/donald-trump-and-rhetorical-norms-in-debates/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250214210531/https://kansaspress.ku.edu/blog/2024/09/17/donald-trump-and-rhetorical-norms-in-debates/ |archive-date=February 14, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |website=[[University Press of Kansas]]}} The aforementioned populist overtones bore parallels to authoritarian leaders. The former President's rhetoric was unprecedentedly vitriolic and extreme to the point that some scholars and journalists labelled it as [[Comparisons between Donald Trump and fascism|fascist]],{{cite news |last=Lehmann |first=Chris |date=November 14, 2023 |title=The "Is Donald Trump a Fascist?" Debate Has Been Ended—by Donald Trump |url=https://www.thenation.com/article/politics/donald-trump-fascist-vermin/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240926135406/https://www.thenation.com/article/politics/donald-trump-fascist-vermin/ |archive-date=September 26, 2024 |access-date=December 8, 2023 |work=[[The Nation]]}}{{cite magazine |last=Cassidy |first=John |date=November 14, 2023 |title=Trump's Fascistic Rhetoric Only Emphasizes the Stakes in 2024 |url=https://www.newyorker.com/news/our-columnists/trumps-fascistic-rhetoric-only-emphasizes-the-stakes-in-2024 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241009121304/https://www.newyorker.com/news/our-columnists/trumps-fascistic-rhetoric-only-emphasizes-the-stakes-in-2024 |archive-date=October 9, 2024 |access-date=December 8, 2023 |magazine=[[The New Yorker]]}}{{cite news |last=Browning |first=Christopher R. |date=July 25, 2023 |title=A New Kind of Fascism |url=https://www.theatlantic.com/ideas/archive/2023/07/trump-second-term-isolationist-fascism/674791/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240926135407/https://www.theatlantic.com/ideas/archive/2023/07/trump-second-term-isolationist-fascism/674791/ |archive-date=September 26, 2024 |access-date=December 8, 2023 |work=[[The Atlantic]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Bender |first1=Michael Charles |author-link1=Michael C. Bender |last2=Gold |first2=Michael |date=2023-11-20 |title=Trump's Dire Words Raise New Fears About His Authoritarian Bent |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2023/11/20/us/politics/trump-rhetoric-fascism.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240121062857/https://www.nytimes.com/2023/11/20/us/politics/trump-rhetoric-fascism.html |archive-date=January 21, 2024 |access-date=2023-12-19 |work=[[The New York Times]] |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}} {{subscription required}} comparable to that of [[Juan Perón]], [[Fidel Castro]], and [[Adolf Hitler]].{{Cite news |last=Ward |first=Myah |date=October 12, 2024 |title=We watched 20 Trump rallies. His racist, anti-immigrant messaging is getting darker. |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2024/10/12/trump-racist-rhetoric-immigrants-00183537 |access-date=October 12, 2024 |work=[[Politico]]}}{{Cite news |last=Svitek |first=Patrick |date=2024-10-07 |title=Trump suggests 'bad genes' to blame for undocumented immigrants who commit murders |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/10/07/trump-undocumented-immigrants-bad-genes/ |access-date=2024-10-07 |newspaper=Washington Post |language=en-US |issn=0190-8286}}{{Cite news |last=McGraw |first=Meridith |date=May 21, 2024 |title=Trump campaign deletes 'unified Reich' post after widespread backlash |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2024/05/21/trump-unified-reich-truth-social-00159233 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240523023630/https://www.politico.com/news/2024/05/21/trump-unified-reich-truth-social-00159233 |archive-date=May 23, 2024 |access-date=May 23, 2024 |work=[[Politico]]}} [[Christian nationalism in the United States|Christian nationalism]] also defined Trump's rhetoric. In his rallies, he alleged that [[Christianity in the United States|Christianity]] was being besieged and Christians were facing [[Persecution of Christians|persecution]] by Democrats, and that he would guard it and reclaim its rightful role in U.S. society.{{harvnb|Singam|2024|p=32}} The former President and his allies appealed to Christians' grievances by calling out "woke indoctrination" in schools, [[Legal status of transgender people|trans rights]] initiatives, and even the [[Mexico–United States border|crisis at the Southern border]]. To this extent, their partisan conservative messaging and Christian messaging were indistinguishable.{{Cite news |last=Herman |first=Alice |date=October 21, 2024 |title=Trump courts Christian vote and says 'God saved me for a purpose' |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/oct/21/trump-christians-vote-us-election |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250308170903/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/oct/21/trump-christians-vote-us-election |archive-date=March 8, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} The conservative pastor [[Guillermo Maldonado (pastor)|Guillermo Maldonado]] said of the election, "You know, we’re now in spiritual warfare … It’s beyond warfare between the left and the right. It’s between [[good and evil]]. There’s a big fight right now that is affecting our country and we need to take back our country". Oftentimes, Trump cast himself as a [[messiah]]. Following his [[Attempted assassination of Donald Trump in Pennsylvania|assassination attempt in Pennsylvania]], he claimed that "God saved me for a purpose, and that’s to make our country greater than ever before". To this end, the campaign catered to the Christian and [[Evangelicalism in the United States|evangelical]] vote. === Disinformation === [[File:20240524 Trump groundwork for election denial.svg|alt=Trump's sowing of election doubt|thumb|Trump's [[Election denial movement in the United States|sowing of election doubt]] throughout the campaign compared with his two previous runs]] Throughout the campaign, Trump spread many [[False or misleading statements by Donald Trump|lies and misleading claims]], to the extent that journalists found it "especially difficult" to keep up with them.{{Cite news |last=Kessler |first=Glenn |date=March 14, 2024 |title=Trump has a bunch of new false claims. Here's a guide. |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/03/14/trump-crazy-new-claims-fact-checked/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240315001325/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/03/14/trump-crazy-new-claims-fact-checked/ |archive-date=March 15, 2024 |access-date=August 24, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |issn=0190-8286}}{{Cite news |last=Dale |first=Daniel |date=2024-11-01 |title=Analysis: Donald Trump's campaign of relentless lying {{!}} CNN Politics |url=https://www.cnn.com/2024/11/01/politics/analysis-donald-trumps-campaign-of-relentless-lying/index.html |access-date=2024-11-02 |work=[[CNN]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Itkowitz |first1=Colby |last2=Allam |first2=Hannah |date=August 19, 2024 |title=With false 'coup' claims, Trump primes supporters to challenge a Harris win |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/08/19/trump-stolen-election-coup-overthrow/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240824204443/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/08/19/trump-stolen-election-coup-overthrow/ |archive-date=August 24, 2024 |access-date=August 24, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |issn=0190-8286}} They found that he created an alternate reality: an America in which 15 million illegal immigrants, not 5 million, had entered the country under Biden, and in which [[inflation]] had gone up to 50%, not 9%. During a 64-minute news conference of his held in August 2024, ''[[NPR]]'' counted over 162 lies, misstatements, and vast exaggerations, an average of more than two per minute.{{Cite news |last=Calmes |first=Jackie |date=October 3, 2024 |title=Donald Trump turns on a firehose of lies as the election nears. It matters |url=https://www.latimes.com/opinion/story/2024-10-03/election-donald-trump-kamala-harris |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250313003435/https://www.latimes.com/opinion/story/2024-10-03/election-donald-trump-kamala-harris |archive-date=March 13, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[The Los Angeles Times]]}} Trump repeatedly embraced [[Conspiracy theory|conspiracies]] such as [[QAnon]]. There was a strategy behind this persistent lying. By "flooding the zone with shit," Trump's campaign received unrivaled media attention, and better resonated with voters disillusioned by the [[Presidency of Joe Biden|Biden administration]].{{Cite news |last=Smith |first=David |date=September 22, 2024 |title=Why Trump and Vance's strategy is 'say anything, make up anything' |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/sep/22/trump-vance-create-fake-stories-lies-election |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250304002640/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/sep/22/trump-vance-create-fake-stories-lies-election |archive-date=March 4, 2025 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} Vance himself admitted, "If I have to create stories so that the American media actually pays attention to the suffering of the American people, then that’s what I’m going to do". Moreover, repeated lies tethered Trump's base even closer to him,{{harvnb|Arceneaux|Truex|2023|pp=863–864}} fostering loyalty to the point that he could not be held accountable for his actions.{{harvnb|Moniz|Swann|2025|p=2}} This method paralleled the [[firehose of falsehood]] propaganda tactic.{{Cite news |last=Velshi |first=Ali |date=June 29, 2024 |title='Not a healthy democracy' that calls Trump a winner for 'firehose of falsehoods' |url=https://www.msnbc.com/ali-velshi/watch/-not-a-healthy-democracy-that-calls-trump-a-winner-for-firehose-of-falsehoods-213948485983 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240702083226/https://www.msnbc.com/ali-velshi/watch/-not-a-healthy-democracy-that-calls-trump-a-winner-for-firehose-of-falsehoods-213948485983 |archive-date=July 2, 2024 |access-date=May 1, 2025 |work=[[MSNBC]]}} Another method was the [[big lie]],{{harvnb|Moniz|Swann|2025|pp=1–5}}{{harvnb|Arceneaux|Truex|2023|pp=863–865}} defined as disinformation "so grand that it is difficult to believe that someone would have the gall to make [it] up".{{harvnb|Arceneaux|Truex|2023|p=864}} From as early as 2020, he incessantly [[Attempts to overturn the 2020 United States presidential election|claimed]] that that year's election was rigged, so much so that it developed into a big lie. This narrative would be repeated throughout the campaign.{{Cite news |last1=Yourish |first1=Karen |last2=Smart |first2=Charlie |date=May 24, 2024 |title=Trump's Pattern of Sowing Election Doubt Intensifies in 2024 |url=https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2024/05/24/us/politics/trump-election-results-doubt.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250428125221/https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2024/05/24/us/politics/trump-election-results-doubt.html |archive-date=April 28, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} He, and his allies, spoke of "election integrity" not just to motivate the Republican base, but to [[Election denial movement in the United States|cast doubt]] on the U.S. electoral process, with the ultimate aim of enfeebling democracy. In the lead up to the [[2024 United States presidential election|2024 election]], they made false claims of massive noncitizen voting by illegal immigrants in a Democratic operation to steal the election.Multiple sources: *{{cite news |date=April 12, 2024 |title=Fact checking Trump and Johnson's election integrity announcement |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/04/12/politics/fact-check-trump-johnson-elections/index.html |work=[[CNN]]}} *{{cite news |last1=Thompson |first1=Stuart A. |date=July 5, 2022 |title=On Conservative Radio, Misleading Message Is Clear: 'Democrats Cheat' |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2022/07/05/business/media/conservative-radio-democrats-cheat.html |work=[[The New York Times]]}} *{{cite news |last1=Corasaniti |first1=Nick |date=February 12, 2024 |title=Election Deniers Seek to Rewrite the Law |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/02/21/us/politics/election-deniers-seek-to-voting-legislation.html |work=[[The New York Times]]}} *{{cite news |last1=Przybyla |first1=Heidi |date=June 1, 2022 |title='It's going to be an army': Tapes reveal GOP plan to contest elections |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2022/06/01/gop-contest-elections-tapes-00035758 |work=[[Politico]]}} *{{cite news |last1=Lutz |first1=Eric |date=April 19, 2024 |title=No, Trump's Plan to Deploy 100,000 Poll Workers Isn't About "Election Integrity" |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/news/story/trump-campaign-poll-workers-election-integrity |work=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]]}} *{{cite news |last1=Timm |first1=Jane C. |date=April 19, 2024 |title=Trump campaign, RNC pledge to deploy 100,000 attorneys and volunteers to monitor the vote |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trump-rnc-vote-monitor-election-integrity-rcna148557 |work=[[NBC News]]}} *House G.O.P. Moves to Crack down on Noncitizen Voting, Sowing False Narrative on ''The New York Times''{{Cite news |last=Basu |first=Zachary |date=May 22, 2024 |title=Trump spreads false "assassination" claims as voters fear violence |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/05/22/trump-assassination-biden-fbi-violence |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240523020552/https://www.axios.com/2024/05/22/trump-assassination-biden-fbi-violence |archive-date=May 23, 2024 |access-date=May 23, 2024 |work=[[Axios (website)|Axios]] |quote=Former President Trump and his allies have already signaled they will not accept the results of the election if they believe it's "unfair", reviving the type of rhetoric that helped incite the Jan. 6 Capitol riot.}} In reality, [[Electoral fraud in the United States|voter fraud]] is extremely rare.{{Cite web |last=Morales-Doyle |first=Sean |date=April 12, 2024 |title=Noncitizen Voting Isn't Affecting State or Federal Elections — Here's Why |url=https://www.brennancenter.org/our-work/analysis-opinion/noncitizens-are-not-voting-federal-or-state-elections-heres-why |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250427110101/https://www.brennancenter.org/our-work/analysis-opinion/noncitizens-are-not-voting-federal-or-state-elections-heres-why |archive-date=April 27, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |website=[[Brennan Center for Justice]]}}{{Cite web |last=Kamarck |first=Elaine |date=October 28, 2024 |title=How widespread is election fraud in the United States? Not very |url=https://www.brookings.edu/articles/how-widespread-is-election-fraud-in-the-united-states-not-very/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250406020938/https://www.brookings.edu/articles/how-widespread-is-election-fraud-in-the-united-states-not-very/ |archive-date=April 6, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |website=[[Brookings Institution|Brookings]]}}{{Cite news |last=Lee |first=Ella |date=October 31, 2024 |title=Election fraud rare in US elections, despite persistent claims otherwise |url=https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/4962270-us-election-fraud-rare/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411180415/https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/4962270-us-election-fraud-rare/ |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} Trump vilified [[Postal voting|mail-in voting]] and [[early voting]], two alleged culprits of voter fraud, even as Republicans were advising supporters to use those voting methods in the coming election.{{cite news |author1=Kendall Ross |author2=Lalee Ibssa |author3=Soo Rin Kim |date=April 5, 2024 |title=Republican Party pushing mail-in voting despite Trump's opposition |url=https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/republicans-push-mail-voting-despite-trumps-opposition/story?id=108890566 |work=[[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]]}}{{Cite web |last=Kashinsky |first=Lisa |date=2024-09-23 |title=Trump tells supporters to vote early even as he still criticizes the practice |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2024/09/23/trump-tells-supporters-to-vote-early-even-as-he-still-criticizes-the-practice-00180650 |access-date=2025-04-27 |website=POLITICO |language=en}}{{Cite news |last1=Kornfield |first1=Meryl |last2=Arnsdorf |first2=Isaac |last3=Vazquez |first3=Maegan |date=October 26, 2024 |title=Trump slams early voting at Michigan rally marking start of early voting |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/10/26/trump-early-voting-criticize/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250427231833/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/10/26/trump-early-voting-criticize/ |archive-date=April 27, 2025 |access-date=April 28, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} When Trump was struck with criminal prosecutions, another big lie ensued—that he was completely innocent.{{harvnb|Moniz|Swann|2025|pp=2, 4}}{{Cite news |last=Greenwood |first=Max |date=March 30, 2023 |title=Trump lashes out at indictment, says he's 'completely innocent' |url=https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/3926877-trump-lashes-out-at-indictment-says-hes-completely-innocent/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240112052806/https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/3926877-trump-lashes-out-at-indictment-says-hes-completely-innocent/ |archive-date=January 12, 2024 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Price |first1=Michelle L. |last2=Colvin |first2=Jill |date=June 1, 2024 |title=Trump tries to move past his guilty verdict by attacking the criminal justice system |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-2024-campaign-trial-4629840240cb308c5eae335532ad17ed |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250415220555/https://apnews.com/article/trump-2024-campaign-trial-4629840240cb308c5eae335532ad17ed |archive-date=April 15, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} Some commentators described the former President's attribution of his "defeats" to a "rigged" system as a "[[Coin flipping|heads I win, tails you cheated]]" strategy.{{Cite news |last=Steinberg |first=Neil |date=May 14, 2024 |title=Trump's politics? Heads I win, tails you lose |url=https://chicago.suntimes.com/columnists/2024/05/14/2024-presidential-election-trump-biden-fraud-capitol-insurrection |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240520233403/https://chicago.suntimes.com/columnists/2024/05/14/2024-presidential-election-trump-biden-fraud-capitol-insurrection |archive-date=May 20, 2024 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[Chicago Sun-Times]]}} === Fearmongering === {{Quote box | quote = There's one request. It's very important. Register to vote. OK? And get everyone you know and everyone you don't know, drag them to register to vote. There's only two days left to register to vote in [[Georgia (U.S. state)|Georgia]] and [[Arizona]], 48 hours. Like, text people now. Now. And then make sure they actually do vote. If they don't, this will be the last election. | source = — [[Elon Musk]], a prominent Trump ally, in an October 2024 rally at [[Butler, Pennsylvania]]{{cite web |title=Speech: Donald Trump Holds a Campaign Rally in Butler, Pennsylvania - October 5, 2024 |url= https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-speech-campaign-rally-butler-pennsylvania-october-5-2024/ |website=[[Roll Call]] |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250221070932/https://rollcall.com/factbase/trump/transcript/donald-trump-speech-campaign-rally-butler-pennsylvania-october-5-2024/ |archive-date=February 21, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025}} | width = 25% }} Trump's campaign heavily relied on [[fearmongering]].{{harvnb|Hass et al.|2025|p=7}}{{Cite news |last=Castillo |first=Andrea |date=July 18, 2024 |title=GOP sticks to the message that migrants are dangerous |url=https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2024-07-18/trump-shooting-republican-campaign-immigrants-migrants-violent-dangerous |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240924092558/https://www.latimes.com/politics/story/2024-07-18/trump-shooting-republican-campaign-immigrants-migrants-violent-dangerous |archive-date=September 24, 2024 |access-date=September 25, 2024 |work=[[Los Angeles Times]] |issn=0458-3035}}{{Cite news |last=Michael |first=Gold |date=October 1, 2024 |title=Trump's Consistent Message Online and Onstage: Be Afraid |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/01/us/politics/trump-fear-speeches.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241001180636/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/01/us/politics/trump-fear-speeches.html/ |archive-date=October 1, 2024 |access-date=October 1, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |issn=0362-4331}} He inflated the economic, crime, and immigration-related state of the U.S. to paint an image of a nation in ruins, a "failed" "[[Third World]]" country, in his words. On [[Economic policy of the Joe Biden administration|Biden's economy]], he alleged that inflation was the highest it had ever been.{{Cite news |last=Horton |first=Jake |date=September 3, 2024 |title=Is US economy better or worse now than under Trump? |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c8xl5vnlzpwo |url-status=live |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |language=en |issn=0190-8286}} Trump made apocalyptic prophecies predicting imminent doom should he lose the election,{{Cite news |last=Lehmann |first=Chris |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250203162551/https://www.thenation.com/article/society/trump-apocalypse/ |archive-date=February 3, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |work=[[The Nation]]}}{{Cite |access-date=September 25, 2024 |work=[[NBC News]]}} including the break out of [[World War III|World War Three]]. Two frequent targets of Trump's fearmongering were illegal immigrants and transgender people. He repeatedly used [[Ethnic stereotype|racial stereotypes]] and [[Dehumanization|dehumanizing]] rhetoric to paint the influx of illegal immigrants as an assault—an "invasion"—on the American public,{{Cite news |last1=Gold |first1=Michael |last2=Huynh |first2=Anjali |date=April 2, 2024 |title=Trump Again Invokes 'Blood Bath' and Dehumanizes Migrants in Border Remarks |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/04/02/us/politics/trump-border-blood-bath.html |access-date=April 3, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}} {{subscription required}}{{Cite news |last1=Shear |first1=Michael D. |last2=Aleaziz |first2=Hamed |last3=Ulloa |first3=Jazmine |date=September 11, 2024 |title=How Trump Uses Vitriol for Migrants to Sideline Other Issues |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/11/us/politics/trump-debate-migrants.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240923163916/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/11/us/politics/trump-debate-migrants.html |archive-date=September 23, 2024 |access-date=September 25, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |issn=0362-4331}} citing baseless accounts of their proclivity for crime. At rallies, the former President stated that they will "walk in your kitchen, they'll cut your throat", and "grab young girls and slice them up right in front of their parents". On multiple occasions, Trump and Republicans promoted the conspiracy that Haitian immigrants in [[Springfield pet-eating hoax|Springfield, Ohio, were looting and eating people's pets]]. As a result of their efforts, dozens of [[bomb threat]]s emerged targeting Springfield schools, hospitals, public buildings, and businesses.{{Cite web |last1=Breuninger |first1=Kevin |last2=Rozzelle |first2=Josephine |date=September 16, 2024 |title=Ohio GOP Gov. DeWine says 'at least 33' bomb threats prompt Springfield to begin daily school sweeps |url=https://www.cnbc.com/2024/09/16/springfield-bomb-threats-ohio-republican.html |url-status=live |website=[[CNBC]] |language=en}} Besides illegal immigrants, Trump used transgender people as [[Scapegoating|scapegoats]].{{Cite news |last=Romano |first=Aja |date=October 30, 2024 |title=The cruel |work=[[Vox (website)|Vox]]}} He attempted to incite a moral panic over their interference in politics and society,{{Cite news |last1=Lawler |first1=Dave |last2=Pandey |first2=Erica |date=November 21, 2024 |title=Trump win emboldens GOP's anti-trans blitz |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/11/21/trump-anti-trans-bathroom-laws-gop |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250427211158/https://www.axios.com/2024/11/21/trump-anti-trans-bathroom-laws-gop |archive-date=April 27, 2025 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |work=[[Axios |date=September 4, 2024 |title=Fact check: Trump falsely claims schools are secretly sending children for gender-affirming surgeries |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/09/04/politics/donald-trump-fact-check-children-gender-affirming-surgery/index.html |url-status=live
