|access-date=May 3, 2025 |work=[[CNN]]}} and that trans women were unfairly infiltrating in [[women's sports]]. Fear drew voters wary of illegal immigration and transgenderism to sympathize with Trump's message, according to commentators. It also energized conservative adherents of his.{{Cite news |last=Collinson |first=Stephen |date=September 24, 2024 |title=Trump plays the fear card on the economy – and it seems to be working |url=https://www.cnn.com/2024/09/24/politics/trump-fear-economy-analysis/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240925213447/https://www.cnn.com/2024/09/24/politics/trump-fear-economy-analysis/index.html |archive-date=September 25, 2024 |access-date=May 3, 2025 |work=[[CNN]]}} == Struggle for the Republican nomination == === Primaries === [[File:The main rivals to Donald Trump for the 2024 Republican nomination.jpg|alt=Republican challengers|thumb|The main rivals to Trump for the [[Republican Party (United States)|Republican]] nomination: [[Ron DeSantis]], [[Nikki Haley]], [[Vivek Ramaswamy]], and [[Chris Christie]] (left to right, top row first)]] As of late November 2022, [[Quinnipiac University Polling Institute|Quinnipiac]] reported that 34% of Americans expressed approval of Donald Trump's candidacy, including just 62% of Republicans.{{Cite news |date=November 22, 2022 |title=Nearly 6 in 10 Americans View Trump's 2024 Run As A Bad Thing, Quinnipiac University National Poll Finds; Split Between Trump Vs. DeSantis For 2024 GOP Nominee |work=[[Quinnipiac University Polling Institute]]}} Some two months after its inception, only 30 out of 271 congressional Republicans had endorsed him.{{Cite news |last=Blake |first=Aaron |date=February 15, 2023 |title=Trump's meager early 2024 endorsements |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2023/02/15/trump-endorsements-2024/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241130104732/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2023/02/15/trump-endorsements-2024/ |archive-date=November 30, 2024 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} Trump was challenged in the primaries by [[Nikki Haley]] (February 14, 2023,{{Cite news |author=Kinnard, Meg |date=February 14, 2023 |title=Nikki Haley announces run for president, challenging Trump |url=https://apnews.com/article/nikki-haley-running-for-president-f28052019e8ac3846cd125b3d9614d4f |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230221040252/https://apnews.com/article/nikki-haley-running-for-president-f28052019e8ac3846cd125b3d9614d4f |archive-date=February 21, 2023 |access-date=February 20, 2023 |work=[[Associated Press]] |language=en}} to March 6, 2024), [[Vivek Ramaswamy]] (February 21, 2023, to January 15, 2024), Asa Hutchinson (April 6, 2023, to January 16, 2024), and [[Ron DeSantis]] (May 24, 2023, to January 21, 2024). Other challengers, who withdrew before the primaries, were [[Perry Johnson (businessman)|Perry Johnson]] (March 2, 2023, to October 20, 2023), [[Larry Elder]] (April 20, 2023, to October 26, 2023), [[Tim Scott]] (May 19, 2023, to November 12, 2023), Mike Pence (June 5, 2023, to October 28, 2023), [[Chris Christie]] (June 6, 2023, to January 10, 2024), [[Doug Burgum]] (June 7, 2023, to December 4, 2023), [[Francis Suarez]] (June 14, 2023, to August 29, 2023), and Will Hurd (June 22, 2023, to October 9, 2023). From August 23 to January 10, 2024, there were five [[2024 Republican Party presidential debates and forums|debates]] among the candidates in the [[2024 Republican Party presidential primaries|campaign]] for the [[Republican Party (United States)|Republican Party]]'s nomination for president. Trump was absent from all of them, and was not planning to attend the debates scheduled for January 18 and 21, 2024.{{Cite news |last=Garrity |first=Kelly |date=January 2, 2024 |title=Trump forgoes CNN debate in favor of Fox town hall |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2024/01/02/trump-debate-fox-town-hall-00133443 |access-date=January 10, 2024 |work=[[Politico]] |language=en-US}} On January 16, when she and Ron DeSantis were the last challengers left, Nikki Haley announced she would not attend the January 18 debate unless Donald Trump took part in it. [[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]] canceled that debate,{{cite news |last=Kinnard |first=Meg |date=January 16, 2024 |title=Next Republican debate canceled after Nikki Haley says she'll only participate if Donald Trump does |url=https://apnews.com/article/nikki-haley-election-2024-trump-desantis-debate-cfa811ad87758a2d92779e793f06bbce |access-date=January 17, 2024 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} and [[CNN]] canceled the January 21 one.{{cite news |last=Doherty |first=Erin |date=January 17, 2024 |title=CNN cancels New Hampshire Republican primary debate |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/01/17/new-hampshire-republican-debate-cnn-cancel-haley |access-date=January 25, 2024 |work=[[Axios (website)|Axios]]}} By mid-January 2024, ''[[Politico]]'' reported that a majority of congressional Republicans had come out in favor of Trump.{{Cite news |last=Isenstadt |first=Alex |date=January 14, 2024 |title=Trump now has majority congressional GOP support |url=https://www.politico.com/live-updates/2024/01/14/iowa-caucus/trump-hits-milestone-00135550 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250321015130/https://www.politico.com/live-updates/2024/01/14/iowa-caucus/trump-hits-milestone-00135550 |archive-date=March 21, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[Politico]]}} After winning the primaries in Washington, D.C. (March 3) and Vermont (March 5), Haley suspended her presidential campaign the day after [[Super Tuesday#2024|Super Tuesday]].{{cite news |last1=Atwood |first1=Kylie |last2=John |first2=Arit |last3=Davis |first3=Ebony |date=March 6, 2024 |title=Nikki Haley exits Republican presidential race |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/03/06/politics/nikki-haley-2024-presidential-race/index.html |access-date=March 18, 2024 |work=[[CNN]]}} National primary polling showed Trump leading by 50 points over other candidates during the Republican primaries.{{Cite news |title=2024 Republican Primary Polls |url=https://pro.morningconsult.com/trackers/2024-gop-primary-election-tracker |access-date=2024-01-16 |website=Morning Consult Pro |language=en}} After he won a [[landslide victory]] in the [[2024 Iowa Republican presidential caucuses]], Trump was generally described as being the Republican Party's [[Presidential nominee|presumptive nominee for president]].{{Cite news |last1=Slattery |first1=Gram |last2=Ulmer |first2=Alexandra |date=January 16, 2024 |title=Ron DeSantis bet the farm on Iowa. |work=[[Reuters]] |quote="The Iowa results confirm a compelling consensus that Trump will be the nominee and there is nothing anyone can do about it absent an act of God or the courts," Jowers said.}}{{Cite news |last1=Politi |first1=James |last2=Fedor |first2=Lauren |date=January 15, 2023 |title='The inevitable nominee': Iowa embraces Trump as rivals left in the dust |url=https://www.ft.com/content/c82d551f-b5fa-44d3-9e9b-d148bc8ad0f6 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240116091743/https://www.ft.com/content/c82d551f-b5fa-44d3-9e9b-d148bc8ad0f6 |archive-date=January 16, 2024 |access-date=January 16, 2023 |website=[[Financial Times]] |quote="Republican voters see him as the inevitable nominee, and they're already falling in line," he added.}}{{Cite magazine |last=Cortellessa |first=Eric |date=2024-01-16 |title=How Trump (magazine)|Time]]}} On March 12, 2024, Trump officially became the presumptive nominee of the Republican Party.{{Cite news |last=Colvin |first=Jill |date=2024-03-12 |title=Trump clinches 2024 Bush has no plans to endorse in the election |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/former-president-george-w-bush-no-plans-endorse-2024-election-rcna170055 |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=[[NBC News]] |language=en}} former vice presidents [[Mike Pence]],{{Cite news |last=Yilek |first=Caitlin |date=March 15, 2024 |title=Pence says he won't endorse Trump in 2024 |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/pence-wont-endorse-trump/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250401013311/https://www.cbsnews.com/news/pence-wont-endorse-trump/ |archive-date=April 1, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[CBS News]]}} and [[Dick Cheney]],{{Cite news |date=2024-09-07 |title=Former Vice President Dick Cheney says he will vote for Harris |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/dick-cheney-kamala-harris-liz-cheney-rcna169979 |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=[[NBC News]] |language=en}} former House Speakers [[John Boehner]]{{Cite news |last=Farhi |first=Arden |date=2023-06-08 |title=Former Republican House Speaker John Boehner says it's time for GOP "to move on" from Trump - CBS News |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/john-boehner-says-its-time-for-gop-to-move-on-from-trump-the-takeout/ |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=[[CBS News]]}} and [[Paul Ryan]],{{Cite news |last=Suter |first=Tara |date=2024-05-08 |title=Paul Ryan says he won't vote for Trump: 'I'm gonna write in a Republican' |url=https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/4650934-paul-ryan-donald-trump-2024-election-voting/ |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} as well as former representatives [[Liz Cheney]]{{Cite news |date=2024-10-13 |title=Liz Cheney issues dire warning about 'fundamentally cruel' Trump, agrees he's a 'fascist' |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/liz-cheney-issues-dire-warning-fundamentally-cruel-trump-agrees-s-fasc-rcna175193 |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=[[NBC News]] |language=en}} and [[Adam Kinzinger]].{{Cite news |last=Rozzelle |first=Josephine |date=2024-08-23 |title=Republican ex-Rep. Adam Kinzinger: Trump 'suffocated the soul of' the GOP |url=https://www.cnbc.com/2024/08/22/adam-kinzinger-republican-trump-harris-dnc.html |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=[[CNBC]] |language=en}} Some of Trump's [[2016 Republican Party presidential primaries|2016]] and [[2024 Republican Party presidential primaries|2024]] primary opponents such as [[Jeb Bush]],{{Cite news |last=Gans |first=Jared |date=2022-10-21 |title=Jeb Bush: GOP will be 'yearning for new leadership' in 2024 |url=https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/3698053-jeb-bush-gop-will-be-yearning-for-new-leadership-in-2024/ |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]] |language=en-US}} [[John Kasich]],{{cite news |last1=Balmert |first1=Jessie |date=2024-10-03 |title=Ex-Ohio Gov. John Kasich once again won't vote for former President Donald Trump |url=https://www.dispatch.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/10/03/ex-ohio-gov-john-kasich-wont-vote-for-trump-doesnt-endorse-harris/75494204007/ |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=The Columbus Dispatch |agency=[[USA Today]] Network Ohio Bureau}} [[Carly Fiorina]],{{Cite news |date=May 13, 2021 |title=A Conversation with Carly Fiorina |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/washington-post-live/2021/05/13/conversation-with-carly-fiorina/ |access-date=October 18, 2024 |newspaper=Washington Post |language=en-US |issn=0190-8286}} [[Chris Christie]],{{citation needed|date=April 2025}} [[Asa Hutchinson]],{{Cite news |last=Stracqualursi |first=Veronica |date=2024-03-18 |title=Asa Hutchinson says he won't endorse Trump and won't vote for Biden |url=https://www.cnn.com/2024/03/18/politics/asa-hutchinson-trump-biden/index.html |access-date=2024-10-18 |work=[[CNN]] |language=en}} and [[Will Hurd]]{{Cite news |last=Berg |first=Matt |date=June 23, 2023 |title=Hurd won't support Trump if he's the nominee, he says |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2023/06/23/hurd-wont-support-trump-00103367 |access-date=October 16, 2024 |work=[[Politico]] |language=en}} also declined to endorse or openly opposed the campaign. Republican organizations such as [[43 Alumni for America]], [[Haley Voters for Harris]], and [[The Lincoln Project]] all [[List of Kamala Harris 2024 presidential campaign endorsements|endorsed Harris]].{{Cite web |last=43_Alumni_Admin |date=2024-08-26 |title=Bush, McCain, and Romney Alumni for Harris Statement |url=https://43alumniforamerica.com/bush-mccain-romney-alumni-for-harris/ |access-date=2024-10-19 |website=[[43 Alumni for America]]}}{{Cite news |last=Navarro |first=Aaron |date=2024-07-24 |title="Haley voters for Harris" group gets cease and desist request from Nikki Haley - CBS News |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/haley-voters-for-harris-cease-and-desist-nikki-haley-kamala-harris/ |access-date=2024-10-19 |work=[[CBS News]]}}{{Cite web |last=Cooper |first=Jack |date=2024-07-29 |title=The Lincoln Project: Kamala for President |url=https://lincolnproject.us/kamala-for-president/ |access-date=2024-10-19 |website=The Lincoln Project |language=en-US}} Half of the members of [[First cabinet of Donald Trump|Trump's cabinet]] did not support his run for president.{{Cite news |date=October 29, 2024 |title=These are the Republicans endorsing Harris over Trump |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/10/12/republicans-for-kamala-harris-2024-election/ |newspaper=Washington Post}}{{Cite news |date=October 31, 2024 |title=A second Trump term comes with unacceptable risks |url=https://www.economist.com/leaders/2024/10/31/a-second-trump-term-comes-with-unacceptable-risks |access-date=2024-10-31 |newspaper=[[The Economist]] |issn=0013-0613}} === Vice-presidential choice === {{main|2024 Republican Party vice presidential candidate selection}} [[File:Senator Vance official portrait. 118th Congress.jpg|alt=JD Vance|thumb|Ohio senator [[JD Vance]], Trump's running mate in 2024]] [[Mike Pence]] served as Trump's vice president from 2017 to 2021, as well as his running mate in 2020. However, the pair had a dramatic falling out on January 6, 2021, when Pence refused to follow Trump's orders to deny the certification of the 2020 election results. The President thereafter tweeted that Pence "didn’t have the courage to do what should have been done to protect our country and our constitution".{{Cite news |last=Pengelly |first=Martin |date=September 14, 2022 |title=Trump tells authors of 'The Divider' book he won't pick Pence for 2024 |url=https://www.theguardian.com/books/2022/sep/14/trump-pence-running-mate-2024-book Kennedy, Jr. won't be his running mate pick |url=https://eu.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/01/28/donald-trump-campaign-rfk-jr-vice-president/72388400007/ |work=[[USA Today]] |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250115210631/https://eu.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/01/28/donald-trump-campaign-rfk-jr-vice-president/72388400007/ |archive-date=January 15, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025}} In April, however, multiple sources close to Trump once again reported that he was considering Kennedy.{{cite news |last=Bender |first=Michael C. |date=April 5, 2024 |title=Trump-Kennedy 2024? Trump, at least, is intrigued by the idea |url=https://www.seattletimes.com/nation-world/trump-kennedy-2024-trump-at-least-is-intrigued-by-the-idea/ |work=[[Seattle Times]] |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250219124028/https://www.seattletimes.com/nation-world/trump-kennedy-2024-trump-at-least-is-intrigued-by-the-idea/ |archive-date=February 19, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025}}}}|[[Kari Lake]], former news anchor and [[Kari Lake#2024 Senate run|2024 candidate for senator from Arizona]]|[[Kristi Noem]], governor of South Dakota (2019–2025)|[[Vivek Ramaswamy]], businessman and former [[Vivek Ramaswamy 2024 presidential campaign|2024 presidential candidate]]|[[Marco Rubio]], senator from Florida (2011–2025) and [[Marco Rubio 2016 presidential campaign|2016 presidential candidate]]|[[Sarah Huckabee Sanders]], governor of Arkansas (2023–present) and former White House press secretary (2017–2019)|[[Tim Scott]], senator from South Carolina (2013–present) and former [[Tim Scott 2024 presidential campaign|2024 presidential candidate]]|[[Elise Stefanik]], representative from New York's 21st congressional district (2015–present)|[[JD Vance]], senator from Ohio (2023–2025) }} By June, the Trump campaign had reportedly delivered vetting paperwork to Burgum, Carson, Cotton, Donalds, Rubio, Scott, Stefanik, and Vance.{{Cite news |last1=Allen |first1=Mike |date=June 6, 2024 |title=Trump's doc requests reveal VP short list |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/06/06/trump-vp-short-list-top-contenders |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250402055411/https://www.axios.com/2024/06/06/trump-vp-short-list-top-contenders |archive-date=April 2, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |website=[[Axios (website)|Axios]] |language=en-US}} Ultimately, JD Vance was chosen to be Trump's running mate. Media analysts deduced this pick to an attempt to court [[Midwestern United States|Midwestern]] and white working-class voters. At 39, he also provided a counterbalance to Trump, 78 years old at the time. Vance's conservative stances, such as his isolationism and prior opposition to abortion even in cases of rape or incest, established the campaign's full commitment to [[Trumpism]].{{Cite news |last=Zurcher |first=Anthony |date=July 16, 2024 |title=Why Trump picked JD Vance as his running mate |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cqv50z8dzj5o |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250212142928/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cqv50z8dzj5o |archive-date=February 12, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Treene |first1=Alayna |last2=Contorno |first2=Steve |last3=Collins |first3=Kaitlan |date=July 16, 2024 |title=How Donald Trump landed on JD Vance as his vice presidential pick |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/07/16/politics/how-trump-chose-vp-pick-vance/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240830155616/https://edition.cnn.com/2024/07/16/politics/how-trump-chose-vp-pick-vance/index.html |archive-date=August 30, 2024 |access-date=April 14, 2024 |work=[[CNN]]}}{{Cite news |last=Osgood |first=Brian |date=July 18, 2024 |title=Why did Republican Donald Trump choose JD Vance as his vice president? |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/18/why-did-republican-donald-trump-choose-jd-vance-as-his-vice-president |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241229184042/https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/18/why-did-republican-donald-trump-choose-jd-vance-as-his-vice-president |archive-date=December 29, 2024 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Al Jazeera]]}} Vance was the first [[Ohio]]an to appear on a major party presidential ticket since [[John W. Bricker|John Bricker]] in [[1944 United States presidential election|1944]],{{Cite news |author=Shoemaker |first=Anthony |date=July 15, 2024 |title=JD Vance would be first Ohio resident to be vice president, is first in 80 years on ticket |url=https://www.dispatch.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/07/15/jd-vance-would-be-first-ohio-resident-to-serve-as-vp/74360390007/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240716142856/https://www.dispatch.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/07/15/jd-vance-would-be-first-ohio-resident-to-serve-as-vp/74360390007/ |archive-date=July 16, 2024 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |website=[[The Columbus Dispatch]]}} and the first veteran since [[John McCain]] in [[2008 United States presidential election|2008]].{{Cite news |last=Cruz |first=Melissa |date=July 16, 2024 |title=Was JD Vance a Marine? A look at the military record of Trump's VP pick |url=https://eu.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/07/16/was-jd-vance-marine-military/74411600007/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250414164257/https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/07/16/was-jd-vance-marine-military/74411600007/ |archive-date=April 14, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[USA Today]]}} He was also the first [[millennial]] and veteran of the [[Iraq War|Iraq war]] (and the wider [[war on terror]]) on a presidential ticket.{{Cite news |last=Levien |first=Simon J. |date=July 17, 2024 |title=J.D. 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} == History of the campaign == === Rallies === {{See also|List of rallies for the 2024 Donald Trump presidential campaign}} [[File:Donald Trump's 2024 campaign rallies.png|alt=Trump's 2024 campaign rallies|thumb|Trump's 2024 campaign rallies, the vast majority of which were held in the [[Swing state|battleground states]] of [[Wisconsin]], [[Michigan]], [[Pennsylvania]], [[North Carolina]], [[Georgia (U.S. state)|Georgia]], [[Arizona]], and [[Nevada]]]] Donald Trump's campaign events were often described as "freewheeling", like a "rock show". It also stated, "Trump’s speeches at rallies can stretch for two hours as he meanders between policy proposals, personal stories and jokes, attacks on his opponents and complaints that he is being persecuted by the courts, and dire warnings about the country’s future". ''[[The New York Times]]'' highlighted an average rally length of 82 minutes compared with 45 minutes in 2016. The most prominent songs used by Trump's campaign were "[[God Bless the U.S.A.]]" by [[Lee Greenwood]],{{Cite news|last=McGartland|first=Aidan|date=November 12, 2024|title=What role did music play in Trump and MAGA's electoral appeal? |url=https://theconversation.com/what-role-did-music-play-in-trump-and-magas-electoral-appeal-243261|work=[[The Conversation (website)|The Conversation]]|access-date=April 14, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250407203812/https://theconversation.com/what-role-did-music-play-in-trump-and-magas-electoral-appeal-243261|archive-date=April 7, 2025|url-status=live}} "[[Hold On, I'm Comin' (song)|Hold On, I'm Comin']]" by [[Sam & Dave]], "[[Merle Haggard discography#Music videos|America First]]" by [[Merle Haggard]],{{Cite news|last=Marcus|first=Josh|date=July 23, 2024|title=JD Vance has a walk-on song. It's about 'liberating' America |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/jd-vance-song-merle-haggard-b2584688.html|work=[[The Independent]]|access-date=April 14, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241115053900/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/jd-vance-song-merle-haggard-b2584688.html|archive-date=November 15, 2024|url-status=live}}{{Cite news|last=Willman|first=Chris|date=July 18, 2024|title=Why Merle Haggard Wrote 'America First,' the Song Suddenly Getting a Revival as JD Vance's Campaign Theme |url=https://variety.com/2024/music/columns/jd-vance-merle-haggard-america-first-theme-song-gop-convention-1236076641/|work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]|access-date=April 14, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250221150028/https://variety.com/2024/music/columns/jd-vance-merle-haggard-america-first-theme-song-gop-convention-1236076641/|archive-date=February 21, 2025|url-status=live}} and "[[Y.M.C.A. (song)|Y.M.C.A.]]" by [[Village People]]{{Cite news|last=Murphy|first=Joe|date=November 28, 2024|title=Trump made 'Y.M.C.A.' a staple at his rallies. Data shows it's sticking around. |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/data-graphics/another-winner-trumps-2024-election-village-people-ymca-rcna181817|work=[[NBC News]]|access-date=April 12, 2025|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250402191325/https://www.nbcnews.com/data-graphics/another-winner-trumps-2024-election-village-people-ymca-rcna181817|archive-date=April 2, 2025|url-status=live}} He also used music for which the artists and owners of copyrights were not compensated.{{Cite news|last=Wagmeister |first=Elizabeth |date=2024-08-28 |title=Can Beyoncé, Celine Dion and Foo Fighters stop Trump from using their music? It's complicated |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/08/28/entertainment/beyonce-celine-dion-foo-fighters-trump-campaign/index.html |access-date=2024-09-26 |work=[[CNN]]}}{{Cite news|last=Greene |first=Leonard |date=2024-08-24 |title=Donald Trump thinks he's above the law, even when illegally using artists' music |url=https://lasvegassun.com/news/2024/aug/24/donald-trump-thinks-hes-above-the-law-even-when-il/ |access-date=2024-09-26 |work=[[Las Vegas Sun]]}} One such use—that of "Hold On, I'm Comin'"—resulted in a federal injunction barring Trump from playing it in his rallies any longer.{{Cite news |last=Luscombe |first=Richard |date=September 3, 2024 |title=Trump ordered by judge to stop playing Isaac Hayes song at campaign rallies |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/sep/03/trump-isaac-hayes |url-status=live |access-date=September 26, 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250317204600/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/sep/03/trump-isaac-hayes |archive-date=March 17, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}}{{Cite news |last=Savage |first=Mark |date=September 4, 2024 |title=Trump ordered to stop using Isaac Hayes' music |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c89w28w13zxo |url-status=live |access-date=April 14, 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250401075132/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c89w28w13zxo |archive-date=April 1, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}} Frequent chants in his rallies were "Fight! Fight! Fight!",{{Cite news |last=Smith |first=David |date=July 19, 2024 |title='Fight! Fight! Fight!': Trump emerges as an American messiah with swagger |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/jul/19/donald-trump-rnc-speech-american-messiah-with-swagger |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250403200714/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/jul/19/donald-trump-rnc-speech-american-messiah-with-swagger |archive-date=April 3, 2025 |access-date=November 4, 2024 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} and "USA!"{{Cite news |date=October 25, 2024 |title=Chants of 'USA' as Trump Makes Campaign Stop at Vegas Restaurant |url=https://uk.news.yahoo.com/chants-usa-trump-makes-campaign-094148276.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241211072742/https://uk.news.yahoo.com/chants-usa-trump-makes-campaign-094148276.html |archive-date=December 11, 2024 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Yahoo! News]]}} === Court cases === From 2023 up until the [[2024 United States presidential election|2024 election]], Trump was engulfed in legal battles. Trump's prosecutions, unprecedented in the nation's history, only bolstered his support, according to commentators. His funding surged, and the [[Republican Party (United States)|Republican Party]] grew ever more allegiant to him, although some commentators warned that moderate Republicans may have been alienated. Trump claimed his trial in New York was "rigged" and accused the Democratic Party of orchestrating his criminal trials to prevent him from returning to the White House, of which there is no evidence.{{Cite news |last=Qiu |first=Linda |date=May 31, 2024 |title=Trump and Allies Assail Conviction With Faulty Claims |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/05/31/us/politics/trump-speech-fact-check.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240601040801/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/05/31/us/politics/trump-speech-fact-check.html |archive-date=June 1, 2024 |access-date=June 1, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |issn=0362-4331}} {{subscription required}} In May 2024, Trump falsely claimed Joe Biden was ready to kill him during the [[FBI search of Mar-a-Lago]] by misrepresenting standard [[United States Department of Justice|Justice Department]] policy on use of force.{{Cite news |last=Astor |first=Maggie |date=May 22, 2024 |title=Trump Falsely Claims Biden Administration Was 'Locked & Loaded' to Kill Him |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/05/22/us/politics/trump-classified-documents-case-search.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240523085530/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/05/22/us/politics/trump-classified-documents-case-search.html |archive-date=May 23, 2024 |access-date=May 23, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |issn=0362-4331 |quote=Former President Donald J. Trump misrepresented a standard Justice Department policy to claim the F.B.I. was ready to kill him when searching his home in 2022.}} {{subscription required}} These statements played into his attempts to project himself as a martyr. === December 2022–March 2024 === ==== Return to social media and establishment of Truth Social ==== In the wake of the [[January 6 United States Capitol attack|January 6 Capitol attack]], many of Trump's social media accounts were banned.{{Clarify|date=April 2025}} In November 2022, [[Elon Musk]], who had recently taken ownership of [[Twitter]], reinstated Trump's accounts.{{cite news |last=Johnson |first=Ted |date=November 19, 2022 |title=Elon Musk Reinstates Donald Trump's Twitter Account |url=https://deadline.com/2022/11/twitter-donald-trump-reinstated-elon-musk-1235177629/ |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]]}} A few months later, [[Facebook]] and [[Instagram]] followed suit.{{Cite web |last=O'Sullivan |first=Donie |date=2023-02-09 |title=Meta restores Trump's Facebook and Instagram accounts |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2023/02/09/tech/trump-facebook-instagram-restored/index.html |access-date=2025-04-21 |website=CNN |language=en}} In October 2021, Trump's own social media platform, [[Truth Social]], was founding, to counter the social bans imposed on him. He would primarily use it to spread messages.{{Cite news |date=2024-03-22 |title=What we know about Truth Social, Donald Trump's social media platform |url=https://apnews.com/article/truth-social-donald-trump-djt-ipo-digital-world-7437d5dcc491a1459a078195ae547987 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241211233015/https://apnews.com/article/truth-social-donald-trump-djt-ipo-digital-world-7437d5dcc491a1459a078195ae547987 |archive-date=December 11, 2024 |access-date=2024-12-09 |work=[[Associated Press]] |language=en}} It is [[alt-tech]].{{Clarify|date=April 2025}} ==== Dinner with Kanye West and Nick Fuentes ==== In November 2022, [[Kanye West]], then a [[Kanye West 2024 presidential campaign|candidate]] for the 2024 election, dined with Trump at Mar-a-Lago, alongside [[White nationalism|white nationalist]] [[Nick Fuentes]].{{Cite news |last=McGraw |first=Meridith |date=November 25, 2022 |title=Donald Trump dined with white nationalist, Holocaust denier Nick Fuentes |url=https://www.haaretz.com/us-news/2022-11-24/ty-article/.highlight/trump-hosts-holocaust-revisionist-nick-fuentes-and-kanye-west-at-mar-a-lago/00000184-a9a8-dd96-ad8c-eba891ed0000 |url-status=live Entertainment]]}}{{Cite news |last=Holmes |first=Kristen |date=August 26, 2023 |title=Trump campaign says it has raised $7.1 million since Atlanta mug shot |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2023/08/26/politics/trump-mugshot-fundraising-campaign/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230829164235/https://edition.cnn.com/2023/08/26/politics/trump-mugshot-fundraising-campaign/index.html |archive-date=August 29, 2023 |access-date=August 31, 2023 |work=[[CNN]]}} Finally, later in August, the federal government and [[Georgia (U.S. state)|Georgia]] separately [[Georgia election racketeering prosecution|indicted]] him for criminal conspiracy and fraud vis-à-vis his efforts to overturn the results of the 2020 election.{{Cite news |last1=Barrett |first1=Devlin |last2=Hsu |first2=Spencer S. |last3=Stein |first3=Perry |last4=Dawsey |first4=Josh |last5=Alemany |first5=Jacqueline |date=August 1, 2023 |title=Trump charged in probe of Jan. 6, efforts to overturn 2020 election |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2023/08/01/trump-indictment-jan-6-2020-election/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230801233818/https://www.washingtonpost.com/national-security/2023/08/01/trump-indictment-jan-6-2020-election/ |archive-date=August 1, 2023 |access-date=August 2, 2023 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}}{{Cite news |last=Grabenstein |first=Hannah |date=August 15, 2023 |title=Read the full Georgia indictment against Trump and 18 allies |url=https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/read-the-full-georgia-indictment-against-trump-and-18-allies |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411141004/https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/read-the-full-georgia-indictment-against-trump-and-18-allies |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |access-date=April 11, 2025 |work=[[PBS News Hour]]}} Trump denied wrongdoing in all four cases. Besides these indictments, he was [[E. Jean Carroll vs. Donald J. Trump|found liable]] in a civil lawsuit for sexual abuse and defamation against journalist [[E. Jean Carroll]].{{Cite news |last=del Valle |first=Lauren |date=May 9, 2023 |title=Jury finds Donald Trump sexually abused E. Jean Carroll in civil case, awards her $5 million |url=https://www.cnn.com/2023/05/09/politics/e-jean-carroll-trump-lawsuit-battery-defamation-verdict/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241009121821/https://www.cnn.com/2023/05/09/politics/e-jean-carroll-trump-lawsuit-battery-defamation-verdict/index.html |archive-date=October 9, 2024 |access-date=May 10, 2023 |work=[[CNN]] |language=en}} === March 2024–November 2024 === ==== At the Libertarian National Convention ==== Trump spoke at the [[2024 Libertarian National Convention]] in May, becoming the first president to address a third party convention in modern U.S. history.{{Cite news |author=Bowden |first=John |date=May 26, 2024 |title=Trump furiously jeered as he taunts Libertarians for winning '3%' in elections at their convention |url=https://www.the-independent.com/news/world/americas/us-politics/trump-libertarian-party-speech-convention-b2551645.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250303222241/https://www.the-independent.com/news/world/americas/us-politics/trump-libertarian-party-speech-convention-b2551645.html |archive-date=March 3, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[The Independent]]}} He urged the [[Libertarian Party (United States)|Libertarian Party]] to nominate him lest they "keep getting [their] 3% every four years".{{Cite news |last=The Associated Press |date=May 26, 2024 |title=Trump confronts repeated booing during Libertarian convention speech |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/05/26/g-s1-1061/trump-confronts-repeated-booing-during-libertarian-convention-speech |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250330045929/https://www.npr.org/2024/05/26/g-s1-1061/trump-confronts-repeated-booing-during-libertarian-convention-speech |archive-date=March 30, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2024 |work=[[NPR]]}} In an attempt to court the crowd, the former President vowed to appoint a [[Libertarianism in the United States|Libertarian]] to his cabinet and commute [[Ross Ulbricht]]'s prison sentence. However, his speech was blanketed with jeers; one attendee even held up a sign that read "No wannabe dictators!"{{Cite news |last=Smith |first=David |date=May 26, 2024 |title='No wannabe dictators!': Donald Trump booed at Libertarian convention |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/may/26/no-wannabe-dictators-donald-trump-booed-at-libertarian-convention |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250303191853/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/may/26/no-wannabe-dictators-donald-trump-booed-at-libertarian-convention |archive-date=March 3, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} Biden did not attend the convention. Come nomination day, Trump had been eliminated during balloting, and [[Chase Oliver]] was selected as the Libertarian nominee for president.{{Cite news |last=Pellish |first=Aaron |date=May 27, 2024 |title=RFK Jr. loses in first round of Libertarian Party's presidential nomination vote. Trump didn't file paperwork to qualify |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/05/26/politics/rfk-jr-nominated-libertarian-party/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250110181020/https://edition.cnn.com/2024/05/26/politics/rfk-jr-nominated-libertarian-party/index.html |archive-date=January 10, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[CNN]]}} ==== Felony conviction ==== In May 2024, Trump was convicted of felonies regarding the Stormy Daniels case. This made him the first former U.S. president ever to be convicted of a crime.{{Cite news |last1=Gamio |first1=Lazaro |last2=Yourish |first2=Karen |last3=Haag |first3=Matthew |last4=Bromwich |first4=Jonah E. |last5=Haberman |first5=Maggie |last6=Lai |first6=K.K. Rebecca |date=May 30, 2024 |title=The Trump Manhattan Criminal Verdict, Count By Count |url=https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2024/05/30/nyregion/trump-hush-money-verdict.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240530210216/https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2024/05/30/nyregion/trump-hush-money-verdict.html |archive-date=May 30, 2024 |access-date=May 30, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |language=en-us}}{{Cite news |last1=Sisak |first1=Michael R. |last2=Peltz |first2=Jennifer |last3=Tucker |first3=Eric |last4=Price |first4=Michelle L. |last5=Colvin |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-trial-deliberations-jury-testimony-verdict-85558c6d08efb434d05b694364470aa0 |url-status=live |access-date=April 11, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} After the election, he was given an "unconditional discharge," shielding him from punishment or incarceration.{{Cite news |last=Durkee |first=Alison |date=January 10, 2025 |title=Trump Formally Convicted—But Faces No Punishment |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/alisondurkee/2025/01/10/trump-not-sentenced-to-any-punishments-in-hush-money-criminal-case/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250415145915/https://www.forbes.com/sites/alisondurkee/2025/01/10/trump-not-sentenced-to-any-punishments-in-hush-money-criminal-case/ |archive-date=April 15, 2025 |access-date=May 2, 2025 |work=[[Forbes]]}} ==== Debate with Joe Biden ==== {{Main|2024 Joe Biden–Donald Trump presidential debate}} {{multiple image|align=thumb|total_width=250|image1=Joe Biden presidential portrait (cropped).jpg|image2=Kamala Harris Vice Presidential Portrait (cropped).jpg|footer=Trump's and Vance's respective opponents prior to [[Withdrawal of Joe Biden from the 2024 United States presidential election|Biden's withdrawal]], Democratic candidates [[Joe Biden]] and [[Kamala Harris]]}} On June 27, 2024, the [[2024 Joe Biden–Donald Trump presidential debate|first]] of [[2024 United States presidential debates|two debates]] in the election season took place, with Trump up against Joe Biden.{{Cite news |last=Megerian |first=Chris |date=September 7, 2024 |title=Biden's disastrous debate performance offers lessons for Harris and warnings for Trump |url=https://apnews.com/article/joe-biden-donald-trump-debate-d62e8e7281fff34be8925adc9b4e6dc2 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250201115110/https://apnews.com/article/joe-biden-donald-trump-debate-d62e8e7281fff34be8925adc9b4e6dc2 |archive-date=February 1, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} The debate was defined by Biden's "disastrous" performance, as he rambled incoherently and repeatedly lost his train of thought. This exacerbated already-existing [[Age and health concerns about Joe Biden|concerns about the President's fitness to serve]].{{Cite news |last=Zurcher |first=Anthony |date=June 28, 2024 |archive-date=April 15, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |website=[[Ipsos]]}}—the former President's lead in [[Nationwide opinion polling for the 2024 United States presidential election|national polls]] expanded,{{Cite web |title=2024 General Election: Trump vs. Biden |url=https://www.realclearpolling.com/polls/president/general/2024/trump-vs-biden |url-status=live |last2=Chao-Fong |first2=Léonie |last3=Belam |first3=Martin |last4=Sullivan and |first4=Helen |last5=Singh |first5=Maanvi |date=June 28, 2024 |title=Calls for Biden to stand aside as surrogates double down in support |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/live/2024/jun/27/presidential-debate-trump-biden-updates?filterKeyEvents=false&page=with%3Ablock-667ea01d8f086bfb3f8f00f0#block-667ea01d8f086bfb3f8f00f0 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241010004425/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/live/2024/jun/27/presidential-debate-trump-biden-updates?filterKeyEvents=false&page=with%3Ablock-667ea01d8f086bfb3f8f00f0#block-667ea01d8f086bfb3f8f00f0 |archive-date=October 10, 2024 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}}{{Cite news |last=Schnell |first=Mychael |date=July 7, 2024 |title=Senior House Democrats say Biden should step aside during private call |url=https://thehill.com/homenews/house/4758958-biden-democratic-leaders-step-aside/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250404200452/https://thehill.com/homenews/house/4758958-biden-democratic-leaders-step-aside/ |archive-date=April 4, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}}{{Cite news |last=Baker |first=Peter |date=November 6, 2024 |title=A Fumbling Performance, and a Panicking Party
