|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411091723/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/06/27/us/politics/biden-debate-democrats.html |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} Nevertheless, some commentators pointed out that Biden's poor performance merely overshadowed Trump's persistent lying throughout the debate. [[Doyle McManus]] of ''[[Los Angeles Times|The Los Angeles Times]]'' opined that "nobody won, but Biden clearly lost".{{Cite news |last=McManus |first=Doyle |date=June 28, 2024 |title=Column: A tongue-tied octogenarian debates a pathological liar. Nobody won, but Biden clearly lost |url=https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2024-06-28/biden-trump-debate-who-won-nobody-but-president-lost-ground |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240628140057/https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2024-06-28/biden-trump-debate-who-won-nobody-but-president-lost-ground |archive-date=June 28, 2024 |access-date=June 28, 2024 |work=[[The Los Angeles Times]]}} ==== ''Trump v. United States'' ==== {{Main|Trump v. United States}} In the legal case ''[[Trump v. United States]]'', Trump argued that [[Constitution of the United States|the Constitution]] allows for absolute immunity for all presidential actions—even those criminal—unless the Senate successfully votes to impeach.{{cite news |last1=Kruzel |first1=John |last2=Chung |first2=Andrew |date=April 25, 2024 |title=US Supreme Court justices in Trump case lean toward some level of immunity |url=https://www.reuters.com/legal/us-supreme-court-weighs-trumps-bid-immunity-prosecution-2024-04-25/ |access-date=April 25, 2024 |work=[[Reuters]]}} His argument was rejected by most political commentators and two lower courts. In a unanimous ruling by the three-judge panel of the [[United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit|U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia]], the court stated that if Trump's theory of constitutional authority were accepted, it would "collapse our system of separated powers" and put a president above the law.{{cite news |last=Dwyer |first=Devin |date=April 24, 2024 |title=Supreme Court to take up Trump's claim of 'absolute immunity' from criminal prosecution |url=https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/supreme-court-takes-trumps-claim-absolute-immunity-criminal/story?id=109251013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240424091329/https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/supreme-court-takes-trumps-claim-absolute-immunity-criminal/story?id=109251013 |archive-date=April 24, 2024 |access-date=April 24, 2024 |work=[[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]]}} Nevertheless, in July 2024, the [[Supreme Court of the United States|U.S. Supreme Court]] sided with Trump in a [[Ideological leanings of United States Supreme Court justices|partisan]] 6–3 decision. It determined that the Constitution affords the President with [[absolute immunity]] for acts within his constitutional purview and presumptive immunity for official acts, but provides no immunity for unofficial acts.{{cite news |last=Hurley |first=Lawrence |date=July 1, 2024 |title=Supreme Court provides win to Trump, ruling he has immunity for many acts in election interference indictment |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/supreme-court-rules-trump-may-immunity-federal-election-inter-rcna149135 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240701143716/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/supreme-court-rules-trump-may-immunity-federal-election-inter-rcna149135 |archive-date=July 1, 2024 |access-date=July 1, 2024 |work=[[NBC News]]}} ==== Attempted assassinations ==== {{Main|Attempted assassination of Donald Trump in Pennsylvania|Attempted assassination of Donald Trump in Florida}} {{External media|title=One of Evan Vucci's iconic photographs of Trump following his assassination attempt at Butler|image1={{center|1=[https://cdn.theatlantic.com/thumbor/VfqSTqxolOPzRLk_bhz6Inue6hs=/0x0:4188x2356/1952x1098/media/img/mt/2024/07/AP24195803307155/original.jpg View] – via ''The Atlantic''}}|float=right}} In the span of three months, Trump faced two assassination attempts. On July 13, 2024, during a rally near [[Butler, Pennsylvania]], he was [[Attempted assassination of Donald Trump in Pennsylvania|shot and wounded]] in the upper right ear. He was escorted out of the venue by [[United States Secret Service|U.S. Secret Service]].{{Cite news |last=Lowenkron |first=Hadriana |date=July 13, 2024 |title=Trump Rushed Away From Rally With What Looks Like Blood on Ear |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-07-13/trump-rushed-off-stage-at-pennsylvania-rally |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250320013724/https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2024-07-13/trump-rushed-off-stage-at-pennsylvania-rally |archive-date=March 20, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Bloomberg News]]}} The Secret Service swiftly killed the identified shooter, [[Thomas Matthew Crooks]].{{Cite news |last=Gurman |first=Sadie |date=July 13, 2024 |title=Donald Trump 'Fine' After Shots at Rally; Shooter Reportedly Killed |url=https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/trump-rally-incident/card/secret-service-killed-the-shooter-law-enforcement-says-7yRgx93eeJmSNHl3eraJ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241129083423/https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/trump-rally-incident/card/secret-service-killed-the-shooter-law-enforcement-says-7yRgx93eeJmSNHl3eraJ |archive-date=November 29, 2024 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[The Wall Street Journal]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Lowe |first1=Yohannes |last2=Bayer |first2=Lili |last3=Tian |first3=Yang |last4=Stein |first4=Chris |last5=Lawther |first5=Fran |date=July 14, 2024 |title=Trump rally shooting live: Male suspect 'tentatively identified'; FBI confirms incident being treated as assassination attempt |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/live/2024/jul/13/trump-rally-gun-shots-pennsylvania-latest-updates |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250408011206/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/live/2024/jul/13/trump-rally-gun-shots-pennsylvania-latest-updates |archive-date=April 8, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} In addition, Crooks also shot three other spectators, including 50-year-old firefighter Corey Comperatore, who was killed instantly.{{Cite news |date=2024-07-14 |title=Trump rally shooter identified as 20-year-old Pennsylvania man |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/politics-news/trump-rally-shooter-identified-rcna161757 |access-date=2024-07-14 |work=[[NBC News]] |language=en}} The assassination attempt was memorialized in a [[Donald Trump raised-fist photographs|series of photographs]] by [[Evan Vucci]]. These depict Trump being escorted off the podium, with blood coating his cheek, his fist raised defiantly, and an American flag fluttering in the background. Vucci's photographs became a symbol of the campaign.{{Cite news |last=Harper |first=Tyler Austin |date=July 14, 2024 |title=A Legendary American Photograph |url=https://www.theatlantic.com/politics/archive/2024/07/trump-assassination-attempt-evan-vucci/679011/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250327201649/https://www.theatlantic.com/politics/archive/2024/07/trump-assassination-attempt-evan-vucci/679011/ |archive-date=March 27, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[The Atlantic]]}}{{Cite news |last=Barr |first=Jeremy |date=July 14, 2024 |title='I have to do my job': Photojournalists capture images of Trump shooting |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/style/media/2024/07/14/trump-shooting-photojournalists/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250210120440/https://www.washingtonpost.com/style/media/2024/07/14/trump-shooting-photojournalists/ |archive-date=February 10, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} Commentators stated that the attempted assassination helped project Trump as a martyr,{{Cite news |last=Basu |first=Zachary |date=July 14, 2024 |title=Trump's martyr moment: Assassination attempt transforms campaign |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/07/14/trump-assassination-attempt-martyr-rnc |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250428120102/https://www.axios.com/2024/07/14/trump-assassination-attempt-martyr-rnc |archive-date=April 28, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[Axios (website)|Axios]]}}{{Cite news |last=Zurcher |first=Anthony |date=July 15, 2024 |title=Tragedy at Trump rally upends election campaign - for now |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cd172rp02e0o |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250207163912/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cd172rp02e0o |archive-date=February 7, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}} with Zachary Basu of [[Axios (website)|''Axios'']] writing that it "turbocharge[d] the persecution narrative Trump has placed at the center of his campaign". It also cemented Republican unity behind his campaign. Later, on September 15, 2024, Trump became the target of a [[Attempted assassination of Donald Trump in Florida|second assassination attempt]] at the [[Trump International Golf Club (West Palm Beach)|Trump International Golf Club]] in [[West Palm Beach, Florida]].{{Cite news |last1=Richer |first1=Alanna Durkin |last2=Long |first2=Colleen |last3=Tucker |first3=Eric |last4=Miller |first4=Zeke |last5=Matat |first5=Stephany |date=September 16, 2024 |title=Trump was the subject of an apparent assassination attempt at his Florida golf club, the FBI says |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-shooting-gunshots-florida-f62f8378d3a8ce7b2e99d6a8fb40aba9 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250410170637/https://apnews.com/article/trump-shooting-gunshots-florida-f62f8378d3a8ce7b2e99d6a8fb40aba9 |archive-date=April 10, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} The secret service agent walking the course before Trump's golf party arrived at the hole and saw a rifle barrel protruding from the bushes which opened fire in that direction. The perpetrator, [[Ryan Wesley Routh]], fled the scene but was quickly apprehended.{{cite news |last1=Bridges |first1=C. A. |last2=Penzenstadler |first2=Nick |last3=Tucker |first3=Grace |date=September 18, 2024 |title=Experts say Trump's would-be assassin had an SKS, not an AK-47. What's the difference? |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/2024/09/16/trump-assassination-gun-rifle-sks-ak47-ar15/75251832007/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250228111508/https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/2024/09/16/trump-assassination-gun-rifle-sks-ak47-ar15/75251832007/ |archive-date=February 28, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[USA Today]]}} Routh was eventually charged with attempted first-degree murder and [[terrorism]].{{Cite news |last=Suter |first=Tara |date=April 10, 2025 |title=Florida charges Trump assassination attempt suspect Ryan Routh |url=https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5243928-florida-charges-trump-assassination-attempt-suspect-ryan-routh/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250412112801/https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5243928-florida-charges-trump-assassination-attempt-suspect-ryan-routh/ |archive-date=April 12, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} ==== Musk, Kennedy, and Gabbard's endorsements ==== [[File:Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. & Donald Trump (53953048159).jpg|alt=Robert F. Kennedy with Donald Trump|thumb|[[Robert F. Kennedy Jr.]] at a Trump rally in August 2024, shortly after endorsing him]] July and August 2024 saw three of the most high profile endorsements of the Trump campaign. Just after the assassination attempt in Pennsylvania, tech magnate [[Elon Musk]] vowed to support the former President.{{Cite news |last1=Shapero |first1=Julia |last2=Klar |first2=Rebecca |date=July 15, 2022 |title=Elon Musk endorses Trump after attempted assassination of former president |url=https://thehill.com/policy/technology/4772858-musk-trump-endorsement-assassination/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250425201943/https://thehill.com/policy/technology/4772858-musk-trump-endorsement-assassination/ |archive-date=April 25, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} He would become the campaign's biggest donor.'''''' As the owner of Twitter, Musk weaponized the platform to [[Echo chamber (media)|circulate]] right-wing talking points and disinformation, and amplify Republican accounts.{{Cite news |last=Mallinder |first=Lorraine |date=November 7, 2024 |title=The Elon Musk effect: How Donald Trump gained from billionaire's support |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2024/11/7/the-elon-musk-effect-how-donald-trump-gained-from-billionaires-support |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250417073546/https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2024/11/7/the-elon-musk-effect-how-donald-trump-gained-from-billionaires-support |archive-date=April 17, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[Al Jazeera]]}}{{Cite news |last=Ingram |first=David |date=October 31, 2024 |title=How Elon Musk turned X into a pro-Trump echo chamber |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/social-media/elon-musk-turned-x-trump-echo-chamber-rcna174321 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250428132502/https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/social-media/elon-musk-turned-x-trump-echo-chamber-rcna174321 |archive-date=April 28, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[NBC News]]}} In August 2024, [[Robert F. Kennedy Jr.]] suspended his [[Robert F. Kennedy Jr. 2024 presidential campaign|independent presidential campaign]] and endorsed Trump.{{Cite news |last=Kuchar |first=Savannah |date=August 23, 2024 |title=RFK Jr. suspends campaign; endorses former President Trump |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/08/23/rfk-drops-out-2024-supports-donald-trump/74896591007/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250206234846/https://eu.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/08/23/rfk-drops-out-2024-supports-donald-trump/74896591007/ |archive-date=February 6, 2025 |access-date=April 27, 2025 |website=[[USA Today]] |language=en-US}} On the campaign trail, Kennedy's trademark message was "[[Make America Healthy Again]]." He and Trump pledged to resolve the [[Chronic condition|chronic disease epidemic]] by targeting [[Pharmaceutical industry|big pharmaceutical companies]], [[Ultra-processed food|ultraprocessed foods]], and certain chemical additives to foods.{{Cite news |last1=Payne |first1=Daniel |last2=Schumaker |first2=Erin |last3=Paun |first3=Carmen |last4=Reader |first4=Ruth |date=November 6, 2024 |title=It's a MAHA world now |url=https://www.politico.com/newsletters/future-pulse/2024/11/06/its-a-maha-world-now-00187574 |url-access=registration |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250307182257/https://www.politico.com/newsletters/future-pulse/2024/11/06/its-a-maha-world-now-00187574 |archive-date=March 7, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Politico]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Lebowitz |first1=Megan |last2=Traylor |first2=Jake |last3=Tabet |first3=Alex |date=August 24, 2024 |title=RFK Jr. joins Trump onstage at Arizona rally hours after endorsing him |url=https://www.politico.com/newsletters/future-pulse/2024/11/06/its-a-maha-world-now-00187574 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250409112913/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/rfk-jr-joins-trump-onstage-arizona-rally-hours-endorsing-rcna167948 |archive-date=April 9, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[NBC News]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Stone |first1=Will |last2=Aubrey |first2=Allison |date=November 15, 2024 |title=RFK Jr. wants to 'Make America Healthy Again.' He could face a lot of pushback |url=https://www.npr.org/sections/shots-health-news/2024/11/15/nx-s1-5191947/trump-rfk-health-hhs |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250405122809/https://www.npr.org/sections/shots-health-news/2024/11/15/nx-s1-5191947/trump-rfk-health-hhs |archive-date=April 5, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[NPR]]}} Former Representative [[Tulsi Gabbard]] soon followed suit. Having previously [[Tulsi Gabbard 2020 presidential campaign|contested]] the Democratic nomination in [[2020 Democratic Party presidential primaries|2020]], she switched allegiance to the Republican Party, citing the [[Presidency of Joe Biden|Biden administration]]'s foreign policy failures and "abuse of power".{{Cite news |last=Smith |first=David |date=August 27, 2024 |title=Ex-Democrat Tulsi Gabbard endorses Donald Trump in 2024 presidential race |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/aug/26/tulsi-gabbard-endorses-donald-trump |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250414161929/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/aug/26/tulsi-gabbard-endorses-donald-trump |archive-date=April 14, 2025 |access-date=April 30, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} ==== Biden's withdrawal ==== {{Main|Withdrawal of Joe Biden from the 2024 United States presidential election}} [[File:Donald Trump Truth Social Post on August 8, 2024—What are the chances that Crooked Joe Biden.jpg|alt=Trump post on Biden's withdrawal|thumb|A Trump post on [[Truth Social]] lambasting [[Democratic Party (United States)|Democrats]] for launching a "coup" to depose [[Joe Biden]] following his [[Withdrawal of Joe Biden from the 2024 United States presidential election|withdrawal]] from the election]] On July 21, 2024, following his poor debate performance, Biden [[Withdrawal of Joe Biden from the 2024 United States presidential election#Trump campaign|withdrew]] from the election. He endorsed [[Kamala Harris]] as his replacement.{{Cite news |last=Shear |first=Michael D. |date=July 21, 2024 |title=Live Updates: Biden Drops Out of Presidential Race, Endorses Harris |url=https://www.nytimes.com/live/2024/07/21/us/biden-drops-out-election |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240721235316/https://www.nytimes.com/live/2024/07/21/us/biden-drops-out-election |archive-date=July 21, 2024 |access-date=July 21, 2024 |work=The New York Times |language=en-US |issn=0362-4331}}{{Cite news |last1=Miller |first1=Zeke |last2=Long |first2=Colleen |last3=Superville |first3=Darlene |date=July 22, 2024 |title=Biden drops out of 2024 race after disastrous debate inflamed age concerns. VP Harris gets his nod |url=https://apnews.com/article/biden-drops-out-2024-election-ddffde72838370032bdcff946cfc2ce6 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250405125626/https://apnews.com/article/biden-drops-out-2024-election-ddffde72838370032bdcff946cfc2ce6 |archive-date=April 5, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} On August 5, she became the Democratic Party's official presidential nominee,{{Cite news |last=Kim |first=Seung Min |date=August 6, 2024 |title=Kamala Harris is now Democratic presidential nominee, will face off against Donald Trump this fall |url=https://apnews.com/article/biden-drops-out-2024-election-ddffde72838370032bdcff946cfc2ce6 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250405125626/https://apnews.com/article/biden-drops-out-2024-election-ddffde72838370032bdcff946cfc2ce6 |archive-date=April 5, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} and [[Minnesota]] governor [[Tim Walz]] was chosen to be her running mate.{{Cite news |last1=Parnes |first1=Amie |last2=Samuels |first2=Brett |last3=Conradis |first3=Brandon |date=August 6, 2024 |title=Harris picks Walz for vice president |url=https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/4789021-kamala-harris-vp-tim-walz-minnesota/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240806130648/https://thehill.com/homenews/campaign/4789021-kamala-harris-vp-tim-walz-minnesota/ |archive-date=August 6, 2024 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} Trump criticized Biden's withdrawal and Harris' subsequent accession without a competitive nominating process, calling it a "coup".{{cite news |last1=Knowles |first1=Hannah |last2=Meckler |first2=Laura |last3=Rozsa |first3=Lori |date=July 26, 2024 |title=Trump calls Harris a 'bum,' complains about Biden's withdrawal |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/07/26/trump-turning-point-speech-vance/ |url-access=limited |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240727082108/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/07/26/trump-turning-point-speech-vance/ |archive-date=July 27, 2024 |access-date=July 28, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |location=[[West Palm Beach, Florida]] |language=en |issn=0190-8286 |oclc=2269358 |quote=The former president urged attendees at a faith-themed event to vote, promising that in four years, 'we'll have it fixed so good you're not going to have to vote.'}}{{cite news |last1=Yourish |first1=Karen |last2=Gamio |first2=Lazaro |date=July 24, 2024 |title='Coup' and 'Cover-Up': How the G.O.P. Is Reacting to the Harris Candidacy |url=https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2024/07/24/us/politics/harris-biden-republican-reactions.html |url-access=limited |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240727173918/https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2024/07/24/us/politics/harris-biden-republican-reactions.html |archive-date=July 27, 2024 |access-date=July 28, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] |language=en |issn=1553-8095 |oclc=1645522}}{{Cite news |last=Klinghard |first=Daniel |date=August 5, 2024 |title=Democratic Party's choice of Harris was undemocratic − and the latest evidence of party leaders distrusting party voters |url=https://theconversation.com/democratic-partys-choice-of-harris-was-undemocratic-and-the-latest-evidence-of-party-leaders-distrusting-party-voters-236002 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250410151431/https://theconversation.com/democratic-partys-choice-of-harris-was-undemocratic-and-the-latest-evidence-of-party-leaders-distrusting-party-voters-236002 |archive-date=April 10, 2025 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[The Conversation (website)|The Conversation]]}} He and his allies would point out that Harris "got zero votes [in the [[2024 Democratic Party presidential primaries|primaries]]]".{{Cite news |last=Fox News Staff |date=August 23, 2024 |title=SEAN HANNITY: Kamala Harris was coronated after winning zero primary votes |url=https://www.foxnews.com/media/sean-hannity-kamala-harris-coronated-after-winning-zero-primary-votes |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241116173057/https://www.foxnews.com/media/sean-hannity-kamala-harris-coronated-after-winning-zero-primary-votes |archive-date=November 16, 2024 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[Fox News]]}} Biden's withdrawal reportedly caused problems within Trump's campaign.Multiple sources: * {{cite news |author=Matt Dixon, Jonathan Allen |date=August 3, 2024 |title=Trump struggles to keep his edge against Harris with fewer than 100 days until the election |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trump-struggles-keep-edge-harris-fewer-100-days-election-rcna164380 |access-date=August 28, 2024 |work=[[NBC News]] |quote=Donald Trump predicted he would face Kamala Harris, but he wasn't ready for the reality of an election reset that put him on his heels.
The former president is diluting his own message with scattershot attacks on the vice president and, for the first time in his political career, has struggled to stay at the center of national attention.}} * {{cite news |author=Shane Goldmacher |date=August 11, 2024 |title=Trump Falsely Claims That the Crowds Seen at Harris Rallies Are Fake |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/08/11/us/politics/trump-harris-crowds-ai.html |url-access=subscription |access-date=August 28, 2024 |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |quote=Mr. Trump has struggled to find his political footing in the weeks since President Biden decided to step aside and Ms. Harris replaced him atop the Democratic ticket: Mr. Trump questioned Ms. Harris's racial identity at a conference for Black journalists, he later attacked Brian Kemp, the popular Republican governor in the key swing state of Georgia, and he has seen new polling that puts him behind Ms. Harris in several key states.}} {{subscription required}} * {{cite news |author=Hugo Lowell |date=August 26, 2024 |title=Fears within Trump campaign that palace intrigue could cause distraction |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/article/2024/aug/26/trump-campaign-palace-intrigue-election |access-date=August 28, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |quote=Donald Trump's campaign remains jittery about the prospect of a power struggle inside the inner circle that could become a major distraction just months until the 2024 election, even if the jockeying for influence by top officials has ended with a truce, according to people familiar with the matter.}} In fact, [[Maggie Haberman]] and [[Jonathan Swan]] of ''[[The New York Times]]'' characterized the ensuing situation as the campaign's "worst three weeks".{{cite news |author=Maggie Haberman, Jonathan Swan |date=August 10, 2024 |title=Inside the Worst Three Weeks of Donald Trump's 2024 Campaign |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/08/10/us/politics/trump-campaign-election.html |url-access=subscription |access-date=August 26, 2024 |newspaper=[[The New York Times]]}} This reflected in national polling. By late August, with Harris as a presidential candidate, polls had her beating Trump by multiple points, giving the Democratic Party back their lead they had lost under Biden.{{Cite news |last=Beaumont |first=Adrian |date=August 25, 2024 |title=Harris gains in US polls during Democratic convention; Labor thumped in NT election |url=https://theconversation.com/harris-gains-in-us-polls-during-democratic-convention-labor-thumped-in-nt-election-237033 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241010035934/https://theconversation.com/harris-gains-in-us-polls-during-democratic-convention-labor-thumped-in-nt-election-237033 |archive-date=October 10, 2024 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[The Conversation (website)|The Conversation]]}}{{Cite news |last=McHugh |first=Calder |date=August 13, 2024 |title=Harris reset the presidential race. New polls show how. |url=https://www.politico.com/newsletters/politico-nightly/2024/08/13/harris-reset-the-presidential-race-new-polls-show-how-00173899 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240917111957/https://www.politico.com/newsletters/politico-nightly/2024/08/13/harris-reset-the-presidential-race-new-polls-show-how-00173899 |archive-date=September 17, 2024 |access-date=April 16, 2025 |work=[[Politico]]}}{{Cite web |title=2024 National: Trump vs. Harris |url=https://www.realclearpolling.com/polls/president/general/2024/trump-vs-harris |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250407043640/https://www.realclearpolling.com/polls/president/general/2024/trump-vs-harris |archive-date=April 7, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |website=[[RealClearPolitics|RealClearPolling]]}} ==== Arlington National Cemetery incident ==== {{Main|Donald Trump Arlington National Cemetery incident}}During an August 2024 visit at [[Arlington National Cemetery]], Trump's entourage brought in a photographer and videographer to [[Arlington National Cemetery#Sections|Section 60]], to capture promotional content for his campaign. However, such content is not permitted in Section 60. When a cemetery official attempted to stop them, two campaign staffers, Justin Caporale and Michel Picard, pushed and verbally abused him.{{Cite news |last1=Lawrence |first1=Quil |last2=Bowman |first2=Tom |date=August 28, 2024 |title=Trump campaign staff had altercation with official at Arlington National Cemetery |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/08/27/nx-s1-5091154/trump-arlington-cemetery |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240827231631/https://www.npr.org/2024/08/27/nx-s1-5091154/trump-arlington-cemetery |archive-date=August 27, 2024 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[NPR]]}}{{Cite magazine |last=Ewe |first=Koh |date=August 29, 2024 |title=What to Know About the Controversy Over Trump's Visit to Arlington National Cemetery |url=https://time.com/7015859/donald-trump-arlington-cemetery-visit-incident-altercation-media-campaign-disrespect/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241210235154/https://time.com/7015859/donald-trump-arlington-cemetery-visit-incident-altercation-media-campaign-disrespect/ |archive-date=December 10, 2024 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |magazine=[[Time (magazine)|Time]]}} Later in August, Trump's campaign released a [[TikTok]] video of Trump's Section 60 visit, as well as photos of the former President standing next to graves while smiling and giving a thumbs up.{{Cite news |last=McCullough |first=Colin |date=August 28, 2024 |title=Fresh controversy brews over Trump's Arlington National Cemetery visit |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/08/28/politics/trump-campaign-arlington-national-cemetery-incident/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241231034157/https://edition.cnn.com/2024/08/28/politics/trump-campaign-arlington-national-cemetery-incident/index.html |archive-date=December 31, 2024 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[CNN]]}}{{Cite web |last=Ibrahim |first=Nur |date=August 28, 2024 |title=Real Photos of Trump Smiling and Giving Thumbs-Up Over Soldiers' Graves? |url=https://www.snopes.com/fact-check/trump-smiling-thumbs-soldiers-graves/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241205082010/https://www.snopes.com/fact-check/trump-smiling-thumbs-soldiers-graves/ |archive-date=December 5, 2024 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |website=[[Snopes]] |language=}} Facing criticism,{{Cite news |last=Page |first=Myriam |date=August 29, 2024 |title=Trump faces fresh backlash as he posts campaign video from Arlington cemetery visit |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/trump-arlington-cemetery-incident-video-tiktok-b2603666.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250328095334/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/trump-arlington-cemetery-incident-video-tiktok-b2603666.html |archive-date=March 28, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |website=[[The Independent]] |language=}}{{Cite news |last1=Barber |first1=Rachel |last2=Meyer |first2=Josh |last3=Anderson |first3=Zac |last4=Vanden Brook |first4=Tom |date=August 28, 2024 |title='Furious': Veterans slam Trump team's altercation at Arlington National Cemetery |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/08/28/trump-arlington-cemetery-veterans/74980080007/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241003124414/https://eu.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/08/28/trump-arlington-cemetery-veterans/74980080007/ |archive-date=October 3, 2024 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |website=[[USA Today]] |language=}} the campaign denied all wrongdoing. In fact, family members accompanying Trump during the visit had accepted to be "respectfully captured".{{Cite news |last1=Lamothe |first1=Dan |last2=Knowles |first2=Hannah |last3=Horton |first3=Alex |date=August 29, 2024 |title=Trump campaign posts video from Arlington Cemetery after alleged altercation |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/08/27/trump-arlington-cemetery-npr-incident/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240909125216/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/08/27/trump-arlington-cemetery-npr-incident/ |archive-date=September 9, 2024 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |language= |issn=}}{{cite news |last1=Kube |first1=Courtney |last2=Traylor |first2=Jake |last3=Uribe |first3=Raquel |date=August 28, 2024 |title=Arlington National Cemetery officials confirm an 'incident' during Trump's visit |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/arlington-national-cemetery-officials-confirm-incident-trump-visit-rcna168549 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250303224119/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/arlington-national-cemetery-officials-confirm-incident-trump-visit-rcna168549 |archive-date=March 3, 2025 |access-date=August 30, 2024 |work=[[NBC News]]}} Vance criticized the media and Democratic party for "[making] a scandal out of something where there really is none",{{cite news |last1=Kornfield |first1=Meryl |date=August 29, 2024 |title=Vance defends telling Harris to 'go to hell' for nonexistent cemetery criticism |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/08/29/trump-harris-cemetery-go-to-hell/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240830013426/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/08/29/trump-harris-cemetery-go-to-hell/ |archive-date=August 30, 2024 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} adding that "[Harris] wants to yell at Donald Trump because he showed up … She can go to hell." Harris had not yet commented on the incident.{{cite news |last1=Vazquez |first1=Maegan |date=August 29, 2024 |title=Vance tells Harris to 'go to hell' for cemetery criticism she didn't give |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/08/29/vance-trump-comments-attacks-harris/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20240829114445/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/08/29/vance-trump-comments-attacks-harris/ |archive-date=August 29, 2024 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} The [[United States Army|U.S. Army]] issued a statement rebuking the Trump campaign, followed by a similar one from the [[United States Defense Department|Defense Department]], the Green Beret Foundation, [[Iraq and Afghanistan Veterans of America]], and [[VoteVets.org]].{{Cite news |last=Britzky |first=Haley |date=August 29, 2024 |title=US Army rebukes Trump campaign for incident at Arlington National Cemetery |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/08/29/politics/us-army-rebukes-trump-campaign-arlington-incident/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250321151942/https://edition.cnn.com/2024/08/29/politics/us-army-rebukes-trump-campaign-arlington-incident/index.html |archive-date=March 21, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[CNN]]}} ==== Foreign interference ==== {{Main|Foreign interference in the 2024 United States elections}} [[File:Donald Trump (53786991212).jpg|alt=Trump at a Turning Point event|thumb|Trump at a [[Turning Point USA|Turning Point]] event in [[Phoenix, Arizona]], in June 2024]] [[Chinese interference in the 2024 United States elections|China]], [[Iranian interference in the 2024 United States elections|Iran]], and [[Russian interference in the 2024 United States elections|Russia]] all interfered with Trump's campaign and the broader presidential election with their general aim being to spread disinformation and propaganda and, ultimately, foment distrust in the electoral process and discredit American democracy. Networks of fake social media accounts and websites were deployed.{{Cite news |last=Bellamy |first=Daniel |date=November 9, 2024 |title=Russia, China and Iran spread misinformation during US election |url=https://www.euronews.com/2024/11/09/russia-china-and-iran-spread-misinformation-during-us-election |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250113040947/https://www.euronews.com/2024/11/09/russia-china-and-iran-spread-misinformation-during-us-election |archive-date=January 13, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Euronews]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Frenkel |first1=Sheera |last2=Hsu |first2=Tiffany |last3=Myers |first3=Steven Lee |date=October 29, 2024 |title=How Russia, China and Iran Are Interfering in the Presidential Election |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/29/technology/election-interference-russia-china-iran.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250409103436/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/29/technology/election-interference-russia-china-iran.html |archive-date=April 9, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}}{{Cite news |last=Menn |first=Joseph |date=September 23, 2024 |title=Russia, Iran use AI to boost anti-U.S. influence campaigns, officials say |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2024/09/23/us-election-foreign-influence-russia-china-iran-ai/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240924000713/https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2024/09/23/us-election-foreign-influence-russia-china-iran-ai/ |archive-date=September 24, 2024 |access-date=September 23, 2024 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} These networks, described by ''The New York Times'' as "sophisticated," were state-run and targeted at particular voter demographics. China, through its [[Spamouflage|Spamouflauge]] influence operation, promoted fabricated content related to divisive political issues, such as that of [[2024 pro-Palestinian protests on university campuses|pro-Palestine protesters]]. It created fake pro-Trump accounts,{{Cite news |last1=Hsu |first1=Tiffany |last2=Myers |first2=Steven Lee |date=April 1, 2024 |title=China's Advancing Efforts to Influence the U.S. Election Raise Alarms |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/04/01/business/media/china-online-disinformation-us-election.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240403091121/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/04/01/business/media/china-online-disinformation-us-election.html |archive-date=April 3, 2024 |access-date=April 3, 2024 |website=[[The New York Times]]}} but its interference in the election did not necessarily favor any particular candidate. In August 2024, Trump's campaign confirmed that it had been hacked by Iranian operatives. According to a [[Microsoft]] report issued the previous day, an [[Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps]] intelligence unit conducted a [[Phishing|spear phishing]] attack.{{Cite news |last=Isenstadt |first=Alex |date=August 10, 2024 |title=We received internal Trump documents from 'Robert.' The campaign just confirmed it was hacked. |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2024/08/10/trump-campaign-hack-00173503 |url-access=registration |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250328104342/https://www.politico.com/news/2024/08/10/trump-campaign-hack-00173503 |archive-date=March 28, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |website=[[Politico]]}}{{Cite news |last=Sabin |first=Sam |date=August 9, 2024 |title=Iranian hackers target U.S. presidential campaign official, Microsoft says |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/08/09/iran-presidential-campaign-2024-hacking |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250206122015/https://www.axios.com/2024/08/09/iran-presidential-campaign-2024-hacking |archive-date=February 6, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |website=[[Axios (website)|Axios]]}} Iran attempted to tip the race in Biden and Harris' favor, even though they too were targeted in disinformation campaigns.{{Cite news |last1=Myers |first1=Steven Lee |last2=Hsu |first2=Tiffany |last3=Fassihi |first3=Farnaz |date=September 4, 2024 |title=Iran Emerges as a Top Disinformation Threat in U.S. Presidential Race |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/04/business/media/iran-disinformation-us-presidential-race.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240904160052/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/04/business/media/iran-disinformation-us-presidential-race.html/ |archive-date=September 4, 2024 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} Russia disseminated Trump-aligned content, such as a video purporting to show voter fraud in [[Georgia (U.S. state)|Georgia]], to aid the former President's effort. Analysts noted his campaign had taken a softer stance on helping Ukraine in its war with Russia relative to Harris'. ==== Debate with Kamala Harris ==== {{Main|2024 United States presidential debates#September 10: Second presidential debate (ABC, Philadelphia)}} [[File:Kamala Harris and Tim Walz in 2024.png|thumb|217x217px|Trump's and Vance's respective opponents following [[Withdrawal of Joe Biden from the 2024 United States presidential election|Biden's withdrawal]], Democratic candidates [[Kamala Harris]] and [[Tim Walz]] ]] On September 10, 2024, Trump [[2024 United States presidential debates#September 10: Second presidential debate (ABC, Philadelphia)|debated]] Harris in the second and final presidential debate of the election season, and the only debate between the two candidates.{{Cite news |date=September 11, 2024 |title=Takeaways from the Trump/Harris presidential debate |url=https://www.pbs.org/newshour/classroom/daily-news-lessons/2024/09/takeaways-from-the-trumpharris-presidential-debate |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250122194851/https://www.pbs.org/newshour/classroom/daily-news-lessons/2024/09/takeaways-from-the-trumpharris-presidential-debate |archive-date=January 22, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[PBS News Hour]]}}{{Cite news |last=Baker |first=Graeme |date=September 11, 2024 |title=Six highlights from Harris-Trump debate |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cdxl15ppvzgo |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250226155036/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cdxl15ppvzgo |archive-date=February 26, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}} He had previously been reluctant to attend another debate unless hosted by [[Fox News]],{{Cite news |last=Haworth |first=Jon |date=August 3, 2024 |title=Trump says he agreed to Fox News debate on Sept. 4 |url=https://abcnews.go.com/US/trump-agrees-fox-news-debate-kamala-harris-sept/story?id=112533650 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240803070956/https://abcnews.go.com/US/trump-agrees-fox-news-debate-kamala-harris-sept/story?id=112533650 |archive-date=August 3, 2024 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]]}} but eventually relented in August.{{Cite news |last1=Colvin |first1=Jill |last2=Price |first2=Michelle L. |last3=Licon |first3=Adriana Gomez |date=August 9, 2024 |title=Trump recommits to a Sept. 10 debate and lashes out at Harris at news conference |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-harris-news-conference-florida-election-2024-94b590ddf0434d9ea7b1241396dbbcc6 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240809105416/https://apnews.com/article/trump-harris-news-conference-florida-election-2024-94b590ddf0434d9ea7b1241396dbbcc6 |archive-date=August 9, 2024 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} During the debate, Trump made several "extreme" false claims.{{Cite news |last=Ulmer |first=Alexandra |date=September 11, 2024 |title=At debate, Trump shares falsehoods about pet-eating, infanticide |url=https://www.reuters.com/world/us/debate-trump-shares-falsehoods-about-pet-eating-infanticide-2024-09-11/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250307185136/https://www.reuters.com/world/us/debate-trump-shares-falsehoods-about-pet-eating-infanticide-2024-09-11/ |archive-date=March 7, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Reuters]]}} He alleged that some states allowed post-birth abortions, and that [[Springfield pet-eating hoax|Haitian migrants in Springfield were looting and eating residents' pets]]. This prompted the debate moderators to fact check him. In response, Trump and his allies criticized these fact checks as "unfair",{{Cite news |last=Bauder |first=David |date=September 11, 2024 |title=As Trump and Harris spar, ABC's moderators grapple with conducting a debate in a polarized country |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-harris-abc-muir-debate-80459d35f25c8fd3207c558bdb7e2339 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250411073346/https://apnews.com/article/trump-harris-abc-muir-debate-80459d35f25c8fd3207c558bdb7e2339 |archive-date=April 11, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Ngo |first1=Emily |last2=Leonard |first2=Kimberly |last3=Allison |first3=Natalie |last4=Piper |first4=Jessica |last5=Otterbein |first5=Holly |date=September 11, 2024 |title=Republicans have an answer for Trump's poor debate performance |url=https://www.politico.com/news/2024/09/11/republicans-blame-moderators-trump-debate-performance-00178591 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250404024448/https://www.politico.com/news/2024/09/11/republicans-blame-moderators-trump-debate-performance-00178591 |archive-date=April 4, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Politico]]}} especially in light of the fact that Harris too lied and yet was never fact checked.{{Cite news |last=Kessler |first=Glenn |date=September 11, 2024 |title=Fact-checking 55 suspect claims, mostly Trump's, in debate with Harris |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/09/11/fact-check-presidential-debate-harris-trump/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250114190047/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/09/11/fact-check-presidential-debate-harris-trump/ |archive-date=January 14, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} Subsequent polling overwhelmingly concluded that Trump lost,{{Cite news |last=Shepard |first=Steven |date=September 11, 2024 |title=CNN instant poll: Kamala Harris ran away with it |url=https://www.politico.com/live-updates/2024/09/10/trump-harris-presidential-debate-tonight/cnn-flash-poll-harris-won-00178566 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250225082844/https://www.politico.com/live-updates/2024/09/10/trump-harris-presidential-debate-tonight/cnn-flash-poll-harris-won-00178566 |archive-date=February 25, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |newspaper=[[Politico]]}}{{Cite web |date=September 11, 2024 |title=Regardless of which presidential candidate you prefer, who do you think won the debate? |url=https://today.yougov.com/topics/politics/survey-results/daily/2024/09/11/c7861/2 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240911111217/https://today.yougov.com/topics/politics/survey-results/daily/2024/09/11/c7861/2 |archive-date=September 11, 2024 |accessdate=September 11, 2024 |website=[[YouGov]]}}{{Cite news |last=Zogby |first=John |date=September 14, 2024 |title=The polling is in and Harris won the debate. But Democrats shouldn't get cocky |url=https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2024/sep/14/harris-poll-debate-win-democrats |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240930151723/https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2024/sep/14/harris-poll-debate-win-democrats |archive-date=September 30, 2024 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} with ''[[Reuters]]'', for instance, finding that only 24% of respondents thought that he won, as opposed to 53% for Harris.{{Cite news |last=Lange |first=Jason |date=September 25, 2024 |title=Exclusive: Harris widens lead over Trump to 47%-40%, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds |url=https://www.reuters.com/world/us/harris-builds-lead-over-trump-voters-see-her-debate-winner-reutersipsos-poll-2024-09-12/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250407043657/https://www.reuters.com/world/us/harris-builds-lead-over-trump-voters-see-her-debate-winner-reutersipsos-poll-2024-09-12/ |archive-date=April 7, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Reuters]]}} Even Fox News writer [[Douglas Schoen|Doug Schoen]] considered Harris the "clear winner".{{Cite news |last=Schoen |first=Doug |date=September 10, 2024 |title=In the Trump-Harris faceoff there was a clear winner but don't believe this election is over |url=https://www.foxnews.com/opinion/trump-harris-face-off-there-clear-winner-dont-believe-election-over |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250323055743/https://www.foxnews.com/opinion/trump-harris-face-off-there-clear-winner-dont-believe-election-over |archive-date=March 23, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Fox News]]}} Trump's brazenly false statements, constant dwelling on the past, such as his [[Attempts to overturn the 2020 United States presidential election|claims of voter fraud in the 2020 election]], and overall irascible and uncomfortable demeanor, were the preeminent cited reasons for his loss.{{Cite news |last=Prest |first=Stewart |date=September 12, 2024 |title=How Kamala Harris won the U.S. presidential debate against Donald Trump |url=https://theconversation.com/how-kamala-harris-won-the-u-s-presidential-debate-against-donald-trump-238857 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250307194316/https://theconversation.com/how-kamala-harris-won-the-u-s-presidential-debate-against-donald-trump-238857 |archive-date=March 7, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[The Conversation (website)|The Conversation]]}} Nonetheless, the debate's impact on the race was questionable. Polling numbers for both candidates did not change much following the debate, with Harris acquiring a minor gain.{{Cite web |last1=Orth |first1=Taylor |last2=Bialik |first2=Carl |date=September 18, 2024 |title=How views of Kamala Harris and Donald Trump changed after their debate |url=https://today.yougov.com/politics/articles/50548-how-views-of-kamala-harris-and-donald-trump-changed-after-their-debate |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250410141902/https://today.yougov.com/politics/articles/50548-how-views-of-kamala-harris-and-donald-trump-changed-after-their-debate |archive-date=April 10, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |website=[[YouGov]]}} Later on, Trump confirmed that he would not participate in another debate.{{Cite news |last1=Debusmann Jr. |first1=Bernd |last2=Drenon |first2=Brandon |date=September 22, 2024 |title=Trump rejects second TV debate as 'too late' |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cwyejk91d2qo |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250326140031/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cwyejk91d2qo |archive-date=March 26, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}} ==== "Kamala is for they/them" advertisement ==== {{Main|Kamala is for they/them}} {{External video|title=Trump's "Kamala is for they/them" Advertisement|video1={{center|1=[https://www.instagram.com/realdonaldtrump/reel/DAI3nriuPxw/ View] – via Trump's official Instagram account}}}} In late September 2024, Trump's campaign launched a 30-second [[Kamala is for they/them|advertisement]] excoriating Harris for supporting taxpayer-funded sex changes for prisoners. It features footage of her saying so in a 2019 interview. Notably, it concludes with the narrator declaring, "Kamala is for [[Singular they|they/them]]. President Trump is for you". This was one of several Trump ads painting his opponent as an out-of-touch radical and playing on Americans' general skepticism over [[Transgender rights in the United States|transgender rights]].{{Cite news |last=Bender |first=Michael C. |date=October 8, 2024 |title=Inside a Trump Ad Ridiculing Harris Over Taxpayer-Paid Gender Transition Surgery |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/25/us/politics/trump-ad-anti-trans-harris.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250407161534/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/25/us/politics/trump-ad-anti-trans-harris.html |archive-date=April 7, 2025 |access-date=April 27, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}}{{cite news |last1=Tomlinson |first1=Hugh |date=November 15, 2024 |title=The two sentences that doomed Kamala Harris's campaign |url=https://www.thetimes.com/world/us-world/article/kamala-harris-trans-gender-democrat-election-campaign-kvzqz9d6c |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250208122717/https://www.thetimes.com/world/us-world/article/kamala-harris-trans-gender-democrat-election-campaign-kvzqz9d6c |archive-date=February 8, 2025 |access-date=April 27, 2025 |work=[[The Times]]}}{{Cite news |last=Dodds |first=Io |date=December 1, 2024 |title=Trump's blitz of anti-trans ads probably worked – but not for the reason you might think |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/trump-anti-trans-campaign-adverts-b2654925.html |url-access=registration |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250313203822/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/americas/us-politics/trump-anti-trans-campaign-adverts-b2654925.html |archive-date=March 13, 2025 |access-date=April 27, 2025 |work=[[The Independent]]}} It, and its variations, aired over 30,000 times.{{Cite news |last=Davis |first=Susan |date=October 11, 2024 |title=Republican campaigns have been blanketing the airwaves with anti-trans ads |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/10/11/nx-s1-5148774/republican-campaigns-have-been-blanketing-the-airwaves-with-anti-trans-ads |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250116140233/https://www.npr.org/2024/10/11/nx-s1-5148774/republican-campaigns-have-been-blanketing-the-airwaves-with-anti-trans-ads |archive-date=January 16, 2025 |access-date=April 27, 2025 |work=[[NPR]]}} In retrospect, many commentators considered it one of the most effective ads of the election season. [[Future Forward PAC|Future Forward]], a Democratic [[Super PAC]], found that it shifted the race by 2.7 percentage points after viewers watched it, although other analyses showed mixed results.{{Cite news |last=Crowley |first=Kinsey |date=October 26, 2024 |title=Recent blitz of anti-trans ads attacks Harris. Advocates question their effectiveness, call them harmful. |url=https://eu.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/10/26/trans-ads-trump-harris/75791374007/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250210090835/https://eu.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/elections/2024/10/26/trans-ads-trump-harris/75791374007/ |archive-date=February 10, 2025 |access-date=April 27, 2025 |work=[[USA Today]]}} ==== Appearance on ''The Joe Rogan Experience'' ==== In October 2024, Trump appeared on ''[[The Joe Rogan Experience]]'' (JRE), the most popular podcast in the U.S. The interview covered a wide range of issues, political: the [[2020 United States presidential election|2020 election]], [[Kim Jong Un]], and apolitical: [[Extraterrestrial life|aliens]], ''[[The Apprentice (American TV series)|The Apprentice]]'', et cetera.{{Cite news |last=Dean |first=Grace |date=October 26, 2024 |title=In three-hour Rogan interview, Trump reveals 'biggest mistake' |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy8nn0913e8o |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250201151300/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy8nn0913e8o |archive-date=February 1, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2024 |work=[[BBC News]]}}{{Cite news |last=Fowler |first=Stephen |date=October 26, 2024 |title=Trump spent 3 hours on Joe Rogan's podcast. Here's what he did — and didn't — say |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/10/26/g-s1-30151/trump-joe-rogan-experience-podcast-traverse-city-michigan-election |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250405083148/https://www.npr.org/2024/10/26/g-s1-30151/trump-joe-rogan-experience-podcast-traverse-city-michigan-election |archive-date=April 5, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[NPR]]}} Trump had already committed much time to podcasts, including [[Theo Von]]'s and [[Logan Paul]]'s—to a greater extent than Harris. The JRE appearance helped him appeal to young male voters.{{Cite news |last=Cabral |first=Sam |date=October 25, 2024 |title=Joe Rogan's path to a once-improbable Trump interview |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cjr4dnv91p7o |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250126173112/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cjr4dnv91p7o |archive-date=January 26, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}} Within a day, it had amassed 27 million views on [[YouTube]],{{Cite news |last=Brandon |first=John |date=October 27, 2024 |title=President Trump Appearing On The Joe Rogan Podcast Is Blowing Up On X |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/johnbbrandon/2024/10/27/president-trump-appearing-on-the-joe-rogan-podcast-is-blowing-up-on-x/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250123035756/https://www.forbes.com/sites/johnbbrandon/2024/10/27/president-trump-appearing-on-the-joe-rogan-podcast-is-blowing-up-on-x/ |archive-date=January 23, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Forbes]]}} more than the opening game of the [[World Series]].{{Cite news |last=Srivastava |first=Aditi |date=October 27, 2024 |title=Trump on Rogan breaks YouTube with record global views in just 20 hours |url=https://www.hindustantimes.com/world-news/us-news/trump-on-rogan-breaks-youtube-with-record-global-views-in-just-20-hours-101730015888424.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241204084400/https://www.hindustantimes.com/world-news/us-news/trump-on-rogan-breaks-youtube-with-record-global-views-in-just-20-hours-101730015888424.html |archive-date=December 4, 2024 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Hindustan Times]]}} ==== Madison Square Garden rally ==== {{Main|2024 Donald Trump rally at Madison Square Garden}} Trump held his last major campaign event at [[Madison Square Garden]], [[Manhattan]], one week before the election.{{cite news |last1=Colvin |first1=Jill |last2=Price |first2=Michelle L. |date=October 28, 2024 |title=Trump's Madison Square Garden event features crude and racist insults |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-madison-square-garden-new-york-election-fcfe75be7f8281fde7bffa3adb3bba5d |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250412171548/https://apnews.com/article/trump-madison-square-garden-new-york-election-fcfe75be7f8281fde7bffa3adb3bba5d |archive-date=April 12, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Gabbatt |first1=Adam |last2=Pilkington |first2=Ed |date=October 28, 2024 |title=Trump fills Madison Square Garden with anger, vitriol and racist threats |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/oct/27/trump-madison-square-garden-rally |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250402182856/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/oct/27/trump-madison-square-garden-rally |archive-date=April 2, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}}{{Cite news |last=Collinson |first=Stephen |date=October 28, 2024 |title=Trump unveils the most extreme closing argument in modern presidential history |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/10/28/politics/trump-extreme-closing-argument/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250404234119/https://edition.cnn.com/2024/10/28/politics/trump-extreme-closing-argument/index.html |archive-date=April 4, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[CNN]]}} Among its featured speakers were comedian [[Tony Hinchcliffe]], who prominently called [[Puerto Rico]] a "floating island of garbage," suggested that Harris had worked as a prostitute, and stated that he and one of his black friends had "carved watermelons" together, as well as Trump's friend David Rem, who referred to Harris as "the [[Antichrist]]". The rally was noted for its vicious rhetoric; Democrats tied it to a [[1939 Nazi rally at Madison Square Garden|Nazi rally held at the same venue in 1939]].{{Cite news |last=Cathey |first=Libby |date=October 27, 2024 |title=DNC projects message tying Trump to Hitler on Madison Square Garden during rally |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-madison-square-garden-rally-dnc-projections/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250331231040/https://www.cbsnews.com/news/trump-madison-square-garden-rally-dnc-projections/ |archive-date=March 31, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[CBS News]]}}{{Cite news |last=Yang |first=Angela |date=October 30, 2024 |title=Comedians criticize Tony Hinchcliffe's jokes at Trump rally |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/news/tony-hinchcliffe-criticized-by-comedians-rcna177862 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250218165224/https://www.nbcnews.com/news/tony-hinchcliffe-criticized-by-comedians-rcna177862 |archive-date=February 18, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[NBC News]]}} ''The New York Times'' labelled Trump's rally as a "Closing Carnival of Grievances, Misogyny and Racism".{{Cite news |last1=Goldmacher |first1=Shane |last2=Haberman |first2=Maggie |last3=Gold |first3=Michael |date=October 27, 2024 |title=Trump at the Garden: A Closing Carnival of Grievances, Misogyny and Racism |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/27/us/trump-msg-rally.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250402205441/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/10/27/us/trump-msg-rally.html |archive-date=April 2, 2025 |access-date=April 12, 2025 |work=[[The New York Times]]}} Hinchcliffe's comments, particularly the "floating island of garbage" remark, proved especially controversial. He responded to Democratic outcry on [[Twitter]], stating they "have no sense of humor" and that he was merely calling out Puerto Rico's [[landfill]] problem. ==== McDonald's and garbage truck stunts ==== {{External video|title=Video of Trump's shift at McDonald's|video1={{center|1=[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gCFE24BtqGo View] – via WFAA on YouTube}}}} In the final days of the campaign, Trump staged two stunts. First, in late October, he worked a half-hour staged shift at [[McDonald's]] serving fries. This was done as a response to Harris' claimed time working at the fast food chain while in college, which Trump denied.{{Cite news |last1=Price |first1=Michelle L. |last2=Levy |first2=Marc |date=October 21, 2024 |title=Trump works the fry station and holds a drive-thru news conference at a Pennsylvania McDonald's |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-harris-mcdonalds-2024-presidential-election-pennsylvania-73e55c8c1db4adc2a547b62bd5142be3# |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250406034206/https://apnews.com/article/trump-harris-mcdonalds-2024-presidential-election-pennsylvania-73e55c8c1db4adc2a547b62bd5142be3 |archive-date=April 6, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}}{{Cite news |last=Mahdawi |first=Arwa |date=October 22, 2024 |title=McDonald Trump had a shift serving fries. Will the stunt supersize his base? |url=https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2024/oct/22/trump-mcdonalds-shift-supporters |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250315113329/https://www.theguardian.com/commentisfree/2024/oct/22/trump-mcdonalds-shift-supporters |archive-date=March 15, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} With the stunt, Trump "troll[ed]" her and "cosplay[ed] as a minimum wage worker". Writing for ''[[The Spectator]]'', Juan P. Villasmil remarked that the visit managed to cast doubt on his opponent's working-class appeal.{{Cite news |last=Villasmil |first=Juan P. |date=October 20, 2024 |title=The brilliance of Trump's McDonald's stunt |url=https://thespectator.com/politics/brilliance-donald-trumps-mcdonalds-stunt/ |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241110224750/https://thespectator.com/politics/brilliance-donald-trumps-mcdonalds-stunt/ |archive-date=November 10, 2024 |access-date=April 15, 2024 |work=[[The Spectator]]}} On the other hand, [[Jonathan Cohn]] in a ''[[The New Republic|New Republic]]'' podcast considered it "almost too casual, it’s a bit insulting".{{Cite magazine |last=The Daily Blast with Greg Sargent |date=October 22, 2024 |title=Transcript: Trump's Bizarre Stunt at McDonald's Blows Up in His Face |url=https://newrepublic.com/article/187377/transcript-trumps-bizarre-stunt-mcdonalds-blows-face |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250212161958/https://newrepublic.com/article/187377/transcript-trumps-bizarre-stunt-mcdonalds-blows-face |archive-date=February 12, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |magazine=[[The New Republic]]}} A few days later, Trump, dressed in a bright orange vest, rode on a personalized garbage truck. This too served to counter a Democratic opponent's statement, namely, Biden calling Trump's supporters "garbage." He subsequently held a rally donning the vest.{{Cite news |last=Stein |first=Chris |date=October 31, 2024 |title=Trump stages garbage truck stunt in bid to turn tables over Puerto Rico backlash |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/oct/31/trump-puts-on-high-vis-vest-to-trash-bidens-garbage-gaffe-at-wisconsin-rally |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250410110716/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/oct/31/trump-puts-on-high-vis-vest-to-trash-bidens-garbage-gaffe-at-wisconsin-rally |archive-date=April 10, 2025 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}}{{Cite news |last=Cleave |first=Iona |date=October 31, 2024 |title=Watch: Donald Trump mocks Joe Biden with garbage truck stunt |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/us/politics/2024/10/31/video-donald-trump-mocks-joe-biden-with-garbage-truck-stunt/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241214195855/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/us/politics/2024/10/31/video-donald-trump-mocks-joe-biden-with-garbage-truck-stunt/ |archive-date=December 14, 2024 |access-date=April 15, 2025 |work=[[The Daily Telegraph]]}} ==== Final rally ==== Trump held his final campaign rally at [[Grand Rapids, Michigan]], on the day before the election.{{Cite news |last1=Shivaram |first1=Deepa |last2=Ordoñez |first2=Franco |last3=Khalid |first3=Asma |last4=Kurtzleben |first4=Danielle |last5=Fowler |first5=Stephen |date=November 5, 2024 |title=Harris and Trump race to the finish line with one last burst of rallies |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/11/04/nx-s1-5177397/2024-election-trump-harris |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250412221415/https://www.npr.org/2024/11/04/nx-s1-5177397/2024-election-trump-harris |archive-date=April 12, 2025 |access-date=May 4, 2025 |work=[[NPR]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Burns |first1=Dasha |last2=Doyle |first2=Katherine |last3=Smith |first3=Allan |last4=Frankel |first4=Jillian |last5=Barnett |first5=Emma |date=November 4, 2024 |title=A fixture of American politics for nearly a decade, Trump rallies are coming to an end |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trump-rallies-are-coming-end-rcna178736 |access-date=May 4, 2025 |work=[[NBC]]}} At this point, he and Harris were roughly even in the polls,{{Cite news |last=Allen |first=Jonathan |date=November 4, 2024 |title=Trump's loose-cannon rallies clash with Harris' cautious endgame |url=https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trumps-loose-cannon-rallies-harris-cautious-endgame-rcna178460 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250415200558/https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/2024-election/trumps-loose-cannon-rallies-harris-cautious-endgame-rcna178460 |archive-date=April 15, 2025 |access-date=May 4, 2025 |work=[[NBC News]]}}{{Cite news |last=The Visual Journalism & Data Teams |date=November 5, 2024 |title=US election polls: Who is ahead - Harris or Trump? |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj4x71znwxdo |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250425204020/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj4x71znwxdo |archive-date=April 25, 2025 |access-date=May 4, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}} with the gap between the two candidates produced in the aftermath of Biden's withdrawal having significantly narrowed. It was to close off nine years of political campaigning. == Polling == === Primaries === {{main|Nationwide opinion polling for the 2024 Republican Party presidential primaries|Statewide opinion polling for the 2024 Republican Party presidential primaries}} [[File:Nationwide opinion polling for the 2024 Republican Party presidential primaries.svg|center|thumb|692x692px]] === Presidential election === {{main|Nationwide opinion polling for the 2024 United States presidential election|Statewide opinion polling for the 2024 United States presidential election}} [[File:2024 United States presidential election (Trump-Harris) polling (2024 polls only).svg|center|thumb|692x692px]] [[File:2024 United States presidential election (Trump-Biden) polling.svg|center|thumb|692x692px]] == Aftermath == {{Main|2024 United States presidential election}} [[File:ElectoralCollege2024.svg|thumb|upright=1.6|[[United States Electoral College|Electoral college]] results of the 2024 presidential election; Trump won a majority of 312 votes]] === Results === Donald Trump's campaign was successful. He won the 2024 presidential election with 312 [[United States Electoral College|electoral votes]] and 49.8% of the popular vote.{{Cite web |date=January 16, 2025 |title=2024 Presidential Election Results |url=https://www.fec.gov/resources/cms-content/documents/2024presgeresults.pdf |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250407144112/https://www.fec.gov/resources/cms-content/documents/2024presgeresults.pdf |archive-date=April 7, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |publisher=[[Federal Election Commission]]}} He carried 31 states out of 50,{{cite news |last=Haddad |first=Mohammed |date=November 10, 2024 |title=US election results map 2024: How does it compare to 2020 |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/10/us-election-results-map-2024-how-does-it-compare-to-2020 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250303222738/https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/11/10/us-election-results-map-2024-how-does-it-compare-to-2020 |archive-date=March 3, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Al Jazeera]]}} including all seven swing states.{{Cite news |last=Lotz |first=Avery |date=November 10, 2024 |title=Trump officially sweeps all 7 swing states |url=https://www.axios.com/2024/11/10/trump-sweeps-seven-swing-states |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250322033430/https://www.axios.com/2024/11/10/trump-sweeps-seven-swing-states |archive-date=March 22, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Axios (website)|Axios]]}} One of them, [[Nevada]], had last gone to the Republican candidate in [[2004 United States presidential election|2004]].{{Cite news |last=Lopez |first=Ashley |date=November 9, 2024 |title=Donald Trump wins Nevada |url=https://www.npr.org/2024/11/09/g-s1-33582/donald-trump-wins-nevada |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250215185219/https://www.npr.org/2024/11/09/g-s1-33582/donald-trump-wins-nevada |archive-date=February 15, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[NPR]]}} Trump's victory was "decisive";{{Cite news |last=Peoples |first=Steve |date=November 6, 2024 |title=Election takeaways: Trump's decisive victory in a deeply divided nation |url=https://apnews.com/article/trump-harris-presidential-election-takeaways-d0e4677f4cd53b4d2d8d18d674be5bf4 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250410060921/https://apnews.com/article/trump-harris-presidential-election-takeaways-d0e4677f4cd53b4d2d8d18d674be5bf4 |archive-date=April 10, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}}{{Cite news |last=Livesay |first=Brandon |date=November 7, 2024 |title=Donald Trump sweeps to victory |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/live/czxrnw5qrprt |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250403130350/https://www.bbc.com/news/live/czxrnw5qrprt |archive-date=April 3, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}}{{Cite news |last=Stein |first=Chris |date=November 28, 2024 |title=Trump victory not a mandate for radical change, top election forecaster says |url=https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/nov/28/trump-victory-election-forecast |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250409220533/https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2024/nov/28/trump-victory-election-forecast |archive-date=April 9, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[The Guardian]]}} he was the first Republican since [[George W. Bush]] in 2004 to win the national popular vote (unlike his [[2016 United States presidential election|2016]] victory and his [[2020 United States presidential election|2020]] defeat),{{Cite news |last=Jachim |first=Nick |date=November 6, 2024 |title=When was the last time the Republican Party won the popular vote? |url=https://thehill.com/homenews/nexstar_media_wire/4976301-when-was-the-last-time-the-republican-party-won-the-popular-vote/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250403021022/https://thehill.com/homenews/nexstar_media_wire/4976301-when-was-the-last-time-the-republican-party-won-the-popular-vote/ |archive-date=April 3, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} as well as the first non-incumbent Republican since [[George H. W. Bush]] in [[1988 United States presidential election|1988]] to do so.{{cite news |last=Bigg |first=Matthew |date=November 6, 2024 |title=Trump Is on Track to Win the Popular Vote |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2024/11/07/us/politics/trump-popular-vote-election-2024.html |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241111074742/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/11/07/us/politics/trump-popular-vote-election-2024.html |archive-date=November 11, 2024 |access-date=November 6, 2024 |work=[[The New York Times]] }} All 50 states, including [[Washington, D.C.|Washington D.C.]], shifted to the Republican Party, for the first time since [[1976 United States presidential election|1976]] in which the popular vote margin in all 50 states and Washington D.C. swung in the same direction from the previous election.{{Cite news |last=Skelley |first=Geoffrey |date=December 10, 2024 |title=America's swing to the right in 2024 was wide, if not always deep |url=https://abcnews.go.com/538/americas-swing-2024-wide-deep/story?id=116639076 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250405221032/https://abcnews.go.com/538/americas-swing-2024-wide-deep/story?id=116639076 |archive-date=April 5, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[ABC News (United States)|ABC News]]}} However, Trump's triumph was not a landslide.{{Cite news |last=FitzGerald |first=James |date=November 23, 2024 |title=Just how big was Donald Trump's election victory? |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn5w9w160xdo |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250328085623/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cn5w9w160xdo |archive-date=March 28, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[BBC News]]}}{{Cite news |last=Wolf |first=Zachary B. |date=November 9, 2024 |title=Trump's win was real but not a landslide. Here's where it ranks |url=https://edition.cnn.com/2024/11/09/politics/donald-trump-election-what-matters/index.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250403211140/https://edition.cnn.com/2024/11/09/politics/donald-trump-election-what-matters/index.html |archive-date=April 3, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[CNN]]}}{{Cite news |last1=Haidar |first1=Mark |last2=Calvelli |first2=Aidan |date=January 20, 2025 |title=A landslide? Just 0.15 percent of all voters determined Trump's 2024 victory |url=https://thehill.com/opinion/campaign/5094602-a-landslide-just-0-15-percent-of-all-voters-determined-trumps-2024-victory/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250330194046/https://thehill.com/opinion/campaign/5094602-a-landslide-just-0-15-percent-of-all-voters-determined-trumps-2024-victory/ |archive-date=March 30, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[The Hill (newspaper)|The Hill]]}} He only won a plurality of the popular vote, with his 49.8% total being one of the slimmest of a winning candidate in American history.{{Cite news |last=Bump |first=Philip |date=November 18, 2024 |title=Trump's 2024 'mandate' isn't as robust as Biden's was in 2020 |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/11/18/trumps-2024-mandate-isnt-robust-bidens-was-2020/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250405035746/https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/11/18/trumps-2024-mandate-isnt-robust-bidens-was-2020/ |archive-date=April 5, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} [[File:Donald Trump takes the oath of office (2025) (alternate).jpg|thumb|Trump being [[Second inauguration of Donald Trump|inaugurated]] on January 20, 2025, as the 47th president of the United States]] Trump became the second president to be reelected to a non-consecutive term, after [[Grover Cleveland]] in [[1892 United States presidential election|1892]].{{Cite news |last=Hajela |first=Deepti |date=November 6, 2024 |title=Trump isn't first to be second: Grover Cleveland set precedent of nonconsecutive presidential terms |url=https://apnews.com/article/grover-cleveland-president-nonconsecutive-terms-7ea2c92c72911462ccb1bc2e7352fa23 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250405103418/https://apnews.com/article/grover-cleveland-president-nonconsecutive-terms-7ea2c92c72911462ccb1bc2e7352fa23 |archive-date=April 5, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[Associated Press]]}} Aged 78 on election day, he remains the oldest candidate ever elected to the presidency.{{Cite news |last1=Bisset |first1=Victoria |last2=Dormido |first2=Hannah |last3=Valiño |first3=Álvaro |date=November 8, 2024 |title=Trump is the oldest person to be elected president: U.S. presidents, by age |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/11/08/oldest-us-presidents-trump/ |url-status=live |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]]}} JD Vance became the first Ohio native to be elected to the vice presidency since [[Charles G. Dawes|Charles Dawes]] in [[1924 United States presidential election|1924]] and the first veteran since [[Al Gore]] in [[1992 United States presidential election|1992]].{{Cite news |last=Brito |first=Christopher |date=July 16, 2024 |title=JD Vance could become first vice president with facial hair in decades |url=https://www.cbsnews.com/news/jd-vance-vice-president-beard-facial-hair-donald-trump/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250128004524/https://www.cbsnews.com/news/jd-vance-vice-president-beard-facial-hair-donald-trump/ |archive-date=January 28, 2025 |access-date=April 14, 2025 |work=[[CBS News]]}} Trump was [[Second inauguration of Donald Trump|inaugurated]] on January 20, 2025 as the 47th president of the United States, and Vance, as the 50th vice president of the United States.{{Cite news |last1=Encinas |first1=Amaris |last2=Walrath-Holdridge |first2=Mary |date=January 16, 2025 |title=Trump, Vance portraits released ahead of Inauguration Day 2025: See the photos
