Notable former students include [[Jane Foster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Jane Foster]], [[Roger Harrington (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Roger Harrington]],<ref name="CulverUniversity" /> and [[Darcy Lewis]],<ref name="CulverUniversity2" /> while notable former faculty include [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]], [[Betty Ross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Betty Ross]], and [[Erik Selvig]].<ref name="CulverUniversity3" /> Action sequences set at the university in ''[[The Incredible Hulk (film)|The Incredible Hulk]]'' were filmed at the [[University of Toronto]] and [[Morningside Park (Toronto)|Morningside Park, Toronto]].<ref name="CulverUniversity4" /> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Golden Dagger Club}}'''<ref name="GoldenDaggerClub" /> (based on the [[Zheng Bao Yu|Golden Daggers]] sect from the Marvel Comics) is an underground fight club in [[Macau|Macau, China]].<ref name="GoldenDaggerClub2" /> Founded by [[Xialing]] when she was 16 years old after running away from the [[Ten Rings (organization)|Ten Rings]] organization, the club makes its income from [[livestreaming]] fights onto the [[dark web]]; the participants have included Black Widows, [[Extremis (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Extremis]] soldiers, [[Emil Blonsky (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Emil Blonsky]], and [[Wong (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wong]]. [[Wenwu]] tricks [[Shang-Chi (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Shang-Chi]] into visiting the club, then raids the club with his men to capture his children and [[Katy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Katy]]. * '''{{Visible anchor|Gulmira}}''' is a fictional city in [[Afghanistan]] and the hometown of [[Ho Yinsen (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ho Yinsen]]. In 2008, following terrorist attacks by the [[Ten Rings (organization)|Ten Rings]], [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]] finishes his new [[Iron Man Mark III armor|Mark III armor]] and flies to the town, where he kills several terrorists and destroys a nearby stockpile of [[#Jericho Missile|Jericho Missiles]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2023}} * The '''{{Visible anchor|Hammer Industries Headquarters}}''' is the main headquarters of [[Hammer Industries (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hammer Industries]], located in [[Queens|Queens, New York]]. In 2010, [[Justin Hammer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Justin Hammer]] invites [[Ivan Vanko (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ivan Vanko]] to the headquarters, hoping to work with him to take down his rival [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]]. [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]] and [[Happy Hogan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Happy Hogan]] later infiltrate the headquarters and take down several guards. The [[SpaceX]] headquarters in [[Hawthorne, California]] served as the set for the facility in ''[[Iron Man 2]]''.<ref name="HammerIndustriesHeadquarters" /> [[File:Forte di Bard 2016.jpg|thumb|220px|The opening sequence of ''[[Avengers: Age of Ultron]]'' was filmed at the [[Fort Bard]] fortified complex in [[Bard, Italy]].<ref name="FortBard" />]] * The '''{{Visible anchor|Hydra Research Base}}''' in [[Sokovia]] is a research facility operated by [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]], where [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda]] and [[Pietro Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pietro Maximoff]] were experimented on by Hydra and exposed to the [[Mind Stone]], which amplified Wanda's [[Chaos magic (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|magical powers]] and granted Pietro [[superhuman abilities]]. Scenes set in the facility in ''Avengers: Age of Ultron'' were filmed at [[Fort Bard]] in [[Bard, Italy]],<ref name="FortBard" /><ref name="HydraResearchBase" /> as well as [[Dover Castle]] in [[Dover|Dover, Kent, England]].<ref name="HydraResearchBase2" /><ref name="HydraResearchBase3" /> * '''{{Visible anchor|Josie's Bar}}''' is a bar in [[Hell's Kitchen, New York]] owned by [[Josie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Josie]]. The bar was permanently closed after an attack by [[Benjamin Poindexter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Benjamin "Dex" Poindexter]], in which he killed eleven people.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Oddo |first=Marco Vitto |date=April 9, 2025 |title=Daredevil: Born Again Reveals Josie's Tragic Fate After Bullseye Attack |url=https://comicbook.com/tv-shows/news/daredevil-born-again-mcu-what-happened-to-josies-bar/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250531124046/https://comicbook.com/tv-shows/news/daredevil-born-again-mcu-what-happened-to-josies-bar/ |archive-date=May 31, 2025 |access-date=October 26, 2025 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> * '''{{Visible anchor|Kamar-Taj}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Kamar-Taj|of the same name]]) is the headquarters of the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts]] in [[Kathmandu]], [[Nepal]].<ref name="KamarTaj" /> [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]] visits searching to cure for his hands after a car accident severely injures them. Its libraries contain many books on the mystic arts, including the ''[[#Book of Cagliostro|Book of Cagliostro]]'', ''Maxim's Primer'', ''Book of the Invisible Sun'', ''Codex imperium'', ''[[Astronomia nova]]'', and ''[[The Key of Solomon]]''.<ref name="KamarTaj2" /> The location of Kamar-Taj was changed from [[Tibet]] to Nepal to prevent censorship by the [[Chinese government]].<ref name="KamarTaj3" /><ref name="KamarTaj4" /> For ''[[Doctor Strange (2016 film)|Doctor Strange]]'', the interior of the building was constructed at [[Longcross Studios]] in [[Surrey|Surrey, England]], and integrated into a real building in Kathmandu.<ref name="RTFilming" /> * '''{{Visible anchor|Madripoor}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Madripoor|of the same name]]) is a fictional island [[city-state]] in the [[Indonesian archipelago]] that is controlled by the [[Sharon Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Power Broker]], and inhabited by several criminals. Sharon Carter is revealed to have been living luxuriously there as a dealer in stolen art since the Sokovia Accords, afraid to return to the U.S. for fear of arrest. The island is divided into two parts: the wealthy Hightown and the deprived Lowtown.{{Citation needed|date=August 2023}} * The '''{{Visible anchor|Midtown School of Science and Technology}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Midtown High School (comics)|of the same name]]) is a fictional [[Science, technology, engineering, and mathematics|STEM]]-focused high school in Queens, New York City.<ref name="MidtownSchool" /><ref name="MidtownSchool2" /> Its students include [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]], [[Ned Leeds (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ned Leeds]], [[Michelle Jones-Watson]], [[Flash Thompson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Flash Thompson]], [[Betty Brant (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Betty Brant]], Jason Ionello, [[Liz (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Liz]], Cindy Moon, Seymour O'Reilly, Tiny McKeever, Charles Murphy, Abe Brown, Sally Avril, Brad Davis, Zach Cooper, and Josh Scarino. Faculty includes Roger Harrington,<ref name="MidtownSchool3" /> Coach Wilson, Mr. Cobbwell, Monica Warren, Barry Hapgood, and Julius Dell. The school's principal is Principal Morita, who is shown to be a descendant of Howling Commandos member [[Jim Morita (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Jim Morita]].<ref name="MidtownSchool4" /> [[Henry W. Grady High School]] in [[Atlanta]], [[Van Nuys High School]] and [[Reseda High School]] in [[Los Angeles]], as well as [[Franklin K. Lane High School]] in [[Brooklyn]] doubled as the school in ''Spider-Man: Homecoming''.<ref name="MidtownSchool5" /><ref name="MidtownSchool6" /><ref name="Sciretta" /> To change the setting from the set to Queens, Trixter created a CGI model of the school and added 360-degree matte paintings.<ref name="HomecomingArtofVFX" /> * '''{{Visible anchor|Mount Wundagore}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Mount Wundagore|of the same name]]), is a mountain that holds the power of the ''Darkhold'' and a shrine dedicated to the Scarlet Witch.<ref name="MountWundagore" /> After the ''Darkhold'' is destroyed. Wanda Maximoff forces [[Wong (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wong]] to lead her to Wundagore, allowing her to dream-walk into the body of her Earth-838 self and be with that universe's versions of [[Billy Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Billy]] and [[Tommy Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tommy Maximoff]]. After seeing Billy and Tommy recoiling in horror at her corruption, she uses her powers to bring down Wundagore, sacrificing and killing herself in the process. [[File:Brygga i Tønsberg.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Tønsberg Wharf in [[Tønsberg]], [[Norway]], where New Asgard is located.<ref name="NewAsgard2" />]] * '''{{Visible anchor|New Asgard}}''', formerly '''[[Tønsberg]]''', is a village in [[Norway]] that housed the Tesseract for centuries until [[Johann Schmidt (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Johann Schmidt]] stole it during World War II.<ref name="NewAsgard" /> The town is also where [[Odin (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Odin]] resides in his final days after being banished by Loki. After the Blip, the town is renamed New Asgard and serves as a refuge for the surviving [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]] and other inhabitants of the [[#Nine Realms|Nine Realms]]. It is recognized by the UN as a city-state, is led by [[Valkyrie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Valkyrie]], and has tourist attractions memorializing Asgard's history such as an ice cream parlour named the "Infinity Conez."<ref name="NewAsgard2" /> [[File:Macdougal Street and Minetta Lane street scene NYC.jpg|thumb|right|[[Greenwich Village]], location of the New York Sanctum.<ref name="SanctumAddress" />]] * The '''{{Visible anchor|New York Sanctum}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Sanctum Sanctorum (Marvel Comics)|of the same name]]) in [[Greenwich Village|Greenwich Village, New York City]], is one of the three Sanctum Sanctorums on Earth. Located on 177A [[Bleecker Street]],<ref name="SanctumAddress" /><ref name="NewYorkSanctum" /> it is used by the Masters of the Mystic Arts to store various relics and serves as one of their bases. It is guarded by Daniel Drumm until his death, whereupon he is replaced by Stephen Strange. In 2017, Strange detains Loki and invites [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]] to the Sanctum, then sends them both to their father Odin. In 2018, following his escape from the ''[[Statesman (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Statesman]]'', [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]] crash-lands in the New York Sanctum and meets Strange and Wong. Strange and Wong then discuss the threat with Banner and [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]].<ref name="NewYorkSanctum2" /> In 2024, the Sanctum is also visited by [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]] who requests Strange's help, in which he performs a memory-removing spell. After the spell goes wrong, Strange tasks Parker and his friends to retrieve [[Multiverse (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|multiversal]] displaced people and bring them to the Sanctum.<ref name="NewYorkSanctum3" /> Also, in 2024, Strange and [[America Chavez (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|América Chávez]] visit Earth-838 New York Sanctum, which has a statue of a deceased Strange and is protected by the Sorcerer Supreme from that universe, Karl Mordo.<ref name="NewYorkSanctum5" /> A set for the building was constructed at Longcross Studios in Surrey, England, for ''Doctor Strange'', which was also used in ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]''.<ref name="RTFilming" /><ref name="NewYorkSanctum4" /> * '''{{Visible anchor|Puente Antiguo}}''' is a fictional town in [[New Mexico]] where in 2010, astrophysicist Jane Foster, her intern Darcy Lewis, and her mentor Erik Selvig were studying atmospheric disturbances when they encounter Thor arriving via the [[Bifrost (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bifröst]]. Upon learning of [[Mjolnir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mjölnir]]'s location nearby, Thor storms the S.H.I.E.L.D. facility surrounding the hammer before being arrested by [[Phil Coulson]]. Later, following the arrival of [[Sif (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sif]] and the [[Warriors Three (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Warriors Three]], the town becomes the battleground for a fight between Thor and the [[Destroyer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Destroyer]], who had been sent by his brother Loki. In 2013, Foster moves back to the town with Thor and they pursue their relationship. However, in 2015, they break up and Thor leaves the town. In 2023, after [[the Blip]], Foster learns she has stage 4 cancer and receives treatments at the hospital. In 2024, Foster continues the treatments but they do not help her. [[Cerro Pelon Ranch]] in [[Galisteo, New Mexico]] doubled as the city in ''[[Thor (film)|Thor]]'',<ref name="PuenteAntiguo" /> which was extensively modified for the film.<ref name="PuenteAntiguo2" /><ref name="PuenteAntiguo3" /> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Pym Technology Headquarters}}''' is the headquarters of [[Pym Technologies]]. Located on [[Treasure Island, San Francisco]], it is destroyed by [[Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Scott Lang]] during his fight with [[Darren Cross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Darren Cross]]. The [[Georgia Archives]] building in [[Downtown Atlanta]] doubled as the building in ''[[Ant-Man (film)|Ant-Man]]''.<ref name="PymTechnologyHeadquarters" /><ref name="PymTechnologyHeadquarters2" /> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Raft}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Raft (comics)|of the same name]]) is a maximum security prison, where [[Thaddeus Ross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thaddeus Ross]] serves as warden. Designed to hold super-powered people, the prison is located underwater in the Atlantic Ocean.<ref name="Raft2" /><ref name="Raft3" /> Sam Wilson, Wanda Maximoff, Clint Barton, and Scott Lang are remanded to the prison after helping Steve Rogers and Bucky Barnes evade capture, thus violating the Sokovia Accords. They are later broken out by Steve Rogers and Natasha Romanoff, though Barton and Lang return to their families under house arrest. In 2018, [[Trish Walker]] and [[Willis Stryker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Willis Stryker]] are imprisoned in the Raft after going on killing sprees. In 2024, days after Helmut Zemo escapes from a prison in Berlin, the [[Dora Milaje (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dora Milaje]] of [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakanda]] bring him to the Raft via the [[#Royal Talon Fighter|Royal Talon Fighter]].<ref name="Raft" /> By 2027, Ross and [[Samuel Sterns (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Samuel Sterns]] were also imprisoned in the raft.<ref name="Raft4"/> [[File:TVtower in Ostankino.jpg|thumb|right|110px|[[Ostankino Tower]] inspired the design of the Red Room.<ref name="Ostankino" />]] * The '''{{Visible anchor|Red Room}}''' is a large aerial facility that acted as the primary base of the program of the same name. Led by General [[Dreykov]], it is where Black Widows are brainwashed and trained to become elite assassins.<ref name="RedRoom" /> It is destroyed in 2016 after [[Melina Vostokoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Melina Vostokoff]] takes down one of its engines. Visual effects for the facility in ''[[Black Widow (2021 film)|Black Widow]]'' were provided by [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM) and [[Digital Domain]], who also worked on the final battle and skydiving sequences.<ref name="RedRoom2" /><ref name="RedRoom3" /> Digital Domain referenced [[Stalinist architecture]], such as the [[Ostankino Tower]] in [[Moscow]], when designing the Red Room,<ref name="Ostankino" /> while the filmmakers hired military consultants to ensure the practicality of the facility.<ref name="RedRoom4" /> * '''{{Visible anchor|Rockford T. Bales High School}}''' is a fictional high school in New York City that is attended by [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]] in an alternate universe. In addition to Parker, other students include [[Nico Minoru (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nico Minoru]], [[Lonnie Lincoln (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Lonnie Lincoln]], and [[Pearl Pangan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pearl Pangan]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bonomolo |first=Cameron |date=January 29, 2025 |title=9 Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man Easter Eggs You Might Have Missed |url=https://comicbook.com/tv-shows/news/your-friendly-neighborhood-spider-man-explained-easter-eggs-references/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250308004011/https://comicbook.com/tv-shows/news/your-friendly-neighborhood-spider-man-explained-easter-eggs-references/ |archive-date=March 8, 2025 |access-date=November 2, 2025 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Rose Hill}}''' is a fictional town in [[Tennessee]] where Tony Stark stays after [[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|his armor]] runs out of power. It is the hometown of [[Harley Keener]], a child who helps Stark to repair his suit and later attends his funeral. The fictional town is named after the [[Rose Hill, North Carolina|city]] in [[North Carolina]] where portions of ''[[Iron Man 3]]'' were filmed.<ref name="RoseHill" /> * '''{{Visible anchor|Sokovia}}''' ({{Lang-sh-Cyrl-Latn|Соковиja|Sokovija|label=none}}) is a fictional [[landlocked country]] in Eastern Europe that is the home of [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda]] and Pietro Maximoff, as well as Helmut Zemo. The United States used Stark Industries-manufactured bombs to attack the country to stabilize it and secure peace, killing Wanda and Pietro Maximoff's parents. [[Mary Walker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mary Walker]] and her [[U.S. Army]] squad were ambushed by the Sokovian Armed Forces, with almost all of them murdered. During the Hydra uprising, Hydra sets up [[#Hydra Research Base|a research facility]] in Sokovia, where they conduct experiments on the Maximoff twins using [[Loki's scepter]]. The country later serves as the battleground for [[Battle of Sokovia|a conflict]] between the Avengers and Ultron, which results in the destruction of the country's capital city, Novi Grad, as well as the ratification of the Sokovia Accords. The country is [[annexed]] by the surrounding countries soon after.<ref name="Sokovia" /> Scenes set in the city were filmed in the [[Aosta Valley]] region in Italy in ''Avengers: Age of Ultron'',<ref name="Sokovia2" /> in which local storefronts were replaced with [[Cyrillic script]].<ref name="Sokovia3" /> [[Hendon Police College]], a training facility for London's [[Metropolitan Police Service]] was also used to portray a city in Sokovia.<ref name="Sokovia4" /> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Stark Eco-Compound}}'''<ref name="StarkEcoCompound" /> is the residence of Tony Stark, [[Pepper Potts (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pepper Potts]], and their daughter, [[Morgan Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Morgan Stark]]. Located in the countryside of Upstate New York,<ref name="EndgameScript" /> it is built by Tony Stark shortly after his marriage with Potts, and he lives there until his death in 2023. His funeral is held in front of the residence. [[File:Flushing Meadows–Corona Park.jpg|thumb|right|170px|[[Flushing Meadows-Corona Park]], location of the Stark Expo.<ref name="StarkExpo" />]] * The '''{{Visible anchor|Stark Expo}}''', also known as the '''World Exposition of Tomorrow''', is an exposition at the [[Flushing Meadows–Corona Park]] in Queens, New York City. Started by Tony Stark's father, Howard, it gathers great minds and showcases new technology.<ref name="StarkExpo" /><ref name="StarkExpo2" /><ref name="StarkExpo3" /> Past attendees include Phineas Horton (showcasing his Synthetic Man) and Peter Parker.<ref name="StarkExpo4" /> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Stark Mansion}}''' is the private residence of Tony Stark, located at 10880 Malibu Point, [[Malibu, California]]. It is destroyed by [[Aldrich Killian (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Aldrich Killian]] (posing as the Mandarin) in a missile attack. The mansion's exterior shots in ''[[Iron Man (2008 film)|Iron Man]]'' were digitally added to footage of [[Point Dume]] in Malibu,<ref name="StarkMansion" /> while its interior shots were filmed on soundstages in [[Playa Vista, Los Angeles]].<ref name="StarkMansion2" /> * '''{{Visible anchor|Subterranea}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Subterranea (comics)|of the same name]]) is an underground realm below New York City of Earth-828, whose inhabitants refer to themselves as [[Moloids]]. Their ruler is [[Harvey Elder (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Harvey Elder / Mole Man]]. * '''{{Visible anchor|Talokan}}''' (based on [[Atlantis (Marvel Comics)|Atlantis]] and [[Tlālōcān]] from Aztec mythology) is an underwater kingdom in the [[Atlantic Ocean]] ruled by [[Namor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Namor]] that is populated by the Talokanil located in the [[Gulf of Mexico]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2023}} * The '''{{Visible anchor|Ten Rings Headquarters}}''' is the main base of operations of the Ten Rings criminal organization, located on an unknown mountaintop in China. The compound was founded a thousand years ago by Wenwu during his early years as a [[warlord]], and also served as his personal retreat for him and his family, with Shang-Chi and Xialing having spent their childhoods there. The compound includes a [[throne room]], training grounds for its warriors, a library housed with relics of [[Ta Lo]], a [[dungeon]], and an underground parking lot. After Xialing assumes leadership of the Ten Rings, she redecorates the compound with [[graffiti]] reminiscent of her [[#Golden Dagger Club|Golden Dagger Club]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2023}} * The '''{{Visible anchor|Vault}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Vault (Marvel Comics)|of the same name]]) is a top-secret, heavily fortified underground facility where [[Valentina Allegra de Fontaine (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Valentina Allegra de Fontaine]] stores incriminating evidence of her illegal activities. * '''{{Visible anchor|Watchtower|Avengers Tower|Stark Tower}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Stark Tower|of the same name]]),<ref name="AvengersTower" /> formerly known as '''Stark Tower''' and '''Avengers Tower''', is a skyscraper in [[Manhattan]], New York City.<ref name="AvengersTower2" /> The building, initially owned by Stark Industries, serves as the headquarters of the Avengers until the Battle of Sokovia.<ref name="AvengersTower3" /> Tony Stark later sells the building to [[Characters of the Marvel Cinematic Universe: A-L#Valentina Allegra de Fontaine|Valentena Allegra de Fontaine]] who rebrands the building as the '''Watchtower'''.<ref name="Watchtower"/> Alternate versions of the building are seen in the [[#Void|Void]] and a universe where [[Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ultron]] defeats the Avengers.<ref name="Qeng" /><ref name="WIEp8Eggs" /> ''Avengers: Age of Ultron'' production designer Charles Wood built an enormous set for the film, one of the largest ever built for an MCU film, with multiple connected environments and levels.<ref name="AvengersTower4" /> * '''{{Visible anchor|Westview}}''' is a fictional town in New Jersey. The [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]] and Wanda Maximoff buy a plot of land in the town, but Vision is killed soon after by Thanos. When Maximoff arrives at the plot, she inadvertently creates an anomaly around the town, placing almost all of its inhabitants under [[Brainwashing|mind control]], transforming objects on a molecular level, and broadcasting a sitcom titled ''[[#WandaVision|WandaVision]]''. Dubbed "the {{visible anchor|Hex|Westview anomaly}}" by [[Darcy Lewis]], the anomaly gives [[Monica Rambeau (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Monica Rambeau]] superhuman abilities after she passes through the barrier three times, and creates simulacrums of [[Billy Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Billy Maximoff]], [[Tommy Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tommy Maximoff]], and Vision. When [[Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Agatha Harkness]] attacks Maximoff, she destroys the boundary, the residents escape, and Wanda's children and Vision disintegrate. The town's name alludes to Feige's hometown of [[Westfield, New Jersey]].<ref name="Westview" /> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Witches' Road}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Features of the Marvel Universe#Witches' Road|of the same name]]) is a fictional mystical pathway described in a [[The Ballad of the Witches' Road|song]] conceived by Agatha Harkness and her son [[Nicholas Scratch (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nicholas Scratch]] in the 1750s. The legend of the Road promises to grant witches their deepest desires for overcoming a series of arcane trials. Over the centuries, Harkness perpetuates and exploits the myth of the Witches' Road, deceiving witches into forming covens with her in order to absorb their powers and kill them. Eventually, the Road is unknowingly willed into existence by Billy Maximoff as a pocket dimension underneath Westview, New Jersey. Harkness' latest coven walks the Road, their expertise challenged through individual trials. Upon exiting the Witches' Road, Maximoff seals the entrance with his magic, leaving an [[epitaph]] tribute to fallen members of Harkness' coven.<ref name="WitchesRoad">{{Cite web |last=Haines |first=Samuel |title=Agatha All Along' Finale Easter Eggs |url=https://collider.com/agatha-all-along-finale-easter-eggs/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20250222205403/https://collider.com/agatha-all-along-finale-easter-eggs/ |archive-date=February 22, 2025 |access-date=February 22, 2025 |website=[[Collider (website)|Collider]] |date=November 2024 |language=en-US}}</ref>
====Wakanda==== [[File:Lake Turkana vicinity.jpg|thumb|right|In the MCU, Wakanda is located just north of [[Lake Turkana]], at a point bordering [[Kenya]], [[Ethiopia]], [[Uganda]] and [[South Sudan]].<ref name="LakeTurkana" />]] '''Wakanda''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Wakanda|of the same name]]) appears in media set in the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] (MCU). Inhabitants from this version of the country speak in the [[Xhosa language]], as [[T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|T'Challa]]'s actor [[Chadwick Boseman]] developed using a "regional accent based on where Wakanda would be. He did great research on the very cultural aspects of the character. Even though it's a fictional culture, [he figured out] ways to tether it into real African culture."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Russell |first=Scarlett |date=March 11, 2016 |title=Captain America: Civil War won't be visiting Black Panther's home country Wakanda |url=http://www.digitalspy.com/movies/captain-america/news/a786524/captain-america-civil-war-wont-be-visiting-black-panthers-home-country-Wakanda/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180923011104/http://www.digitalspy.com/movies/captain-america/news/a786524/captain-america-civil-war-wont-be-visiting-black-panthers-home-country-wakanda/ |archive-date=September 23, 2018 |website=[[Digital Spy]]}}</ref> Additionally, it is located at the northern end of Lake Turkana, at a fictional point bordering Ethiopia, South Sudan, Uganda, and Kenya. In real life, this area is actually a disputed border region known as the [[Ilemi Triangle]], claimed by each of these countries. This follows the location of the country in the comics according to ''Marvel Atlas #2''.
The film ''[[Black Panther (film)|Black Panther]]'' further established that, in keeping with this map location, it is a landlocked country in the central mountains far from the coasts. Impassable mountains and jungles around its borders have helped Wakanda isolate itself from outsiders. Internally, Wakanda consists of lush river valleys, mountain ranges rich in natural resources, and a fabulous capital city that integrates space-age technology with traditional designs.
Wakanda consists of five tribes, four of which are united under the rule of the first Black Panther 10,000 years ago. As in the comics, the four tribes (The River tribe, the Mining tribe, the Merchant tribe, and the Border tribe<ref>{{Cite web |date=February 13, 2018 |title=Teen Vogue, Black Panther |url=https://www.teenvogue.com/story/black-panther-beauty-looks-express-african-pride-afrofuturism |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180219151110/https://www.teenvogue.com/story/black-panther-beauty-looks-express-african-pride-afrofuturism |archive-date=February 19, 2018 |access-date=February 19, 2018}}</ref>) worship Bast, the panther god, amongst others, and also have a strong spiritual tradition of ancestor worship.
* The '''River Tribe''' wear green clothes made from crocodile skin, with some males wearing a [[lip plate]]. * The '''Mining Tribe''' are in charge of the Vibranium that is mined, stored, and utilized. * The '''Merchant Tribe''' are responsible for trades and crafts of art, clothing and pieces of art. They also wear veils during a trade to maintain anonymity. * The '''Border Tribe''' reside on the mountainous borders of Wakanda posing as farmers in order to deceive foreigners of Wakanda's wealth as well as their talent for breeding [[white rhinoceros]] for many purposes. * The fifth tribe are the '''{{visible anchor|Jabari}}''' (or '''Mountain Tribe''') who follow the White Gorilla cult of the god [[Hanuman]] and are staunch traditionalists who isolate themselves in the mountains. While considered part of Wakanda, the Black Panther's hold over the Jabari is tenuous. During the film, their leader [[M'Baku (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|M'Baku]] rejects T'Challa as a worthy heir to the throne during his coronation and challenges him to ceremonial combat to claim it for himself. T'Challa wins the duel but lets M'Baku leave in peace.
The lords of each tribe sit on the king's council, and after the Mountain tribe assists T'Challa in his overthrow of the usurper, [[Erik "Killmonger" Stevens]], M'Baku is also granted a seat on the council in recognition of his loyalty. The four main tribes speak a version of the [[Xhosa language]] while the Jabari speak an [[Igbo language|Igbo]] dialect.<ref name="PressKit"/> The opening animated sequence to ''Black Panther'' explains Wakanda was aware that the outside world was becoming increasingly chaotic throughout various historical events that affected Africa, such as the [[Atlantic slave trade]], the [[Scramble for Africa|colonization]] of Africa by European powers, [[East African campaign (World War I)|World War I]], and [[East African campaign (World War II)|World War II]]. The Black Panthers of the past, however, were devoted to defending their own country and did not interfere, instead choosing to hide Wakanda from the world{{--}}fearing that if they became involved and revealed themselves, it would eventually lead to outsiders trying to invade Wakanda. Instead, Wakanda passes itself off as a small, poor Third World nation of humble herdsmen, using an advanced holographic projection shroud around its borders to hide the advanced technological civilization within. A core tension of the film's narrative is that the new Black Panther, T'Challa, is torn between his loyalty to hide and defend Wakanda as its king, and his own conscience to help the faltering world beyond its borders. Later in the film, Killmonger arrives to try to seize the throne{{--}}sharing T'Challa's desire to end Wakanda's isolationism, but by conquering the outside world using Wakanda's advanced technologies and weapons instead. Ultimately, T'Challa defeats Killmonger and decides to reveal Wakanda's true nature to the world during an address at the United Nations. The film's popularity led to a trend among athletes and celebrities around the world to throw up "Wakanda Forever" salutes after their victories.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Weaver |first=Hilary |title=The "Wakanda Forever" Symbol Is Showing Up Everywhere |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/style/2018/03/the-black-panther-Wakanda-forever-symbol-is-showing-up-everywhere |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190721220345/https://www.vanityfair.com/style/2018/03/the-black-panther-wakanda-forever-symbol-is-showing-up-everywhere |archive-date=July 21, 2019 |access-date=March 27, 2018 |work=Vanities |language=en}}</ref> Director [[Ryan Coogler]] stated that his depiction of Wakanda was inspired by the southern African kingdom of [[Lesotho]].<ref name="TimesLive" /><ref name="Vulture" /> [[Basotho blanket]]s also became more known as a result of the film and its basis on Lesotho.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Black Panther made Basotho blankets world famous – but you wouldn't be able to tell that from local sales |url=https://www.businessinsider.co.za/black-panther-has-not-real-effect-on-the-south-african-sale-of-basotho-blankts-2018-3}}</ref>
Below are Wakanda's featured appearances:
* Wakanda is briefly shown on a holographic map in ''[[Iron Man 2]]'' and is mentioned in ''[[Avengers: Age of Ultron]]'' as the source nation of vibranium. * In the [[mid-credit scene]] of ''[[Captain America: Civil War]]'' (2016), [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]] oversees [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]], having been granted refuge there, going into cryogenic sleep until [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]]'s brainwashing can be removed. This is Wakanda's first appearance. * ''[[Black Panther (film)|Black Panther]]'' (2018) expands on Wakanda's background and culture, establishing that, as in the comics, the Black Panther's superhuman abilities come from consuming the "heart-shaped herb", local vegetation that mutated over millions of years due to exposure to Vibranium. * In ''[[Avengers: Infinity War]]'' (2018), members of the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]]; [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]], [[Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sam Wilson]], [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]], [[James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|James Rhodes]], [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda Maximoff]], [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]], and [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]] travel in a Quinjet to Wakanda, landing in the capital city so [[Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Shuri]]'s advanced science can remove the [[Mind Stone]] from Vision safely. Wakanda is shortly attacked by the [[List of alien races in Marvel Comics#O|Outriders]] led by [[Proxima Midnight]] and [[Cull Obsidian]], members of the [[Black Order (comics)|Black Order]]. The Avengers and Barnes join forces with the Wakandan army to fight them on the open grassland outside of the city. They are aided by the arrival of [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]], [[Rocket Raccoon (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Rocket Raccoon]], and [[Groot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Groot]] who arrive via [[Bifrost]] and assist in defeating the army. In the forest below the Wakandan medical center, the Avengers witness [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] arrive through a [[Wormhole]] and defeat them one by one, ultimately claiming the Mind Stone and completing the Infinity Gauntlet. He then snaps his fingers, [[The Blip|eliminating half the population of the universe]], including Shuri, T'Challa and many other Wakandans. * In ''[[Avengers: Endgame]]'' (2019),<ref>{{Cite web |date=May 15, 2019 |title=The Unexpected Challenge That Came With Creating The Portals For Avengers: Endgame |url=https://www.cinemablend.com/news/2471823/the-unexpected-challenge-that-came-with-creating-the-portals-for-avengers-endgame |access-date=June 6, 2019 |website=CINEMABLEND}}</ref> the restored Wakandans rally behind T'Challa in Wakanda before passing through portals created via sorcery to upstate New York. Wakanda's Golden City holds a celebration for the Blip's victims' restoration. * In a flashback in the [[The Whole World Is Watching (The Falcon and the Winter Soldier)|fourth episode]] of ''[[The Falcon and the Winter Soldier]]'' (2021), Barnes is seen in a Wakandan cave verifying that his brainwashing has been undone. * In ''[[What If...? (TV series)|What If...?]]'' (2021), Wakanda is depicted in different timelines; at the [[What If... T'Challa Became a Star-Lord?|second episode]], [[T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|T'Challa]] reunites with his family in Wakanda after having been mistakenly abducted by [[Yondu Udonta (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Yondu Udonta]] and the [[Ravagers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ravagers]] 20 years earlier. During the final moments of the [[What If... Zombies?!|fifth episode]], Wakanda is shown, besieged by [[zombie]]s and led by a zombified [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] wielding a nearly-complete [[Infinity Gauntlet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Infinity Gauntlet]]. In the [[What If... Killmonger Rescued Tony Stark?|sixth episode]], Killmonger instigates conflict between Wakanda and the United States and becomes the new Black Panther. In the [[What If... the Watcher Broke His Oath|ninth episode]], Shuri leads [[Pepper Potts]] and the Dora Milaje to arrest Killmonger, but he disappears due to him having been recruited by the Watcher to help fight an alternate Ultron. * In ''[[Black Panther: Wakanda Forever]]'' (2022), Wakanda's capital city is attacked by [[Namor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Namor]] and his army, ending with Ramonda's death.<ref name=":4">{{Cite web |last=Ryu |first=Jenna |title=Marvel reveals 'Black Panther' sequel's title and release date in nostalgic megatrailer |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/movies/2021/05/03/black-panther-wakanda-forever-marvel-movie-announcements-megatrailer/4922024001/ |access-date=May 5, 2021 |website=USA TODAY |language=en-US}}</ref> * In ''[[Eyes of Wakanda]]'' (2025), Wakanda is depicted in different timelines. On February 1, 2021, an animated [[Disney+]] series set in Wakanda was announced to be in development, with Coogler being involved through his company, [[Proximity Media]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Fleming |first=Mike Jr. |date=February 1, 2021 |title='Black Panther' Helmer Ryan Coogler Stakes His Proximity Media Banner To 5-Year Exclusive Disney Television Deal; Wakanda Series In Works For Disney+ |url=https://deadline.com/2021/02/black-panther-ryan-coogler-wakanda-series-disney-plus-exclusive-disney-television-deal-proximity-media-1234684707/ |access-date=February 1, 2021 |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]]}}</ref> In 2023, Disney announced the name of the project, ''Eyes of Wakanda'', which will tell the stories of T'Challa and Shuri's ancestors.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Peralta |first=Diego |date=December 12, 2023 |title='Black Panther' Is Getting an Animated Series on Disney+ |url=https://collider.com/black-panther-animated-series-disney-plus/ |access-date=October 31, 2024 |website=Collider |language=en}}</ref>
====S.H.I.E.L.D. facilities==== * '''{{Visible anchor|Camp Lehigh}}''', a [[United States Army]] training facility in the fictional town of Wheaton, [[New Jersey]]. It serves as one of the bases of the [[Strategic Scientific Reserve]] during World War II. After the war, it is taken over by [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] [[Howard Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Howard Stark]] and [[Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hank Pym]] performing research there while [[Arnim Zola (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Arnim Zola]] secretly uploads his consciousness into a series of many computers.<ref name="CampLehigh" /> In 2014, the camp is destroyed by a S.H.I.E.L.D. missile sent by Hydra. Years later, it is rebuilt and in 2025, hosts the first AvengerCon, attended by [[Kamala Khan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kamala Khan]]. * The '''{{Visible anchor|Fridge}}''', a detainment and storage facility at a classified location. * The '''{{Visible anchor|Guest House}}''', a former top-secret storage facility at a classified location, previously affiliated with the Strategic Scientific Reserve and S.H.I.E.L.D.; it became a secret research facility for Project T.A.H.I.T.I. * '''{{Visible anchor|Joint Dark Energy Mission Facility}}''', a top-secret research facility used by S.H.I.E.L.D. and [[NASA]] to study the [[Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tesseract]] as part of [[#Project Pegasus|Project Pegasus]].<ref name="JointDarkEnergyMissionFacility" /> In 1995, [[Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Carol Danvers]] and [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]] infiltrate the base and discover Danvers' involvement with testing an experimental light-speed engine powered by the Tesseract designed by Dr. [[Mar-Vell (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wendy Lawson]] who was a [[Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kree]] scientist. The Facility was destroyed in 2012 by the energy emitted by the Tesseract after [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]]'s arrival at the facility. * The '''{{Visible anchor|Lighthouse}}''', a massive bunker built in case of an apocalyptic-level event, located under a lighthouse in [[Lake Ontario|Lake Ontario, New York]]. Constructed and equipped by S.H.I.E.L.D. under the command of Rick Stoner, it was abandoned from 1972 until late 2017, when it became S.H.I.E.L.D.'s primary base of operations. * The '''{{Visible anchor|Playground}}''', codenamed Ragtag, was a former Strategic Scientific Reserve facility at a classified location that was renovated to become a top-secret S.H.I.E.L.D. facility. After the HYDRA Uprising and upon Phil Coulson becoming Director of S.H.I.E.L.D., it became the headquarters for the new S.H.I.E.L.D. It was destroyed by Melinda May's LMD. * '''{{Visible anchor|Providence}}''', one of Nick Fury's secret S.H.I.E.L.D. bases, in [[Ontario|Ontario, Canada]], and operatered by [[Eric Koenig (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Eric Koenig]] * The '''{{Visible anchor|Triskelion}}''', a compound located on [[Little Island (Washington, D.C.)|Little Island, Washington, D.C.]], south of the [[Theodore Roosevelt Island]] at the foot of the [[Theodore Roosevelt Bridge]]. S.H.I.E.L.D.'s headquarters and main base of operations, the base is taken over by Hydra during their uprising within S.H.I.E.L.D. to use three [[Helicarrier (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Helicarriers]] to kill people they deem to be threats. It is destroyed by a disabled Helicarrier. While most shots of Washington, D.C., in ''[[Captain America: The Winter Soldier]]'' were digitally created due to [[Prohibited airspace#United States|numerous flight restrictions in the city]], aerial footage of the city was used for live-action plate photography for shots that involved the Triskelion.<ref name="Triskelion" />
===Space=== [[File:Hypothetical ice planet illustration.jpg|thumb|Hypothetical illustration of an ice planet]] * '''{{visible anchor|Contraxia}}''' is an [[ice planet]] which is commonly visited by the [[Ravagers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ravagers]] as a place to relax, especially at the Iron Lotus [[brothel]]. Sometime in 2014, [[Stakar Ogord (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stakar Ogord]] comes across [[Yondu Udonta (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Yondu Udonta]] on Contraxia, while [[Howard the Duck (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Howard the Duck]] also appears in the bar. A set for the Iron Lotus was built at [[Pinewood Atlanta Studios]] in [[Atlanta]], Georgia, which ''Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2'' production designer Scott Chambliss sought to make it appear to have been put together from "repurposed junk", creating a "neon jungle" covered in ice and snow.<ref name="Guardians2PressNotes" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Counter-Earth}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Counter-Earth (comics)|of the same name]]) also known as '''Halfworld''', was an artificial planet located in the Keystone Quadrant. It was designed by the [[High Evolutionary (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|High Evolutionary]] as a replication of Earth and was inhabited by the Humanimals. * '''{{visible anchor|Ego's planet}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Ego the Living Planet|of the same name]]) is a living mass of matter that the Celestial Ego formed around himself thousands of years ago, causing him to resemble a large red planet with a face. The planet is destroyed by a bomb planted by the Guardians of the Galaxy in Ego's brain, and is also destroyed by [[Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ultron]] in an alternate reality.<ref name="WIEp8Eggs" /> Visual effects of the planet in ''Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2'' were provided by [[Animal Logic]], Method Studios, and [[Weta Digital]]. Weta and Animal Logic's work were heavily based on [[fractal art]], including [[Apollonian gasket]]s and [[Mandelbulb]]s,<ref name="Guardians2FXGuide">{{Cite web |last=Seymour |first=Mike |date=June 6, 2017 |title=The fractal nature of Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/the-fractal-nature-of-guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181228035039/https://www.fxguide.com/featured/the-fractal-nature-of-guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2/ |archive-date=December 28, 2018 |access-date=July 23, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Nugent |first=John |date=March 20, 2017 |title=Guardians of the Galaxy Vol 2: See Three Exclusive New Images |url=https://www.empireonline.com/movies/guardians-galaxy-2/guardians-galaxy-vol-2-see-three-exclusive-new-images/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170322020639/http://www.empireonline.com/movies/guardians-galaxy-2/guardians-galaxy-vol-2-see-three-exclusive-new-images/ |archive-date=March 22, 2017 |access-date=July 23, 2021 |website=[[Empire (magazine)|Empire]]}}</ref> and was described by director [[James Gunn]] as "the biggest visual effect of all time".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Giardina |first=Carolyn |date=May 8, 2017 |title='Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2': A Digital Kurt Russell and Other VFX Tricks Revealed |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/behind-screen/guardians-galaxy-vol-2-vfx-tricks-revealed-1001266 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170515204109/http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/behind-screen/guardians-galaxy-vol-2-vfx-tricks-revealed-1001266 |archive-date=May 15, 2017 |access-date=July 23, 2021 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Garden|Planet 0259-S}}''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Beck |first=Lia |date=May 1, 2019 |title=Where's Thanos After The Snap? "The Garden" Has Some Interesting Significance In Marvel Comics |url=https://www.bustle.com/p/wheres-thanos-after-the-snap-the-garden-has-some-interesting-significance-in-marvel-comics-17239040 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210517034459/https://www.bustle.com/p/wheres-thanos-after-the-snap-the-garden-has-some-interesting-significance-in-marvel-comics-17239040 |archive-date=May 17, 2021 |access-date=May 17, 2021 |website=[[Bustle (magazine)|Bustle]]}}</ref> also known as '''Planet 0259-S'''<ref>{{Cite web |last=Grebey |first=James |date=April 29, 2019 |title=An Exhaustive List of All the Easter Eggs in Avengers: Endgame |url=https://www.vulture.com/2019/04/avengers-endgame-easter-eggs.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210427210527/https://www.vulture.com/2019/04/avengers-endgame-easter-eggs.html |archive-date=April 27, 2021 |access-date=2021-05-17 |website=[[Vulture (magazine)|Vulture]]}}</ref> and '''Titan II''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Drum |first=Nicole |date=December 2, 2018 |title='Avengers: Infinity War' Directors Clarify Location of Thanos' Farm |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/avengers-infinity-war-thanos-farm-titan-russo-brothers/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210703042931/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/avengers-infinity-war-thanos-farm-titan-russo-brothers/ |archive-date=July 3, 2021 |access-date=February 25, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> is a greenfield planet where [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] resides following his "retirement". After fulfilling his lifelong goal of "making balance" by wiping out half of the Universe, he teleports to the planet and smiles at the sunrise as he reflects on his success. Three weeks later, the Avengers travel there and Thor decapitates him upon learning that he had destroyed the [[Infinity Stones]]. * '''{{visible anchor|Knowhere}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Knowhere|of the same name]]) is the severed head of an ancient deceased Celestial which acts as the homeworld of the Exitar mining colony, founded by [[Taneleer Tivan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Taneleer Tivan]], the Collector. In 2014, Knowhere is visited by the Guardians of the Galaxy, who try to sell the [[Power Stone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Power Stone]] to the Collector, but after an explosion caused by the Stone, the Collector's museum is destroyed. Shortly after, [[Ronan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ronan the Accuser]] and his enforcers arrive and the Guardians leave. In 2018, Knowhere is attacked by Thanos who acquires the Reality Stone. In 2025, Knowhere is purchased by the Guardians from Tivan, who begin to renovate it into their new headquarters. They host a Christmas celebration for their leader, [[Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Quill]], by bringing [[Kevin Bacon]] there. In 2026, Knowhere has become a mobile city. It comes under attack from the [[High Evolutionary]]'s Hellspawn and becomes the safe haven for the captured animals and engineered children. [[Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nebula]] decides to take leadership over Knowhere.<ref name="HolidaySpecial">{{Cite web |last=Davis |first=Brandon |date=November 18, 2022 |title=Guardians of the Galaxy Holiday Special's Vol. 3 Connections Revealed (Exclusive) |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/guardians-of-the-galaxy-holiday-specials-vol-3-connections-mcu-james-gunn-interview/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221118194411/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/guardians-of-the-galaxy-holiday-specials-vol-3-connections-mcu-james-gunn-interview/ |archive-date=November 18, 2022 |access-date=November 19, 2022 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> In an alternate reality, [[Star-Lord T'Challa]] leads the Ravagers on a mission against the Collector on Knowhere.<ref name="WIEp2Eggs" /> Visual effects of the planet were created by [[Framestore]].<ref name="GuardiansFXGuide" /> * The '''{{visible anchor|Kyln}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Kyln|of the same name]]) is a high-security prison run by the [[Nova Corps (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nova Corps]]. The Guardians of the Galaxy are brought together in the prison and execute an escape plan, with [[Ronan the Accuser (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ronan]] ordering [[Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nebula]] to massacre all of its inhabitants upon learning of Gamora's escape. Visual effects of the prison were created by Framestore.<ref name="GuardiansFXGuide" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Lamentis-1}}''' is a purple-hued moon that is destroyed by a nearby planet in the year 2077. [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]] and [[Sylvie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sylvie]] arrive on the moon through a Time Door, but are unable to escape due to their [[TemPad]] having run out of power. After failing to board an Ark to escape, they are rescued and recaptured by the [[Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|TVA]]. ''[[Loki (TV series)|Loki]]'' production designer Kasra Farahani opted to build an enormous practical set piece of the town Sharoo instead of using [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM)'s [[StageCraft]] technology,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Barnhardt |first=Adam |date=July 13, 2021 |title=Loki Crew Practically Built a Full Town for Lamentis Episode |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/loki-production-design-built-lamentis-massive-practical-set/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210713211810/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/loki-production-design-built-lamentis-massive-practical-set/ |archive-date=July 13, 2021 |access-date=July 16, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> implementing a "blocky [[ziggurat]] language" and using black-light paint to distinguish it from other alien worlds in the MCU.<ref name="Tangcay">{{Cite web |last=Tangcay |first=Jazz |date=July 14, 2021 |title=How the Look of 'Loki' Was Influenced By 'Mad Men' and 'Blade Runner' |url=https://variety.com/2021/artisans/news/loki-production-design-blade-runner-1235019744/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210715143813/https://variety.com/2021/artisans/news/loki-production-design-blade-runner-1235019744/ |archive-date=July 15, 2021 |access-date=July 17, 2021 |website=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}}</ref> Visual effects for the moon were provided by [[Digital Domain]], who also considered making the planet "a lush world covered in greenery", one "dominated by massive oceans", and one containing a [[molten core]] which later [[Gravitational collapse|implodes]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Webster |first=Andrew |date=July 30, 2021 |title=The Special Effects Behind Loki's Doomed Planet of Lamentis |url=https://www.theverge.com/22601899/loki-lamentis-special-effects-interview-marvel-disney-plus |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210730163335/https://www.theverge.com/22601899/loki-lamentis-special-effects-interview-marvel-disney-plus |archive-date=July 30, 2021 |access-date=August 20, 2021 |website=[[The Verge]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Morag}}''' is an abandoned [[ocean planet]] located in the [[Andromeda Galaxy]], with its oceans only receding to expose its landmasses every 300 years. In 2014, Peter Quill arrives on the planet to obtain the [[Power Stone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Orb]], a mission that is replicated by [[Star-Lord T'Challa]] in an alternate reality.<ref name="WIEp2Eggs" /> In 2023, [[James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|James Rhodes]] and [[Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nebula]] time-travel to 2014 Morag and knock Peter Quill out before acquiring the Orb. Visual effects of the planet in ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy (film)|Guardians of the Galaxy]]'' were created by [[Moving Picture Company]] (MPC).<ref name="GuardiansFXGuide" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Sakaar}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Sakaar|of the same name]]) is a planet ruled by the [[Grandmaster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Grandmaster]], who holds his [[#Contest of Champions|Contest of Champions]] on the planet. In 2015, it was visited by [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]] and in 2017, by [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]] and Loki. In 2025, it is visited again by Banner, who retrieves his son, Skaar. In an alternate universe, the planet is destroyed by Ultron.<ref name="WIEp8Eggs" /> The art of Thor co-creator [[Jack Kirby]] served as one of the primary inspirations for Sakaar's depiction in ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Han |first=Angie |date=December 20, 2016 |title='Thor: Ragnarok' Will Head to Planet Hulk's Sakaar |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/thor-ragnarok-sakaar/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161221133948/https://www.slashfilm.com/thor-ragnarok-sakaar/ |archive-date=December 21, 2016 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> and was described by executive producer Brad Winderbaum as "the toilet of the Universe" surrounded by an endless number of wormholes.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Tommy |date=September 7, 2017 |title=Everything You Need To Know About 'Thor: Ragnarok' |url=https://collider.com/thor-ragnarok-spoilers-trivia/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170907200613/http://collider.com/thor-ragnarok-spoilers-trivia/ |archive-date=September 7, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[Collider (website)|Collider]]}}</ref> A set for the planet was constructed at the [[Village Roadshow Studios]] in [[Oxenford, Queensland|Oxenford]], Queensland, including the Grandmaster's palace and the surrounding junkyard.<ref name="Thor3PressKit">{{Cite web |title=Thor: Ragnarok Press Kit |url=https://www.wdsmediafile.com/media/ThorRagnarok/writen-material/ThorRagnarok59d97e5bd2b22.pdf |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181103153628/http://www.wdsmediafile.com/media/ThorRagnarok/writen-material/ThorRagnarok59d97e5bd2b22.pdf |archive-date=November 3, 2018 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |publisher=[[Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures]]}}</ref> Visual effects for the planet's junkyard landscape and wormholes were created by [[Double Negative (VFX)|Double Negative]] and Digital Domain.<ref name="Thor3FXGuide">{{Cite web |last=Seymour |first=Mike |date=November 20, 2017 |title=Building Character with Thor (Part 2) |url=https://www.fxguide.com/fxfeatured/building-character-with-thor-part2/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171208004116/https://www.fxguide.com/featured/building-character-with-thor-part2/ |archive-date=December 8, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref> A Sakaarian [[national anthem]] is featured in an unused version of the second [[post-credits scene]] of ''Ragnarok'', which was improvised by [[Jeff Goldblum]] and Waititi.<ref>{{Cite web |last1=Fletcher |first1=Rosie |last2=Ashurst |first2=Sam |date=October 20, 2017 |title=Exclusive: Thor: Ragnarok's post-credits sequence has an alternate version, says director Taika Waititi |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/thor/news/a841071/thor-ragnarok-post-credits-sequence-alternate-version/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171020212809/https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/thor/news/a841071/thor-ragnarok-post-credits-sequence-alternate-version/ |archive-date=October 20, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[Digital Spy]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Sanctuary}}''' is an [[asteroid field]] inhabited by the [[Chitauri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Chitauri]] which acts as the domain of Thanos, where he gives orders to [[The Other (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|The Other]] and [[Ronan the Accuser (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ronan]]. Visual effects of the location were created by [[Digital Domain]] in ''[[The Avengers (2012 film)|The Avengers]]''.<ref name="AvengersFXGuide" /> * The '''{{visible anchor|Sovereign}}''' is an amalgamation of planets artificially fused together which serves as the homeworld to the [[genetically-engineered]] [[Sovereign (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|species of the same name]]. Powered by [[#Anulax Batteries|Anulax Batteries]], it is ruled by [[Ayesha (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ayesha]]. The amalgamation is destroyed by Ultron in an alternate reality.<ref name="WIEp8Eggs" /> Visual effects for Ayesha's lair in ''Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2'' were provided by Framestore,<ref name="Guardians2Title" /> while [[Luma Pictures]] worked on the Sovereign world and its people.<ref name="Guardians2FXGuide" /> A set for the planet was also built at Pinewood Atlanta Studios in Atlanta, Georgia, which employed a "1950s pulp fiction variation on 1930s art deco design aesthetic".<ref name="Guardians2PressNotes" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Titan}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Titan (Marvel Comics location)|of the same name]]) is an [[exoplanet]] and the homeworld of [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] before its inhabitants were wiped out from [[overpopulation]].<ref>{{Cite magazine |last=Breznican |first=Anthony |date=March 8, 2018 |title=Behind the scenes of Avengers: Infinity War as new heroes unite — and others will end – page 2 |url=https://ew.com/movies/2018/03/08/avengers-infinity-war-behind-the-scenes/2/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180309120305/http://ew.com/movies/2018/03/08/avengers-infinity-war-behind-the-scenes/2/ |archive-date=March 9, 2018 |access-date=March 8, 2018 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]]}}</ref> In 2018, [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]], [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]], and [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]] ally with the [[Guardians of the Galaxy (2008 team)|Guardians of the Galaxy]] to confront Thanos, who acquires the Time Stone following a battle and teleports to [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakanda]]. In 2023, Parker, Strange, and the Guardians depart Titan through a portal. In an alternate 2018, in the 838 universe, the Illuminati kill Thanos on Titan, and then kill that universe's version of Stephen Strange after he is corrupted by the ''Darkhold''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Russell |first=Bradley |date=May 5, 2022 |title=Doctor Strange 2 Easter eggs: The biggest Marvel references |url=https://www.gamesradar.com/doctor-strange-2-easter-eggs-marvel-references/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220505173228/https://www.gamesradar.com/doctor-strange-2-easter-eggs-marvel-references/ |archive-date=May 5, 2022 |access-date=May 5, 2022 |website=[[GamesRadar+]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Vormir}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Vormir|of the same name]]) is a barren planet and the location of the [[Soul Stone]], which is guarded by the [[Red Skull (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Red Skull]]. In 2018, Thanos coerces [[Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Gamora]] into revealing the Stone's location before teleporting there, where she is sacrificed for Thanos to obtain the Stone. Similarly, [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]] sacrifices herself in 2023 for [[Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Clint Barton]] to acquire the Stone. * '''{{visible anchor|Xandar}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Xandar|of the same name]]) is the capital of the Nova Empire and home of the [[Nova Corps (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nova Corps]]. In 2014, [[Ronan the Accuser (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ronan]] attacks Xandar in retaliation for the [[Kree–Nova War]], killing most of the Nova Corps before being defeated by the Guardians of the Galaxy and the [[Ravagers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ravagers]]. It is later decimated by Thanos in 2018, an event replicated by Ultron in an alternate reality.<ref name="WIEp8Eggs" /> Scenes set on the planet in ''Guardians of the Galaxy'' were filmed at [[Millennium Bridge, London|Millennium Bridge]], London,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shahid |first=Sharnaz |date=August 11, 2013 |title=PHOTOS: Guardians Of The Galaxy Cast Get Physical In London Shoot |url=https://www.entertainmentwise.com/photos/123327/1/PHOTOS-Guardians-Of-The-Galaxy-Cast-Get-Physical-In-London-Shoot |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141202065302/http://www.entertainmentwise.com/photos/123327/1/PHOTOS-Guardians-Of-The-Galaxy-Cast-Get-Physical-In-London-Shoot |archive-date=December 2, 2014 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[Entertainmentwise]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Hunt |first=James |date=July 8, 2014 |title="Guardians Of The Galaxy" Set Visit Puts Concerns To Rest |url=https://www.comicbookresources.com/?page=article&id=53910 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140713045725/http://www.comicbookresources.com/?page=article&id=53910 |archive-date=July 13, 2014 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> while visual effects were done by [[Moving Picture Company]] (MPC).<ref name="GuardiansFXGuide">{{Cite web |last=Failes |first=Ian |date=August 13, 2014 |title=The VFX of Guardians of the Galaxy |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/the-vfx-of-guardians-of-the-galaxy/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140817034337/http://www.fxguide.com/featured/the-vfx-of-guardians-of-the-galaxy/ |archive-date=August 17, 2014 |access-date=August 16, 2014 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref>
===Nine Realms=== {{See also|Nine Realms|Asgard (comics)#The Nine Realms}} * '''{{visible anchor|Asgard}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Asgard (comics)|of the same name]]) is a small, flat planetary body and the home of the [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]]. It is destroyed by [[Surtur (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Surtur]]. [[Double Negative (VFX)|Double Negative]] embedded a [[computer-generated imagery|computer-generated]] rendering of Asgard onto footage of the coast of Norway filmed with an Arri Alexa camera in a helicopter in ''[[Thor: The Dark World]]''.<ref name="Thor2FXGuide">{{Cite web |last=Failes |first=Ian |date=November 18, 2013 |title=The dark side: behind the VFX of Thor: The Dark World |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/the-dark-side-behind-the-vfx-of-thor-the-dark-world/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131122033116/http://www.fxguide.com/featured/the-dark-side-behind-the-vfx-of-thor-the-dark-world/ |archive-date=November 22, 2013 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref> For its appearance in ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'', production designer [[Dan Hennah]] sought to give the realm "more of a humanity" than in previous films by adding smaller building perspectives, making it appear more practical and utilitarian.<ref name="HennahRagnarok" /> A set for the realm was constructed at the [[Village Roadshow Studios]] in [[Oxenford, Queensland|Oxenford]], [[Gold Coast, Queensland|Gold Coast]], Queensland, Australia, based on the aesthetics of previous ''Thor'' films,<ref name="Thor3PressKit" /> while visual effects were provided by [[Framestore]] based on assets [[Double Negative (VFX)|Double Negative]] had from ''The Dark World''.<ref name="Thor3FXGuide" /> ** The '''{{visible anchor|Rainbow Bridge}}''' is a long magical bridge that runs from the center of [[Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgard]] to the edge, connecting the Royal Palace of Valaskjalf to Himinbjörg, the generator of the [[Bifrost (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bifröst Bridge]]. In 2011, the Rainbow Bridge is destroyed by [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]], but is later repaired by [[Heimdall (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Heimdall]] using the power of the [[Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tesseract]]. ** '''{{visible anchor|Odin's vault}}''', also known as '''Odin's treasure room''', is a room in the Royal Palace of Valaskjálf that contained many powerful and magical artifacts, including a replica of the [[Infinity Gauntlet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Infinity Gauntlet]], the Eternal Flame, the [[#Casket of Ancient Winters|Casket of Ancient Winters]], and the [[Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tesseract]]. * '''{{visible anchor|Hel}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Hel (comics)|of the same name]]) is a region in [[Niflheim]] that serves as the home of the dead. After [[Hela (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hela]] is banished there by her father, [[Odin (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Odin]], she attempts to escape and slaughters almost all of the Valkyrie, only to be defeated by Odin once again. The flashback sequence in ''Thor: Ragnarok'' featuring the Valkyrie was produced by [[Rising Sun Pictures]], who achieved its surreal ethereal appearance through a combination of [[motion capture]], [[computer graphics]], a 900 fps [[frame rate]], and a special 360-degree lighting rig containing 200 strobe lights.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Seymour |first=Mike |date=November 6, 2017 |title=Thor Ragnarok: Hela and back (Part 1) |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/thor-ragnarok-hela-and-back-part-1/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171208004120/https://www.fxguide.com/featured/thor-ragnarok-hela-and-back-part-1/ |archive-date=December 8, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Jotunheim}}''' (based on the Norse mythological location [[Jötunheimr|of the same name]]) is an icy planet that is home of the [[Frost Giants (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Frost Giants]] and the birthplace of [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki Laufeyson]]. Visual effects for the planet were created by [[Digital Domain]] in ''[[Thor (film)|Thor]]'', who were sent paintings from classic studies by [[J. M. W. Turner]] by director [[Kenneth Branagh]].<ref name="Desowitz">{{Cite web |last=Desowitz |first=Bill |date=May 6, 2011 |title=Branagh's VFX Journey with Tho |url=https://www.awn.com/articles/3d/branaghs-vfx-journey-thor |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110624092803/http://www.awn.com/articles/3d/branaghs-vfx-journey-thor |archive-date=June 24, 2011 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[Animation World Network]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Muspelheim}}''' (based on the Norse mythological location [[Muspelheim|of the same name]]) is a fiery realm that is home to the Fire Demons, most notably [[Surtur (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Surtur]]. Two years following the [[Battle of Sokovia]], [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]] journeys there to confront Surtur on [[Ragnarok (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ragnarök]] before killing him. ''Thor: Ragnarok'' production designer Dan Hennah described the realm as a [[Dyson sphere]] which draws power out of a dying star to energize its inhabitants.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Anderton |first=Ethan |date=September 7, 2017 |title='Thor Ragnarok' Set Visit: Everything We Learned About the Cosmic Sequel |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/thor-ragnarok-set-visit/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170907160520/http://www.slashfilm.com/thor-ragnarok-set-visit/ |archive-date=September 7, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Nidavellir}}''' (based on the Norse mythological location [[Niðavellir|of the same name]]) is an [[Alderson disk]] surrounding a dying star, inhabited by gigantic [[Dwarves (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dwarves]] who served as blacksmiths for the [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]], forging weapons such as [[Mjolnir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mjölnir]], [[Stormbreaker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stormbreaker]], and the [[Infinity Gauntlet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Infinity Gauntlet]] out of [[#Uru|uru]]. Sometime between 2014 and 2015, Thanos visits the forge, forcing the Dwarves to make the Infinity Gauntlet before slaughtering them and smelting [[Eitri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Eitri]]'s hands. [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]], [[Groot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Groot]], and [[Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Rocket]] visit the forge several years later, aiding Eitri in creating Stormbreaker. [[File:Landmannalaugar in June.jpg|thumb|160px|[[Iceland]] doubled as Svartalfheim in ''[[Thor: The Dark World]]''.<ref name="Thor2FXGuide" />]] * '''{{visible anchor|Svartalfheim}}''' (based on the Norse mythological location [[Svartálfar|of the same name]]), also known as the '''Dark World''', is a planet that is wreathed in perpetual darkness and ruled by the [[Dark Elves (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dark Elves]], led by [[Malekith (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Malekith the Accursed]]. Visual effects of ''[[Thor: The Dark World]]''{{'}}s prologue scene were done by Blur Studio, and mainly consisted of CGI with live-action shots interwoven throughout.<ref name="Thor2FXGuide" /> Subsequent scenes in the film were shot in [[Iceland]], with Double Negative adding ruins, mountains, Dark Elf ships, and skies.<ref name="Thor2FXGuide" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Midgard}}''' (based on the Norse mythological location [[Midgard|of the same name]]), is another name for Earth.
===Multiverse=== {{main|Multiverse (Marvel Cinematic Universe)}}
===Dimensions=== * The '''{{Visible anchor|Astral Dimension}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Features of the Marvel Universe#Astral Plane|of the same name]]), also known as the '''[[Astral plane]]''', is a dimension in which the soul resides outside the body. It is mainly featured in the form of [[astral projection]] in ''[[Doctor Strange (2016 film)|Doctor Strange]]'' when the [[Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ancient One]] pushes [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]]'s astral form out of his body. The fight sequence in ''[[Doctor Strange (2016 film)|Doctor Strange]]'' between [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]] and a Zealot's astral forms was the first scene in the film written by director [[Scott Derrickson]], who was inspired by the comic ''[[Doctor Strange: The Oath]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Giroux |first=Jack |date=November 4, 2016 |title=Interview: 'Doctor Strange' Screenwriter Jon Spaihts Discusses the Brilliant, Cocky, Swashbuckling Superhero |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/doctor-strange-jon-spaihts-interview/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161105131049/http://www.slashfilm.com/doctor-strange-jon-spaihts-interview/ |archive-date=November 5, 2016 |access-date=July 22, 2021 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> Visual effects for scenes set in the dimension were provided by [[Framestore]], who described the process as "one of the hardest effects [they've] had to deal with".<ref name="DSFXGuide" /> This imagery is reused in ''[[Avengers: Endgame]]'', ''[[WandaVision]]'', ''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]'', and ''[[Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness]]''. The Astral Dimension is also connected to the MCU's afterlife, which is interpreted as being a subjective reality depending on the belief system of the individual. * The '''{{Visible anchor|Ancestral Plane}}''' is a location where deceased Wakandans reside, specifically those who have worn the mantle of Black Panther. The living are able to communicate with these past rulers upon ingestion of the [[#Heart-shaped herb|heart-shaped herb]].{{Citation needed|date=July 2025}} * The '''{{Visible anchor|Duat}}''' is the name for the Egyptian perspective of the afterlife, where the goddess [[Taweret (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Taweret]] ferries [[Marc Spector (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Marc Spector and Steven Grant]] across an ocean of sand to reach the Field of Reeds. If a person's heart does not balance against the Feather of Truth, they are consumed by other lost souls in the sands. Spector and Grant make it back to the real world through the Gates of Osiris.{{Citation needed|date=July 2025}} * '''{{Visible anchor|Valhalla}}''' is revealed to be the [[#Asgard|Asgardian]] afterlife.{{Citation needed|date=July 2025}} [[File:Hearst Castle.jpg|thumb|170px|[[Hearst Castle]] inspired the design of the Citadel at the End of Time.<ref name="Hearst" />]] * The '''{{Visible anchor|Citadel at the End of Time}}''' is a castle atop an [[asteroid]] at the [[Ultimate fate of the Universe|end of time]] where [[He Who Remains (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|He Who Remains]] resides and watches over the Sacred Timeline, which orbits the place. Carved ''[[in situ]]'' from the asteroid and made a "black stone with gold vein embellishments", the Citadel is mostly abandoned except for He Who Remains' office, with ''[[Loki (TV series)|Loki]]'' production designer Kasra Farahani intending to reflect the loneliness of He Who Remains. Outside his office, there are also numerous 13-foot-tall statues of "sentinels of time" in the "Hall of Heroes", each holding half of an hourglass. A [[nebula]] outside the window and a fireplace were used as light sources in {{not a typo|He's}} office.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Dinh |first=Christine |date=July 14, 2021 |title=Loki: Deconstructing He Who Remains' Life's Work at the Citadel at the End of Time |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/loki-he-who-remains-citadel-end-of-time-kang-statue |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210714075002/https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/loki-he-who-remains-citadel-end-of-time-kang-statue |archive-date=July 14, 2021 |access-date=July 15, 2021 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref> The design and architecture of the Citadel was inspired by [[Hearst Castle]] and compared to [[Sunset Boulevard]].<ref name="Hearst">{{Cite web |last=Robinson |first=Joanna |date=July 20, 2021 |title=What Loki's Final Location Reveals About Its Big Villain |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2021/07/loki-finale-production-design-kasra-farahani-interview-citadel-statue-he-who-remains |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210721074020/https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2021/07/loki-finale-production-design-kasra-farahani-interview-citadel-statue-he-who-remains |archive-date=July 21, 2021 |access-date=July 29, 2021 |website=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Langmann |first=Brady |date=July 16, 2021 |title=''Loki'' Director Kate Herron Breaks Down That MCU Miracle of a Finale |url=https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/tv/a37050778/kate-herron-loki-finale-season-2-interview/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210720185206/https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/tv/a37050778/kate-herron-loki-finale-season-2-interview/ |archive-date=July 20, 2021 |access-date=July 29, 2021 |website=[[Esquire (magazine)|Esquire]]}}</ref> The Production Designer for Loki was inspired by the Japanese repair technique [[Kintsugi]] as it uses gold to repair cracks in broken pottery.<ref>{{cite news |last=Chang |first=Tom |date=26 Jul 2021 |title=Loki Prod Designer: He Who Remains Citadel Details You Might've Missed |url=https://bleedingcool.com/tv/loki-prod-designer-he-who-remains-citadel-details-you-mightve-missed/ |work=bleedingcool.com |location=bleedingcool.com |access-date=26 Jul 2021}}</ref> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Dark Dimension}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Dark Dimension (comics)|of the same name]]) is a timeless dimension inhabited by [[Dormammu (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dormammu]]. It is an amalgamation of itself and all other dimensions Dormammu had conquered and absorbed into it. [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]] visits it to bargain with Dormammu after [[Kaecilius (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kaecilius]] contacts it to absorb the Earth. Years later, [[Clea (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Clea]], who reigns from the dimension, recruits Strange to help save it from an incursion. Visual effects of the dimension in ''Doctor Strange'' were provided by [[Method Studios]] and [[Luma Pictures]].<ref name="DSFXGuide" /> ''Doctor Strange'' visual effects supervisor [[Stephane Ceretti]] described the Dark Dimension as a "dynamic environment", with the Luma team using art by [[Steve Ditko]] as a reference.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Collura |first=Scott |date=November 9, 2016 |title=How Doctor Strange's Big Bad Was Created |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2016/11/09/how-doctor-strangeas-big-bad-was-created |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161111164639/https://www.ign.com/articles/2016/11/09/how-doctor-strangeas-big-bad-was-created |archive-date=November 11, 2016 |access-date=July 22, 2021 |website=[[IGN]]}}</ref> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Mirror Dimension}}''' is a dimension which causes the surroundings to be reflected in different directions, similar to the function of a [[mirror]], without affecting the real world. Due to its nature, it is used by the sorcerers for training and controlling threats. The [[Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ancient One]] uses it during the [[Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of New York]], while [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]] uses it against [[Kaecilius (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kaecilius]], [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]], [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]], and [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda Maximoff]].
