According to ''Doctor Strange'' director Scott Derrickson, the action sequences set in the dimension is an attempt to take ''[[Inception]]'' "to the Nth degree and take it way more surreal and way farther".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gonzales |first=Umberto |date=November 3, 2016 |title='Doctor Strange' Director Scott Derrickson Has No Appetite for Destruction |url=https://www.thewrap.com/doctor-strange-director-scott-derrickson/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161105180150/http://www.thewrap.com/doctor-strange-director-scott-derrickson/ |archive-date=November 5, 2016 |access-date=July 22, 2021 |website=[[TheWrap]]}}</ref> [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM) was primarily responsible for visual effects of the Manhattan folding sequence, which consisted of 200 shots and was mainly CGI, although some real-life shots of New York were used.<ref name="DSFXGuide" /> Meanwhile, Luma Pictures worked on the first mirror sequence at the beginning of the film.<ref name="DSFXGuide" /> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Quantum Realm}}''' (based on the [[Microverse]] from the Marvel Comics) (for the theory in physics, see [[quantum mechanics]]) is a subatomic universe that exists outside of space and time. It can only be entered through [[subatomic particles]] or forms of mystical magic.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Holmes |first=Adam |date=July 20, 2015 |title=The Quantum Realm: What To Know After You've Seen Ant-Man |url=https://www.cinemablend.com/new/Quantum-Realm-What-Know-You-Seen-Ant-Man-72677.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150721205126/https://www.cinemablend.com/new/Quantum-Realm-What-Know-You-Seen-Ant-Man-72677.html |archive-date=July 21, 2015 |access-date=May 28, 2022 |website=[[CinemaBlend]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walsh |first=Michael |date=December 11, 2018 |title=Everything You Need To Know About The Quantum Realm Before Avengers: Endgame |url=https://nerdist.com/article/avengers-endgame-and-the-quantum-realm-ant-man/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190518064412/https://nerdist.com/article/avengers-endgame-and-the-quantum-realm-ant-man/ |archive-date=May 18, 2019 |access-date=May 28, 2022 |website=[[Nerdist]]}}</ref> In 1987, [[Janet van Dyne (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Janet van Dyne]] went subatomic and was stranded in the realm for thirty years. Additionally, the Council of Kangs exiled [[Kang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kang the Conqueror]] to the realm. He befriended van Dyne, who helped him fix his time chair, only to learn from a neurolink who he really was. She then betrayed him, causing him to remain trapped. During this time, Kang conquered the realm and took control over it, building an empire, and displacing its residents.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Robinson |first=Joanna |date=November 28, 2017 |title=What RoboCop Has to Do with the Future of the Marvel Cinematic Universe |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2017/11/the-future-of-the-marvel-cinematic-universe-robocop |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171202223510/https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2017/11/the-future-of-the-marvel-cinematic-universe-robocop |archive-date=December 2, 2017 |access-date=December 2, 2017 |website=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Sciretta |first=Peter |date=July 3, 2018 |title=Kevin Feige Explains How 'Ant-Man And The Wasp' Plays With The Rules Of The Quantum Realm |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/559356/ant-man-and-the-wasp-quantum-realm/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220528043225/https://www.slashfilm.com/559356/ant-man-and-the-wasp-quantum-realm/ |archive-date=May 28, 2022 |access-date=May 28, 2022 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> In 2015, [[Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Scott Lang]] goes subatomic to defeat [[Darren Cross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Darren Cross]] and enters the realm, but manages to escape, while Cross remains stuck in the realm. In 2018, van Dyne is rescued by [[Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hank Pym]] and they leave the realm. That same year, Lang becomes stuck in the realm's time vortex for five years, although he only experienced five hours in the realm. In 2019, agents of [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] return to the main timeline through the realm from an alternate 1983 to defeat the [[Chronicoms]]. In 2023, Lang and the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]] use the realm to travel to alternate timelines to reverse [[the Blip]]. An [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|alternate Thanos]] acquires their technology and uses the realm to travel himself, his ship, and his army to their universe. In 2026, van Dyne, Pym, Lang, [[Hope van Dyne]], and [[Cassie Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Cassie Lang]] are transported into the realm after Cassie's quantum satellite is heard by Cross. They find themselves hunted by Kang, who wants revenge on van Dyne. After they find a portal to leave the realm, Kang tries to follow, but are ultimately defeated and pulled into his multiversal power core. The Quantum Realm is so named because the name "Microverse" is associated with the [[Micronauts (comics)|Micronauts]], whose rights are held by [[Hasbro Entertainment]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ryan |first=Mike |date=July 6, 2015 |title='Ant-Man' Director Peyton Reed Details What's New And What Was Kept From Edgar Wright's 'Ant-Man' Script |url=https://uproxx.com/movies/2015/07/ant-man-peyton-reed/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150707072619/https://uproxx.com/movies/2015/07/ant-man-peyton-reed/ |archive-date=July 7, 2015 |access-date=July 15, 2021 |website=[[Uproxx]]}}</ref> [[Quantum physicist]] and [[California Institute of Technology]] staff researcher Spiros Michalakis suggested the new name.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Francisco |first=Eric |date=May 24, 2017 |title=Ant-Man Could Destroy Superman, Says Quantum Physics |url=https://www.inverse.com/article/32022-ant-man-quantum-physics-dr-spiros-superman-captain-marvel |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170612005754/https://www.inverse.com/article/32022-ant-man-quantum-physics-dr-spiros-superman-captain-marvel |archive-date=June 12, 2017 |access-date=July 15, 2021 |website=[[Inverse (website)|Inverse]]}}</ref> Visual effects for the dimension in ''[[Ant-Man (film)|Ant-Man]]'', ''Doctor Strange'', and ''[[Ant-Man and the Wasp]]'' were provided by Method Studios.<ref name="DSFXGuide" /> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Soul World}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics location [[Soul World|of the same name]]), also known as the '''Way Station''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Brooks |first=Nicholas |date=April 11, 2021 |title=Yes, Avengers: Infinity War Had a Name For the Place Inside the Soul Stone |url=https://www.cbr.com/avengers-infinity-war-soul-stone-way-station/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210412135603/https://www.cbr.com/avengers-infinity-war-soul-stone-way-station/ |archive-date=April 12, 2021 |access-date=April 14, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> is a [[pocket dimension]] inside the [[Soul Stone]]<ref>{{Cite web |last=Keene |first=Allison |date=May 4, 2018 |title=Infinity War Soul Stone Theory Confirmed by Director |url=https://collider.com/infinity-war-soul-stone-theory-gamora/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210414170322/https://collider.com/infinity-war-soul-stone-theory-gamora/ |archive-date=April 14, 2021 |access-date=April 14, 2021 |website=[[Collider (website)|Collider]]}}</ref> which Thanos finds himself in for a brief moment after he snapped his fingers and wiped out half of the Universe's population, where he encountered a young [[Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Gamora]]. Christopher Markus, co-writer of ''Avengers: Endgame'', also stated that Banner met the Hulk in the Soul World. The Soul World was originally also going to be visited by [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]] in a deleted scene of ''Avengers: Endgame'', where he would have met an older version of his daughter [[Morgan Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Morgan]];<ref>{{Cite web |last=Schmidt |first=Joe |date=November 27, 2019 |title=Katherine Langford Breaks Silence Over Her Deleted Avengers: Endgame Role |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/katherine-langford-breaks-silence-deleted-scene-avengers-endgame/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201017145138/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/katherine-langford-breaks-silence-deleted-scene-avengers-endgame/ |archive-date=October 17, 2020 |access-date=March 24, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> however, in the final cut of the movie, it is where [[Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Clint Barton]] briefly ends up after [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]] sacrifices herself for him to obtain the Soul Stone. * '''{{Visible anchor|Ta Lo}}''' (based on [[Ta-Lo]] from the Marvel Comics) is a a dimension that exists outside of space and time which acts as the headquarters of the [[Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Variance Authority]] (TVA). A [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki variant]] is brought to the headquarters by the TVA, where he is recruited to help track down [[Sylvie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|another variant of himself]]. The [[Atlanta Marriott Marquis]] hotel in [[Atlanta]], Georgia was used to portray the main building,<ref name="Marquis">{{Cite web |last=Whitbrook |first=James |date=April 5, 2021 |title=Marvel Secrets in the New Loki Trailer: The Avengers, Time-Keepers, and More |url=https://io9.gizmodo.com/marvel-secrets-in-the-new-loki-trailer-the-avengers-t-1846621398 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210405212852/https://io9.gizmodo.com/marvel-secrets-in-the-new-loki-trailer-the-avengers-t-1846621398 |archive-date=April 5, 2021 |access-date=April 5, 2021 |website=[[io9]]}}</ref> while working frosted [[incandescent]] ceiling lights were used as the set's [[key light]]s.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Davids |first=Brian |date=June 9, 2021 |title='Loki' Director Kate Herron on Shooting New 'Avengers: Endgame'-Era Footage |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-features/loki-avengers-endgame-marvel-disney-1234965274/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210609195951/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-features/loki-avengers-endgame-marvel-disney-1234965274/ |archive-date=June 9, 2021 |access-date=June 9, 2021 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]}}</ref> * The '''Veil of Noor''' separates Earth from a dimension inhabited by supernatural cosmic beings known as the Clandestines, or the ''djinn'' in Pakistani culture. Several individuals were exiled from the ''Noor'' dimension to Earth in the 1940s, shortly before the [[Partition of India]]. Most seek mystical bangles imbued with ''Noor'' power to tear down the Veil and return home. One, named Aisha, fled with one of the bangles, which was passed down through the generations to Kamala Khan. * The '''{{Visible anchor|Void}}''', also known as the '''Void at the End of Time''', is a place of existence at the end of time containing the remains of alternate universes pruned by the Time Variance Authority. It is inhabited by Victor Timely/He Who Remains' [[Alioth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Alioth]], a monstrous cloud-storm universe-devouring entity. It features many [[Easter egg (media)|Easter eggs]] from the comics, such as [[Thanos]]' helicopter, [[Throg]]<ref name="Sepinwall">{{Cite magazine |last=Sepinwall |first=Alan |author-link=Alan Sepinwall |date=July 7, 2021 |title='Loki' Episode 5 Recap: You Want Lokis? |access-date=July 7, 2021 |magazine=[[Rolling Stone]]}}</ref> and his [[Mjolnir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mjölnir]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Welch |first=Andy |date=July 7, 2021 |title=Loki episode five recap: glorious mystery with Richard E Grant |url=https://www.theguardian.com/tv-and-radio/2021/jul/07/loki-episode-five-recap-glorious-mystery-with-richard-e-grant |url-status=live |access-date=July 7, 2021 |website=[[The Guardian]]}}</ref> Qeng Tower, a tower associated with [[Kang the Conqueror]],<ref name="Qeng" /> and the [[Living Tribunal]]'s head,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Stewart |first=Brenton |date=July 7, 2021 |title='Loki': Marvel's Most Powerful Entity Just Made His MCU Debut (Sort Of) |url=https://www.cbr.com/loki-episode-5-living-tribunal-easter-egg/ Atlanta Studios]] in [[Atlanta]], Georgia,<ref name="Martin" /> where they installed an "old [[Santa Claus|Santa]] throne from a mall" from another reality.<ref name="Tangcay" />
==Objects==
=== Vehicles === ==== Spacecraft ==== * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Benatar}}''''' is an M-type spaceship used by the [[Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Guardians of the Galaxy]] and piloted by [[Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Quill]]. Named after [[Pat Benatar]], it is acquired after the ''[[#Milano|Milano]]'' is severely damaged. After being used to rescue [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]] from the remnants of the ''[[Statesman (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Statesman]]'', it takes the Guardians to [[Knowhere (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Knowhere]] and [[Titan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Titan]] before being carried back to Earth by [[Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Carol Danvers]]. It is used by the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]] to reach the Garden planet and in the [[Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Heist]]. It is succeeded by the ''[[#Bowie|Bowie]]'', named after singer [[David Bowie]].<ref name="HolidaySpecial" /> * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Bowie}}'''''.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.slashfilm.com/1329402/guardians-of-the-galaxy-3-new-bowie-ship-design-inspired-by-circular-saw/ | title=Guardians of the Galaxy 3's New Bowie Ship Design Was Inspired by a Circular Saw | date=July 9, 2023 }}</ref> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Chitauri mother ship}}''' was a large spaceship which served as the [[mother ship]] of the [[Chitauri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Chitauri]] army during the [[Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of New York]]. It was destroyed by [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]] when he carried a [[nuclear missile]] through a wormhole into space, severing the connection between the mothership and the Chitauri as well as the [[#Leviathans|Leviathans]] and ending the battle. In a [[deleted scene]] of ''Avengers: Endgame'', [[Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Rocket]] teases the Avengers about not destroying it earlier.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Cardona |first1=Ian |title=Avengers: Endgame Deleted Scene Confirms the Chitauri Were Terrible |url=https://www.cbr.com/avengers-endgame-deleted-scene-chitauri-terrible/ |website=[[Comic Book Resources]] |access-date=April 25, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190730201037/https://www.cbr.com/avengers-endgame-deleted-scene-chitauri-terrible/ |archive-date=July 30, 2019 |date=July 30, 2019 |url-status=live}}</ref> * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Commodore}}''''' is one of the Grandmaster's starships, used primarily for self pleasure reasons. It's stolen in ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'' by Thor, Bruce Banner and Valkyrie and is later used by Loki to escape from the destruction of Asgard. In ''[[Avengers: Infinity War]]'', its destroyed by Thanos along with the ''Statesman''. In ''[[What If...? (TV series)|What If...?]]'', an alternate universe version of the ship is stolen by Howard the Duck and Darcy Lewis.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a851005/thor-ragnarok-taika-waititi-reveals-adorable-easter-egg-hidden-spaceships/ | title=Thor: Ragnarok's spaceships hid an adorable Easter egg | website=[[Digital Spy]] | date=February 27, 2018 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web | url=https://screenrant.com/thor-ragnarok-ship-colors-aboriginal-maori-flag/ | title=Thor: Ragnarok's Most Personal Easter Egg Was Hidden in Plain Sight | website=[[Screen Rant]] | date=November 16, 2021 }}</ref> * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Dark Aster}}''''' is the flagship spaceship of [[Ronan the Accuser (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ronan the Accuser]], a three-mile wide ship in the [[Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kree]] Accusers fleet. Angered by the signing of a peace treaty with the Nova Empire, he used it to attack [[Xandar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Xandar]], but is destroyed by the ''Milano''. A similar spacecraft known as the ''Silver Aster'' is used by him during the [[Kree–Skrull War (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kree–Skrull War]]. In ''[[What If...? (TV series)|What If...?]]'', an alternate universe version of the ''Dark Aster'' lays siege to Xandar for five years before being destroyed by [[Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nebula]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bell |first=Wesley |date=March 9, 2021 |title=MCU: Every Important Vehicle From The Movies, Ranked |url=https://screenrant.com/mcu-every-important-vehicle-movies-ranked-worst-best/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210310141708/https://screenrant.com/mcu-every-important-vehicle-movies-ranked-worst-best/ |archive-date=March 10, 2021 |access-date=October 10, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> Visual effects of the spaceship were created by [[Moving Picture Company]] (MPC) and [[Sony Pictures Imageworks]].<ref name="GuardiansFXGuide" /> * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Domo}}'''''<ref>{{Cite web |date=August 20, 2021 |title='Eternals': Exclusive first look at Funko, Lego and Hasbro's cosmically cool Marvel toys |url=https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/eternals-marvel-cinematic-universe-toys-reveal-hasbro-lego-funko-pop-160058604.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210820165809/https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/eternals-marvel-cinematic-universe-toys-reveal-hasbro-lego-funko-pop-160058604.html |archive-date=August 20, 2021 |access-date=August 21, 2021 |website=[[Yahoo! Entertainment]]}}</ref> is the [[Eternals (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Eternals]]' starship which serves as their primary base of operations. Composed of three large, circular rooms,<ref name="DomoSetVisit">{{Cite web |last=Bacon |first=Thomas |date=October 11, 2021 |title=Eternals Spaceship, The Domo, Explained |url=https://screenrant.com/eternals-movie-spaceship-domo-marvel-comics-explained/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211012011709/https://screenrant.com/eternals-movie-spaceship-domo-marvel-comics-explained/ |archive-date=October 12, 2021 |access-date=November 8, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> it is completely silent, controlled by the Eternals' golden cosmic energy, and is used by them to arrive on Earth 7,000 years ago.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Abdulbaki |first=Mae |date=May 26, 2021 |title=The Eternals' Spaceship & Technology Explained |url=https://screenrant.com/eternals-marvel-movie-spaceship-technology-explained/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210526225748/https://screenrant.com/eternals-marvel-movie-spaceship-technology-explained/ |archive-date=May 26, 2021 |access-date=August 20, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> The most important room is known as the "bridge", which contains a large statue of the [[Celestials (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Celestial]] [[Arishem (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Arishem]] and pattern-covered walls which generate the Eternals' suits; ''[[Eternals (film)|Eternals]]'' production designer [[Eve Stewart]] explained that the room was designed to look like mosques, synagogues, churches, and temples, and the set was constructed in eight weeks, illuminated by lights through a [[fiberglass]] below.<ref name="DomoSetVisit" /> Another room contains numerous ancient artifacts and [[List of mythological objects|mythological objects]], including the ''[[Emerald Tablet]]'', [[King Arthur (Marvel Comics)|King Arthur]]'s [[Excalibur]], and the [[Holy Grail]].<ref name="EternalsEggs" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Ridgely |first=Charlie |date=November 28, 2021 |title=Eternals Name Drop Might Tease Arrival of Another Marvel Hero to the MCU |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/eternals-excalibur-reference-marvel-hero-mcu-arrival-faiza-hussain/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211129060752/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/eternals-excalibur-reference-marvel-hero-mcu-arrival-faiza-hussain/ |archive-date=November 29, 2021 |access-date=December 4, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> The unique design of the ship was inspired by the art of [[Jack Kirby]], [[meteor]] dust, as well as [[sacred geometry]],<ref>{{Cite magazine |last=Coggan |first=Devan |date=August 19, 2021 |title=New ''Eternals'' trailer teases an epic cosmic showdown |url=https://ew.com/movies/eternals-full-trailer-epic-showdown/ |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210819080902/https://ew.com/movies/eternals-full-trailer-epic-showdown/ |archive-date=August 19, 2021 |access-date=August 20, 2021 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]]}}</ref><ref name="DomoSetVisit" /> and it is named after the Marvel Comics character [[Domo (comics)|of the same name]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Whitbrook |first=James |date=May 24, 2021 |title=Marvel's ''Eternals'' Trailer Breakdown: Who's Who and WTF Is That? |url=https://gizmodo.com/marvels-eternals-trailer-breakdown-whos-who-and-wtf-is-1846956785 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210530225042/https://gizmodo.com/marvels-eternals-trailer-breakdown-whos-who-and-wtf-is-1846956785 |archive-date=May 30, 2021 |access-date=August 20, 2021 |website=[[io9]]}}</ref> * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Eclector}}''''' is a large spaceship used by [[Yondu Udonta (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Yondu]]'s [[Ravagers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ravager]] clan until its destruction in 2014. However, Yondu managed to escape along with [[Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Rocket]] and [[Groot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Groot]] on the ship's third quadrant prior to its destruction. Visual effects for scenes set in the spaceship in ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2]]'' were provided by [[Framestore]];<ref name="Guardians2Title">{{Cite web |last1=Landekic |first1=Lola |last2=Perkins |first2=Will |date=August 23, 2017 |title=Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 (2017) |url=https://www.artofthetitle.com/title/guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170824133653/http://www.artofthetitle.com/title/guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2/ |archive-date=August 24, 2017 |access-date=July 23, 2021 |website=[[Art of the Title]]}}</ref> the destruction of the ship was handled by [[Weta Digital]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hogg |first=Trevor |date=May 30, 2017 |title=Weta Digital Confronts the Monstrous Ego of 'Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2' |url=https://www.awn.com/vfxworld/weta-digital-confronts-monstrous-ego-guardians-galaxy-vol-2 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181026222738/https://www.awn.com/vfxworld/weta-digital-confronts-monstrous-ego-guardians-galaxy-vol-2 |archive-date=October 26, 2018 |access-date=July 23, as to move sections around and portray different areas of the ship.<ref name="Guardians2PressNotes">{{Cite web |title=Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 Production Notes |url=https://www.wdsmediafile.com/media/GuardiansVol2/writen-material/GuardiansVol258f522ece623e.pdf |url-status=dead 23, 2021 |publisher=[[Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures]]}}</ref> * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Excelsior}}''''' is a spaceship used by the [[Fantastic Four (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Fantastic Four]]. * The '''''{{visible anchor|Milano}}''''' is an M-type spaceship flown by [[Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Quill]] since he was ten, named after his childhood crush, [[Alyssa Milano]]. It later becomes the main ship for the [[Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Guardians of the Galaxy]], but is destroyed during the [[Battle of Xandar]]. After being rebuilt by the [[Nova Corps (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nova Corps]], it is severely damaged by an asteroid field and abandoned on Berhert, with the ''[[#Benatar|Benatar]]'' becoming the Guardians' new ship. To limit the amount of [[chroma key|blue screen]] the actors had to interact with, interiors for the ship were constructed at [[Pinewood Atlanta Studios]] in [[Atlanta]], Georgia for ''Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2'',<ref name="Guardians2PressNotes" /> including the cockpit that had been built for the first film and originally stored in London.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Mulcahey |first=Matt |date=June 8, 2017 |title=DP Henry Braham on Shooting Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 with the First Weapon 8K Vista Vision Full Frame Camera |url=https://filmmakermagazine.com/102645-dp-henry-braham-on-shooting-guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2-with-the-first-weapon-8k-vista-vision-full-frame-camera/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171029172918/http://filmmakermagazine.com/102645-dp-henry-braham-on-shooting-guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2-with-the-first-weapon-8k-vista-vision-full-frame-camera/ |archive-date=October 29, 2017 |access-date=July 23, 2021 |website=[[Filmmaker (magazine)|Filmmaker]]}}</ref> The real-life Alyssa Milano called the homage "pretty cool" upon learning of the reference.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Labonte |first=Rachel |date=January 18, 2022 |title=Alyssa Milano Reacts To Star-Lord's GOTG Ship Being Named After Her |url=https://screenrant.com/guardians-galaxy-movie-ship-alyssa-milano-reaction-response/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220118194215/https://screenrant.com/guardians-galaxy-movie-ship-alyssa-milano-reaction-response/ |archive-date=January 18, 2022 |access-date=March 20, 2022 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> In an alternate reality where [[Star-Lord|T'Challa]] became Star-Lord, the spaceship is named the ''Mandela'' instead, a reference to [[Nelson Mandela]].<ref name="WIEp2Eggs">{{Cite web |last=Elvy |first=Craig |date=August 18, 2021 |title=Every MCU Easter Egg In What If? Episode 2 |url=https://screenrant.com/marvel-what-if-episode-2-mcu-marvel-easter-eggs/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210818212744/https://screenrant.com/marvel-what-if-episode-2-mcu-marvel-easter-eggs/ |archive-date=August 18, 2021 |access-date=August 22, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> * '''{{Visible anchor|Q-Ship}}s''' are ring-shaped spaceships used by Thanos and [[Children of Thanos|his children]]. They are stored in the ''[[#Sanctuary II|Sanctuary II]]'', and deployed from the ship when invading other planets. The Q-Ships also contain Outrider dropships, allowing them to unleash Outriders onto a battlefield. When [[Ebony Maw (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ebony Maw]] and [[Cull Obsidian]] attack New York City in 2018, [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Doctor Strange]] is taken prisoner on a Q-Ship until his rescue by Tony Stark and [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]]. One is also used by Proxima Midnight and Corvus Glaive to leave [[Scotland]], while another deploys Outrider dropships into the atmosphere during the [[Infinity War (event)|Battle of Wakanda]]. * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Sanctuary II}}''''' is a {{convert|12|mi|km|adj=mid|-long}} long spaceship owned by Thanos, which serves as an orbital base while an invasion is in progress as well as a heavily armed warcraft.<ref>{{Cite web |last1=Owen |first1=Phil |last2=Gonzalez |first2=Umberto |date=November 2, 2017 |title='Thor: Ragnarok': Marvel Boss Kevin Feige Explains That Mid-Credits Scene |url=https://www.thewrap.com/thor-ragnarok-post-mid-credits-scene-explained-kevin-feige/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171107021831/https://www.thewrap.com/thor-ragnarok-post-mid-credits-scene-explained-kevin-feige/ |archive-date=November 7, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[TheWrap]]}}</ref> It can also carry four [[#Q-Ship|Q-Ships]] under its wings. Following the [[Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Heist]], an alternate version of Thanos and his army from 2014 is transported to 2023 on the ''Sanctuary II'', and the [[Avengers Compound (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers Compound]] is destroyed by its missiles. During the subsequent [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]], Thanos orders his troops to "rain fire" on the battlefield, but the ship is destroyed by [[Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Carol Danvers]]. * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Statesman}}''''' is a large spaceship owned by the [[Grandmaster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Grandmaster]] stolen by [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]] and used to transport the [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]] away from [[Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgard]] before it is destroyed during [[Ragnarok (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ragnarök]]. However, on its way to Earth, it is attacked by the ''[[#Sanctuary II|Sanctuary II]]'' and destroyed by Thanos using the [[Power Stone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Power Stone]].
==== Aircraft ==== * '''{{Visible anchor|Asgardian skiff}}s''' are a group of [[hovercraft]] warships resembling [[Viking longship]]s used by the [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]] for transportation and patrol. * The '''{{Visible anchor|Fantasticar}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics vehicle [[Fantasticar|of the same name]]) is a [[flying car]] owned by the [[Fantastic Four (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Fantastic Four]] and used for transportation.<ref>{{Cite web |last1=Stephan |first1=Katcy |last2=Moreau |first2=Jordan |date=July 26, 2024 |title=45 'Deadpool & Wolverine' Cameos and Easter Eggs From the X-Men, MCU and Beyond |url=https://variety.com/lists/deadpool-and-wolverine-cameos-easter-eggs/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240727011149/https://variety.com/lists/deadpool-and-wolverine-cameos-easter-eggs/ |archive-date=July 27, 2024 |access-date=July 26, 2024 |website=[[Variety (website)|Variety]]}}</ref> Two models of the Fantasticar were made for the filming of ''[[The Fantastic Four: First Steps]]'', one that allowed for interior filming with the actors and another that was "stripped down" for use in effects shots. The Fantasticar was based on "mid-'60s American concept cars that were actually referencing European cars". Its accent features, such as its turbine intakes and interface controls, have a 1950s "retrofuture" look.<ref name="EWCoverStoryApr2025">{{Cite magazine |last=Holub |first=Christian |date=April 10, 2025 |title=Welcome to the (retro)future: Inside the Fantastic Four's 'first steps' into the MCU |url=https://ew.com/the-fantastic-four-first-steps-mcu-plot-character-details-cover-story-11712063 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250410204208/https://ew.com/the-fantastic-four-first-steps-mcu-plot-character-details-cover-story-11712063 |archive-date=April 10, 2025 |access-date=April 10, 2025 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]]}}</ref> * '''{{Visible anchor|Helicarrier}}s''' (based on the Marvel Comics vehicle [[Helicarrier|of the same name]]) are flying [[aircraft carriers]] used by [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] as mobile command centers. They are equipped with [[optical camouflage]] and, later, large-sized cannons and repulsor engines courtesy of Tony Stark, as well as two stacked carrier decks and a hull number of 64.<ref name="AvengersFXGuide">{{Cite web |last=Seymour |first=Mike |date=May 6, 2012 |title=VFX roll call for The Avengers (updated) |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/vfx-roll-call-for-the-avengers/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120510024801/https://www.fxguide.com/featured/vfx-roll-call-for-the-avengers/ |archive-date=May 10, 2012 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref> During the [[Hydra uprising]], [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]] attempts to use three Helicarriers linked to S.H.I.E.L.D. satellites to assassinate potential threats, but they are destroyed by [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]] and his allies. Following the dissolution of S.H.I.E.L.D., the original Helicarrier is recommissioned and used to assist the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]] during the [[Battle of Sokovia]]. In ''[[Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.]]'', the original Helicarrier is revealed to have been secretly repaired by Phil Coulson's team and it reappears in active use in the series finale. However, in ''[[Secret Invasion (miniseries)|Secret Invasion]]'', Nick Fury states that the Helicarrier has since been [[aircraft boneyard|mothballed]]. Visual effects for the Helicarrier in ''[[The Avengers (2012 film)|The Avengers]]'' were provided by [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM), Scanline VFX, Evil Eye Pictures, and Luma Pictures,<ref name="AvengersFXGuide" /> while visual effects in ''[[Captain America: The Winter Soldier]]'' were also provided by ILM.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Giardina |first=Carolyn |date=April 17, 2014 |title='Captain America: Winter Soldier' Visual Effects Explained |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/behind-screen/captain-america-winter-soldier-visual-697270 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140422002451/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/behind-screen/captain-america-winter-soldier-visual-697270 |archive-date=April 22, 2014 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]}}</ref> [[File:Ford E-Series wagon.jpg|thumb|right|A typical [[Ford Econoline]]]] * '''{{Visible anchor|Leviathans}}''' are large cybernetically enhanced serpentine creatures used by the [[Chitauri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Chitauri]] under the command of Thanos to transport troops and warships, weighing approximately three million tonnes each. Following the [[Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of New York]], the Leviathans are salvaged by [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]], with one of them stored in the [[#Hydra Research Base|Hydra Research Base]] in [[Sokovia]]. Another group of Leviathans are later used by an alternate version of Thanos from 2014 during the [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]], and are destroyed by Tony Stark using the [[#Nano Gauntlet|Nano Gauntlet]]. * '''{{Visible anchor|Quinjet}}s''' (based on the Marvel Comics vehicle [[Quinjet|of the same name]]) are technologically advanced jets used by [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]], the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]], and [[S.T.R.I.K.E. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.T.R.I.K.E.]] teams. After the [[Battle of Sokovia]], the [[Hulk (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hulk]] leaves on a Quinjet, escaping Earth's atmosphere before crash-landing on [[Sakaar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sakaar]] via a wormhole. The interior of the jet was built for ''The Avengers'' and later shipped to Australia for ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kois |first=Dan |date=October 19, 2017 |title=The Superweirdo Behind 'Thor: Ragnarok' |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2017/10/19/magazine/the-superweirdo-behind-thor-ragnarok.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171107202409/https://www.nytimes.com/2017/10/19/magazine/the-superweirdo-behind-thor-ragnarok.html |archive-date=November 7, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[The New York Times Magazine]]}}</ref> [[File:WLA3quarters.JPG|thumb|upright|A restored [[Harley-Davidson WLA]]]]
==== Land vehicles ==== * '''{{Visible anchor|Luis' van}}''' is a brown 1972 [[Ford Econoline]] owned by [[Luis (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Luis]] and used by him, [[Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Scott Lang]], [[Dave (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dave]], and [[Kurt (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kurt]]. It is later used by them for their company, [[Teams and organizations of the Marvel Cinematic Universe#X-Con Security Consultants|X-Con Security Consultants]], and is outfitted with a miniaturized [[#Quantum Tunnel|Quantum Tunnel]]. Lang is later trapped in the Quantum Realm for five years until a rat ran over the control panel of the Quantum Tunnel, allowing him to escape. It is destroyed by Thanos during the [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]]. * '''{{Visible anchor|Steve Rogers' motorcycle}}''' is a [[Harley-Davidson]] motorcycle used by Steve Rogers. In ''Captain America: The First Avenger'', Rogers uses a weaponized 1942 [[Harley-Davidson WLA|WLA]] motorcycle in [[World War II]] in his fights against [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cavaciuti |first=Anne |date=September 14, 2020 |title=Here Are The Harley-Davidson Motorcycles Driven By Captain America |url=https://www.hotcars.com/captain-america-motorcycle-harley-davidson/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210430191937/https://www.hotcars.com/captain-america-motorcycle-harley-davidson/ |archive-date=April 30, 2021 |access-date=April 5, 2021 |website=Hot Cars}}</ref> In ''Captain America: The Winter Soldier'' and ''The Falcon and the Winter Soldier'', this model is shown to be displayed in the Captain America exhibit in the [[Smithsonian Air and Space Museum]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Singh |first=Olivia |date=April 23, 2021 |title=47 details you might have missed on 'The Falcon and the Winter Soldier' |url=https://www.insider.com/the-falcon-and-the-winter-soldier-marvel-details-references-easter-eggs#the-motorcycle-seen-on-display-at-the-smithsonian-is-the-same-one-that-was-part-of-the-previous-captain-america-exhibit-with-a-few-adjustments-6 |url-status=live |archive-url=http://wayback.vefsafn.is/wayback/20210325194113/https://www.insider.com/the%2Dfalcon%2Dand%2Dthe%2Dwinter%2Dsoldier%2Dmarvel%2Ddetails%2Dreferences%2Deaster%2Deggs#the-motorcycle-seen-on-display-at-the-smithsonian-is-the-same-one-that-was-part-of-the-previous-captain-america-exhibit-with-a-few-adjustments-6 |archive-date=March 25, 2021 |access-date=April 30, 2021 |website=[[Insider Inc.|Insider]]}}</ref> In ''The Avengers'', Rogers is shown to have switched to a [[Softail]] Slim model for commuting around New York City, before using the [[Harley-Davidson Street 750|Street 750]] model when fleeing from pursuing Hydra agents in ''The Winter Soldier''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bornhop |first=Andrew |date=March 14, 2014 |title=Captain America Motorcycle is a Harley-Davidson Street 750 |url=https://www.cycleworld.com/2014/03/14/harley-davidson-street-750-featured-in-captain-america-the-winter-soldier-movie/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210430191946/https://www.cycleworld.com/2014/03/14/harley-davidson-street-750-featured-in-captain-america-the-winter-soldier-movie/ |archive-date=April 30, 2021 |access-date=April 30, 2021 |website=[[Cycle World]]}}</ref> Rogers later uses other models such as the Breakout,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lieback |first=Ron |date=November 14, 2013 |title=Harley Street 750 & Breakout in 'Captain America: The Winter Soldier' |url=https://ultimatemotorcycling.com/2013/11/14/harley-street-750-breakout-captain-america-winter-soldier/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210430191936/https://ultimatemotorcycling.com/2013/11/14/harley-street-750-breakout-captain-america-winter-soldier/ |archive-date=April 30, 2021 |access-date=April 30, 2021 |website=Ultimate Motorcycling}}</ref> [[V-Rod]] and the Softail Slim S.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Benjamin |date=December 25, 2020 |title=What Kind of Motorcycles Does Captain America Ride? |url=https://moneyinc.com/what-kind-of-motorcycles-does-captain-america-ride/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210430191937/https://moneyinc.com/what-kind-of-motorcycles-does-captain-america-ride/ |archive-date=April 30, 2021 |access-date=April 30, 2021 |website=Money Inc}}</ref> * The '''{{Visible anchor|Wakandan maglev train}}''' is an advanced [[maglev]] train used for public transport in Golden City, [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakanda]], as well as for transporting vibranium inside the mines in Mount Bashenga. The train has been used to compare Wakanda's [[Afrofuturism]] with [[Transportation in the United States|transport infrastructure in the United States]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bliss |first=Laura |date=February 21, 2018 |title=The Attainable Wonders of Wakandan Transit |url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2018-02-21/black-panther-s-maglev-system-is-the-transit-we-deserve |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210523122357/https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2018-02-21/black-panther-s-maglev-system-is-the-transit-we-deserve |archive-date=May 23, 2021 |access-date=May 23, 2021 |website=[[Bloomberg News]]}}</ref>
==== Ships ==== * The '''''Iliad''''' is an aircraft carrier in the second season of ''[[Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.]]'' operated by Robert Gonzales and his S.H.I.E.L.D. faction as their headquarters. Having survived the HYDRA uprising, Gonzales and his faction saw themselves as the true S.H.I.E.L.D. until Phil Coulson's team earned their trust following the events of ''[[Avengers: Age of Ultron]]'', causing the two factions to merge. The ''Iliad'' was later the scene of a battle between S.H.I.E.L.D. and Jiaying who sought to induce terragenesis in all Inhumans. During the battle, the Quinjet carrying the necessary crystals was blasted off of the ship and into the ocean where the crystals would spread throughout the ocean's ecosystem. * The '''''{{Visible anchor|Lemurian Star}}''''' is a ship owned by [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] and used to launch [[Project Insight (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Project Insight]] satellites. During the [[Hydra Uprising]], the ship is hijacked by a group of pirates led by [[Georges Batroc (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Georges Batroc]] and under the employment of [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]], until a [[S.T.R.I.K.E. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.T.R.I.K.E.]] team arrives to retake the ship. [[Captain Carter]] replicates the mission in an alternate reality before being recruited to the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] by the [[Watcher (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Watcher]], later discovering the [[Hydra Stomper]] armor with someone inside.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Elvy |first=Craig |date=October 6, 2021 |title=Every MCU Easter Egg In What If? Episode 9 |url=https://screenrant.com/marvel-what-if-episode-9-mcu-marvel-easter-eggs/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211009045133/https://screenrant.com/marvel-what-if-episode-9-mcu-marvel-easter-eggs/ |archive-date=October 9, 2021 |access-date=October 7, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> Scenes set on the ship in ''[[Captain America: The Winter Soldier]]'' were filmed on the ''[[Sea Launch Commander]]'' docked in [[Long Beach, California]].<ref>{{Cite AV media |title=On the Frontline: An Inside Look at Captain America's Battlegrounds |type=Featurette |publisher=[[Walt Disney Home Entertainment]] |year=2014 |place=''Captain America: The Winter Soldier'' Blu-Ray}}</ref>
===Suits=== *The '''{{visible anchor|Ant-Man suit}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Ant-Man suit|of the same name]]) enables the wearer to change size while retaining strength, as well as communicate with and control ants. It was designed by [[Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hank Pym]] and used during [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] missions until the [[Pym Particles (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pym Particles]] began to have an effect on him, and is later stolen by [[Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Scott Lang]], who becomes the next Ant-Man. [[Double Negative (VFX)|Double Negative]] and [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM) worked on Ant-Man's shrinking effect in [[Ant-Man (film)|the first film]], which shows the outline of his body as in the comics.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Seymour |first=Mike |date=July 19, 2015 |title=Ant-Man: Marvel's heist film |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/ant-man-marvels-heist-film/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150721232516/https://www.fxguide.com/featured/ant-man-marvels-heist-film/ |archive-date=July 21, 2015 |access-date=July 16, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref> **Similar to the Ant-Man suit, the '''{{visible anchor|Wasp suit}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Wasp suit|of the same name]]) utilises Pym Particles and enables the wearer to change size using special regulators, but most notably also features wings that expand when the wearer shrinks. The suit also include wrist adapters which allows the wearer to blast their enemies. The suit was first designed by Hank Pym and [[Janet van Dyne (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Janet van Dyne]] for Janet's use during S.H.I.E.L.D. missions. The two begin work on a second suit for their daughter [[Hope van Dyne]], in which she utilises for the film ''[[Ant-Man and the Wasp]]'' (2018). In ''[[Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania]]'' (2023), Van Dyne's suit is upgraded to feature more yellow colours, similar to the comic version of the Wasp suit.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cbr.com/ant-man-and-the-wasp-quantumania-hope-pym-wasp-costume/|title=Quantumania Reveals the Best Look Yet at Hope Pym's New Wasp Costume|accessdate=March 25, 2025|website=[[Comic Book Resources]]|date=January 20, 2023|last=Dela Paz|first=Maggie}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.popsugar.com/entertainment/what-wasp-powers-44990849|title=The Wasp's Incredible Powers Go Far Beyond Her Suit|accessdate=March 25, 2025|website=Pop Sugar|date=July 15, 2018|last=Fernandez|first=Celia}}</ref> [[File:FanimeCon 2018 347 (40640219200).jpg|thumb|right|A [[cosplay]] of the Black Panther suit at [[FanimeCon]] 2018]] *The '''{{visible anchor|Black Panther suit}}''' <!--(based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Black Panther suit|of the same name]])--> is a protective [[nanotech]] suit woven from [[Vibranium (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|vibranium]] that is worn by the King of Wakanda in his duties as the Black Panther. The suit features retractable claws made of vibranium and is nearly impenetrable. Versions of the suit have been worn by [[T'Chaka (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|T'Chaka]], [[T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|T'Challa]], [[N'Jadaka (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|N'Jadaka]], and [[Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Shuri]]. T'Challa's second suit is also able to be shrunk down into a necklace as well as absorb energy for future redistribution. The suit is a combination of a practical costume and visual effects, featuring a vibranium mesh weave similar to [[chainmail]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hewitt |first=Chris |date=November 25, 2015 |title=Captain America: Civil War trailer breakdown |url=https://www.empireonline.com/movies/features/captain-america-civil-war-trailer-breakdown/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151126084807/https://www.empireonline.com/movies/features/captain-america-civil-war-trailer-breakdown/ |archive-date=November 26, 2015 |access-date=November 25, 2015 |website=[[Empire (magazine)|Empire]]}}</ref> ''Captain America: Civil War'' costume designer [[Judianna Makovsky]] called the Black Panther costume "difficult" since "you needed sort of a feline body, but it's hard and practical at the same time. You needed a feeling of some sort of ethnicity in there, but of a world [Wakanda] we weren't really creating yet, so you didn't want to go too far and say too much about that world."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Busch |first=Jenna |date=September 24, 2016 |title=Civil War Costume Designer Judianna Makovsky On Returning Spider-Man To His Roots |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2016/09/24/civil-war-costume-designer-judianna-makovsky-on-returning-spider-man-to-his-roots |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160924234051/https://www.ign.com/articles/2016/09/24/civil-war-costume-designer-judianna-makovsky-on-returning-spider-man-to-his-roots |archive-date=September 24, 2016 |access-date=September 25, 2016 |website=[[IGN]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Captain America's uniform}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Captain America's uniform|of the same name]]) is the costume worn by the bearers of the Captain America mantle whilst on missions. ** {{anchor|Steve Rogers' uniform}}The first uniform, worn by [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]], is a cloth [[United Service Organizations|USO]] costume based on his original costume from the comics, along with a [[heater shield]]. Upon hearing that [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]]' unit was [[Missing in action|MIA]], he alters his USO uniform for the rescue mission, wearing a combat jacket and pants over the costume and donning a blue helmet from a USO [[chorus girl]]. [[Howard Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Howard Stark]] later designs a combat uniform made of carbon polymer, with leather pouches and a holster, a wingless mask, and a [[Captain America's shield (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|round vibranium shield]]. After being unfrozen, he uses a costume designed by [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] which resembles his USO uniform. During his time with [[S.T.R.I.K.E. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.T.R.I.K.E.]], he uses a new uniform designed for stealth missions which has a darker shade of blue. He later returns to a variant of his [[World War II]] costume, taken from a display at the Captain America exhibit at the Smithsonian. [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]] later creates a new uniform for Rogers, which incorporates magnetic gauntlets, allowing him to recall his shield. A slightly different version of this suit is used during the [[Avengers Civil War]]. During his exile, the suit is altered to remove the star and the Avengers logo. He is equipped with Wakandan shields on his arms before the [[Infinity War (event)|Battle of Wakanda]]. After the Avengers reunite, he uses another new uniform. ** {{anchor|John Walker's uniform}}When [[John Walker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|John Walker]] is handed the Captain America mantle, he uses an entirely new design, based on [[U.S. Agent's uniform|that from the comics]]: the uniform is blue, with red highlights and chest stripes, and includes red fingerless gloves. In place of the Avengers logo, it has the [[flag of the United States]] on the arms, and a stylized star on the mask and chest. He also carries a handgun and a version of Captain America's shield given by Steve Rogers to Sam Wilson. After he is stripped of the title, he builds a new shield, and dyes his uniform black, becoming the "[[U.S. Agent]]". ** {{anchor|Sam Wilson's flight suit}}[[Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sam Wilson]] dons a new version of the uniform as the new Captain America, incorporating his new [[Vibranium (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|vibranium]] wings.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Perine |first=Aaron |date=April 28, 2021 |title=Falcon and the Winter Soldier Star Anthony Mackie Confirms New Captain America Wings are Vibranium |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/falcon-and-winter-soldier-captain-america-anthony-mackie-vibranium-wings-confirms/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210430075705/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/falcon-and-winter-soldier-captain-america-anthony-mackie-vibranium-wings-confirms/ |archive-date=April 30, 2021 |access-date=April 30, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> This version, a gift from Wakanda, sticks closely to the version that he wears in the comics, with the main color being white. The star design spreads across the whole chest, and resembles the logo of the [[U.S. Air Force]]. The white mask incorporates Wilson's red goggles and stretches from the shoulders to just above his ears. {{multiple image |align=right |direction=vertical |width=200 |image1=Masaai-women.jpg |image2=Dora Milaje in film.jpg |footer=The [[Maasai people]] of [[Kenya]] (top) inspired about 80% of the design of the [[Dora Milaje (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dora Milaje]], Wakanda's all-female special forces (bottom).<ref name="Fashionista">{{Cite news |last=Hoo |first=Fawina Soo |date=February 12, 2018 |title=The Costume, Hair and Makeup in Marvel's 'Black Panther' Are A Celebration of Black Culture and Heritage |url=https://fashionista.com/2018/02/marvel-black-panther-movie-costumes-hair-makeup |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20210727225603/https://fashionista.com/2018/02/marvel-black-panther-movie-costumes-hair-makeup |archive-date=July 27, 2021 |access-date=February 27, 2018 |website=[[Fashionista (website)|Fashionista]]}}</ref> |footer_align=left}} * The '''{{visible anchor|Dora Milaje uniform}}''' is the uniform worn by the [[Dora Milaje (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dora Milaje]] of [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakanda]]. It is made up of a body suit, harness, vibranium shoulder armor, neck rings, knee-high boots, and a waist cape. Silver rings are worn on the neck and arms (with the exception of [[Okoye (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Okoye]], whose rings are gold to denote her status as general). The design of the uniform was partially inspired by tribal [[Filipinos|Filipino]] costume, as well as African influences.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Russo |first=Carla Herreria |date=February 24, 2018 |title=Tribal Filipinos Were A Surprising Muse For 'Black Panther's' Dora Milaje |url=https://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/entry/black-panther-filipino-influence_n_5a90b27fe4b03b55731c44a9 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210422222720/https://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/entry/black-panther-filipino-influence_n_5a90b27fe4b03b55731c44a9 |archive-date=April 22, 2021 |access-date=April 22, 2021 |website=[[Huffington Post]]}}</ref> ''[[Black Panther (film)|Black Panther]]'' costume designer [[Ruth E. Carter]] wanted to avoid the "girls in the bathing suits" look, and instead had the Dora Milaje wear full armor that they would practically need for battle.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Trumbore |first=David |date=January 24, 2018 |title='Black Panther': 90 Things to Know about the MCU's Game-Changing Movie |url=https://collider.com/black-panther-things-to-know/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180124230455/https://collider.com/black-panther-things-to-know/ |archive-date=January 24, 2018 |access-date=January 24, 2018 |website=[[Collider (website)|Collider]]}}</ref> Anthony Francisco, the Senior Visual Development Illustrator, noted the Dora Milaje costumes were based 80 percent on the [[Maasai people]], five percent on [[samurai]], five percent on [[ninja]]s, and five percent on the [[Ifugao people]] from the [[Philippines]].<ref name="Fashionista" /> * The '''{{visible anchor|EXO-7 Falcon}}''' is a winged harness created by the [[U.S. military]] for the [[Air National Guard]]. It is used by former [[Pararescuemen]] [[Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sam Wilson]] and Riley, the latter of whom was killed during a mission. Sam then left active duty and joined the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]] after assisting Steve Rogers and [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]] during the [[Hydra uprising]]. The suit features retractable wings and a pair of collapsible [[Steyr SPP]] submachine guns. Tony Stark later creates a new set of bulletproof retractable wings, featuring [[Redwing (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|a drone]] as well as missiles and a wrist-mounted submachine gun. During a battle with [[John Walker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|John Walker]], the suit is severely damaged beyond repair, and Sam leaves it with [[Joaquin Torres (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Joaquin Torres]]. * '''[[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Iron Man's armor]]''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Iron Man's armor|of the same name]]) is a set of [[Powered exoskeleton|armored suits]] created by Tony Stark to combat threats. Most follow the same red and gold color scheme and contain similar functions. Stark would eventually create up to 85 armors, 34 of which are part of the original "[[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)#Iron Legion|Iron Legion]]". Many of his early suits were highly mobile and versatile, with the ability to be transformed or stored in various objects including a suitcase (Mark V), a cylindrical pod (Mark VII), and detachable parts (Mark XLII). Eventually, beginning with the Mark L armor, Stark is able to store his armor in the form of nanobots in his [[Arc reactor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|arc reactor]] which could flow over his body, assembling based on cybernetic commands, allowing Stark to create endless combinations and new weapons to manifest out of the armor. ** The '''[[Hulkbuster armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hulkbuster armor]]''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Hulkbuster armor|of the same name]]) is a modular add-on to Tony Stark's regular armor. Developed by Stark and [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]], its purpose is to restrain the Hulk and minimize the damage caused by him. The first-generation armor (the '''Iron Man Mark XLIV armor''') is remotely controlled by a mobile service module named Veronica (named after the [[Archie Comics]] character [[Veronica Lodge]])<ref>{{Cite web |last=Burlingame |first=Russ |date=April 11, 2015 |title=Avengers: Age of Ultron Features an Archie Comics Reference |url=https://comicbook.com/news/avengers-age-of-ultron-features-an-archie-comics-reference/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150511041001/https://comicbook.com/2015/04/11/avengers-age-of-ultron-features-an-archie-comics-reference |archive-date=May 11, 2015 |access-date=February 25, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> and is used to restrain the Hulk following a rampage by him in [[Johannesburg]], South Africa. In 2018, Banner is seen wearing an upgraded version of the armor (the '''Iron Man Mark XLVIII armor'''), which he uses during the [[Infinity War (event)|Battle of Wakanda]] and the killing of [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]]. ** The '''{{visible anchor|Hydra Stomper armor}}''' is a suit of armor built by Howard Stark for Steve Rogers during World War II as the [[Hydra Stomper]] in an alternate reality in which [[Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peggy Carter]] becomes [[Captain Carter]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Howard |first=Kirsten |date=August 11, 2021 |title=Marvel's What If...? Episode 1 Review: Peggy Carter Changes MCU History |url=https://www.denofgeek.com/tv/marvel-what-if-episode-1-review-captain-carter-mcu/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210811113739/https://www.denofgeek.com/tv/marvel-what-if-episode-1-review-captain-carter-mcu/ |archive-date=August 11, 2021 |access-date=September 1, 2021 |website=[[Den of Geek]]}}</ref> Powered by the [[Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tesseract]], the writers of ''[[What If...? (TV series)|What If...?]]'' originally named the armor the "Hydra Smasher" before [[Kevin Feige]] suggested the name change.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Thomas |first=Eric |date=August 13, 2021 |title=Breaking Down Captain Carter with 'What If...?' Head Writer A.C. Bradley – Exclusive Interview |url=https://discussingfilm.net/2021/08/13/breaking-down-captain-carter-with-what-if-head-writer-a-c-bradley-exclusive-interview/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210813212009/https://discussingfilm.net/2021/08/13/breaking-down-captain-carter-with-what-if-head-writer-a-c-bradley-exclusive-interview/ |archive-date=August 13, 2021 |access-date=September 1, 2021 |website=DiscussingFilm}}</ref> ** The '''[[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)#Iron Legion|Iron Legion]]''' (based on the Marvel Comics element [[Iron Legion|of the same name]]) is two separate sets of armor built by Tony Stark. The first is a set of specialized armors (the '''Mark VIII–XLI armors''') built due to his insomnia for various situations that he might encounter, which he uses against [[Advanced Idea Mechanics (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|A.I.M.]] He eventually destroys them due to the friction they cause between him and Pepper. The second set is a series of drones built by Stark to aid the Avengers, which are later taken control by [[Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ultron]] and destroyed in battle with the Avengers. ** The '''{{visible anchor|Iron Monger armor}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Iron Monger armor|of the same name]]) is an armored suit similar to the [[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Iron Man armor]]. After [[Obadiah Stane (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Obadiah Stane]] gains Stark's salvaged Mark I armor from the [[Ten Rings (organization)|Ten Rings]], he reverse engineers it to create an even more powerful suit with added weapons, such as a minigun on the right arm. The suit is powered by Stark's personal [[Arc reactor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|arc reactor]], forcing Stark to use a replacement to power his own suit, although he manages to defeat Stane. ** The '''{{visible anchor|Iron Spider armor}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit of the same name), also known as '''Item 17A''', is an armored nanotech suit created by Tony Stark for [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]]'s use as an Avenger. The suit features four mechanical legs that can be unfolded from the back of the suit, allowing enhanced mobility and climbing skills, as well as [[Web-shooters (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|web-shooters]]. Following his fight with the [[Adrian Toomes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vulture]], Stark offers Parker the suit and membership to the Avengers, but Parker declines both. Two years later, Stark uses it to rescue Parker after he falls from [[Ebony Maw (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ebony Maw]]'s [[#Q-Ship|Q-Ship]], and Parker uses it during the [[Infinity War (event)|Battle of Titan]], the [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]], and a local charity event. The suit was then confiscated by the Department of Damage Control, but Parker eventually got it back and used it to find an MIT administrator, and later to battle [[Otto Octavius (film character)|Otto Octavius]]. For the suit's first appearance at the end of ''[[Spider-Man: Homecoming]]'', [[Framestore]] created models and textures in anticipation for future MCU projects, while Trixter created the "clean, high tech" vault that the suit appears in.<ref name="HomecomingArtofVFX" /> ** The '''{{visible anchor|Rescue armor}}'''<ref name="EndgameScript" /> (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Rescue armor|of the same name]]), also known as the '''Iron Man Mark XLIX armor''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Fallon |first=Sean |date=May 24, 2019 |title=Hot Toys' Avengers: Endgame Pepper Potts Rescue Suit Figure Unveiled |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/hot-toys-avengers-endgame-pepper-pots-rescue-suit-figure/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210523173242/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/hot-toys-avengers-endgame-pepper-pots-rescue-suit-figure/ |archive-date=May 23, 2021 |access-date=2021-05-23 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> is an armored nanotech suit created by Tony Stark for his wife, [[Pepper Potts (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pepper Potts]]. It features a blue and silver color scheme, and many of the same abilities as [[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Iron Man's armor]]. Potts uses it in the [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]] against [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] and his forces.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cardona |first=Ian |date=April 10, 2020 |title=The MCU's Strongest Armor Is NOT the Hulkbuster – Or Even an Iron Man Suit |url=https://www.cbr.com/avengers-endgae-mcu-strongest-armor-rescue/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200420000524/https://www.cbr.com/avengers-endgae-mcu-strongest-armor-rescue/ |archive-date=April 20, 2020 |access-date=April 18, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> ** The '''[[War Machine armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|War Machine armor]]''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[War Machine armor|of the same name]]) is a powered suit of armor originally developed by Tony Stark as the '''Iron Man Mark II armor''' before it is confiscated by [[James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|James Rhodes]] and enhanced by [[Justin Hammer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Justin Hammer]] with machine guns in the wrists, a minigun on the right shoulder and a grenade launcher on the left, weapons which later proved to be ineffective. Stark later removes the modifications and rebuilds the suit himself using his own superior technology. This upgraded suit is briefly given a red, white, and blue color scheme and renamed the '''[[Iron Patriot]]''' by the U.S. government. It is later changed back to the gray color scheme and upgraded again, but accidentally disabled by the [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]] mid-flight during the [[Avengers Civil War]], causing Rhodes to crash and become [[Paralysis|paralyzed]]. For the [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]], Rhodes dons a new suit reminiscent of the original Iron Patriot armor, featuring multiple advanced weapons such as rocket launchers. * The '''{{visible anchor|Spider-Man suit}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics suit [[Spider-Man suit|of the same name]]) is a suit worn by [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]] while fighting crime as the [[vigilante]] known as Spider-Man. His first suit, a simple homemade costume, consisted of a red hoodie, blue pants, a blue shirt, a red mask with black goggles, and homemade Web-shooters. After Tony Stark recruits him during the [[Avengers Civil War]], he is given a new, more advanced suit, featuring a more modern and streamlined design, [[Karen (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|a built-in AI]], improved goggles, a [[remote drone]], a holographic interface, a parachute, a tracking device, a heater, an airbag, and more advanced Web-shooters.<ref name="IGNSpideySuit">{{Cite web |last=Collura |first=Scott |date=April 3, 2017 |title=Spider-Man's Costume: Even More Tech Secrets Revealed |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2017/04/03/spider-mans-costume-even-more-tech-secrets-revealed |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170403171049/https://www.ign.com/articles/2017/04/03/spider-mans-costume-even-more-tech-secrets-revealed |archive-date=April 3, 2017 |access-date=July 28, 2021 |website=[[IGN]]}}</ref> [[Joe Russo (director)|Joe Russo]] described this suit as "a slightly more traditional, [[Steve Ditko]] influenced suit",<ref>{{Cite web |last=Thompson |first=Simon |date=March 16, 2016 |title=Russo Brothers Talk 'Captain America: Civil War', Their Plans for Spider-Man And Post-Credits Scenes |url=https://www.forbes.com/sites/simonthompson/2016/03/16/russo-brothers-talk-captain-america-civil-war-their-plans-for-spider-man-and-post-credits-scenes/4/#762ade235b11 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160320202006/https://www.forbes.com/sites/simonthompson/2016/03/16/russo-brothers-talk-captain-america-civil-war-their-plans-for-spider-man-and-post-credits-scenes/4/ |archive-date=March 20, 2016 |access-date=March 16, 2016 |website=[[Forbes]]}}</ref> while ''Spider-Man: Homecoming'' co-producer Eric Hauserman Carroll noted that Marvel intentionally included many "fun and wacky" features from the comics in the suit.<ref name="IGNSpideySuit" /> He ceases to use this suit during the [[Infinity War (event)|Infinity War]], instead using the [[Iron Spider (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Iron Spider armor]], which offers more protection and abilities. In an effort to conceal Spider-Man's identity, [[Talos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Talos]] (disguised as [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]]) has a seamstress make Peter a new stealth suit in Europe, later dubbed the "Night Monkey" suit by [[Ned Leeds (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ned Leeds]]. This version is entirely black in color, with the hood consisting of a black mask and flip-up goggles. After the suit is stolen by a [[prison warden]], Peter builds himself a new one using the late Stark's technology, which he uses during his battle against [[Mysterio (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mysterio]] in London. Upon the arrival of multiple villains from alternate realities due to [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]]'s miscasting of a spell intended to erase the world's knowledge of his identity as Spider-Man, Parker battles a displaced [[Otto Octavius (film character)|Otto Octavius]], who damages the Iron Spider armor and forces Parker to use his defaced red-and-black suit inside-out; after Octavius is cured, he returns the nanotechnology which his tentacles had absorbed onto Parker, embellishing it into a more advanced suit. Later, after Strange erases the world's knowledge of Parker's existence, reconcealing his secret identity as Spider-Man, Parker once again dons a brand new red-and-blue suit stitched from homemade materials in his apartment.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Schedeen |first=Jesse |date=December 23, 2021 |title=Spider-Man: No Way Home's New Costumes Explained |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/spider-man-no-way-home-costume-integrated-suit |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211223170521/https://www.ign.com/articles/spider-man-no-way-home-costume-integrated-suit |archive-date=December 23, 2021 |access-date=December 29, 2021 |website=[[IGN]]}}</ref> Trixter provided visual effects for the Stark suit and the spider drone in ''Spider-Man: Homecoming'', and also applied a rigging, muscle and cloth system to [[Sony Pictures Imageworks]]' homemade suit to "mimic the appearance of the rather loose training suit".<ref name="HomecomingArtofVFX" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Thanos' armor}}''' is a suit of armor worn by Thanos during his time as a warlord. It consists of a helmet, breastplate, greaves, cuisses, gauntlets, and metal boots. He discards the armor following his attack on the ''[[Statesman (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Statesman]]'', and uses it as a [[scarecrow]] after he completes his mission and retires to the [[#Garden|Garden]]. An alternate version of Thanos from 2014 wears the armor during the Battle of Earth, during which the armor is heavily damaged by [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda Maximoff]]. The armor is eventually destroyed by Tony Stark. Gamora kills Thanos in an alternate reality before seizing his armor and blade.<ref name="GamoraThanos">{{Cite web |last=Collington |first=Faefyx |date=October 7, 2021 |title=Why Marvel Gives Gamora Thanos' Endgame Armor & Weapon |url=https://screenrant.com/what-if-gamora-thanos-endgame-armor-sword-reason/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211008182727/https://screenrant.com/what-if-gamora-thanos-endgame-armor-sword-reason/ |archive-date=October 8, 2021 |access-date=October 9, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Time Suits}}''',<ref>{{Cite news |last=Wilding |first=Josh |date=November 13, 2019 |title=Avengers: Endgame Disney+ Deleted Scenes Include Katherine Langford, Alternate Black Widow Death, And More |url=https://www.comicbookmovie.com/avengers/avengers_endgame/avengers-endgame-disney-deleted-scenes-include-katherine-langford-alternate-black-widow-death-and-more-a171597 |url-status=live |access-date=March 24, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210703042852/https://www.comicbookmovie.com/avengers/avengers_endgame/avengers-endgame-disney-deleted-scenes-include-katherine-langford-alternate-black-widow-death-and-more-a171597 |archive-date=July 3, 2021 |website=Comic Book Movie}}</ref>{{better source needed|date=September 2021}} also known as the '''Advanced Tech Suits'''<ref>{{Cite web |last=Schmidt |first=Joe |date=February 17, 2019 |title='Avengers: Endgame': New Costumes Finally Have a Name |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/avengers-endgame-new-costumes-finally-have-a-name/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210703042930/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/avengers-endgame-new-costumes-finally-have-a-name/ |archive-date=July 3, 2021 |access-date=March 24, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> or '''Quantum Suits''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Frei |first=Vincent |date=May 13, 2019 |title=AVENGERS – ENDGAME: Dan DeLeeuw – Overall VFX Supervisor – Marvel Studios |url=https://www.artofvfx.com/avengers-endgame-dan-deleeuw-overall-vfx-supervisor-marvel-studios/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210119060920/https://www.artofvfx.com/avengers-endgame-dan-deleeuw-overall-vfx-supervisor-marvel-studios/ |archive-date=January 19, 2021 |access-date=March 24, 2021 |website=The Art of VFX}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Thomas |date=September 9, 2022 |title=D23 Expo: Live Updates on Future Experiences Coming to Disney Parks and Beyond |url=https://disneyparks.disney.go.com/blog/2022/09/d23-expo-tune-in-sunday-for-live-updates-on-future-experiences-coming-to-disney-parks-and-beyond/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220909153423/https://disneyparks.disney.go.com/blog/2022/09/d23-expo-tune-in-sunday-for-live-updates-on-future-experiences-coming-to-disney-parks-and-beyond/ |archive-date=September 9, 2022 |access-date=September 14, 2022 |publisher=[[Disney Parks]]}}</ref> are a variation of the [[Ant-Man suit (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ant-Man suit]], allowing the Avengers to shrink down to microscopic size and travel back in time through the [[Quantum Realm]]. They are used by the surviving Avengers and [[Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Guardians of the Galaxy]]. The suits as depicted in ''[[Avengers: Endgame]]'' were entirely digital creations, and were designed by [[Marvel Studios]] head of visual development [[Ryan Meinerding]] as a combination of Ant-Man, Iron Man, and the Guardians' technologies.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Haasch |first=Palmer |date=August 5, 2019 |title=Avengers: Endgame's time travel suits, and MCU canon, challenged the design team |url=https://www.polygon.com/2019/8/5/20752064/avengers-endgame-time-travel-suits-design |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190805182010/https://www.polygon.com/2019/8/5/20752064/avengers-endgame-time-travel-suits-design |archive-date=August 5, 2019 |access-date=January 3, 2020 |website=[[Polygon (website)|Polygon]]}}</ref>
===Weapons=== * '''{{visible anchor|Bucky Barnes' prosthetic arm}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Winter Soldier's arm|of the same name]]) is a metal [[prosthesis]] worn on the left arm by [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]]. The original arm is created by [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]] after Barnes falls off a train during [[World War II]], and contains a [[red star]] on the shoulder. In 2016, the original arm is destroyed by Tony Stark during the [[Avengers Civil War]]. In 2018, a new [[Vibranium (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|vibranium]] arm is created by [[Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Shuri]] and contains a fail-safe that removes it. In 2025, the arm is stolen and given to [[Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Rocket Raccoon]]. Visual effects for the arm were completed by [[Luma Pictures]] in ''[[Captain America: Civil War]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Seymour |first=Mike |date=May 24, 2016 |title=Captain America: a very civil war |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/captain-america-a-very-civil-war/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160618192021/https://www.fxguide.com/featured/captain-america-a-very-civil-war/ |archive-date=June 18, 2016 |access-date=July 21, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref> [[File:Sebastian Stan & Anthony Mackie (48469219356).jpg|thumb|right|The shield, as depicted in the MCU, being held by [[Anthony Mackie]] and [[Sebastian Stan]], who portray [[Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sam Wilson]] and [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]] respectively, at the 2019 [[San Diego Comic-Con]]]] * '''[[Captain America's shield (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Captain America's shield]]''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Captain America's shield|of the same name]]) is a weapon made of vibranium used by the bearers of the [[Captain America]] mantle, including Steve Rogers, [[John Walker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|John Walker]], and [[Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sam Wilson]]. It is created by [[Howard Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Howard Stark]] and given to Rogers during World War II. The shield is destroyed during the [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]]. After Rogers' retirement, an alternate version of the shield is given by Wilson to the Smithsonian, but the government passes it to John Walker, who uses it to murder a [[Flag Smashers|Flag Smasher]]. After he is stripped away of his title as Captain America, John Walker creates a new homemade shield from [[scrap metal]] and his [[Medal of Honor]], which he later abandons in New York City.<ref>{{Cite web |date=April 16, 2021 |title=Falcon and Winter Soldier Finally Has an End-Credits Scene – and It's Chilling |url=https://www.cbr.com/falcon-winter-soldier-episode-5-post-credits-iron-man/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210424164231/https://www.cbr.com/falcon-winter-soldier-episode-5-post-credits-iron-man/ |archive-date=April 24, 2021 |access-date=April 24, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> The shield is seen as a symbol of Captain America's strength and legacy.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Captain America (Steve Rogers) On Screen Full Report |url=https://www.marvel.com/characters/captain-america-steve-rogers/on-screen |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210423014247/https://www.marvel.com/characters/captain-america-steve-rogers/on-screen |archive-date=April 23, 2021 |access-date=March 29, 2021 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref> A replica of the shield also appears in ''[[Iron Man (2008 film)|Iron Man]]'' and ''[[Iron Man 2]]'', which director [[Jon Favreau]] included because he felt it was important to include [[in-joke|inside references]] for fans of the comics.<ref name="EasterEggs">{{Cite web |last=Vespe |first=Eric |date=October 29, 2008 |title=Part 2 of Quint's interview with Jon Favreau! Iron Man 2, Stark's alcoholism, Empire Strikes Back and The Avengers! |url=https://www.aintitcool.com/node/38906 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181116172846/https://www.aintitcool.com/node/38906 |archive-date=November 16, 2018 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[Ain't It Cool News]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Chitauri guns}}''' were the primary weapons used by the [[Chitauri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Chitauri]] during the [[Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of New York]].
