Many of these guns were abandoned on Earth and salvaged by various humans in attempts to reverse-engineer them. * The '''{{visible anchor|Destroyer}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Destroyer (Thor)|of the same name]]) is an [[automaton]] used by [[Odin (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Odin]] to stop threats such as the [[Frost Giants (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Frost Giants]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Weintraub |first=Steve |date=April 29, 2010 |title=Has the First Image of THE DESTROYER From Marvel's THOR Leaked? |url=https://collider.com/has-the-first-image-of-the-destroyer-from-marvels-thor-leaked/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200105081842/https://collider.com/has-the-first-image-of-the-destroyer-from-marvels-thor-leaked/ |archive-date=January 5, 2020 |access-date=November 24, 2019 |website=[[Collider (website)|Collider]]}}</ref> [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]] later uses it to attack [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]] on Earth before Thor regains his powers and kills the Destroyer.<ref name="HelaDestroyer">{{Cite web |last=Dolloff |first=Matt |date=November 11, 2017 |title=Hela Originally Battled The Destroyer in Thor: Ragnarok |url=https://screenrant.com/hela-destroyer-thor-ragnarok/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190203210512/https://screenrant.com/hela-destroyer-thor-ragnarok/ |archive-date=February 3, 2019 |access-date=November 24, 2019 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=July 26, 2017 |title=Every MCU Movie Villain Ranked Worst To Best |url=https://www.looper.com/77198/every-mcu-movie-villain-ranked-worst-best/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191213025802/https://www.looper.com/77198/every-mcu-movie-villain-ranked-worst-best/ |archive-date=December 13, 2019 |access-date=March 21, 2021 |website=[[Looper (website)|Looper]]}}</ref> Later, parts of it were reassembled by [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] agents into a prototype gun which was later used by [[Phil Coulson]] in the film ''[[The Avengers (2012 film)|The Avengers]]'' and the ABC series ''[[Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kelly |first=Autumn Noel |date=March 9, 2018 |title='Avengers: Infinity War' is time for Coulson's revenge |url=https://www.newsweek.com/avengers-infinity-war-coulson-loki-shield-100-episode-839331 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190328163025/https://www.newsweek.com/avengers-infinity-war-coulson-loki-shield-100-episode-839331 |archive-date=March 28, 2019 |access-date=November 24, 2019 |website=[[Newsweek]]}}</ref> In an early version of the screenplay for ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'', [[Hela (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hela]] easily defeats the Destroyer, but this idea was abandoned.<ref name="HelaDestroyer" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Extremis}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Extremis virus|of the same name]]) is a form of genetic manipulation developed by [[Maya Hansen (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Maya Hansen]]. It gives a person an advanced healing factor, meaning that they are able to regenerate from injury, [[deformities]], and [[psychological trauma]], as well as the ability to generate fire. [[Aldrich Killian (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Aldrich Killian]] uses it to heal his weak physique and cure injured war veterans such as [[Coldblood#In other media|Eric Savin]] and [[Ellen Brandt]]. Later, Extremis is a part of the Centipede Serum with HYDRA figuring out how to stabilize it using the abnormal [[blood platelet]]s of pyrokinetic Chan Ho Yin. The rebel [[Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Skrull]] [[Gravik]] uses Extremis as part of his [[Super-Skrull]] program, and uses it on himself, as he heals himself after being stabbed in the hand by [[Talos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Talos]] and he quickly heals from being shot several times by Nick Fury, including from having part of his face blown off. [[G'iah]] also secretly uses it while working undercover in Gravik's organization which allows her to survive Gravik's attempt to kill her when G'iah is exposed as a spy. During the final battle between the two Super-Skrulls, Extremis repeatedly heals their injuries until G'iah delivers a fatal wound that even Extremis can't heal using [[Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Captain Marvel]]'s powers. * '''{{visible anchor|Gungnir}}''' (based on the Norse mythological object [[Gungnir|of the same name]]) was Odin's spear, capable of channelling the [[#Odinforce|Odinforce]]. It has also been used by Loki and Thor. The spear was presumably destroyed during [[Ragnarok (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ragnarök]]. * The '''{{visible anchor|Hammer Drones}}''' were [[Remotely controlled robot|remotely-controlled]] humanoid [[robot|drones]] designed by [[Ivan Vanko (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ivan Vanko]] and commissioned by [[Justin Hammer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Justin Hammer]] following his previous failed attempts to recreate the [[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Iron Man armor]]. They were designed for use by various branches of the military, with Hammer hoping that they would replace Iron Man. However, Vanko secretly takes control of the drones and used them to wreak havoc at the [[Stark Expo]], though they are ultimately defeated by Tony Stark and [[James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|James Rhodes]] and destroyed by Vanko. * '''{{visible anchor|Hawkeye's bow and quiver}}''' are a pair of tools used by [[Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Clint Barton]] that serve as his primary weapons. The bow is a collapsible [[recurve bow]], whilst the quiver is mechanized, able to store and deploy his signature trick arrows. After [[the Blip]], he swaps his bow for a [[katana]] which he uses to murder criminals such as the Japanese [[Yakuza]]. * '''{{visible anchor|Hofund}}''' (based on the Norse mythological object [[Hǫfuð|of the same name]]), also known as the '''Bifröst Sword''', is a magical sword used by [[Heimdall (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Heimdall]] (and, during his exile, [[Skurge (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Skurge]]) that is able to channel the [[Bifrost (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bifröst]]. It also served as the key to activate the Bifröst. It is last used by Heimdall to transport the [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hulk]] to Earth before he is killed by [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]], and was presumably destroyed along with the ''[[Statesman (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Statesman]]''. [[File:Infinity Gauntlet - 2018 Atlanta Comic Con.jpg|thumb|right|A model of the Infinity Gauntlet at the 2018 [[Atlanta]] Comic-Con]] * The '''[[Infinity Gauntlet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Infinity Gauntlet]]''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[The Infinity Gauntlet (weapon)|of the same name]]) is a left-handed metal gauntlet owned by [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] and forged from [[#Uru|uru]] by [[Eitri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Eitri]] and the Dwarves of [[#Nidavellir|Nidavellir]]. It is capable of harnessing the power of all six [[Infinity Stones]] at once, thus making the wearer able to do anything in their imagination. A replica of the Gauntlet is also kept by Odin in [[#Odin's vault|his vault]] on Asgard, which originally appeared in ''[[Thor (film)|Thor]]'' as an [[Easter egg (media)|Easter egg]] before Marvel Studios realized that it could not be the actual one and formulated an internal theory that the gauntlet was a fake, which led to a scene in ''Thor: Ragnarok'' where Hela declares it fake.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sciretta |first=Peter |date=November 6, 2017 |title=Kevin Feige Answers Your Lingering 'Thor: Ragnarok' Spoiler Questions |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/thor-ragnarok-spoiler-questions/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171107110118/http://www.slashfilm.com/thor-ragnarok-spoiler-questions/ |archive-date=November 7, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Jericho}}''' is an experimental [[guided missile]] developed by Stark Industries for the [[United States Armed Forces]] that can separate into 16 smaller missiles when launched. At a demonstration for the weapon in [[Afghanistan]], Tony Stark's convoy is ambushed, and he is captured by the [[Ten Rings (organization)|Ten Rings]], who forces him to build the missile for them. However, Stark secretly builds the first [[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Iron Man armor]] and escapes. * '''{{visible anchor|Loki's scepter|Scepter}}''', also known as the '''Chitauri Scepter'''<ref name="PreviouslyOnScript" /> or simply as the '''Scepter''', is a bladed weapon with an extendable handle given as a gift to Loki by [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]]. It has a blue gem at the top containing the [[Mind Stone]], allowing Loki to [[brainwash]] and mind control others by touching them with it. After the [[Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of New York]], it is taken by [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]] agents disguised as [[S.T.R.I.K.E. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.T.R.I.K.E.]] team agents and used by [[Wolfgang von Strucker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Strucker]] and Dr. [[List (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|List]] to unlock and amplify [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda]] and [[Pietro Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pietro Maximoff]]'s abilities. It is later recaptured by the Avengers and used by Tony Stark and Bruce Banner to create [[Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ultron]]. Ultron then uses it to brainwash [[Helen Cho]], who in turn creates the [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]], with the gem becoming embedded in his forehead. During the [[Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Heist]], [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]] uses his knowledge of the future to gain the Scepter from the S.T.R.I.K.E. team before using it to brainwash an alternate version of himself. [[File:Natalie Portman by Gage Skidmore.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Mjölnir, as depicted in the Marvel Cinematic Universe, being held by [[Natalie Portman]], who portrays [[Jane Foster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Jane Foster]], at the 2019 [[San Diego Comic-Con]]]] * '''[[Mjolnir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mjölnir]]''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Mjolnir (comics)|of the same name]]) is an enchanted hammer owned by Thor (and previously, Hela) and made of uru by the Dwarves of Nidavellir that is capable of controlling lightning and allows the user to fly if it is spun rapidly and released with enough power. Before Thor is banished to Earth, Odin enchants the hammer so that only those deemed "worthy" would be able to wield it and be granted the power of Thor, which include Vision and Steve Rogers. An alternate version of the hammer is acquired by Thor during the [[Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Heist]], and is later returned to its original timeline by Rogers. The hammer is also used by [[Jane Foster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Jane Foster]] when she becomes the Mighty Thor in ''[[Thor: Love and Thunder]]''. * The '''{{visible anchor|Nano Gauntlet}}''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Fallon |first=Sean |date=May 9, 2019 |title=Hot Toys' Avengers: Endgame Life-Size Nano Gauntlet Unveiled |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/hot-toys-avengers-endgame-life-size-nano-gauntlet/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210503213540/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/hot-toys-avengers-endgame-life-size-nano-gauntlet/ |archive-date=May 3, 2021 |access-date=May 3, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> also known as the '''Iron Gauntlet'''<ref>{{Cite web |last=Rothman |first=Michael |date=April 27, 2019 |title='Avengers: Endgame': The girl-power moment everyone is talking about and more |url=https://www.goodmorningamerica.com/culture/story/avengers-endgame-girl-power-moment-talking-62635055 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210503213542/https://www.goodmorningamerica.com/culture/story/avengers-endgame-girl-power-moment-talking-62635055 |archive-date=May 3, 2021 |access-date=May 3, 2021 |website=[[Good Morning America]]}}</ref> or the '''Power Gauntlet''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Macy |first=Seth G. |date=June 7, 2019 |title=Where to Preorder the Marvel Legends Series Avengers Endgame Iron Man Power Gauntlet – IGN |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2019/06/07/where-to-preorder-the-marvel-legends-series-avengers-endgame-power-gauntlet |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210414170441/https://www.ign.com/articles/2019/06/07/where-to-preorder-the-marvel-legends-series-avengers-endgame-power-gauntlet |access-date=April 14, 2021 |archive-date=April 14, 2021 |url-status=live |website=[[IGN]]}}</ref> is a right-handed metal gauntlet created by Tony Stark, Bruce Banner, and [[Rocket (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Rocket]] using Stark nanotechnology. It was designed to harness the power of the Infinity Stones akin to the [[Infinity Gauntlet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Infinity Gauntlet]] and created to reverse [[the Blip]]. After the Avengers retrieve alternate versions of the six Infinity Stones during the Time Heist, Smart Hulk uses it to snap his fingers and resurrect the lives of half the Universe before an alternate version of Thanos from 2014 arrives and attempts to acquire the Gauntlet for himself. During the subsequent [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]], the gauntlet is passed around multiple individuals before ending up in the hands of Thanos, but the Stones are secretly removed by Tony Stark, who snaps his fingers and disintegrates Thanos and his army. * A '''{{visible anchor|Widow's Veil}}''',<ref name="WidowsVeil">{{Cite web |last=Freitag |first=Lee |date=July 19, 2023 |title=Secret Invasion Brings Back a Popular Black Widow Gadget |url=https://www.cbr.com/secret-invasion-black-widow-gadget-episode-5/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230719182832/https://www.cbr.com/secret-invasion-black-widow-gadget-episode-5/ |archive-date=July 19, 2023 |access-date=July 20, 2023 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> also known as a '''nano mask'''<ref>{{Cite web |last=Weseman |first=Lisa |date=October 15, 2014 |title=Behind the Scenes of May's Kick-Ass Fight...with Herself |url=https://abc.com/shows/marvels-agents-of-shield/news/fan-hq/20141014-behind-the-scenes-of-mays-kick-ass-fight-costume |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200929090114/https://abc.com/shows/marvels-agents-of-shield/news/fan-hq/20141014-behind-the-scenes-of-mays-kick-ass-fight-costume |archive-date=September 29, 2020 |access-date=April 24, 2021 |website=[[ABC Signature]]}}</ref> is a device used by S.H.I.E.L.D. agents to impersonate others. Designed by a [[University of California, Berkeley]] graduate named Selwyn, the mask is capable of imitating one's appearance and voice to create a disguise. It has been used by Natasha Romanoff, [[Sunil Bakshi]], [[Kara Palamas]], [[Melina Vostokoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Melina Vostokoff]], [[Sharon Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sharon Carter]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Tallerico |first=Brian |date=April 23, 2021 |title=The Falcon and the Winter Soldier Finale Recap: All-New Captain America |url=https://www.vulture.com/article/the-falcon-and-the-winter-soldier-episode-6-finale-recap-one-world-one-people.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210423155507/https://www.vulture.com/article/the-falcon-and-the-winter-soldier-episode-6-finale-recap-one-world-one-people.html |archive-date=April 23, 2021 |access-date=April 24, 2021 |website=[[Vulture (magazine)|Vulture]]}}</ref> and [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]].<ref name="WidowsVeil" /> * The '''{{visible anchor|Necroswords}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[All-Black the Necrosword|of the same name]]) were obsidian swords generated and handled by the Asgardian goddess of death, [[Hela (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hela]], powered with the necro-energy that she transforms from the power she draws from [[Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgard]]. In ''[[Thor: Love and Thunder]]'', the necrosword a powerful weapon wielded by the Dark Shadow Lord and later by [[Gorr (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Gorr the God Butcher]]. Gorr obtained the sword from the deceased Shadow Lord and used it to slay his first god. * The '''{{visible anchor|Quad Blasters}}''' are [[Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Quill]]'s primary weapons. The blasters have two separate triggers controlling two separate barrels, which are fired using the index and middle finger. The bottom barrel of each gun fires non-lethal electric shots, while the top barrel fires lethal plasma shots. [[Property master|Prop master]] Russell Bobbitt created two sets of the blasters for ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2]]'', which contained removable blaster cartridges.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sciretta |first=Peter |date=February 6, 2017 |title='Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2': Everything We Learned While on Set – Page 3 |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2-set-visit/3/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170206233402/http://www.slashfilm.com/guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2-set-visit/3/ |archive-date=February 6, 2017 |access-date=July 23, 2021 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Reset charges}}''' are contraptions used by the [[Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Variance Authority]] (TVA) to "prune" alternate timelines, erasing them from existence to preserve the Sacred Timeline. [[Michael Waldron]], the [[head writer]] of ''[[Loki (TV series)|Loki]]'', said the charges use magic "or perhaps something a bit more technical", and that the audience is "kind of in the dark with what is exactly is going on with these reset charges".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chitwood |first=Adam |date=June 16, 2021 |title='Loki': What Are Those Reset Charges and What Do They Do? |url=https://collider.com/loki-reset-charges-explained/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210625123202/https://collider.com/loki-reset-charges-explained/ |archive-date=June 25, 2021 |access-date=June 25, 2021 |website=[[Collider (website)|Colldier]]}}</ref> They are later used by [[Sylvie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sylvie]] to "bomb" the Sacred Timeline.<ref name="LokiEp2">{{Cite magazine |last=Morgan |first=Lauren |date=June 16, 2021 |title=Loki recap: Double trouble |url=https://ew.com/tv/recaps/loki-season-1-episode-2/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210616120824/https://ew.com/tv/recaps/loki-season-1-episode-2/ |archive-date=June 16, 2021 |access-date=June 18, 2021 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Siede |first=Caroline |date=June 16, 2021 |title=Loki's second episode is a buddy show in more ways than one |url=https://www.avclub.com/loki-s-second-episode-is-a-buddy-show-in-more-ways-than-1847094961 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210616222718/https://www.avclub.com/loki-s-second-episode-is-a-buddy-show-in-more-ways-than-1847094961 |archive-date=June 16, 2021 |access-date=June 16, 2021 |website=[[The A.V. Club]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Welch |first=Andy |date=June 16, 2021 |title=Loki episode two recap: did the god of mischief win the Tour de France? |url=https://www.theguardian.com/tv-and-radio/2021/jun/16/loki-episode-two-recap-did-the-god-of-mischief-win-the-tour-de-france |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210616222718/https://www.theguardian.com/tv-and-radio/2021/jun/16/loki-episode-two-recap-did-the-god-of-mischief-win-the-tour-de-france |archive-date=June 16, 2021 |access-date=June 17, 2021 |website=[[The Guardian]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Shocker's gauntlet}}''' is a mechanical weapon originally owned by [[Brock Rumlow (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Brock Rumlow]] and ripped off by Steve Rogers in [[Lagos]]. The gauntlet is then recovered by the [[Damage Control (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Department of Damage Control]] and stolen by [[Adrian Toomes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Adrian Toomes]]. [[Phineas Mason (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Phineas Mason]] modifies the gauntlet before passing it to [[Jackson Brice (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Jackson Brice]], who uses the gauntlet while calling himself the "Shocker". After Brice is disintegrated, it is used by [[Herman Schultz (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Herman Schultz]] until his defeat at the hands of [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Spider-Man]]. Visual effects for the gauntlet were provided by Trixter in ''[[Spider-Man: Homecoming]]''.<ref name="HomecomingArtofVFX" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Shuri's gauntlets}}''' are a pair of vibranium [[gauntlet (glove)|gauntlets]] designed and used by [[Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Shuri]]. Shaped like a [[Black panther|panther]]'s head, they emit a powerful sonic blast capable of subduing a Black Panther. They are ultimately destroyed by [[Killmonger (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Killmonger]]. After Killmonger's demise at the hands of her brother, [[T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|T'Challa]], Shuri designs a second pair which she uses during the [[Infinity War (event)|Battle of Wakanda]] and the Battle of Earth. * {{anchor|Stombreaker}}'''[[Stormbreaker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stormbreaker]]''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Stormbreaker (comics)|of the same name]]) is a large battle axe made of uru and forged by Thor and the dwarf king Eitri. The weapon, meant to be the most powerful in the Asgardian king's arsenal, has powers similar to Mjölnir and is also capable of summoning the Bifröst. Unlike Mjölnir, Stormbreaker has no worthiness enchantment, allowing anyone to wield it. Thor nearly dies trying to create it, but before its completion, so [[Groot (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Groot]] cuts off his own arm to hastily finish Stormbreaker and then Thor heals himself with the completed Stormbreaker. Thor then uses it to defeat the Outriders in Wakanda, attack Thanos, kill him on the [[#Garden|Garden]], and during the Battle of Earth.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McMillan |first=Graeme |date=April 29, 2018 |title=How Thor's Big 'Avengers' Moment Played Out in the Comics |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/heat-vision/avengers-infinity-war-a-guide-thors-axe-stormbreaker-1105983 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210115220005/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/heat-vision/avengers-infinity-war-a-guide-thors-axe-stormbreaker-1105983 |archive-date=January 15, 2021 |access-date=March 28, 2021 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Ten Rings|Ten rings}}''' (based on the [[Mandarin's rings]] from the Marvel Comics) are a set of ten mystical [[iron rings]] used by [[Wenwu]] and [[Shang-Chi (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Shang-Chi]],<ref name="TenRingsSR">{{Cite web |last=Atkinson |first=John |date=June 25, 2021 |title=How The Ten Rings' Powers Work In The MCU & Marvel Comics |url=https://screenrant.com/shang-chi-ten-rings-bracelet-powers-mcu-comics-explained/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210629183554/https://screenrant.com/shang-chi-ten-rings-bracelet-powers-mcu-comics-explained/ |archive-date=June 29, 2021 |access-date=August 20, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> which provide the namesake and emblem for the criminal organization [[Ten Rings (organization)|of the same name]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lovett |first=Jamie |date=April 20, 2021 |title=Shang-Chi Trailer Reveals Major Change to the Ten Rings |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/shang-chi-trailer-mandarin-power-rings-changes/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210420130611/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/shang-chi-trailer-mandarin-power-rings-changes/ |archive-date=April 20, 2021 |access-date=August 20, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> The Rings grant their user enhanced strength and longevity, emit concussive energy blasts, and can be telepathically controlled as projectiles and tendrils.<ref name="TenRingsSR" /> The appearance of the aura projected by the rings varies on the user, with Wenwu's resembling violent blue lightning and Shang-Chi's resembling graceful orange flames to reflect their distinct personalities.<ref name="ShangChiIW">{{Cite web |last=Desowitz |first=Bill |date=September 17, 2021 |title='Shang-Chi': How Marvel Created Its First Dragons and the Adorable, Furry Morris |url=https://www.indiewire.com/2021/09/shang-chi-and-the-ten-rings-marvel-visual-effects-1234665539/ |url-status=live producer)|Jonathan Schwartz]], the Rings were changed from being worn on the fingers as rings as in the comics to being worn on the wrists was due to its impracticality and similarity with the Infinity Stones.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Perine |first=Aaron |date=September 1, 2021 |title=Shang-Chi Producer Confirms Infinity Stone Influence on the Ten Rings |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/shang-chi-producer-confirms-infinity-stone-influence-ten-rings/ |url-status=live |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-features/shang-chi-post-credit-scene-cameo-destin-daniel-cretton-1235008302/ |url-status=live 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> Marvel Studios concept artist Jerad S. Marantz also considered making the Rings green.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gelman |first=Samuel |date=April 24, 2022 |title=Shang-Chi Concept Artist Shares Alternate Ten Rings Designs |url=https://www.cbr.com/shang-chi-alternate-ten-rings-designs/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220424144201/https://www.cbr.com/shang-chi-alternate-ten-rings-designs/ |archive-date=April 24, 2022 |access-date=April 25, 2022 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> The Ten Rings were later integrated into mainstream [[Marvel Universe]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=March 29, 2022 |title=The True Power of the Ten Rings Is Unleashed on the Marvel Universe in Gene Luen Yang & Marcus To's New 'Shang-Chi and the Ten Rings' #1 |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/comics/shang-chi-and-the-ten-rings-1-gene-luen-yang-marcus-to-true-power-unleashed |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220330193821/https://www.marvel.com/articles/comics/shang-chi-and-the-ten-rings-1-gene-luen-yang-marcus-to-true-power-unleashed |archive-date=March 30, 2022 |access-date=March 29, 2022 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Thanos' blade}}'''<ref name="EndgameScript" /> is a large double-sided sword used by an alternate version of Thanos from 2014 during the Battle of Earth. Thanos uses it to break [[Captain America's shield (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Captain America's shield]] as well as [[#Luis' van|Luis' van]] before it is destroyed by Wanda Maximoff using her telekinetic powers. The blade's design was based on a helicopter used by Thanos in the comics,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Barnhardt |first=Adam |date=July 22, 2021 |title=Jim Starlin Says Thanos' Avengers: Endgame Weapon Is Inspired By The ThanosCopter |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/avengers-endgame-thanos-sword-inspired-by-thanoscopter-jim-starlin/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210602215633/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/avengers-endgame-thanos-sword-inspired-by-thanoscopter-jim-starlin/ |archive-date=June 2, 2021 |access-date=May 31, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> an [[Easter egg (media)|easter egg]] which Thanos creator [[Jim Starlin]] criticized.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sandwell |first=Ian |date=July 20, 2019 |title=Thanos creator reveals the one thing he'll "never forgive" about Avengers: Endgame |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a28453689/avengers-endgame-thanos-jim-starlin-criticism/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210602215936/https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a28453689/avengers-endgame-thanos-jim-starlin-criticism/ |archive-date=June 2, 2021
