|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210602215502/https://www.cinemablend.com/news/2476953/thanos-creator-hilariously-reveals-why-he-wont-forgive-avengers-endgame |archive-date=June 2, 2021 |access-date=May 31, 2021 |website=[[CinemaBlend]]}}</ref> Gamora kills Thanos in an alternate reality before seizing his armor and blade.<ref name="GamoraThanos" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Time Sticks}}'''<ref>{{Cite web |last=Dinh |first=Christine |date=July 7, 2021 |title=Loki: Episode 5 Event Report |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/loki-episode-5-event-report-recap |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210707152421/https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/loki-episode-5-event-report-recap |archive-date=July 7, 2021 |access-date=July 9, 2021 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref> are [[Baton (law enforcement)|batons]] used by Minutemen of the Time Variance Authority (TVA) to "prune" variants. [[Ravonna Renslayer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ravonna Renslayer]], a former Hunter for the TVA, also wields a baton, which she uses against Loki and Sylvie. When designing the pruning effect for ''Loki'', visual effects vendor [[FuseFX]] sought to differentiate it from [[the Blip]], taking inspiration from the documentary series ''[[Cosmos: A Spacetime Odyssey]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Barnhart |first=Adam |date=August 18, 2021 |title=Loki: How the VFX Team Kept Pruning Separate From Dusting |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/loki-vfx-wanted-pruning-to-stand-out-from-dusting-infinity-war/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210818083437/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/loki-vfx-wanted-pruning-to-stand-out-from-dusting-infinity-war/ |archive-date=August 18, 2021 |access-date=August 20, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Ultron Sentries}}''', also known as the '''Sub-Ultrons''', are a large army of robots that acted as extensions of Ultron. They were created by Ultron using resources from the [[#Hydra Research Base|Hydra Research Base]] in [[Sokovia]] and were directly controlled by him, thus acting as his personal army. They were ultimately destroyed by the Avengers during the [[Battle of Sokovia]]. * '''{{visible anchor|Ulysses Klaue's prosthetic arm}}''' is a [[prosthetic arm]] used by [[Ulysses Klaue (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ulysses Klaue]] after his arm was chopped off by Ultron. Actually a modified [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakandan]] tool used for mining vibranium, it functioned as a [[sonic cannon]], capable of shooting out high-energy blasts powerful enough to destroy a car and temporarily subdue a [[Black Panther suit (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Black Panther]]. The sonic cannon could be retracted and hidden inside the prosthesis when not in use. It was later destroyed by [[T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|T'Challa]] during a skirmish in [[Busan]], Korea. * The '''{{visible anchor|Web-shooters}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Web-shooters|of the same name]]) are a pair of [[electromechanical]] gauntlets developed by Peter Parker for his use as the [[Vigilante|crime-fighter]] known as [[Spider-Man]]. They are capable of shooting synthetic webbing stored in small cartridges on the gauntlets. The first version of the Web-shooters, which were homemade by Parker, are upgraded by Tony Stark before the Avengers Civil War. This version has a variety of different settings, a capability first teased in the [[mid-credits scene]] of ''[[Captain America: Civil War]]''. This was compared by ''Spider-Man: Homecoming'' co-producer Eric Hauserman Carroll to a [[DSLR]] camera.<ref name="Sciretta" /> Visual effects for the synthetic webbing were provided by [[Digital Domain]] and [[Sony Pictures Imageworks]] in ''Spider-Man: Homecoming'', who based the design on [[polar bear]] hair due to its [[translucent]] nature as well as its design in ''Civil War'' and [[Spider-Man in film|previous ''Spider-Man'' films]].<ref name="HomecomingFXGuide">{{Cite web |last=Seymour |first=Mike |date=July 16, 2017 |title=Spider-Man: by Sea and by Air |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/spider-man-by-sea-and-by-air/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170806003756/https://www.fxguide.com/featured/spider-man-by-sea-and-by-air/ |archive-date=August 6, 2017 |access-date=July 28, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref> The [[Iron Spider (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Iron Spider armor]] also features its own Web-shooters, which are more streamlined and technologically advanced. After Stark's death, Peter uses his technology to craft himself a new pair after his old ones are destroyed. * The '''{{visible anchor|Widow's Bite}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Widow's Bite|of the same name]]) is an electroshock weapon used by Natasha Romanoff in combat. Created by S.H.I.E.L.D., it delivers powerful electrical discharges from two gauntlets worn on the wrists. Tony Stark later creates a more powerful version for her, which causes the piping in her suit to light up and glow. It has been used by Romanoff to momentarily disable the [[Bucky Barnes' prosthetic arm (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Winter Soldier's metal arm]] and the [[Black Panther suit (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Black Panther suit]], as well as to attack other Black Widows in the [[Red Room (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Red Room]]. * The '''{{visible anchor|Yaka arrow}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Yaka arrow|of the same name]]) is a sound-sensitive arrow owned by [[Yondu Udonta (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Yondu Udonta]]. Made of Yaka metal by the Centaurians, it is controlled by a red fin worn on Yondu's head combined with his [[whistling]], and is carried in a holster on his belt when not in use. His use of the arrow is extremely skilled, allowing him to accurately control its direction and speed, killing multiple beings within seconds. After Yondu's death, [[Kraglin (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kraglin]] acquires the arrow and a new cybernetic head fin, but struggles to control his arrow due to his lack of experience.
===Artifacts=== * The '''{{visible anchor|Bloodstone}}''' is a red gem owned by the Bloodstone family, first wielded by [[Ulysses Bloodstone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ulysses Bloodstone]]. Following Ulysses' death, his widow [[Verussa Bloodstone|Verussa]] organizes a competition between monster hunters to determine its new wielder.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Webster |first=Andrew |date=October 6, 2022 |title=Marvel's experimental Werewolf by Night is like nothing else in the MCU |url=https://www.theverge.com/23389129/werewolf-by-night-review-marvel-disney-plus |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221006213812/https://www.theverge.com/23389129/werewolf-by-night-review-marvel-disney-plus |archive-date=October 6, 2022 |access-date=October 8, 2022 |website=[[The Verge]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Lussier |first=Germain |date=September 26, 2022 |title=Werewolf By Night Expertly Brings Monsters Into the Marvel Cinematic Universe |url=https://gizmodo.com/werewolf-by-night-review-marvel-disney-plus-horror-stre-1849579625 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220927013514/https://gizmodo.com/werewolf-by-night-review-marvel-disney-plus-horror-stre-1849579625 |archive-date=September 27, 2022 |access-date=September 27, 2022 |website=[[Gizmodo]]}}</ref> Verussa uses the Bloodstone to force [[Jack Russell (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Jack Russell]] into his [[werewolf]] form, who kills her, and the gem is left behind with Verussa and Ulysses' estranged daughter [[Elsa Bloodstone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Elsa]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Adams |first=Timothy |date=October 7, 2022 |title=Werewolf By Night: What Is the Bloodstone? |url=https://comicbook.com/movies/news/werewolf-by-night-bloodstone-explained/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221007203449/https://comicbook.com/movies/news/werewolf-by-night-bloodstone-explained/ |archive-date=October 7, 2022 |access-date=October 15, 2022 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> The Bloodstone is the only object with color for the majority of ''[[Werewolf by Night (TV special)|Werewolf by Night]]'', and grants its wielder increased strength and longevity.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Fienberg |first=Daniel |date=October 6, 2022 |title='Werewolf by Night' Review: Disney+'s Marvel Horror Special Keeps It Refreshingly Simple |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-reviews/werewolf-by-night-disney-marvel-gael-garcia-bernal-1235233567/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221006170139/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-reviews/werewolf-by-night-disney-marvel-gael-garcia-bernal-1235233567/ |archive-date=October 6, 2022 |access-date=October 6, 2022 |website=[[The Hollywood Reporter]]}}</ref> * The '''''{{visible anchor|Book of Cagliostro}}''''' is an ancient spellbook housed in the [[Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ancient One]]'s private library in [[Kamar-Taj (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kamar-Taj]]. The book focuses on [[#Dark magic|dark magic]], causing many students who studied the book to lose their way. [[Kaecilius (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kaecilius]] tears pages out of the book to allow him to perform a ritual to contact [[Dormammu (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dormammu]] and draw energy from the [[#Dark Dimension|Dark Dimension]], extending his life forever. [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]] also studies the book, learning how to use the [[Eye of Agamotto (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Eye of Agamotto]]. * The '''{{visible anchor|Casket of Ancient Winters}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Casket of Ancient Winters|of the same name]]) is a relic owned and used by the [[Frost Giants (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Frost Giants]]. When opened, it projects an icy wind that freezes everything in its path, and can plunging an entire planet into an ice age. The Casket is captured in 965 AD by the [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]], who stored it in [[#Odin's vault|Odin's vault]]. Over a millennium later, Frost Giants attack Asgard, seeking to reclaim the Casket, but are once again defeated. It is presumably destroyed during Ragnarök. * The '''{{visible anchor|Cloak of Levitation}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Cloak of Levitation|of the same name]]) is a magical relic that enables its user to [[Levitation (paranormal)|levitate]]. It is among the relics owned by the Masters of the Mystic Arts stored in the [[New York Sanctum]]. It "chooses" [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]] as its master during a fight with [[Kaecilius (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kaecilius]]. It has a [[Sentience|consciousness of its own]] and is able to move independently and defend Strange against threats. It is used by Strange during the [[Infinity War (event)|Battle of Titan]] and the [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]]. During the battle with the universe-displaced villains, the Cloak saves Ned Leeds' life, apparently of its own volition, when he falls off of the [[Statue of Liberty]]. Visual effects for the artifact were provided by [[Framestore]] in ''[[Doctor Strange (2016 film)|Doctor Strange]]''.<ref name="DSFXGuide" /> * The '''{{visible anchor|Crimson Bands of Cyttorak}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics character [[Cyttorak|of the same name]]) is a magical wooden relic housed in the New York Sanctum. When thrown at an opponent, it restrains them, binding their hands behind their back, with Stephen Strange using it on Kaecilius in ''Doctor Strange''. A second, more comics-accurate version is manifested during Strange's fight with Thanos, a spell that appears as red bands. Visual effects for the original version were provided by [[Framestore]] in ''Doctor Strange''.<ref name="DSFXGuide" /> * The '''''{{visible anchor|Darkhold}}''''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Darkhold|of the same name]]), also known as the '''Book of the Damned''', is a magical [[grimoire]] that corrupts the reader. Containing spells written by the demon Chthon on Mount Wundagore, the book passed through many hands before coming to [[Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Agatha Harkness]], who used it to determine that [[Wanda Maximoff]] is the Scarlet Witch. After defeating Harkness, Maximoff takes the book to study it while in hiding, unaware of the book's evils, and becomes corrupted. After breaking out of the corruption, she destroys Wundagore and every copy of the ''Darkhold'' across the multiverse. The '''''Book of Vishanti''''' serves as the [[antithesis]] to the ''Darkhold''. The ''Darkhold''{{'}}s appearance in ''[[WandaVision]]'' was pitched by co-executive producer [[Mary Livanos]], who felt it would increase the level of danger posed by Harkness to Maximoff.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Amaya |first=Erik |date=March 12, 2021 |title=WandaVision Creative Team Discusses The Darkhold, the Scarlet Witch, and Fan Theories |url=https://editorial.rottentomatoes.com/article/wandavisions-creative-team-discusses-the-darkhold-the-scarlet-witch-and-fan-theories/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210313165720/https://editorial.rottentomatoes.com/article/wandavisions-creative-team-discusses-the-darkhold-the-scarlet-witch-and-fan-theories/ |archive-date=March 13, 2021 |access-date=May 17, 2021 |website=[[Rotten Tomatoes]]}}</ref> A visually distinct iteration of the book appears in the ABC series ''[[Agents of S.H.I.E.L.D.]]'' and the Hulu series ''[[Runaways (TV series)|Runaways]]'', in which it is used by [[Holden Radcliffe (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Holden Radcliffe]] and [[Aida (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Aida]] before [[Robbie Reyes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Robbie Reyes]] takes to the Dark Dimension with [[Morgan le Fay (Marvel Comics)#Television|Morgan le Fay]] later bringing it back to Earth.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bacon |first=Thomas |date=March 7, 2021 |title=WandaVision Just Made Every Marvel TV Show Redundant |url=https://screenrant.com/wandavision-canon-irrelevent-marvel-shows-runaways-agents-shield/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210308053007/https://screenrant.com/wandavision-canon-irrelevent-marvel-shows-runaways-agents-shield/ |archive-date=March 8, 2021 |access-date=March 11, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> While this had not been discussed by the writers of ''WandaVision'',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Davis |first=Brandon |date=March 10, 2021 |title=WandaVision Head Writer Jac Schaeffer On Changes, Endings, and Vision's Beautiful Words (Exclusive) |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/wandavision-writer-jac-schaeffer-ending-explained-vision-interview/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210311001831/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/wandavision-writer-jac-schaeffer-ending-explained-vision-interview/ |archive-date=March 11, 2021 |access-date=March 12, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> director [[Matt Shakman]] stated that he believes they are the same book.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Deckelmeier |first=Joe |date=March 15, 2021 |title=Matt Shakman Interview: WandaVision |url=https://screenrant.com/wandavision-show-director-matt-shakman-interview/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210316010126/https://screenrant.com/wandavision-show-director-matt-shakman-interview/ |archive-date=March 16, 2021 |access-date=March 17, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> The ''Darkhold'' was designed by the props team of ''[[Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness]]'' for ''WandaVision'',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Perine |first=Aaron |date=April 18, 2022 |title=Doctor Strange 2: Marvel Prop Master Details Collaboration on WandaVision's Darkhold Design |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/doctor-strange-in-the-multiverse-of-madness-darkhold-design-wandavision-marvel-prop-master-details-collaboration/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220418202424/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/doctor-strange-in-the-multiverse-of-madness-darkhold-design-wandavision-marvel-prop-master-details-collaboration/ |archive-date=April 18, 2022 |access-date=April 18, 2022 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> and visual effects were created by [[Luma Pictures]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=April 13, 2021 |title=Inside the VFX of 'Wandavision's' The Darkhold |url=http://www.lumapictures.com/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210612125421/https://www.luma.inc/news/wanda-vision-the-darkhold-visual-effects |archive-date=June 12, 2021 |access-date=June 12, 2021 |website=[[Luma Pictures]]}}</ref> * The '''''Macchina di Kadavus''''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bacon |first=Thomas |date=February 6, 2022 |title=No Way Home Easter Egg Hints At Doctor Strange's Most Powerful Relic |url=https://screenrant.com/no-way-home-doctor-strange-crystal-kadavus-easter-egg/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220207095915/https://screenrant.com/no-way-home-doctor-strange-crystal-kadavus-easter-egg/ |archive-date=February 7, 2022 |access-date=March 5, 2022 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> also known as '''The Box''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Mathai |first=Jeremy |date=November 30, 2021 |title=Doctor Strange's Weird Box In Spider-Man: No Way Home Has A Truly Remarkable Name! |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/673375/doctor-stranges-weird-box-in-spider-man-no-way-home-has-a-truly-remarkable-name/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211201082622/https://www.slashfilm.com/673375/doctor-stranges-weird-box-in-spider-man-no-way-home-has-a-truly-remarkable-name/ |archive-date=December 1, 2021 |access-date=March 5, 2022 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> is a relic owned and used by the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts]] to contain potentially hazardous spells. In 2024, it is used by [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]] to contain the corrupted Runes of Kof-Kol, before it is destroyed by [[Norman Osborn (2002 film series character)|Norman Osborn]] with a [[Pumpkin Bomb (comics)|pumpkin bomb]], releasing the spell and creating rifts in the multiverse. * '''{{visible anchor|Sling Rings}}''' are small two-ring mystical artifacts used by the Masters of the Mystic Arts to teleport between locations via an interdimensional portal.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jayson |first=Jay |date=September 6, 2017 |title=Doctor Strange: What Magical Artifacts Are In The Film? |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/doctor-strange-what-artifacts-are-in-the-film/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210509182910/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/doctor-strange-what-artifacts-are-in-the-film-/ |archive-date=May 9, 2021 |access-date=February 25, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> The sling ring is also worn and used by [[Ned Leeds (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ned Leeds]] and Cassandra Nova.
===Infinity Stones=== {{Main|Infinity Stones}} {{See also|Infinity Gems}}
* The '''[[Mind Stone]]''', originally housed in [[Loki's scepter]] and later on [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]]'s forehead, is the Infinity Stone that controlled the aspect of the [[mind]]. It grants the user the ability to [[Brainwashing|control minds]] and give sentience to beings, as well as to project energy blasts. In 2015, [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]] and [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]] use it to learn to create [[Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ultron]], who later fuses the Stone on Vision. Exposure to the Mind Stone also granted [[Pietro Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pietro Maximoff]] superhuman speed and amplified [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda]]'s innate magical abilities. Her connection to the Stone also allows her to create a [[simulacrum]] of Vision and two sons, [[Billy Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Billy]] and [[Tommy Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tommy]]. * The '''[[Power Stone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Power Stone]]''', originally housed in the '''Orb''' and later in [[Ronan the Accuser (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ronan]]'s [[#Cosmi-Rod|Cosmi-Rod]], is the Infinity Stone that controlled the aspect of [[Power (physics)|power]]. It grants the user superhuman strength and durability, and is capable of overpowering [[Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Carol Danvers]]. * The '''[[Reality Stone]]''', originally in the form of the '''Aether''' (based on the classical element [[Aether (classical element)|of the same name]]), was the Infinity Stone that controlled the aspect of reality. It first appears in a fluid-like state, and grants the user the ability to change reality, create illusions, suck the life force out of mortals, disrupt the laws of physics, and repel any threats that it detects. * The '''[[Soul Stone]]''', originally located on the planet [[Vormir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vormir]], is the Infinity Stone that controlled the aspect of the [[soul]]. It grants the user the ability to manipulate living souls, and also contains a pocket dimension called the [[#Soul World|Soul World]]. Uniquely, it has a guard over its location, the Stonekeeper, who guides those through the ritual required to gain it: "a soul for a soul", via a sacrifice. * The '''[[Space Stone]]''', originally housed in the '''Tesseract''' (based on the [[Cosmic Cube]] from the Marvel Comics),<ref>{{Cite web |last=Yamato |first=Jen |date=April 30, 2012 |title=Marvel's Kevin Feige on Avengers, Iron Man 3, Thor 2, Universe-Building, and Elektra |url=http://movieline.com/2012/04/30/kevin-feige-on-avengers-marvel-universe-building-and-the-legacy-of-elektra/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120501233425/http://movieline.com/2012/04/30/kevin-feige-on-avengers-marvel-universe-building-and-the-legacy-of-elektra |archive-date=May 1, 2012 |access-date=May 3, 2012 |website=[[MovieLine]]}}</ref> is the Infinity Stone that controlled the aspect of [[space]]. It grants the user the ability to open wormholes and to travel between places instantaneously, and has been used by [[Red Skull (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Johann Schmidt]], [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]], and [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]]. The energy generated by the Stone is also used by the [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]] to repair the [[Bifrost (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bifröst Bridge]], [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]] and [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] to power weapons, and [[#Project Pegasus|Project Pegasus]] to develop light-speed engines. * The '''[[Time Stone]]''', originally housed in the '''Eye of Agamotto''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Eye of Agamotto|of the same name]]), is the Infinity Stone that controlled the aspect of time. It grants the user the ability to manipulate time and foresee possible futures. It has been used by [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]], the [[Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ancient One]], and Thanos.
===Creatures=== * The '''{{visible anchor|Abilisk}}''' is an interdimensional [[tentacle]]d creature that feeds on [[#Anulax Batteries|Anulax Batteries]], the power source of the [[#Sovereign|Sovereign]] planetary amalgamation. In 2014, the [[Sovereign (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sovereign]] people hire the [[Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Guardians of the Galaxy]] to slay the beast, and the creature is killed when [[Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Gamora]] uses her sword to slice it open.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cotter |first=Padraig |date=April 29, 2021 |title=Guardians Of The Galaxy 2's Monster Fight Explained (& How It Was Defeated) |url=https://screenrant.com/guardians-galaxy-2-movie-abilisk-tentacle-monster/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210429151057/https://screenrant.com/guardians-galaxy-2-movie-abilisk-tentacle-monster/ |archive-date=April 29, 2021 |access-date=July 24, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> In 2026, the Abilisks are revealed to have been created by the [[High Evolutionary (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|High Evolutionary]]. They are seen when [[Mantis (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mantis]], [[Nebula (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nebula]], and [[Drax the Destroyer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Drax]] are thrown in a chamber with them. Mantis calms them down and adopts them, taking them with her on her journey of self discovery. According to [[head writer]] [[A. C. Bradley (screenwriter)|A.C. Bradley]], the tentacled monster seen in the [[What If... Captain Carter Were the First Avenger?|first]] and [[What If... Doctor Strange Lost His Heart Instead of His Hands?|fourth]] episodes of ''[[What If...? (TV series)|What If...?]]'' was inspired by the Abilisk.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Keane |first=Sean |date=August 12, 2021 |title=What If...? episode 1 recap: Captain Carter battles Hydra in retro Marvel adventure |url=https://www.cnet.com/news/what-if-episode-1-recap-captain-carter-battles-hydra-in-retro-marvel-adventure/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210812164013/https://www.cnet.com/news/what-if-episode-1-recap-captain-carter-battles-hydra-in-retro-marvel-adventure/ |archive-date=August 12, 2021 |access-date=September 23, 2021 |website=[[CNET]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Alioth}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics creature [[Alioth (comics)|of the same name]]) is a cloud-like, matter-consuming entity which resides in the [[#Void|Void]] and can devour universes. Alioth was created during the first Multiversal War and was harnessed and weaponized by the [[Kang the Conqueror|Kang]] variant [[He Who Remains (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|He Who Remains]] to end the war. Afterwards, Alioth was tasked with guarding the [[Citadel at the End of Time]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Brooks |first=Nicholas |date=July 17, 2021 |title=Loki: How Was the MCU's Multiversal War Actually Won? |url=https://www.cbr.com/loki-how-he-who-remains-won-multiversal-war/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210717160707/https://www.cbr.com/loki-how-he-who-remains-won-multiversal-war/ |archive-date=July 17, 2021 |access-date=July 20, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Anderson |first=Jenna |date=July 16, 2021 |title=Loki Finale Reveals Truth About MCU's Secret Multiverse War |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/loki-finale-mcu-multiversal-war-kang-the-conqueror/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210716042330/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/loki-finale-mcu-multiversal-war-kang-the-conqueror/ |archive-date=July 16, 2021 |access-date=July 20, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> ''[[Loki (TV series)|Loki]]'' visual development artist Alexander Mandradjiev took inspiration from the anime film ''[[Princess Mononoke]]'' (1997) when designing Alioth.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Barnhardt |first=Adam |date=November 6, 2022 |title=Marvel's Loki Villain Was Inspired by Iconic Studio Ghibli Movie |url=https://comicbook.com/tv-shows/news/loki-villain-alioth-inspired-studio-ghibli-movie-princess-mononoke/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221107102607/https://comicbook.com/tv-shows/news/loki-villain-alioth-inspired-studio-ghibli-movie-princess-mononoke/ |archive-date=November 7, 2022 |access-date=December 9, 2022 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> * '''[[Alligator Loki]]''' is a [[reptile|reptilian]] variant of [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]] which takes the form of an alligator.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Langmann |first=Brady |date=June 30, 2021 |title=Is It Alligator Loki or Crocodile Loki? We Asked Some Scientists to Confirm. |url=https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/tv/a36887068/loki-episode-4-end-credits-scene-alligator-crocodile-kid-boastful/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210630152026/https://www.esquire.com/entertainment/tv/a36887068/loki-episode-4-end-credits-scene-alligator-crocodile-kid-boastful/ |archive-date=June 30, 2021 |access-date=July 24, 2021 |website=[[Esquire (magazine)|Esquire]]}}</ref> It was pruned by the Time Variance Authority (TVA) and banished to the [[#Void|Void]]. The character was included in ''Loki'' "because he's green", according to head writer [[Michael Waldron]], and was doubled by a stuffed alligator during filming to allow actors to interact with it.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Paige |first=Rachel |date=July 7, 2021 |title='Loki': The Glorious Debut of Alligator Loki |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/glorious-alligator-loki |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210707084520/https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/glorious-alligator-loki |archive-date=July 7, 2021 |access-date=July 7, 2021 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Ant-thony}}''', originally codenamed '''#247''', is a [[carpenter ant]] used by [[Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hank Pym]] to spy on [[Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Scott Lang]]. Lang eventually befriends the ant, using it to fly, but it is killed by [[Darren Cross (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Darren Cross]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Truitt |first=Brian |date=November 12, 2015 |title=Peyton Reed's 'doubly excited' for 'Ant-Man' sequel |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/life/movies/2015/11/12/peyton-reed-ant-man-exclusive-clip/75657630/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151112231733/https://www.usatoday.com/story/life/movies/2015/11/12/peyton-reed-ant-man-exclusive-clip/75657630/ |archive-date=November 12, 2015 |access-date=July 24, 2021 |website=[[USA Today]]}}</ref> According to [[Deborah Gordon]], a [[biologist]] at [[Stanford University]], the portrayal of Ant-thony (who is female) in ''[[Ant-Man (film)|Ant-Man]]'' is inaccurate, as queen ants only fly when they are about to reproduce.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Pinchefsky |first=Carol |date=July 21, 2015 |title=A Biologist Reviews the Ants of Ant-Man |url=https://www.syfy.com/syfywire/biologist-reviews-ants-ant-man |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171116015239/https://www.syfy.com/syfywire/biologist-reviews-ants-ant-man |archive-date=November 16, 2017 |access-date=July 24, 2021 |website=[[Syfy Wire]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Blurp}}''' (voiced by [[Dee Bradley Baker]]) is a furry F'saki and the pet of [[Adam Warlock (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Adam Warlock]]. He appeared in ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Volume 3]]''. * The '''{{visible anchor|Dweller-in-Darkness}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics creature [[Dweller-in-Darkness|of the same name]]) is a soul-consuming demon kept imprisoned for thousands of years by the people of [[#Ta Lo|Ta Lo]] who impersonates Wenwu's deceased wife, [[Ying Li (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ying Li]], manipulating him into using the [[#Ten Rings|Ten Rings]] to release it.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lebovitz |first=David L. |date=September 2, 2021 |title=The Ending Of Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings Explained |url=https://www.looper.com/589956/the-ending-of-shang-chi-and-the-legend-of-the-ten-rings-explained/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210903172734/https://www.looper.com/589956/the-ending-of-shang-chi-and-the-legend-of-the-ten-rings-explained/ |archive-date=September 3, 2021 |access-date=September 3, 2021 |website=[[Looper (website)|Looper]]}}</ref> The Dweller kills Wenwu upon being freed, but is defeated by Shang-Chi. Visual effects for the creature were provided by [[Weta Digital]] in ''[[Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings]]'', which supplemented its winged, tentacled, and eyeless appearance with a large jaw and tongue. Weta also drew inspiration from a variety of references for each part of its body, including crabs and horns for its armor, a mixture of [[porous rock]], rhinos and elephants for its skin, and [[obsidian]] for its teeth.<ref name="ShangChiIW" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Fenris}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics creature [[Fenris Wolf (Marvel Comics)|of the same name]]) is a 23-foot-tall wolf owned by [[Hela (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hela]], who resurrects it using the [[#Eternal Flame|Eternal Flame]] following her release from [[#Hel|Hel]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Anderton |first=Ethan |date=July 23, 2016 |title='Thor: Ragnarok' Footage: Hulk vs Thor, Surtur the Demon & More [Comic-Con 2016] |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/thor-ragnarok-comic-con-footage/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160725143135/https://www.slashfilm.com/thor-ragnarok-comic-con-footage/ |archive-date=July 25, 2016 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Vejvoda |first=Jim |date=July 23, 2016 |title=Comic-Con 2016: Thor: Ragnarok Includes Surtur and Fenris |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2016/07/24/thor-ragnarok-includes-surtur-and-fenris |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160724052121/https://www.ign.com/articles/2016/07/24/thor-ragnarok-includes-surtur-and-fenris |archive-date=July 24, 2016 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[IGN]]}}</ref> She charges at [[Heimdall (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Heimdall]], preparing to fight him, but is attacked by the [[Hulk (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hulk]], who ultimately throws her off [[Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgard]] and into space. Visual effects for the creature in ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'' were provided by [[Framestore]], which used camera angles and tight framing to communicate her enormous size.<ref name="Thor3FXGuide" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Gargantos}}''' (based on [[Shuma-Gorath]] from the Marvel Comics)<ref>{{Cite web |last=Welch |first=Alex |date=December 16, 2021 |title='Doctor Strange 2' Trailer Explained: 5 Huge Reveals From the 'Multiverse of Madness' |url=https://www.inverse.com/entertainment/doctor-strange-2-trailer-explained-shuma-gorath-america-chavez |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211217043606/https://www.inverse.com/entertainment/doctor-strange-2-trailer-explained-shuma-gorath-america-chavez |archive-date=December 17, 2021 |access-date=December 19, 2021 |website=[[Inverse (website)|Inverse]]}}</ref> is an interdimensional octopus-like being sent by [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda Maximoff]] to pursue [[America Chavez (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|America Chavez]] with the aim of stealing her ability to travel the multiverse. [[List of Marvel Comics characters: G#Gargantos|Gargantos]] and Shuma-Gorath are two different creatures in the comics, with the film ''[[Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness]]'' referring to the creature as Gargantos because the rights to the name Shuma-Gorath are owned by [[Heroic Signatures]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Barnhardt |first=Adam |date=February 14, 2022 |title=Doctor Strange 2: The Reason Marvel Is Unable to Use Shuma-Gorath Revealed (Exclusive) |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/doctor-strange-2-shuma-gorath-why-marvel-cant-use-revealed/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220215035542/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/doctor-strange-2-shuma-gorath-why-marvel-cant-use-revealed/ |archive-date=February 15, 2022 |access-date=February 14, 2022 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> Its eye was modeled after that of Olsen's to foreshadow her role as the film's antagonist.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Welch |first=Alex |date=June 23, 2022 |title=Doctor Strange 2 Producer Reveals A Genius Easter Egg That Everyone Missed |url=https://www.inverse.com/entertainment/doctor-strange-2-producer-reveals-the-easter-egg-everyone-missed |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220625041002/https://www.inverse.com/entertainment/doctor-strange-2-producer-reveals-the-easter-egg-everyone-missed |archive-date=June 25, 2022 |access-date=June 25, 2022 |website=[[Inverse (website)|Inverse]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Great Protector}}''' is a water [[Chinese dragon|dragon]] which acts as the guardian of Ta Lo. Thousands of years ago, the Great Protector and the people of Ta Lo sealed the [[#Dweller-in-Darkness|Dweller-in-Darkness]] within the Dark Gate. When the Dweller escapes its seal, the Great Protector defeats it with the help of Shang-Chi, [[Katy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Katy]] and [[Xialing]]. The Great Protector can bestow her power onto people, giving them the ability to manipulate wind; and her scales can be fashioned into weapons and armor that are effective against the Dweller's forces. A life-sized replica of the dragon's head was built for the production of ''Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings'', which Weta Digital then digitally added visual effects to.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jirak |first=Jamie |date=September 17, 2021 |title=Shang-Chi Props Built a Life-Size Dragon for Simu Liu to Ride |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/shang-chi-props-built-a-life-size-dragon-for-simu-liu-to-ride/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210917234107/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/shang-chi-props-built-a-life-size-dragon-for-simu-liu-to-ride/ |archive-date=September 17, 2021 |access-date=September 17, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> Weta also based the dragon's eyes on those of [[Fala Chen]], who portrays [[Ying Li (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ying Li]] in the film,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Barnhardt |first=Adam |date=September 17, 2021 |title=Shang-Chi Used Star's Real Eyes for the Great Protector Dragon |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/shang-chi-fala-chen-shang-chis-mom-eyes-used-dragon/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210917234511/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/shang-chi-fala-chen-shang-chis-mom-eyes-used-dragon/ |archive-date=September 17, 2021 |access-date=September 17, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> and referenced [[sea snakes]] and eels when designing her flight movements.<ref name="ShangChiIW" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Goose}}''' (based on [[Chewie (comics)|Chewie]] from the Marvel Comics) is a [[Flerken (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Flerken]] and [[Mar-Vell (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mar-Vell]]'s pet during her time on Earth. In the 1990s, she is found by [[Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Carol Danvers]] at the [[#Joint Dark Energy Mission Facility|Joint Dark Energy Mission Facility]] and adopted by [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]]. With the ability to deploy long tentacles from inside her mouth as well as store objects in her stomach, she single-handedly takes out a squad of [[Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kree]] soldiers and blinds Fury's left eye with a scratch. In 2018, Goose survived [[the Blip]], and was taken in under Danvers' care. In 2026, during Danvers's team-up with Monica Rambeau and Kamala Khan, Goose is overseen by the agents of S.A.B.E.R., and begins breeding Flerken eggs around the station, giving birth to numerous offsprings which eventually help evacuate the agents when the S.A.B.E.R. station begins to collapse. She was introduced in ''[[Captain Marvel (film)|Captain Marvel]]'' and returned in ''[[The Marvels]]''. Hand-drawn pictures of her were seen in ''[[Ms. Marvel (miniseries)|Ms. Marvel]]'' and she was mentioned on Fury's file in ''[[Secret Invasion (miniseries)|Secret Invasion]]''. An alternate version of Goose also appears in the animated series [[What If...? (TV series)|''What If...?'']] episode "[[What If... Peter Quill Attacked Earth's Mightiest Heroes?]]". * '''{{visible anchor|Lucky the Pizza Dog}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics creature [[Lucky the Pizza Dog|of the same name]]) is [[Kate Bishop (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kate Bishop]]'s pet dog. In December 2024, he was rescued by Bishop in New York City and adopted by her. He then stayed with her and [[Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Clint Barton]] for a few days until they all moved to Barton's house in Iowa. Kate Bishop relocated to a new apartment in New York City, taking Lucky with her. One night, Lucky witnessed [[Kamala Khan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kamala Khan]] break into the place while Bishop was out. When Bishop returned, she gave Lucky a slice of pizza, which he happily ate as Khan introduced herself to Bishop.<ref name="LuckyTheMarvels">{{Cite web |last=Truitt |first=Brian |date=November 11, 2023 |title=How many post-credit scenes and cameos in 'The Marvels'? All the best movie spoilers here |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/movies/2023/11/11/the-marvels-movie-spoilers/71523263007/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231130142206/https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/movies/2023/11/11/the-marvels-movie-spoilers/71523263007/ |archive-date=November 30, 2023 |access-date=March 1, 2024 |website=[[USA Today]]}}</ref> He was introduced in ''[[Hawkeye (miniseries)|Hawkeye]]'',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Dinh |first=Christine |date=November 13, 2019 |title=What's Next For The Marvel Cinematic Universe After 'Avengers: Endgame' |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/marvel-cinematic-universe-after-avengers-endgame |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191114091331/https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/marvel-cinematic-universe-after-avengers-endgame |archive-date=November 14, 2019 |access-date=July 29, 2021 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Clarke |first=Cass |date=December 10, 2020 |title=Hawkeye's Pizza Dog Has Her Own Instagram |url=https://www.cbr.com/hawkeyespizza-dog-has-instagram/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201212230112/https://www.cbr.com/hawkeyespizza-dog-has-instagram/ |archive-date=December 12, 2020 |access-date=July 29, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> and reappeared in ''[[The Marvels]]''.<ref name="LuckyTheMarvels" /> [[File:Common wombat 8.jpg|thumb|180px|[[Trixter (company)|Trixter]] looked at [[wombats]] for inspiration when designing the appearance of Morris.<ref name="ShangChiET" />]] * '''{{visible anchor|Morris}}''' (voiced by [[Dee Bradley Baker|Dee Baker]])<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sarkisian |first=Jacob |date=September 4, 2021 |title=19 details you may have missed in 'Shang-Chi and the Legend of the Ten Rings' |url=https://www.insider.com/shang-chi-details-easter-eggs-marvel-references-mcu-2021-9 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://archive.today/20210904142619/https://www.insider.com/shang-chi-details-easter-eggs-marvel-references-mcu-2021-9 |archive-date=September 4, 2021 |access-date=September 6, 2021 |website=[[Insider (website)|Insider]]}}</ref> is a six-legged, faceless [[hundun]] who befriends [[Trevor Slattery]] during his imprisonment by the [[Ten Rings (organization)|Ten Rings]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Welch |first=Alex |date=September 2, 2021 |title=Shang-Chi Spoilers: How That Surprise Cameo Fixes Marvel's Biggest Faux Pas |url=https://www.inverse.com/entertainment/shang-chi-spoilers-ben-kingsley-trevor-slattery |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210903062522/https://www.inverse.com/entertainment/shang-chi-spoilers-ben-kingsley-trevor-slattery |archive-date=September 3, 2021 |access-date=September 6, 2021 |website=[[Inverse (website)|Inverse]]}}</ref> Morris later escapes with Slattery, Shang-Chi, Katy, and Xialing and leads them to his home, Ta Lo. The creature was inspired by ''Shang-Chi'' director [[Destin Daniel Cretton]]'s 15-year-old [[dachshund]] of the same name, and was doubled by a green-screen cushion during filming; the creatives of the film found it difficult to make Morris cute due to it not having eyes or a face to convey emotion, leading them to rely on its fur, feathers, and voice.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Paige |first=Rachel |date=September 9, 2021 |title='Shang-Chi and The Legend of The Ten Rings': Meet Morris, the Creature from Ta Lo |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/movies/meet-morris-shang-chi-and-the-legend-of-the-ten-rings |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210909214228/https://www.marvel.com/articles/movies/meet-morris-shang-chi-and-the-legend-of-the-ten-rings |archive-date=September 9, 2021 |access-date=September 11, 2021 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref><ref name="ShangChiET" /> Visual effects for the creature were provided by [[Trixter (company)|Trixter]] in ''Shang-Chi'', who looked at [[wombats]] and puppies for inspiration.<ref name="ShangChiET" /><ref name="ShangChiIW" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Señor Scratchy}}''' (based on [[Agatha Harkness#Ebony|Ebony]] and [[Nicholas Scratch]] from the Marvel Comics) is [[Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Agatha Harkness]]' rabbit who also acts as her [[familiar]]. In an early draft of "[[The Series Finale]]", the rabbit would have transformed into a demon in a similar fashion to ''[[American Werewolf in London]]'' before leading to a ''[[The Goonies]]'' (1985)-style chase sequence.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bui |first=Hoai-Tran |date=March 8, 2021 |title='WandaVision' Finale Originally Had Larger Roles for Monica Rambeau and That Creepy Rabbit, Director Matt Shakman Reveals |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/wandavision-finale-matt-shakman-cut-storylines/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210308184007/https://www.slashfilm.com/wandavision-finale-matt-shakman-cut-storylines/ |archive-date=March 8, 2021 |access-date=March 8, 2021 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> * '''[[Throg (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Throg]]''' (voiced by [[Chris Hemsworth]] and based on the Marvel Comics character [[Throg|of the same name]])<ref name="Sepinwall" /><ref>{{Cite web |last=Nelson |first=Jeff |date=July 10, 2021 |title=Chris Hemsworth's Loki Cameo As Frog Thor Revealed By Director |url=https://screenrant.com/loki-show-chris-hemsworth-cameo-frog-thor-details/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210710201947/https://screenrant.com/loki-show-chris-hemsworth-cameo-frog-thor-details/ |archive-date=July 10, 2021 |access-date=July 10, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> is a variant of Thor who is turned into a frog at one point in his life. Shortly after this occurred, he is detained by the Time Variance Authority (TVA) in a transparent container and sent to the Void.
===Magic and spells=== * '''{{visible anchor|Asgardian magic}}''' is a type of magic that is practiced by [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardian]] sorcerers such as [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]], [[Frigga (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Frigga]], [[Sylvie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sylvie]], [[Odin (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Odin]], [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]] and [[Heimdall (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Heimdall]]. This form of magic has many colors, with Loki's and Sylvie's magic being a bright green and Thor's being [[lightning]] based. ** The '''{{visible anchor|Odinforce}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics energy [[Odinforce|of the same name]]) is a powerful mystical energy used by Odin in his capacity as King of Asgard. It is the source of power for his spear, [[#Gungnir|Gungnir]], also passed down through the Kings of Asgard, and is the power source and weapon of the [[Destroyer (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Destroyer]]. It is periodically replenished by entering the '''Odinsleep''', a state that leaves the user vulnerable. The parts of the disabled Destroyer are later assembled into a prototype gun. * '''{{visible anchor|Chaos magic}}''' is an extremely powerful and rare form of magic that gives the user the ability to alter reality, wielded by [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda Maximoff]], thus making her the [[Scarlet Witch]], a being once thought to be mythical. This form of magic's primary color is red mixed with white and black hues. * '''{{visible anchor|Dark magic}}''' is a type of magic that harnesses energies from the Dark Dimension, practiced by [[Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Agatha Harkness]], Morgan la Fey, Nico Minoru, [[Kaecilius (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kaecilius]] and his Zealots, and the [[Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ancient One]]. In the case of Nico and Kaecilius's group, the usage of this form of magic without protection is corrosive with the latter forming a direct link to Dormammu. Asgardians like [[Odin (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Odin]] and [[Heimdall (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Heimdall]] can use it. A darker version of Stephen Strange dubbed "[[Doctor Strange Supreme]]" also practices dark magic. This form of magic's color is either red or purple. * '''{{visible anchor|Dreamwalking}}''' is a spell in the ''Darkhold'' that allows sorcerers to possess a variant of themselves in the multiverse.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Dinh |first=Christine |date=July 5, 2022 |title='Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness': The Cost of Dreamwalking and Incursions |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/movies/doctor-strange-in-the-multiverse-of-madness-dreamwalking-incursions |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20251004034208/https://www.marvel.com/articles/movies/doctor-strange-in-the-multiverse-of-madness-dreamwalking-incursions |archive-date=October 4, 2025 |access-date=October 26, 2025 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Eldritch magic}}''',<ref>{{Cite web |date=October 10, 2016 |title=''Doctor Strange'' |url=https://www.framestore.com/doctorstrange |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191007014745/https://www.framestore.com/doctorstrange |archive-date=October 7, 2019 |access-date=June 13, 2021 |website=[[Framestone]]}}</ref> often referred to as the '''mystic arts''', is a type of magic that is practiced by the [[Masters of the Mystic Arts]] on Earth (including [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]], [[Wong (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wong]], [[Karl Mordo (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Karl Mordo]], [[Kaecilius (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kaecilius]], the [[Ancient One (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ancient One]], and [[Jonathan Pangborn]]) as well as [[Krugarr (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Krugarr]]. It is a light-based magic that produces sparks and fiery energy of a yellow or orange color that gives off light and warmth. Visual effects for various magical elements in ''[[Doctor Strange (2016 film)|Doctor Strange]]'' (including mandalas, runes, whips, stalks, lily pads, and portals) were provided by various VFX vendors, including [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM), [[Method Studios]], [[Framestore]], Lola VFX, [[Luma Pictures]], [[Rise FX]], [[Crafty Apes]], and SPOV.<ref name="DSFXGuide">{{Cite web |last=Seymour |first=Mike |date=November 14, 2016 |title=Doctor Strange's Magical Mystery Tour in time |url=https://www.fxguide.com/featured/dr-stranges-magical-mystery-tour-in-time/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161121044733/https://www.fxguide.com/featured/dr-stranges-magical-mystery-tour-in-time/ |archive-date=November 21, 2016 |access-date=July 22, 2021 |website=[[Fxguide]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Frei |first=Vincent |date=October 24, 2016 |title=Doctor Strange |url=https://www.artofvfx.com/doctor-strange/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161109193900/https://www.artofvfx.com/doctor-strange/ |archive-date=November 9, 2016 |access-date=July 22, 2021 |website=The Art of VFX}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Vipers of Valtorr}}''' is a spell that allows the user to summon a group of snakes to attack opponents.<ref name="DoctorStrange2AudioCommentary">{{Cite web |last=Gilmore |first=Brian |date=July 6, 2022 |title=Every Secret & Reveal In Doctor Strange 2's Audio Commentary |url=https://screenrant.com/doctor-strange-multiverse-madness-audio-commentary-reveals/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220928201131/https://screenrant.com/doctor-strange-multiverse-madness-audio-commentary-reveals/ |archive-date=September 28, 2022 |access-date=September 6, 2024 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref>
===Artificial intelligences and robots=== * '''{{visible anchor|Beerbot 5000}}''' (voiced by Hamish Parkinson)<ref>{{Cite web |last=Zalben |first=Alex |date=December 25, 2024 |title=What If...? Season 3, Episode 4 Voice Cast: Who's Who, From Zeus to Howard The Duck |url=https://comicbookclublive.com/2024/12/25/what-if-season-3-episode-4-voice-cast-whos-who/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250126174512/https://comicbookclublive.com/2024/12/25/what-if-season-3-episode-4-voice-cast-whos-who/ |archive-date=January 26, 2025 |access-date=August 29, 2025 |website=Comic Book Club}}</ref> is a [[Sakaar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sakaaran]] robot programmed to work as a bartender. * '''[[E.D.I.T.H.]]''' (voiced by Dawn Michelle King and portrayed by [[Emily Hampshire]]),<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sherlock |first=Ben |date=November 14, 2019 |title=10 Amazing Behind-The-Scenes Facts About The MCU's ''Spider-Man'' Movies |url=https://screenrant.com/mcu-spider-man-movie-franchise-behind-scenes-facts/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191115155329/https://screenrant.com/mcu-spider-man-movie-franchise-behind-scenes-facts/ |archive-date=November 15, 2019 |access-date=April 21, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Andreeva |first=Nellie |date=June 4, 2025 |title='Vision': Emily Hampshire Cast as E.D.I.T.H. In Marvel Series For Disney+ |url=https://deadline.com/2025/06/emily-hampshire-edith-vision-marvel-series-disney-plus-1236422447/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250604162353/https://deadline.com/2025/06/emily-hampshire-edith-vision-marvel-series-disney-plus-1236422447/ |archive-date=June 4, 2025 |access-date=June 4, 2025 |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]]}}</ref> which stands for '''Even Dead, I'm the Hero''', is an advanced [[augmented reality]] [[artificial intelligence]] with numerous security, defense and tactical abilities created by [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]] and built into [[#Tony Stark's glasses|his pair of sunglasses]]. Following his death, the sunglasses are handed over to [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Parker]], giving him access to the AI as well as [[Stark Industries (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stark Industries]]' large arsenal of missiles and weaponized drones. * '''[[F.R.I.D.A.Y. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|F.R.I.D.A.Y.]]'''<!-- Please do not add her acronym from the comics, which is not confirmed to be the same in the MCU --> (voiced by [[Kerry Condon]], portrayed by [[Orla Brady]], and based on the Marvel Comics character [[F.R.I.D.A.Y.|of the same name]]),<ref>{{Cite web |last=Blevins |first=Adam |date=August 9, 2025 |title=Marvel's 'Vision Quest' Recasts a Major MCU Character With a 'Star Trek: Picard' Fan-Favorite |url=https://collider.com/vision-quest-cast-orla-brady-friday-recast-terry-matalas/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250809175026/https://collider.com/marvel-vision-quest-todd-stashwick-role-paladin-plot-details-james-spader-return-explained/ |archive-date=August 9, 2025 |access-date=August 9, 2025 |website=[[Collider (website)|Collider]]}}</ref> is a [[natural-language]] [[user interface]] created by Tony Stark to operate [[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|his armor]], serving as a replacement for [[J.A.R.V.I.S. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|J.A.R.V.I.S.]] after it is seemingly destroyed by [[Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ultron]]. * '''{{visible anchor|Griot}}''' (voiced by [[Trevor Noah]])<ref>{{Cite web |last=Desta |first=Yohana |title=Trevor Noah Had a Small but Vital Cameo in Black Panther |url=https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2018/05/trevor-noah-black-panther-cameo |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210128001021/https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2018/05/trevor-noah-black-panther-cameo |archive-date=January 28, 2021 |access-date=2021-05-08 |website=[[Vanity Fair (magazine)|Vanity Fair]]|date=May 22, 2018 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bonomolo |first=Cameron |date=May 21, 2018 |title=Trevor Noah Had a Secret Cameo in 'Black Panther' That Fans Just Now Noticed |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/black-panther-trevor-noah-cameo-voice/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210508165227/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/black-panther-trevor-noah-cameo-voice/ |archive-date=May 8, 2021 |access-date=2021-05-08 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> is [[Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Shuri]]'s artificial intelligence. The name "[[Griot]]" is a West African term for a historian or storyteller.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Oko |first=Lize |date=May 24, 2018 |title=What Is a Griot and Why Are They Important? which stands for '''Humanoid Experimental Robot B-Type Integrated Electronics''', is the [[Fantastic Four (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Fantastic Four's]] robot companion who generally serves as [[Reed Richards (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Reed Richards']] assistant. * '''[[J.A.R.V.I.S.]]''' (voiced by [[Paul Bettany]]) is a complex artificial intelligence matrix created by Tony Stark and named after his father's butler, [[Edwin Jarvis (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Edwin Jarvis]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Trumbore |first=Dave |date=July 25, 2014 |title=Agent Carter to Feature Edwin Jarvis, aka Howard web |date=May 16, 2008 |title=Paul Bettany on Voicing Iron Man's Jarvis |url=https://www.superherohype.com/features/96605-paul-bettany-on-voicing-iron-mans-jarvis |url-status=live System'''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kistler |first=Alan |title='Iron Man 2' Special: A Brief History Of Tony Stark's A.I. Assistants |url=https://www.mtv.com/news/2596711/iron-man-2-special-a-brief-history-of-tony-starks-ai-assistants/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191110103500/https://www.mtv.com/news/2596711/iron-man-2-special-a-brief-history-of-tony-starks-ai-assistants/ |archive-date=November 10, 2019 |access-date=November 21, 2019 |website=[[MTV News]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Karen}}''' (voiced by [[Jennifer Connelly]]), initially called "'''Suit Lady'''", is the name given by Peter Parker for the natural-language user interface created by Tony Stark and embedded in his second [[Spider-Man suit (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Spider-Man suit]].<ref>{{Cite magazine |last=Vilkomerson |first=Sara |date=July 5, 2017 |title=About That Voice Inside Peter Parker's 'Spider-Man: Homecoming' Suit... |url=https://ew.com/movies/2017/07/05/spider-man-homcoming-peter-parker-suit-voice/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210304140405/https://ew.com/movies/2017/07/05/spider-man-homcoming-peter-parker-suit-voice/ |archive-date=March 4, 2021 |access-date=March 24, 2021 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]]}}</ref> Originally hidden until Parker had completed the [[#Training Wheels Protocol|Training Wheels Protocol]], Karen is unlocked by [[Ned Leeds (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ned Leeds]] after he hacks into the suit, and is destroyed along with the suit following an attack by [[Mysterio (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mysterio]] in Europe. ''[[Spider-Man: Homecoming]]'' co-writer [[Jonathan Goldstein (filmmaker)|Jonathan Goldstein]] was initially skeptical of the idea, feeling that it was too similar to J.A.R.V.I.S. and F.R.I.D.A.Y.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Moss |first=Ethan |date=December 2, 2021 |title=Spider-Man: Homecoming Writers Didn't Want Spidey's Suit to Talk |url=https://www.cbr.com/spider-man-homecoming-writers-objected-talking-suit/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211202183138/https://www.cbr.com/spider-man-homecoming-writers-objected-talking-suit/ |archive-date=December 2, 2021 |access-date=December 4, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|K.E.V.I.N.}}''' (voiced by an uncredited [[Brian T. Delaney]]), which stands for '''Knowledge Enhanced Visual Interconnectivity Nexus''', is an advanced [[artificial intelligence]] which in ''[[She-Hulk: Attorney at Law]]'' is revealed to be the mastermind behind all Marvel Cinematic Universe storyline decisions. She-Hulk had to go through ''[[Marvel Studios: Assembled]]'' on the [[Disney+]] app to file a complaint to K.E.V.I.N. about the confusing storyline done in her show's final episode. It is depicted as operating through a robot in a secure room inside Marvel Studios where the entire Marvel Cinematic Universe can be surveyed simultaneously. Elements of the robot and A.I., including its name, call back to Marvel Studios President [[Kevin Feige]], who helped the show's head writer [[Jessica Gao]] with the show's season finale.<ref>{{Cite web |title='She-Hulk': Introducing Marvel Studios' K.E.V.I.N. |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/tv-shows/she-hulk-finale-kevin |access-date=2022-10-13 |website=Marvel Entertainment |language=en}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Mainframe}}''' (voiced by [[Miley Cyrus]] and then by [[Tara Strong]] and based on the Marvel Comics character [[Mainframe (comics)|of the same name]]), is an artificial intelligence and a leader of Mainframe Ravager Clan, as well as a member of Stakar Ogord's Team. * '''{{visible anchor|Miss Minutes}}''' (voiced by [[Tara Strong]] in ''[[Loki (TV series)|Loki]]'' and by [[Shelby Young]] in ''What If...? – An Immersive Story'')<ref>{{Cite web |last=Mitovich |first=Matt Webb |date=June 8, 2021 |title=Loki Review: After a Talky-Talky Start, Disney+'s Third Marvel Series Is a Super Fun 'Time Detectives' Two-Hander |url=https://tvline.com/2021/06/08/loki-review-tom-hiddleston-owen-wilson-disney-plus-marvel-series/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210608160753/https://tvline.com/2021/06/08/loki-review-tom-hiddleston-owen-wilson-disney-plus-marvel-series/ |archive-date=June 8, 2021 |access-date=June 10, 2021 |website=[[TVLine]]}}</ref> is [[He Who Remains]]' creation.
