More! |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5LestgxsoZY |access-date=February 6, 2025 |time=12:51–18:11 |publisher=[[Marvel Entertainment]] |via=[[YouTube]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|W.E.R.N.E.R.}}''' (voiced by [[Ross Marquand]])<ref>{{Cite web |last=Amin |first=Arezou |date=December 24, 2023 |title='What If...?' Season 2 Episode 3 Recap: Yippee Ki-Yay, Happy Hogan |url=https://collider.com/what-if-season-2-episode-3-recap/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231224085817/https://collider.com/what-if-season-2-episode-3-recap/ |archive-date=December 24, 2023 |access-date=December 24, 2023 |website=[[Collider (website)|Collider]]}}</ref> is a natural-language user interface created by Tony Stark.
===Elements=== * '''{{visible anchor|Adamantium}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics element [[Adamantium|of the same name]]) is an incredibly strong metallic element found in [[Celestials (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Celestials]] and present as an alloy in [[Logan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wolverine's]] skeleton, [[Wade Wilson (film character)|Deadpool's]] katanas, [[Laura (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|X-23's]] claws, [[Yuriko Oyama (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Lady Deathstrike's]] nails, [[Seth Voelker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sidewinder]]’s throwing knives and tomahawk, and Copperhead's knuckle daggers. It is more indestructible than vibranium. * '''{{visible anchor|Uru}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics element [[Uru (comics)|of the same name]]) is a metal used by the [[Dwarves (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Dwarves]] to forge the weapons [[Mjolnir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mjölnir]] and [[Stormbreaker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stormbreaker]], and the [[Infinity Gauntlet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Infinity Gauntlet]]. It has magical properties. The metal is forged in the furnaces of [[#Nidavellir|Nidavellir]] as it is so powerful that only the heat of a dying star can melt it. * '''{{visible anchor|Vibranium}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics element [[Vibranium|of the same name]]) is an element used to create [[Captain America's shield (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Captain America's shield]], the [[Black Panther suit (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Black Panther suit]], the [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]], [[#Sam Wilson's flight suit|Sam Wilson's Captain America suit]] and the [[Bucky Barnes' prosthetic arm (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Winter Soldier's cybernetic arm]]. It came from a [[meteorite]] collision with Earth and is regarded as the strongest metal in the world, stronger than steel and a third of the weight.<ref name="Made of">{{Cite web |last=Lue |first=Alex |date=April 3, 2021 |title=Captain America's Shield: What Is It Made Of? |url=https://insidethemagic.net/2021/04/captain-americas-shield-al1mmb/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210415141530/https://insidethemagic.net/2021/04/captain-americas-shield-al1mmb/ |archive-date=April 15, 2021 |access-date=May 8, 2021 |website=Inside the Magic}}</ref> The Wakandans originally hid it for years, and it thus became rare and extremely expensive, allowing vibranium smugglers such as [[Ulysses Klaue (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ulysses Klaue]] to make billions of dollars. Vibranium is also shown to be [[bulletproof]] to most high-caliber [[bullets]].<ref name="Made of" /> It is also absorbent of sound, vibration, and [[kinetic energy]], and can deflect high-energy blasts.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Kerr |first=Mandy |date=November 21, 2021 |title='Avengers: Endgame' Director Joe Russo Explains How Thanos Broke Captain America's Shield |url=https://www.cheatsheet.com/entertainment/avengers-endgame-thanos-captain-america-shield.html/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210414174953/https://www.cheatsheet.com/entertainment/avengers-endgame-thanos-captain-america-shield.html/ |archive-date=April 14, 2021 |access-date=May 8, 2021 |website=Showbiz Cheat Sheet}}</ref> Its main source is Mount Bashenga in [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakanda]], but it is eventually revealed at least a few other meteorites brought vibranium in small amounts to other parts of the world. However, when the Eternals prevented Tiamut the Communicator from being unleashed, this created the more indestructible element called adamantium.
===Projects and initiatives=== * The '''{{visible anchor|Avengers Initiative}}''', originally known as the '''Protector Initiative''', is the initiative for gathering a group of superheroes from various backgrounds, described as "a group of remarkable people", into the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]], to protect Earth from various threats. It was initiated by [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]] in the 1990s and was renamed in honor of Carol Danvers' [[call sign]], "Avenger". In 2011, Fury directs [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]] to measure [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]]'s suitability for the initiative, though Stark was initially rejected and only used as a consultant. Fury also recruits [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]], [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]], and [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]], and also assigns [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] agents Romanoff and [[Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Clint Barton]] to the team. * '''{{visible anchor|Cataract}}''' is a project conducted by [[S.W.O.R.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.W.O.R.D.]] with the goal of reactivating the [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]] as a "sentient weapon" following his death at the hands of [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] during the [[Infinity War (event)|Infinity War]]. Under Acting Director [[Tyler Hayward]]'s orders, S.W.O.R.D. acquires Vision's body following the Battle of Wakanda and dismantles it, hoping to study its components and rebuild it. Though initially unsuccessful, S.W.O.R.D. uses energy collected from [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda Maximoff]] on a [[Stark Industries (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stark Industries]] drone to reactivate Vision, turning his body white in the process. * '''{{visible anchor|Project Insight}}''' is a secret S.H.I.E.L.D. operation that was begun as a direct response to the [[Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of New York]]. It involved three advanced [[Helicarrier (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Helicarriers]] that would patrol Earth, using an algorithm to evaluated people's behavior to detect possible future threats and using satellite-guided guns to eliminate those individuals. The project was led by [[Alexander Pierce (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Alexander Pierce]], who intended to use the project as a means of eliminating individuals who posed a threat to Hydra. Notable targeted figures included Tony Stark, Bruce Banner, and [[Stephen Strange (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stephen Strange]]. His plans were eventually foiled by [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]] and his allies. ''[[Captain America: The Winter Soldier]]'' directors [[Anthony and Joe Russo]] sought to include references to [[Unmanned combat aerial vehicle|drone warfare]], [[targeted killing]] and [[global surveillance]] in the film, which became more topical during [[principal photography]] due to the disclosure of several [[National Security Agency]] [[Global surveillance disclosures (2013–present)|surveillance-related documents]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Suebsaeng |first=Asawin |date=April 4, 2014 |title="Captain America: The Winter Soldier" Is About Obama's Terror-Suspect Kill List, Say the Film's Directors |url=https://www.motherjones.com/mixed-media/2014/04/captain-america-winter-soldier-obama-kill-list-politics-drones-nsa |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140406195105/https://www.motherjones.com/mixed-media/2014/04/captain-america-winter-soldier-obama-kill-list-politics-drones-nsa |archive-date=April 6, 2014 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[Mother Jones (magazine)|Mother Jones]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Project Pegasus}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics project [[Project Pegasus (comics)|of the same name]]) is a joint project between [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]], [[NASA]], and the [[United States Air Force]] to study the [[Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tesseract]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Schmidt |first=Joe |date=March 8, 2019 |title='Captain Marvel': What Is Project Pegasus? |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/captain-marvel-what-is-project-pegasus/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210430195314/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/captain-marvel-what-is-project-pegasus-/ |archive-date=April 30, 2021 |access-date=April 30, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> It is reactivated by the [[World Security Council]] following an alien attack in [[#Puente Antiguo|Puente Antiguo, New Mexico]] until it is terminated after [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]] takes the Tesseract back to [[Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgard]] after the [[Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of New York]]. * '''{{visible anchor|Project Rebirth}}''' (based on [[Weapon I]] from the Marvel Comics), also known as the '''Super Soldier Program''', is a collaboration between U.S., British and German scientists led by Dr. [[Abraham Erskine (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Abraham Erskine]] under the supervision of [[Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peggy Carter]], [[Howard Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Howard Stark]], and [[Chester Phillips (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Chester Phillips]] to create a new breed of super-soldiers. The first successful test leads to the creation of Captain America by enhancing the sickly [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]], but is abandoned following the assassination of Erskine by [[Heinz Kruger (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Heinz Kruger]]. * {{anchor|Red Room 2}}The '''Red Room''' (based on the Marvel Comics program [[Red Room (comics)|of the same name]]), also known as the '''Black Widow Program''', is a top-secret Soviet (and later Russian) training program led by [[Dreykov]]. The program takes young orphan girls and turns them into elite assassins named "[[Black Widows (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Black Widows]]", and is overseen by various individuals, including Madame B. and [[Melina Vostokoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Melina Vostokoff]]. [[Antonia Dreykov|Taskmaster]] is another head agent working directly for Dreykov. Graduates of the program include [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]], [[Yelena Belova (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Yelena Belova]] and [[Ruth Bat-Seraph (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ruth Bat-Seraph]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://screenrant.com/every-black-widow-mcu/|title=Every Black Widow In The MCU After Captain America: Brave New World|accessdate=March 13, 2025|website=[[Screen Rant]]|date=February 22, 2025|last=Rangel|first=Felipe}}</ref> The program was terminated in 2016 following the destruction of the Red Room's headquarters. In an alternate universe seen in ''[[What If...? (TV series)|What If...?]]'', the Red Room captures Steve Rogers in 1953 and brainwashes him into being an assassin rather than Hydra brainwashing Bucky Barnes. During a final confrontation between the brainwashed Rogers, the Black Widows, led by Vostokoff, and Romanoff and Captain Carter, Carter manages to break through to Rogers who destroys the Red Room's headquarters, seemingly at the cost of his own life. * The '''{{visible anchor|Sokovia Accords}}''' (based on the [[Registration Acts (comics)|Registration Acts]] from the Marvel Comics), officially titled the '''Sokovia Accords: Framework for the Registration and Deployment of Enhanced Individuals''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Abad-Santos |first=Alex |date=November 28, 2015 |title=The important clue in the Captain America: Civil War trailer you may have missed |url=https://www.vox.com/2015/11/26/9802112/captain-america-civil-war-clue |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151128045516/https://www.vox.com/2015/11/26/9802112/captain-america-civil-war-clue |archive-date=November 28, 2015 |access-date=October 6, 2021 |website=[[Vox (website)|Vox]]}}</ref> were a group of legislative [[documents]] ratified by the United Nations (UN), with the support of 117 countries, following the [[Battle of Sokovia]]. They establish UN oversight over the Avengers, and were supported by Tony Stark, James Rhodes, Vision, T'Challa, and Natasha Romanoff and opposed by Steve Rogers, Sam Wilson, and Clint Barton, leading to the [[Avengers Civil War]]. By the events of ''[[She-Hulk: Attorney at Law]]'', the Accords were repealed and no longer active.<ref>{{cite web |title='She-Hulk' finally solves a 6-year-old Avengers mystery |url=https://www.inverse.com/entertainment/she-hulk-sokovia-accords-repealed |website=Inverse |access-date=26 August 2024 |language=en |date=6 October 2022}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Ultron Program}}''' is an attempt by Tony Stark and Bruce Banner to create an artificial intelligence, "[[Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ultron]]", as a means of protecting the world against incoming extraterrestrial threats. The program becomes a failure, with the program being infected due to the Mind Stone and turning genocidal, seeking to wipe out the [[Human|human race]]. * The '''{{visible anchor|Winter Soldier Program}}''' is a top secret [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]] super-soldier program started by [[Nazi]] scientist Dr. [[Arnim Zola (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Arnim Zola]] in the 1940s. It took soldiers, brainwashed them, and enhanced them with a recreation of the [[Super Soldier Serum (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Super Soldier Serum]], turning them into deadly assassins known as "Winter Soldiers", who were kept in [[Suspended animation in fiction|cryostasis]] while not on a mission. Each soldier had a set of codewords, recorded in the [[#Winter Soldier Book|Winter Soldier Book]], which, when recited to a Winter Soldier, would make them completely obedient to that person. The Winter Soldiers, with the exception of [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]], are later executed by [[Helmut Zemo (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Helmut Zemo]].
===Terms and phrases=== * "'''{{visible anchor|Avengers, assemble!}}'''" is a rallying cry for the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]]. * "'''{{visible anchor|I could do this all day}}'''" is a [[catchphrase]] spoken by [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]]. [[Captain Carter]] also utters the line in ''[[Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness]]''. * The "'''{{visible anchor|Peter Tingle}}'''" (based on the [[Spider-Sense]] from the Marvel Comics) is the name given by [[May Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|May Parker]] to her nephew [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter]]'s [[precognition]] ability. * A "'''{{visible anchor|variant}}'''" is an individual who has deviated from the [[Sacred Timeline]]. * "'''{{visible anchor|Wakanda Forever}}'''" is a [[slogan]] widely used by [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakandans]]. * "'''[[With great power, there must also come great responsibility]]'''" is a line spoken to Peter Parker by his aunt May before her death at the hands of [[Norman Osborn (2002 film series character)|Norman Osborn]].
===Substances=== * The '''{{visible anchor|Black Widow antidote}}''' is a red-colored synthetic gas stored in vials which acts as the antidote to the chemical mind-control that the Red Room employs on its Black Widows and [[Antonia Dreykov|Taskmaster]], created by a rogue former Black Widow. [[Yelena Belova (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Yelena Belova]] and [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]] acquire the antidote in 2016 and use it to free various Widows. * The '''{{visible anchor|Heart-shaped herb}}''' is a [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakandan]] plant enriched through exposure to [[Vibranium (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|vibranium]], giving it a glowing purple color. It is ingested in a ceremony by the new Black Panther, granting them superhuman abilities. It also allows for the communication with the dead in the [[#Astral Dimension|Ancestral Plane]] upon ingestion. After becoming King of Wakanda, [[N'Jadaka (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|N'Jadaka]] ingests the herb and orders the rest of the stocks to be incinerated. One of them is extracted by [[Nakia (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nakia]], who uses it to heal [[T'Challa (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|T'Challa]]. Shuri laters attempts to develop a synthetic Heart-shaped herb, eventually succeeding and allowing her to receive the same enhancements that N'Jadaka and T'Challa had acquired. * '''{{visible anchor|Pym Particles}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Pym Particles|of the same name]]) are extradimensional subatomic particles capable of reducing or increasing the distance between atoms, allowing the user to shrink or grow. The formula for the particles was created by [[Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hank Pym]] and they appear in the form of a liquid stored in vials. They appear red when used to shrink and blue when used to grow. The particles also power Pym Discs and the [[Hope van Dyne|Wasp]]'s blasters, and are used by the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]] to time-travel via the Quantum Realm during the [[Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Heist]]. * The '''{{visible anchor|Super Soldier Serum}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics object [[Super Soldier Serum|of the same name]]) is a serum used to enhance humans to the peak of human perfection. It was originally developed by Dr. [[Abraham Erskine (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Abraham Erskine]] and was given to [[Johann Schmidt (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Johann Schmidt]] (turning him into the [[Red Skull]]) and [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]] (turning him into [[Captain America]]). After Erksine's death, numerous other versions of the serum are created with varying degrees of success. Hydra used a version of the serum to transform Bucky Barnes into the [[Winter Soldier (comics)|Winter Soldier]], and later used variants of the serum taken from Howard Stark after [[Assassination of Howard and Maria Stark|his assassination]] to enhance other assassins as well, but the program fails and is shut down. Another serum was given to [[Isaiah Bradley (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Isaiah Bradley]] by the [[U.S. government]] during the [[Cold War]], allowing him to confront and defeat the Winter Soldier in combat. [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]] tried to replicate the serum using [[gamma radiation]] as a substitute for vita radiation, turning him into the Hulk. Meanwhile, a more successful version of the serum was given to [[Emil Blonsky (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Emil Blonsky]] by the U.S. government, and Dr. Wilfred Nagel replicated the serum and gave them to the [[Sharon Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Power Broker]] until they were stolen by [[Karli Morgenthau]] and the Flag Smashers. Helmut Zemo later destroys all but one of the vials, which is taken by [[John Walker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|John Walker]].
===Technologies=== * The '''{{visible anchor|Arc reactor}}''' is an energy source originally designed by [[Howard Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Howard Stark]] and [[Anton Vanko (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Anton Vanko]], and later independently built by their sons, [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony]] and [[Ivan Vanko (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ivan]]. It was initially designed as part of an attempt to replicate the [[Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tesseract]]'s energy based on Howard's study of the object. Tony Stark eventually builds two versions—a large industrial reactor for powering his machines at the [[#Stark Industries Headquarters|Stark Industries Headquarters]], and a miniature version embedded in his chest to power [[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|his armor]] (also known as an '''RT''') and prevent the [[Shrapnel shell|shrapnel]] from reaching his heart. The first miniature version used a [[palladium]] core, although he later synthesizes [[#Badassium|a new element]] when the palladium begins to poison him. He continues to develop the reactor throughout the years (even after the shrapnel is removed from his body), with the final version containing nanobots that make up his armor. Ivan Vanko, [[James Rhodes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|James Rhodes]], and [[Pepper Potts (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pepper Potts]] also use arc reactors in their armors. [[Max Dillon (film character)|Max Dillon]] from an alternate universe also briefly uses one before it is removed by [[Otto Octavius (film character)|Otto Octavius]], also from an alternate universe and takes it back with him to his reality.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gladman |first=Andrew |date=December 5, 2021 |title=Spider-Man 3: Electro Wears an arc reactor in No Way Home – But Why? |url=https://www.cbr.com/spider-man-3-electro-arc-reactor/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211206112907/https://www.cbr.com/spider-man-3-electro-arc-reactor/ |archive-date=December 6, 2021 |access-date=December 29, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|B.A.R.F.}}''', which stands for '''Binarily Augmented Retro-Framing''', is a [[Hologram|holographic technology]] created by [[Quentin Beck (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Quentin Beck]] during his time at [[Stark Industries (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stark Industries]]. Despite the technology's potential, Stark used the technology for therapeutic purposes and gave it a deliberately humorous name, humiliating and disgusting Beck. After he is fired for his unstable nature, Beck further developed the technology and equipped drones with advanced holographic projectors to create large monsters known as [[Elementals (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Elementals]]. * '''{{visible anchor|Dum-E}}''' is Tony Stark's [[automated]] [[hydraulic]] arm. Built by a young Stark in his father Howard's garage, it acts as his workshop assistant, and often "hands" him things, such as bringing his arc reactor when Stark was unable to reach it due to having his previous one stolen by [[Obadiah Stane (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Obadiah Stane]]. However, Dum-E has also often been of annoyance to Stark.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Mason |first=Heather |date=November 11, 2019 |title=Chosen One Of The Day: Tony Stark's Sassy Robot Dum-E |url=https://www.syfy.com/syfywire/chosen-one-of-the-day-tony-starks-sassy-robot-dum-e |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210425165521/https://www.syfy.com/syfywire/chosen-one-of-the-day-tony-starks-sassy-robot-dum-e |archive-date=April 25, 2021 |access-date=April 25, 2021 |website=[[Syfy Wire]]}}</ref> In 2013, it is severely damaged by [[Aldrich Killian (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Aldrich Killian]]'s attack on [[#Stark Mansion|his mansion]] and later pulled out of the wreckage and hauled away by Stark. By 2024, it has been fully repaired and moved into [[Happy Hogan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Happy Hogan]]'s apartment.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Russell |first=Bradley |date=December 15, 2021 |title=Spider-Man: No Way Home Easter eggs: the 30 biggest Marvel references |url=https://www.gamesradar.com/spider-man-no-way-home-easter-eggs-references-cameos/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211215175623/https://www.gamesradar.com/spider-man-no-way-home-easter-eggs-references-cameos/ |archive-date=December 15, 2021 |access-date=January 9, 2022 |website=[[GamesRadar+]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Kimoyo Beads}}''' are an advanced piece of technology developed by [[Shuri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Shuri]] and used in [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakanda]]. They are made to serve a vast range of purposes according to the needs of the wearer, such as deprogramming [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]]. [[File:Dme motorola.jpg|thumb|upright|A [[Motorola]] LX2 pager]] * '''{{visible anchor|Nick Fury's pager}}''' is a [[pager]] belonging to [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]] that was upgraded by [[Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Carol Danvers]] before she left Earth. With the new enhancements, it could now contact her no matter where she was in the galaxy, although he was only to use it in the event of an emergency. Nick Fury activates it for the first time in years during [[the Blip]], prompting Danvers to return to Earth and meet the surviving Avengers. * '''{{visible anchor|Peter Quill's Walkman}}''' is a [[TPS-L2|Sony TPS-L2 Walkman]] given to [[Peter Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peter Quill]] by his mother, [[Meredith Quill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Meredith]], when he was a child. It contained a [[cassette tape]] titled ''[[Awesome Mix Vol. 1]]'' which included a series of songs from the [[1960s in music|1960s]] and [[1970s in music|1970s]], incorporated by ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy (film)|Guardians of the Galaxy]]'' director [[James Gunn]] as "cultural reference points" to remind audiences of Quill's Earthly origins.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ashurst |first=Sam |date=February 19, 2014 |title=Guardians of the Galaxy: James Gunn's Trailer Breakdown: Star Lord's Walkman |url=https://www.totalfilm.com/features/guardians-of-the-galaxy-james-gunn-s-trailer-breakdown/star-lord-s-walkman |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140227195355/http://www.totalfilm.com/features/guardians-of-the-galaxy-james-gunn-s-trailer-breakdown/star-lord-s-walkman |archive-date=February 27, 2014 |access-date=August 22, 2021 |website=[[Total Film]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Tilly |first=Chris |date=May 16, 2014 |title=15 Reasons To Get Excited About Guardians Of The Galaxy |url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2014/05/16/why-guardians-of-the-galaxy-will-rock-your-world-this-summer?page=2 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140519100305/http://www.ign.com/articles/2014/05/16/why-guardians-of-the-galaxy-will-rock-your-world-this-summer?page=2 |archive-date=May 19, 2014 |access-date=August 22, 2021 |website=[[IGN]]}}</ref> Deeply cherished by Quill, he happened to have his Walkman on him when abducted by [[Yondu Udonta (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Yondu]], which he continued to listen to during his adult years. Following the [[Battle of Xandar]], Quill opens a gift from his mother, which is revealed to be another [[mixtape]] titled ''[[Awesome Mix Vol. 2]]'', described by Gunn as "better" and "more diverse" than ''Vol. 1''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Crossan |first=Ashley |date=April 14, 2015 |title='Guardians of the Galaxy' Director James Gunn Says Sequel's Soundtrack 'Is Better' Than the First |url=https://www.etonline.com/movies/162787_guardians_of_the_galaxy_director_james_gunn_says_sequel_s_soundtrack_is_better_than_the_first/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150417131007/http://www.etonline.com/movies/162787_guardians_of_the_galaxy_director_james_gunn_says_sequel_s_soundtrack_is_better_than_the_first/ |archive-date=April 17, 2015 |access-date=April 22, 2021 |website=[[Entertainment Tonight]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Peters |first=Megan |date=December 12, 2016 |title=Guardians Of The Galaxy Vol. 2 Soundtrack Is More Diverse According To James Gunn |url=https://comicbook.com/2016/12/12/guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2-soundtrack-is-more-diverse-accordi/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20161214011209/http://comicbook.com/2016/12/12/guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2-soundtrack-is-more-diverse-accordi/ |archive-date=December 14, 2016 |access-date=August 22, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> After his Walkman is destroyed by [[Ego (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ego]], Kraglin gives him a [[Zune]] formerly owned by Yondu as a replacement, a scene which [[Microsoft]] was displeased with.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Alexander |first=Julia |date=August 22, 2017 |title=Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2 director explains importance of the Microsoft Zune |url=https://www.polygon.com/2017/8/22/16186506/guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2-microsoft-zune-james-gunn |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170823031533/https://www.polygon.com/2017/8/22/16186506/guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2-microsoft-zune-james-gunn |archive-date=August 23, 2017 |access-date=August 22, 2021 |website=[[Polygon (website)|Polygon]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Mitchell |first=Bea |date=May 16, 2017 |title=Microsoft wasn't happy about Guardians of the Galaxy Vol 2's Zune reference |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a828461/microsoft-zune-guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201111232734/https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a828461/microsoft-zune-guardians-of-the-galaxy-vol-2/ |archive-date=November 11, 2020 |access-date=August 22, 2021 |website=[[Digital Spy]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Quantum Tunnel}}''' is an inter-dimensional gateway designed by [[Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hank Pym]], [[Bill Foster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bill Foster]], and [[Egghead (Marvel Comics)|Elihas Starr]] to transport individuals to and from the [[Quantum Realm]]. Six versions of the tunnel have been created over time: the first incarnation was built by Pym, Foster, and Starr but was destroyed in an explosion; a second version of the tunnel was used by Pym and [[Hope van Dyne]] to rescue [[Janet van Dyne (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Janet van Dyne]] from the Quantum Realm; a third version was placed inside [[#Luis' van|Luis' van]] and used to send [[Scott Lang (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Scott Lang]] into the Quantum Realm to acquire quantum energy to heal [[Ava Starr]]; a fourth tunnel designed by Tony Stark, Bruce Banner, and Rocket was used by the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]] to travel back in time to collect the six [[Infinity Stones]] in alternate timelines; a fifth was created shortly after the [[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]] by Banner to send [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]] back in time to return the [[Infinity Stones]] and [[Mjolnir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mjölnir]] back to their respective timelines; and a sixth tunnel was used by [[Leo Fitz]], [[Jemma Simmons]], and [[Enoch (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Enoch]] to travel across different timelines. [[File:Northrop Grumman Bat UAV in flight in June 2014.JPG|thumb|170px|The [[Northrop Grumman Bat]], a typical weaponized reconnaissance drone]] * '''{{visible anchor|Redwing}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics animal [[Redwing (Marvel Comics)|of the same name]]), officially designated the '''Stark Drone MK82 922 V 80Z V2 Prototype Unit V6''',<ref>{{Cite web |last=Meisfjord |first=Tom |date=March 19, 2021 |title=The Falcon And The Winter Soldier: The Truth About Redwing |url=https://www.looper.com/359739/the-falcon-and-the-winter-soldier-the-truth-about-redwing/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210523143353/https://www.looper.com/359739/the-falcon-and-the-winter-soldier-the-truth-about-redwing/ |archive-date=May 23, 2021 |access-date=March 20, 2021 |website=[[Looper (website)|Looper]]}}</ref> is an advanced [[drone (aircraft)|drone]] used in combat and [[reconnaissance]] by [[Sam Wilson (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sam Wilson]]. It was originally designed by [[Stark Industries (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stark Industries]] after Wilson joins the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]], and was equipped into his [[EXO-7 Falcon]] suit. In 2023, Wilson acquires a new version of the drone along with a new combat suit, but the drone is destroyed by [[Karli Morgenthau]]. Wilson later uses a new version of the drone along with [[#Sam Wilson's flight suit|his uniform]] as Captain America, both designed in Wakanda. * The '''{{visible anchor|Regeneration Cradle}}''' is a piece of medical equipment created by Dr. [[Helen Cho]] that is able to heal serious injuries by grafting artificial tissue onto them. [[Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Clint Barton]]'s life is saved through this treatment. [[Ultron (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ultron]] later brainwashes Dr. Cho using [[Loki's scepter]] into grafting the tissue to the [[Mind Stone]] and [[Vibranium (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|vibranium]] to create a new body for himself. The Avengers intervene, and with [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]]'s help, the new body is awoken and dubbed the "[[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]]". * '''{{visible anchor|TemPads|Time Door|Time Doors}}''' are devices used by the [[Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Variance Authority]] (TVA) to travel through time. Their interface was inspired by [[SNES]] video games and [[Game Boy]]s, with ''[[Loki (TV series)|Loki]]'' director [[Kate Herron]] describing them as "the closest thing to our phones" that the TVA has.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Webster |first=Andrew |date=June 10, 2021 |title=Loki director Kate Herron says the show is a 'big love letter to sci-fi' |url=https://www.theverge.com/22525909/loki-director-kate-herron-interview |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210628130410/https://www.theverge.com/22525909/loki-director-kate-herron-interview |archive-date=June 28, 2021 |access-date=July 8, 2021 |website=[[The Verge]]}}</ref> The TemPads create Time Doors, amber-colored [[interdimensional doorway|interdimensional portals]] used by the TVA to travel between alternate timelines to preserve the Sacred Timeline. They can also lead to Time Cells, where prisoners are forever stuck in [[time loop]]s and the Doors are colored red. [[FuseFX]], which provided the portals' visual effects for the first season of ''Loki'', explained that this color change was to reflect the amount of suffering which [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]] undergoes when inside the Time Cells.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Barnhardt |first=Adam |date=August 16, 2021 |title=Loki VFX Boss Breaks Down the TVA's Different Door Colors |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/loki-vfx-team-tva-door-different-color-reasons/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210817004808/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/loki-vfx-team-tva-door-different-color-reasons/ |archive-date=August 17, 2021 |access-date=September 11, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> * '''{{visible anchor|Thor's prosthetic eye}}''' is a [[bionic eye]] that he wears in his left eye socket, replacing the organic one gouged out by Hela. He is given it by Rocket, who stole it from one of Yondu's Ravagers, Vorker, who takes the eye out when he sleeps. Rocket stored it in his rectum until giving it to Thor. The eye has a brown [[iris (anatomy)|iris]], in contrast to Thor's natural blue eyes. * The '''{{visible anchor|Time-Keepers}}''' (voiced by [[Jonathan Majors]]<ref name="Majors">{{Cite magazine |last=Agard |first=Chancellor |date=July 16, 2021 |title=''Loki'' director Kate Herron and star Jonathan Majors on his pivotal character's wild debut |url=https://ew.com/tv/loki-kate-herron-jonathan-majors-kang/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210716221403/https://ew.com/tv/loki-kate-herron-jonathan-majors-kang/ |archive-date=July 16, 2021 |access-date=July 16, 2021 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]]}}</ref> and based on the Marvel Comics characters [[Time-Keepers|of the same name]]) were three [[Android (robot)|androids]] created by [[He Who Remains (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|He Who Remains]] to pose as the creators of the Time Variance Authority (TVA). Believed to be alive by workers of the TVA, statues of them and their likenesses are featured in several locations throughout the [[#TVA Headquarters|TVA's headquarters]]. Majors voicing both the Time-Keepers and their controller He Who Remains is a reference to ''[[The Wizard of Oz (1939 film)|The Wizard of Oz]]'' (1939).<ref name="Majors" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Tony Stark's glasses|E.D.I.T.H. glasses}}''' are a pair of technologically advanced sunglasses created by him. They are able to polarize and contain his AI [[F.R.I.D.A.Y. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|F.R.I.D.A.Y.]] After his death, F.R.I.D.A.Y. is replaced by [[E.D.I.T.H.]], passing into the hands of Peter Parker. Parker passes them on to Quentin Beck, who uses them to better control his illusions, before reacquiring them in London. * The '''{{visible anchor|Universal Neural Teleportation Network}}'''<ref>{{Cite web |last=Gunn |first=James |author-link=James Gunn |title=''Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2'' Screenplay |url=https://waltdisneystudiosawards.com/media/scripts/GotG2.pdf |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171121231203/https://waltdisneystudiosawards.com/media/scripts/GotG2.pdf |archive-date=November 21, 2017 |access-date=May 9, 2021 |publisher=[[Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures]]}}</ref> is the universal system for space travel. The system enables spaceships to travel through hexagonal-shaped wormholes known as '''jump points''' to [[Hyperspace|instantaneously travel]] between planetary systems.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Brooks |first=Nicholas |date=October 30, 2022 |title=The MCU's Best Mode of Transportation Could Tie to an X-Men Ally |url=https://www.cbr.com/jump-points-may-connect-x-men-shiar-empire-mcu/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221031000537/https://www.cbr.com/jump-points-may-connect-x-men-shiar-empire-mcu/ |archive-date=October 31, 2022 |access-date=April 11, 2023 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> In ''[[Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 2]]'', Yondu says it is not healthy for a [[Mammal|mammalian lifeform]] to go through more than fifty jumps at once, which will result in extreme discomfort and [[Disfigurement|temporary disfigurement]] for those on board.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Mallenbaum |first=Carly |date=May 26, 2017 |title=Disney's 'Guardians of the Galaxy' ride: Here's what to expect |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/life/entertainthis/2017/05/26/guardians-of-the-galaxy-california-adventure-tower-of-terror/102161522/ |url-status=live |archive-date=May 27, 2017 |access-date=April 11, 2023 |newspaper=[[USA Today]]}}</ref> By 2024, a [[S.A.B.E.R.]] space station has been established outside Earth, near a jump point.<ref>{{Cite Bifrost.<ref name="Desowitz" />]] * {{anchor|Bifrost}}The '''Bifröst Bridge''' (based on the Norse mythological location [[Bifröst|of the same name]]), often simply referred to as the '''Bifröst''', is an energy that allows for near-instantaneous travel via a [[wormhole]], used primarily for travel within the [[Nine Realms (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nine Realms]] by [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]]. The energy is harnessed using the [[#Rainbow Bridge|Rainbow Bridge]], which connected to [[Himinbjörg]]. [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]] intends to use this to destroy [[Jotunheim (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Jotunheim]], proving himself worthy of the throne to Odin, but his plans are foiled after [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]] destroys the Rainbow Bridge. The Bridge is later repaired using the [[Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tesseract]], but destroyed again during [[Ragnarok (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ragnarok]]. The energy can also be generated through [[#Dark magic|dark magic]] and using [[Stormbreaker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Stormbreaker]]. Visual effects of the Bifröst in ''[[Thor (film)|Thor]]'' were influenced by [[Hubble Space Telescope|Hubble]] photography as well as other images of deep space,<ref name="Desowitz" /> and were done by [[BUF Compagnie]] and [[Fuel VFX]].<ref>{{Cite web |last1=Hopewell |first1=John |last2=Keslassy |first2=Elsa |date=February 2, 2010 |title=Films draw French rebates |url=https://variety.com/2010/biz/news/films-draw-french-rebates-1118014554/l |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200804055401/https://variety.com/2010/biz/news/films-draw-french-rebates-1118014554/ |archive-date=August 4, 2020 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Frei |first=Vincent |date=2011 |title=THOR: Paul Butterworth – VFX Supervisor & Co-founder – Fuel VFX |url=https://www.artofvfx.com/?p=1304 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |website=The Art of VFX}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Captain America PSAs}}''' are a series of [[public service announcements]] (website)|Collider]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Contest of Champions}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics storyline [[Marvel Super Hero Contest of Champions|of the same name]]) is a gladiator tournament held on [[Sakaar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sakaar]] by the [[Grandmaster (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Grandmaster]]. His tower displays models of the heads of past champions, which resemble [[Man-Thing]], [[Ares (Marvel Comics)|Ares]], [[Bi-Beast]], Dark-Crawler, [[Fin Fang Foom]], and [[Beta Ray Bill]] from the comics in addition to the [[Hulk (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hulk]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Flint |first=Hanna |date=July 24, 2017 |title=Thor: Ragnarok Trailer May Have Man-Thing & Beta Ray Bill Easter Eggs |url=https://screenrant.com/thor-ragnarok-trailer-man-thing-beta-ray-bill-easter-eggs/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170725045659/https://screenrant.com/thor-ragnarok-trailer-man-thing-beta-ray-bill-easter-eggs/ |archive-date=July 25, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Russell |first=Bradley |date=October 19, 2017 |title=Kevin Feige reveals his favourite Thor: Ragnarok Easter egg (and you may have already seen it) |url=https://www.gamesradar.com/kevin-feige-reveals-his-favourite-thor-ragnarok-easter-egg-and-you-may-have-already-seen-it/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171019134510/https://www.gamesradar.com/kevin-feige-reveals-his-favourite-thor-ragnarok-easter-egg-and-you-may-have-already-seen-it/ |archive-date=October 19, 2017 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[GamesRadar+]]}}</ref> Other gladiators include Thor, [[Korg (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Korg]], and [[Miek (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Miek]]. Loki lands on the planet as well but is able to ingratiate himself with the Grandmaster and watches the games from his private [[Box (theatre)|box]]. When designing the gladiator arena on Sakaar for ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]'', production designer [[Dan Hennah]] studied [[Roman gladiators]] and decided to go "all alien with it", surrounding the arena with "standing up bleachers".<ref name="HennahRagnarok">{{Cite web |last=Fujikawa |first=Jenn |date=October 19, 2017 |title=Jack Kirby's Design Influence Is A Force In 'Thor: Ragnarok' |url=https://www.marvel.com/articles/movies/jack-kirby-s-design-influence-is-a-force-in-thor-ragnarok |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180118025155/https://news.marvel.com/movies/78891/jack-kirbys-design-influence-force-thor-ragnarok/ |archive-date=January 18, 2018 |access-date=July 31, 2021 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref> * The '''{{visible anchor|Elementals}}''' (based on the Marvel Comics team [[Elementals (Marvel Comics)|of the same name]]) are a series of illusions created by the use of projectors and drones used by [[Quentin Beck (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Quentin Beck / Mysterio]] to wreak havoc across the world. To mask their nature, Beck claimed that the Elementals were superpowered entities from Earth-833 that emerged from an inter-dimensional rift caused by the Snap. This iteration consists of the Wind, Earth, Fire, and Water Elementals; who are modeled after [[Cyclone (Marvel Comics)|Cyclone]], [[Sandman (Marvel Comics)|Sandman]], [[Molten Man]], and [[Hydro-Man]] respectively.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Holmes |first=Adam |date=July 4, 2019 |title=How Kevin Feige And Jon Watts Feel About Potentially Bringing Green Goblin And Doc Ock Back To The Spider-Man Movies |url=https://www.cinemablend.com/news/2476052/how-kevin-feige-and-jon-watts-feel-about-potentially-bringing-green-goblin-and-doc-ock-back-to-the-spider-man-movies |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190705100329/https://www.cinemablend.com/news/2476052/how-kevin-feige-and-jon-watts-feel-about-potentially-bringing-green-goblin-and-doc-ock-back-to-the-spider-man-movies |archive-date=July 5, 2019 |access-date=July 5, 2019 |website=[[CinemaBlend]]}}</ref> Quentin Beck, operating under the guise of Mysterio, claimed that they were born in a black hole and ravaged his reality of Earth-833. After Mysterio defeats the Wind and Earth Elementals [[off-screen]], he goes on to fight the Water Elemental in Venice while [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]] and [[Maria Hill (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Maria Hill]] persuade [[Peter Parker (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Spider-Man]] to help Mysterio defeat the Fire Elemental in Prague. After finding a holographic projector, Peter Parker and [[MJ (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|MJ]] learn the truth and are hunted down by Beck and his accomplices, who create an Elemental fusion monster to distract the world while he sets out to kill them. His plans are foiled when Spider-Man deactivates the drones. * The '''{{visible anchor|Emergence}}''' is an [[Global catastrophic risk|apocalyptic event]] that results in a new [[Celestials (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Celestial]] being born after being incubated in a [[Planetary core|planet's core]] for millennia while the planet's [[Indigenous peoples|native population]] flourishes on the surface. Once a planet's population reaches a suitable amount, the Celestial bursts through the planet's [[Mantle (geology)|mantle]] and [[Crust (geology)|crust]], destroying its egg along with its inhabitants to propagate life elsewhere in the Universe.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bojalad |first=Alec |date=November 5, 2021 |title=Marvel's Eternals Ending Explained |url=https://www.denofgeek.com/movies/marvels-eternals-ending-explained/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211106012653/https://www.denofgeek.com/movies/marvels-eternals-ending-explained/ |archive-date=November 6, 2021 |access-date=November 10, 2021 |website=[[Den of Geek]]}}</ref> A group of ten [[Eternals (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Eternals]] are sent by the Celestial [[Arishem (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Arishem]] to Earth to eradicate the invasive [[Deviants (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Deviants]] and ensure the continual growth and advancement of Earth's population, but they instead develop a love for humanity and prevent the Emergence of the Celestial [[Tiamut]] after [[the Blip]] temporarily delays the event.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Keane |first=Sean |date=November 8, 2021 |title=Eternals movie ending explained: Marvel links, sequel hints |url=https://www.cnet.com/news/eternals-movie-ending-explained-marvel-links-sequel-hints/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211108140917/https://www.cnet.com/news/eternals-movie-ending-explained-marvel-links-sequel-hints/ |archive-date=November 8, 2021 |access-date=November 10, 2021 |website=[[CNET]]}}</ref> [[File:Mursi Tribe, Ethiopia (7999701171).jpg|upright|thumb|130px|Surma person with arm scarification]] * '''{{visible anchor|Killmonger's scars}}''' are a series of around 3000 self-inflicted "[[Scarification|crocodile scars]]" covering [[Killmonger (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Erik "Killmonger" Stevens]]' body. Each one represents a confirmed kill from his time as an American [[black ops]] [[United States Navy SEALs|Navy SEAL]]. The scars are intended to resemble the scar tattoos of the [[Mursi people|Mursi]] and [[Surma people|Surma]] tribes,<ref>{{Cite magazine |last=Breznican |first=Anthony |date=July 12, 2017 |title=Black Panther: 20 New Images From the Marvel Adventure! – Killmonger's Tribal Scars |url=https://ew.com/movies/black-panther-new-images-marvel/killmongers-tribal-scars |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170715082018/https://ew.com/movies/black-panther-new-images-marvel/killmongers-tribal-scars/ |archive-date=July 15, 2017 |access-date=July 12, 2017 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]]}}</ref> and consisted of 90 individually sculpted silicone molds that took two-and-a-half hours to apply.<ref>{{Cite web |title=''Black Panther'' Press Kit |url=https://www.wdsmediafile.com/media/BlackPanther/writen-material/BlackPanther5a7e0853a38c3.pdf |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190310201206/https://www.wdsmediafile.com/media/BlackPanther/writen-material/BlackPanther5a7e0853a38c3.pdf |archive-date=March 10, 2019 |access-date=February 16, 2018 |publisher=[[Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures]] |page=21}}</ref> [[Michael B. Jordan]], who portrays Killmonger, had to sit in a sauna for two hours at the end of the day to remove the prosthetics when filming ''[[Black Panther (film)|Black Panther]]''.<ref name="Fashionista" /> * '''{{visible anchor|Loki's horned helmet}}''' is a golden horned helmet worn by Loki as well as his variants, including [[Sylvie (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Sylvie]], [[Classic Loki]], [[Kid Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kid Loki]], [[President Loki]], and [[Alligator Loki]]. The helmet is a symbol of Asgardian royalty and also bears [[Satan]]ic imagery, symbolizing the devil, according to Loki actor [[Tom Hiddleston]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Burchman |first=Cori |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190428164826/https://gizmodo.com/captain-americas-to-do-list-is-different-depending-on-1562463536 |archive-date=April 28, 2019 |access-date=April 24, 2021 |website=[[Gizmodo]]}}</ref> Later, the notebook was passed on to Rogers' best friend, [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]], who used it to keep a list of people whom he had [[Wrongdoing|wronged]] during [[#Winter Soldier Program|his time as the Winter Soldier]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ambrose |first=Anthony |date=April 3, 2021 |title=Falcon and Winter Soldier Reveals What Happened to Steve Rogers' Notebook |url=https://www.cbr.com/falcon-winter-soldier-steve-rogers-notebook/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210403231055/https://www.cbr.com/falcon-winter-soldier-steve-rogers-notebook/ |archive-date=April 3, 2021 |access-date=April 24, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Erdmann |first=Kevin |date=April 7, 2021 |title=Bucky's Notebook Makes Him The Opposite Of Steve Rogers (Not The Same) |url=https://screenrant.com/falcon-winter-soldier-bucky-notebook-steve-rogers-different/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210408002050/https://screenrant.com/falcon-winter-soldier-bucky-notebook-steve-rogers-different/ |archive-date=April 8, 2021 |access-date=April 24, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> Eventually, after Barnes finishes making amends with everyone on the list, he leaves the notebook with his therapist, Dr. Christina Raynor, thanking her for her help.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jirak |first=Jamie |date=April 24, 2021 |title=The Falcon and the Winter Soldier: Why Did Bucky Give Away Steve Rogers' Book? |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/the-falcon-and-the-winter-soldier-why-did-bucky-give-away-steve-rogers-book/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210424210334/https://comicbook.com/marvel/news/the-falcon-and-the-winter-soldier-why-did-bucky-give-away-steve-rogers-book/ |archive-date=April 24, 2021 |access-date=April 24, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> * '''''{{visible anchor|WandaVision}}''''' is a sitcom broadcast by [[Wanda Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wanda Maximoff]] in the [[Westview anomaly]] using her [[#Chaos magic|Chaos Magic]] abilities. Set in the town of [[Westview, New Jersey]], the sitcom centered on Wanda Maximoff's family, which included the [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]], [[Billy Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Billy Maximoff]], and [[Tommy Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tommy Maximoff]]. It also "starred" [[Agatha Harkness (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Agatha Harkness]] as Agnes, [[Ralph Bohner]] as [[Pietro Maximoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Pietro Maximoff]], [[Monica Rambeau (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Monica Rambeau]] as Geraldine, Todd and Sharon Davis as Arthur and Mrs. Hart, Abilash Tandon as Norm, Harold and Sarah Proctor as Phil and Dottie Jones, John Collins as Herb, Isabel Matsueda as Beverly, an unknown actor as Dennis, and [[Darcy Lewis]] as an unnamed character. The show was ultimately "[[Cancellation (broadcasting)|canceled]]" by Wanda (as described by [[Tyler Hayward]]) after her actions were discovered by Vision and S.W.O.R.D. and she expanded the Hex to save Vision's "life". * The '''{{visible anchor|Winter Soldier Book}}''' is a book formerly used by [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]] which contained Russian trigger words that would activate their Winter Soldiers into deadly assassins when spoken. It was later discovered by [[Helmut Zemo (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Helmut Zemo]] and used to activate Barnes into the Winter Soldier, using the words from the book: "Longing, rusted, seventeen, daybreak, furnace, nine, benign, homecoming, one, freight car".{{efn|{{langx|ru|link=no|Желание, ржавый, семнадцать, рассвет, печь, девять, добросердечный, возвращение на родину, один, товарный вагон}}}} The trigger words' effect is eventually nullified after Barnes is healed in Wakanda.
==Major events== {{For-multi|a timeline of events in the MCU|Marvel Cinematic Universe timeline|a list of major events from the Marvel Comics|Major events of the Marvel Universe}}
===Pre–21st century=== * The '''{{visible anchor|Kree–Skrull War}}''' (995 AD–present) (based on the Marvel Comics storyline [[Kree–Skrull War|of the same name]]) is an ongoing thousand-year-long conflict between the [[Kree (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Kree]] and [[Skrulls (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Skrulls]], with the Kree striving to wipe out the entire Skrull race.<ref>{{Cite magazine |title=Exclusive 'Captain Marvel' video breaks down the intergalactic Kree-Skrull War |url=https://ew.com/movies/2019/02/22/captain-marvel-video-kree-skrull-war/ |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |access-date=June 19, 2021}}</ref> In the 20th Century, the Kree scientist [[Mar-Vell (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Mar-Vell]] rebels and travels to Earth to help Skrull refugees escape from Kree forces and later assists [[Carol Danvers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Carol Danvers]]. [[File:Cap's Shield.jpg|thumb|right|[[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]] was equipped with a [[Captain America's shield (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|vibranium shield]], depicted here, in [[World War II]].]] * '''[[World War II]]''' (1939–1945) was a [[global conflict]] between the [[Allies (World War II)|Allies]] and [[Axis powers|Axis]]. The United States create the super-soldier [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Captain America]] (Steve Rogers) to fight in the conflict, while [[Nazi Germany]] create the [[Johann Schmidt (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Red Skull]] (Johann Schmidt), leader of [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]]. In an alternate reality explored in [[What If... Captain Carter Were the First Avenger?|the first episode]] of ''[[What If...? (TV series)|What If...?]]'', [[Peggy Carter (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Peggy Carter]] takes the [[Super Soldier Serum (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Super Soldier Serum]] instead of Rogers and becomes [[Captain Carter]].<ref name="WIDeadline">{{Cite web |last=D'Alessandro |first=Anthony |date=August 1, 2021 |title='What If...?' Actor Jeffrey Wright On Chadwick Boseman's Final "Mythic" Turn As T'Challa In MCU |url=https://deadline.com/2021/08/chadwick-boseman-what-if-jeffrey-wright-creators-disney-series-1234807268/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210801202517/https://deadline.com/2021/08/chadwick-boseman-what-if-jeffrey-wright-creators-disney-series-1234807268/ |archive-date=August 1, 2021 |access-date=September 28, 2021 |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]]}}</ref> * The '''[[Cold War]]''' (1947–1991) was a period of [[geopolitical]] tension between the United States and the [[Soviet Union]] during which the U.S. experimented with the Super Soldier Serum on Black soldiers, resulting in the creation of the super-soldier [[Isaiah Bradley (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Isaiah Bradley]]. Meanwhile, Hydra (having infiltrated the Soviet Union) establishes the [[#Winter Soldier Program|Winter Soldier Program]] and the [[Red Room (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Red Room]], transforming [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]] and [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]] into elite assassins. * The '''{{visible anchor|assassination of Howard and Maria Stark|Assassination of Howard and Maria Stark}}''' (1991) was the murder of [[Howard Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Howard]] and [[Maria Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Maria Stark]] by a brainwashed Bucky Barnes under Vasily Karpov's control on December 16, 1991, in [[Long Island]], New York. When the couple are on their way to [[the Pentagon]], Barnes arrives on a motorcycle and slashes the front tire of their car, causing it to crash before [[Assault|beating]] Howard to death and [[strangling]] Maria. The assassination is covered as a car crash, results in [[Obadiah Stane (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Obadiah Stane]] assuming control of [[Stark Industries]], and is used by [[Helmut Zemo (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Helmut Zemo]] to instigate tensions between [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]] and Steve Rogers during the [[Avengers Civil War]].
===2010s=== * The '''[[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)#Becoming Iron Man|kidnapping of Tony Stark]]''' (2010)<!--See [[Marvel Cinematic Universe timeline]], which supports this year--> was carried out by the [[Ten Rings (organization)|Ten Rings]] terrorist organization under orders of [[Obadiah Stane (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Obadiah Stane]], a rival of [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]]. While performing a demonstration of his new [[#Jericho Missile|Jericho Missile]] for the [[U.S. Air Force]] in [[Kunar Province|Kunar]], [[Afghanistan]], Stark is kidnapped and forced to build the Jericho missile, but secretly builds [[Iron Man's armor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|a suit of armor]] with the help of [[Ho Yinsen (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ho Yinsen]] instead and using it to escape. In an alternate reality explored in [[What If... Killmonger Rescued Tony Stark?|the sixth episode]] of ''[[What If...? (TV series)|What If...?]]'', Stark is rescued by [[Killmonger (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Erik "Killmonger" Stevens]] and never becomes Iron Man.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Elvy |first=Craig |date=September 15, 2021 |title=Every MCU Easter Egg In What If? Episode 6 |url=https://screenrant.com/marvel-what-if-episode-6-mcu-marvel-easter-eggs/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210915200338/https://screenrant.com/marvel-what-if-episode-6-mcu-marvel-easter-eggs/ |archive-date=September 15, 2021 |access-date=September 28, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> * '''[[Fury's Big Week#Plot|Fury's Big Week]]''' (2011) was a series of events monitored by [[S.H.I.E.L.D. (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|S.H.I.E.L.D.]] which transpired in one week.<ref>{{Cite web |date=March 6, 2012 |title=Writing The Avengers Movie Prelude |url=http://marvel.com/news/comics/2012/2/3/18059/writing_the_avengers_movie_prelude |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140606230006/http://marvel.com/news/comics/2012/2/3/18059/writing_the_avengers_movie_prelude |archive-date=June 6, 2014 |access-date=April 28, 2021 |website=[[Marvel.com]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite magazine |last=Franich |first=Darren |date=May 17, 2012 |title='Avengers' timeline: Nick Fury's busy week |url=https://ew.com/article/2012/05/17/avengers-marvel-movie-timeline/ |url-status=live |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150215103135/https://ew.com/article/2012/05/17/avengers-marvel-movie-timeline/ |archive-date=February 15, 2015 |access-date=April 28, 2021}}</ref> Over the course of the seven days, S.H.I.E.L.D. director [[Nick Fury (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Nick Fury]] sends [[Natasha Romanoff (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Natasha Romanoff]] to spy on Tony Stark and [[Bruce Banner (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bruce Banner]], [[Phil Coulson]] and [[Clint Barton (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Clint Barton]] investigate [[atmospheric disturbance]] above [[New Mexico]] and encounter [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]], while [[Erik Selvig]] is recruited to [[#Project Pegasus|Project Pegasus]]. In an alternate reality explored in [[What If... the World Lost Its Mightiest Heroes?|the third episode]] of ''What If...?'', Fury's plans to assemble the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]] are disrupted when its candidates are assassinated by [[Hank Pym (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hank Pym]].<ref>{{Cite web |last1=Maytum |first1=Matt |last2=Bradshaw |first2=Paul |date=July 22, 2021 |title=Exclusive: Marvel's What If...? will feature a "tragic love story" and an "Agatha Christie episode" |url=https://www.gamesradar.com/marvel-what-if-exclusive-image-killmonger/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210722165742/https://www.gamesradar.com/marvel-what-if-exclusive-image-killmonger/ |archive-date=July 22, 2021 |access-date=August 26, 2021 |website=[[Total Film]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Anderton |first=Ethan |date=August 10, 2021 |title=Marvel's 'What If...?' Review: The Multiverse Offers Mixed Results with Stylish Animation and Rushed Storytelling |url=https://www.slashfilm.com/marvels-what-if-review-the-multiverse-offers-mixed-results-with-stylish-animation-and-rushed-storytelling/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210810175800/https://www.slashfilm.com/marvels-what-if-review-the-multiverse-offers-mixed-results-with-stylish-animation-and-rushed-storytelling/ |archive-date=August 10, 2021 |access-date=August 26, 2021 |website=[[/Film]]}}</ref> * The '''[[Battle of New York (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of New York]]''' (2012) was a large-scale battle between [[Loki (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Loki]] and his [[Chitauri (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Chitauri]] forces and the Avengers in [[Midtown Manhattan]], New York. The [[World Security Council]] later orders a [[nuclear strike]] against the city in an attempt to end the battle, but Tony Stark intervenes and used the nuclear missile to destroy the Chitauri mother ship. In an alternate reality explored in ''[[Avengers: Endgame]]'' and [[Glorious Purpose (Loki season 1)|the first episode]] of ''[[Loki (TV series)|Loki]]'', Loki escapes using the [[Tesseract (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tesseract]] following the battle, but is captured by the [[Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Variance Authority]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sandwell |first=Ian |date=July 21, 2019 |title=Marvel finally confirm Phase 4 movies at Comic-Con |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a28394640/marvel-mcu-phase-4-movies-release-dates/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190721090513/https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a28394640/marvel-mcu-phase-4-movies-release-dates/ |archive-date=July 21, 2019 |access-date=September 29, 2021 |website=[[Digital Spy]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Davis |first=Brandon |date=May 15, 2019 |title=How Loki's New Timeline Plays Out After Avengers: Endgame |url=https://comicbook.com/marvel/2019/05/15/avengers-endgame-loki-new-timeline-space-stone/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190518101127/https://comicbook.com/marvel/2019/05/15/avengers-endgame-loki-new-timeline-space-stone/ |archive-date=May 18, 2019 |access-date=September 29, 2021 |website=[[ComicBook.com]]}}</ref> * The '''[[Hydra Uprising]]''' (2014–2015) was an attempt by [[Hydra (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hydra]] to seize world control and establish a global totalitarian state using [[Project Insight (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Project Insight]] to eliminate influential individuals who posed a threat to the group. The plan fails after the [[Helicarriers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Helicarriers]] used are disabled and [[Alexander Pierce (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Alexander Pierce]] is killed by Nick Fury, resulting in the collapse of S.H.I.E.L.D. Hydra is defeated shortly after by the Avengers in [[Sokovia]]. The idea of S.H.I.E.L.D.'s collapse was suggested by Marvel Studios president [[Kevin Feige]], which writers [[Christopher Markus and Stephen McFeely]] then implemented into ''[[Captain America: The Winter Soldier]]''.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hunt |first=James |date=August 14, 2014 |title=Christopher Markus interview: writing Captain America 2 and 3 |url=https://www.denofgeek.com/movies/christopher-markus/31707/christopher-markus-interview-writing-captain-america-2-and-3 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140905231020/http://www.denofgeek.com/movies/christopher-markus/31707/christopher-markus-interview-writing-captain-america-2-and-3 |archive-date=September 5, 2014 |access-date=July 14, 2021 |publisher=Den of Geek}}</ref> * The '''[[Battle of Sokovia]]''' (2015) was a battle in Sokovia between the Avengers and Ultron, with Ultron attempting to cause [[human extinction]] by dropping the capital city of Sokovia on Earth. The plan is foiled after the city is destroyed by the Avengers, and Ultron is killed by the [[Vision (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vision]]. In an alternate reality explored in [[What If... Ultron Won?|the eighth episode]] of ''What If...?'', Ultron successfully transfers his consciousness to Vision's body and gains the power of the [[Infinity Stones]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Elvy |first=Craig |date=September 22, 2021 |title=Every MCU Easter Egg In What If? Episode 7 |url=https://screenrant.com/marvel-what-if-episode-7-mcu-marvel-easter-eggs/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210923012429/https://screenrant.com/marvel-what-if-episode-7-mcu-marvel-easter-eggs/ |archive-date=September 23, 2021 |access-date=September 22, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> * The '''[[Avengers Civil War]]''' (2016) (based on the Marvel Comics storyline [[Civil War (comics)|of the same name]]) was a conflict between the Avengers over the Sokovia Accords instigated by [[Helmut Zemo (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Helmut Zemo]]. The conflict is further instigated by the accidental bombing of [[Lagos]], [[Nigeria]] by Wanda Maximoff, the assassination of King [[T'Chaka (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|T'Chaka]] by Helmut Zemo, and the [[assassination of Howard and Maria Stark]] by [[Bucky Barnes (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Bucky Barnes]], culminating in clashes at the [[Leipzig/Halle Airport]] in Germany and the Hydra Siberian Facility in Siberia. * '''[[Ragnarok (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ragnarök]]''' (2017) (based on the Norse mythological event [[Ragnarök|of the same name]]) was the prophesied destruction of [[Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgard]] by [[Surtur (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Surtur]]. Classified by the [[Time Variance Authority (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Variance Authority]] as a Class Seven [[apocalypse|apocalyptic event]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=David |first=Margaret |date=June 16, 2021 |title=Loki Confirms the TRUE Toll of Ragnarok |url=https://www.cbr.com/loki-thor-ragnarok-asgardian-casualties-confirmed/ |access-date=June 19, 2021 |website=[[Comic Book Resources]]}}</ref> it is initiated by Thor after he realizes that it is the only way to defeat [[Hela (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Hela]], sending Loki to unite [[#Surtur's crown|Surtur's crown]] with the [[#Eternal Flame|Eternal Flame]]. * The '''[[Infinity War (event)|Infinity War]]''' (2018) (based on the Marvel Comics storylines ''[[The Infinity Gauntlet]]'' and ''[[Infinity (comic book)|Infinity]]'') was a conflict between [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] and the Avengers, Guardians of the Galaxy, and [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakanda]]ns over the Infinity Stones, with Thanos seeking to use them to wipe out half of life in the universe due to his fears of universal [[overpopulation]] and [[poverty|deprivation]]. It commences with Thanos obtaining the [[Power Stone (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Power]] and [[Space Stone]]s from [[Xandar (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Xandar]] and the ''[[Statesman (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Statesman]]'', followed by an attack in [[Greenwich Village]] by [[Ebony Maw (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ebony Maw]] and [[Cull Obsidian]]. Thanos then retrieves the [[Reality Stone]] on [[Knowhere (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Knowhere]] and the [[Soul Stone]] on [[Vormir]], sacrificing his daughter [[Gamora (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Gamora]] in the process. The conflict culminates in two concurrent battles in Wakanda and on [[Titan (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Titan]], both of which end in Thanos' victory and allow him to obtain the [[Time Stone|Time]] and [[Mind Stone]]s and complete the [[Infinity Gauntlet (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Infinity Gauntlet]]. Thanos then proceeds to snap his fingers, wiping out half of all life in the universe and initiating [[the Blip]]. In an alternate reality explored in [[What If... Zombies?!|the fifth episode]] of ''What If...?'', Thanos' crusade coincides with a [[zombie apocalypse]] on Earth.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Howard |first=Kirsten |date=September 8, 2021 |title=What If...? Episode 5 Review: Magnificent Marvel Zombie Chaos |url=https://www.denofgeek.com/tv/what-if-episode-5-review/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210908173925/https://www.denofgeek.com/tv/what-if-episode-5-review/ |archive-date=September 8, 2021 |access-date=September 28, 2021 |website=[[Den of Geek]]}}</ref> * '''[[The Blip]]''' (2018–2023) was the five-year period between the [[Genocide|extermination]] of half of all life in the universe by Thanos and their subsequent resurrection by Bruce Banner.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Greby |first=James |date=July 4, 2019 |title=How Spider-Man: Far From Home Explains Post-Snap Life in the MCU |work=[[Vulture (website)|Vulture]] |url=https://www.vulture.com/2019/07/how-far-from-home-explains-post-snap-life-in-the-mcu.html |url-status=live |access-date=March 7, 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210212135330/https://www.vulture.com/2019/07/how-far-from-home-explains-post-snap-life-in-the-mcu.html |archive-date=February 12, 2021}}</ref> It manifested in the form of the mass disintegration of individual beings into ashes, while the reversal had the same ashes reforming into the previously deceased individuals, who mostly reappeared in the same location with no direct awareness of what occurred. The [[Global Repatriation Council]] (GRC) is later set up to aid those displaced by the Blip, although many such as the [[Flag Smashers]] oppose their efforts.
===2020s=== * The '''[[Time Heist (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Time Heist]]''' (2023) was the retrieval of the six [[Infinity Stones]] by the [[Avengers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers]] from four [[Parallel universes in fiction|alternate timelines]] to undo [[the Blip]], in which three teams [[Time travel|travel back in time]] using the [[#Quantum Tunnel|Quantum Tunnel]] to 2012 New York and 1970 [[Camp Lehigh]], 2013 [[Asgard (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgard]], as well as 2014 [[#Morag|Morag]] and [[Vormir (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Vormir]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bacon |first=Thomas |date=April 27, 2019 |title=Every Previous Marvel Movie Visited In Avengers: Endgame |url=https://screenrant.com/avengers-endgame-marvel-movies-visited-time-travel/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210109084006/https://screenrant.com/avengers-endgame-marvel-movies-visited-time-travel/ |archive-date=January 9, 2021 |access-date=February 15, 2021 |website=[[Screen Rant]]}}</ref> The Avengers then unite the six stones into the [[#Nano Gauntlet|Nano Gauntlet]] before Banner snaps his fingers, restoring the lives of half of the Universe. * The '''[[Battle of Earth (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Battle of Earth]]''' (2023) was a large-scale battle that took place in the ruins of the [[Avengers Compound (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Avengers Compound]] between the Avengers, [[Guardians of the Galaxy (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Guardians of the Galaxy]], [[Masters of the Mystic Arts]], [[Asgardians (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Asgardians]], [[Ravagers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Ravagers]], and [[Wakanda (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Wakandans]] against alternate versions of [[Thanos (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thanos]] and his army from 2014. After subduing [[Tony Stark (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Tony Stark]], [[Thor (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Thor]], and [[Steve Rogers (Marvel Cinematic Universe)|Steve Rogers]], Thanos summons his army from the ''[[#Sanctuary II|Sanctuary II]]'' with the goal of acquiring the Nano Gauntlet, but is interrupted by the arrival of the restored Guardians, Asgardians, Ravagers, and Wakandans. The two sides clash, and the battle culminates with Stark sacrificing himself to disintegrate Thanos and his army.
